emails.subject: Fw: CI Event - Recording, Slides and Resources – Developing Custom GPTs for CI
emails.body:
BUMP
________________________________

From: Rosalind French <rosalind.french@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 02 June 2026 4:36 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: CI Event - Recording, Slides and Resources – Developing Custom GPTs for CI 
 

Dear Kevin,

I’ve received a request for access to today’s CI CoP event, Developing Custom GPTs for Continuous Improvement.

 

The recording, presentation slides and transcript from the session are now available here <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-strategy-governance-planning/SitePages/Events.aspx#developing-custom-gpts-for-continuous-improvement>  along with recordings of our previous CI events.

 

A huge thank you to Vincent Bryce (Head of Continuous Improvement, Faculty of Law) and Lyndsey Middleton (Lead Business Analyst, IT Services) for sharing their knowledge, expertise and practical insights with the CI Community. Their engaging demonstration helped demystify custom GPTs and showed that creating useful AI-powered tools is often less about technical expertise and more about understanding users, defining a clear purpose and making thoughtful design decisions.

During the session, Vincent and Lyndsey explored what custom GPTs are, how they can support Continuous Improvement activities, and the key considerations involved in designing, sharing and maintaining them. Together, attendees helped create a prototype CI-focused GPT, demonstrating how AI can be used to make knowledge, tools and resources more accessible.

Key Takeaways

*	Custom GPTs can help reduce repetitive prompting and provide more consistent outputs.
*	Effective GPTs are built around clear user needs, well-defined instructions and relevant knowledge sources.
*	AI can support CI activities such as process mapping, analysing feedback, exploring case studies, identifying improvement opportunities and drafting standard outputs.
*	Human judgement remains essential – GPTs are a tool to support decision-making, not replace it.
*	Ownership, governance and maintenance are critical considerations, particularly when sharing GPTs with others.
*	Custom GPTs should be regularly reviewed and updated to ensure their knowledge remains accurate and relevant.

 

As promised, here are the resources and links referenced during the session:

*	AI Competency Centre <https://oerc.ox.ac.uk/ai-centre> 
*	AI Competency Centre Training Events <https://oerc.ox.ac.uk/ai-centre/training-events> 
*	University Guidance on Safe and Responsible Use of Generative AI <https://www.ox.ac.uk/students/life/it/guidance-safe-and-responsible-use-gen-ai-tools> 
*	Oxford GPT Library <https://oerc.ox.ac.uk/ai-centre/generative-ai-tools/oxford-gpt-library> 
*	AICC ChatGPT tool guide: custom GPTs <https://canvas.ox.ac.uk/courses/289256/pages/custom-gpts?module_item_id=3073469>   
*	Logging into ChatGPT Edu with your Oxford account (AICC) <https://oerc.ox.ac.uk/ai-centre/generative-ai-tools/chatgpt-edu>  

 

The prototype GPT created during the event can be accessed here - Ci-ci the CI Helper <https://chatgpt.com/g/g-6a1ecb0f573c81919e02910824862d03-ci-ci-the-ci-helper> 

Please note that this GPT was created live during the session as a demonstration and prototype. The knowledge files used within it will not be actively maintained or updated. As a result, information contained within the GPT may become out of date over time and should not be relied upon as an authoritative source. For the latest Continuous Improvement resources, guidance, tools and case studies, please refer to the Focus Toolkit <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-strategy-governance-planning/SitePages/Focus-toolkit.aspx> 

 

We'd love your feedback

Please take a minute to complete our short feedback form <https://forms.office.com/e/dKsMBZSwMm> . Your feedback is incredibly important and plays a key role in shaping future CI CoP events, helping us understand what is most valuable to t

emails.subject: College Staff Structure
emails.to: Crispin Muncaster; Anne Mortimer; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Steve McBrearty; Kevin Lelitte
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

I have been made aware of Katherine Corr’s message below:

 

Can I please check; does this impact the way we have configured the structure of Company 90 in PeopleXD? The proposed structure would introduce new Entity 2 codes, which will either be in this format: 



Or this format:



 

Level 2 codes map directly to Management Unit in PeopleXD, and we have set this up as follows:



 

Katherine’s message seemed to suggest that this change would need to happen so that college staff could be recorded in PeopleXD, but I’m not sure that’s really true is it? Especially as we have already set up a structure which is different to their proposal.

 

I’m not going to lie, I think the proposal in general is long overdue, I have no idea why we keep colleges at level 2 and don’t pad the structure so that equivalent entities sit at the same level, but I’m just wanting to understand the impact on what we’re doing if this goes ahead – would you expect us to amend the structure to align with the level 2/management unit codes that PACS are using? And what would that mean for the rest of the structure? Where would we split colleges and permanent private halls?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: Support Call# 11688462: Ash VEA team
emails.body:
Hi Cathy,

Thank you for getting in touch.
I have been asked to pick this up and sorry to hear the Visitor Experience team are having difficulties with rostering and the Leave and Absence module, particularly as issues were raised previously without a response. 
I understand you were going to ask the team to put together a written summary of their concerns. If you are able to share that with me when it is ready, that would be helpful and will allow us to prepare properly before we meet.
We will review the position internally first and will then come back to you with next steps.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 






Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Sent: 16 June 2026 12:20
To: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Linda Pope <linda.pope@bodleian.ox.ac.uk <mailto:linda.pope@bodleian.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Ash VEA team

 

Dear support team,

 

Would it be possible for us to set up a meeting (Teams or in person) with someone from the support team to discuss the Visitor experience team in the Ash.  We met with the manager there today and there are some issues with the Leave and Absence module but also- they are not really able to use rostering any more and have highlighted some major concerns with rostering not working for them at all.  They have said they raised all of these issues with the project team but have never had a response- I have asked for them to summarise the issues and I can share this once I have it,

 

We also need some support with helping this team with their variable hours staff and how they can use the system for them as at the moment they are using XD as well as relying on spreadsheets,

 

Let me know if there is someone who might be available to meet with us as well as the managers in this team,

 

Many thanks

Cathy

 

Cathy Hamer | HR Operations Manager | GLAM HR Shared Service (Gardens, Libraries and Museums)  | University of Oxford

HR Shared Service Office ǀ Clarendon Building, Broad Street  ǀ Oxford ǀ OX1 3BG

Tel: 01865 (2)77170 | Email: cathy.hamer@bodleian.ox.ac.uk <mailto:cathy.hamer@bodleian.ox.ac.uk> 

 

Have you viewed your Staff Intranet? You may find the answer to your query here:

Bodleian Intranet <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/BODL-BodleianLibrariesIntranet/SitePages/Welcome-to-the-GLAM-HR-Shared-Service.aspx>

emails.subject: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD - Weekly Team Meeting notes - 23-Jun-26
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Simon Burford; Steve McBrearty; Kevin Lelitte
emails.body:
Dear All

 

Some notes from our team meeting earlier in the week. Apologies for the delay in getting these to you. Please note actions.

 

*	Simon explained that there are three types of appointment which can be used: 1)Normal standard post 2) Auto-authorized post 3) Auto-commenced post
*	Anne stated a preference for the auto ones since these allowed for the creation of buckets and generally offer more flexibility. The auto route is currently used for casuals, TSS and non-employees.  Action: AM to give some thought about what buckets are wanted – what parameters and generic roles are needed. Before deciding however, it was recommended that Action: AM to undertake the training first. 
*	Action: SM - Simon noted the need to consult Access Group on whether we needed planned appointments to do the data loads or whether we can load directly into a bucket post.
*	Personal Number sequencing was discussed. All entries in C90 will be given a new number, and it was agreed that there should not be a separate numbering system for C90. This means that a mechanism needs to be found for ensuring that numbers are not double allocated. There already exist PXD ‘no input days’ and so it was suggested that we could use these periods to do the imports.
*	It was agreed that we should start preparing dummy data ready for testing. Action: NK to prepare some dummy data. 
*	Simon confirmed that the environment is built with hierarchy structure in place - but we are awaiting the creation of posts then we can test import.
*	The ‘Proposal’ – is with Marie but sufficiently approved to allow us to move forward.
*	We will need to organize another meeting with Conor. Action: SB will anyway be talking to Conor and so will confirm.

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

 

 

Present: AM,CM,NC,NK,SB,SM

Apologies: KL

 

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Crispin Muncaster
PROJECT MANAGER
IT Services | University of Oxford

T: 01865 Â 616324
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/>

Hi Michelle,
Thanks for this. It's good to have clarity that this is a guidance issue rather than a fault or amendment. TIMDEP03 is clearly the right report for sickness absence, and hopefully the guidance will resolve Anna's concerns once you've been back to her.
Please do let me know if you need any further support in the meantime.

Kevin

From: Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, June 24, 2026 4:07 PM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Owner Required - Task# 50945166 for ServiceReq# 30404938


Hi Kevin,

 

I had previously queried this with Michael, please see response below – I will go back to the user that raised it.

 

I’ve reviewed this and ran TIMDEP03 Sickness Absence Reasons across the full University. When filtering the results to show records where the Sickness End Date is blank, the report returns 165 open‑ended sickness absences. In these cases, the Length of Sickness Episode (Days/Hours) displays as 0, which is expected behaviour for an absence that has not yet been closed.

 

TIMDEP04 Absence Reasons has been intentionally designed to return absences within a defined date range, using a “Start Date From” and “End Date To” parameter. Because of this structure, absences with no end date fall outside the logic of the report and will not be returned. Although TIMDEP04 includes sickness as part of its broader absence coverage, its primary purpose is to report on other leave types such as family leave and other leave. 

 

For sickness‑specific reporting, TIMDEP03 is the designated report and will return both closed and open‑ended sickness absences, as per the example extract below:

emails.to: Marie Cooksey; HR Systems Support; Anna Carter-Windle
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
Thanks for looping me in, and also to @Anna Carter-Windle <mailto:anna.carter-windle@tss.ox.ac.uk>  for the useful context.
I will reach out to Cathy directly and will also look for a date next week to set up a meeting with you, Simon and the wider team so we can review the position before we meet with Cathy.
I will keep you updated.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 






Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

From: Anna Carter-Windle <anna.carter-windle@tss.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 22 June 2026 5:30 PM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Support Call# 11688462: Ash VEA team
 

Hi Marie

 

I have had a hunt through my emails and will look through the files, however, as I wasn’t involved in the Rostering side of the project, I have limited info but will see what I can find. This is what I have so far:

 

In October, they had an issue with work group managers and access this was escalated to Sharon and Tracy, who replied it was in hand.

 

In March, they emailed asking how to manage variable hours staff through the rostering with leave and absence function, especially in recording sickness for variable hours staff. This very well could be one of the issues, and I recently emailed a proposal to Michael on how we might be able to manage sickness for variable hours staff.

 

The request to find a solution on this was raised to Tracy in 2025 and investigated by Sonhal (I believe ) but am unsure what the outcome was. However, the launch of leave and absence with rostering comms stated the following:

 

‘Detailed guidance outlining what each role can see and do within the system is available here <https://staff.web.ox.ac.uk/employee-self-service-view-roster-and-set-availability#collapse5699126> .

For any variable hour’s staff that won't be recording absence through the leave and absence functionality, the roster managers can continue to input this via the roster as per the previous process.’

 

I recently came across a work around using unrostered booking function within the system, which seemed to enable absence to be recorded for VH staff, and that absence would appear in reporting. If this is one of their issues, we may now have a potential solution that is with Michael on the FA roadmap.

 

In April it came to light that the rostering staff records were not being kept up to date by the GLAM HR team, they were not adding work schedules, team areas etc to the new appointments for rostered staff. This was escalated to Cathy and Sarah on 14 April by Tracy. I believe this was resolved at this point.

 

Hope this initial background info helps.

 

I will happily join the call if you want me to as I may be able to provide some context and insight into project approach and whether the system is being used correctly.

 

Kind regards

 

Anna

Advise Cathy we will review and then as a team we can agree a way forward, before we go and meet with Cathy formally. I would like this to move quite quickly, as we really

Jasmin

Subject: RE: UCEA Clinical pay uplift - April 2026

 

Morning, just to provide a quick update...

The Clinical Uplift has been input into our UAT environment, and Tracy has completed all the necessary checks and testing, confirming that everything is working as expected.

I also liaised with X5 to issue the revised rates and confirm our intention to implement the changes into the live system on 27 July 2026. They decided to release the revised rates last evening to ensure that research projects are costed as accurately as possible. They will be issuing communications to their users to reflect this.  Also, just for information, Steven Dawber has now taken over from Tricia Murkin as our contact.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

emails.from: Clive Poole - Head of Pensions and Payroll
emails.body:
USS 2026 valuation - message to all eligible staff members	  	 View newsletter online <https://newsletter.admin.ox.ac.uk/q/11mgrUy4gJ5x2jkLBFJpqpa/wv>  	
 	 
 <https://newsletter.admin.ox.ac.uk/i/amf_ouc/project_81/USS_PENSIONS_banner-01.jpg?size=580x0&w=Bp0dKVyLRiRJQ9kBK7nu7F3MFNSU> 	
 	 
To: all USS members and all staff eligible to join the USS pension scheme
From: Clive Poole, the University's new Head of Pensions and Payroll <https://newsletter.admin.ox.ac.uk/c/11eWmypzK0HgqNRtwXqj8OT77huMe> 
  

USS 2026 valuation: what you need to know


Dear colleague,

You may have seen references to the USS 2026 valuation and wondered what it means for you.

The valuation is a regular review of the financial health of the Universities Superannuation Scheme (USS). It is an important part of how the scheme is managed and helps determine whether contributions and benefits remain sustainable over the long term.

USS has recently reported that the scheme is in a strong financial position. The 2026 valuation will review the scheme's funding position in detail and consider what this means for the future.

We know that pensions can be complex and that many members may not be familiar with how valuations work. To help, we have put together a short question and answer guide <https://newsletter.admin.ox.ac.uk/c/11eWmysl1Rbq9MX4N4FHeqnUfMV4Q>  explaining: 

*	what the valuation is
*	why it happens
*	what could happen next
*	what members can expect during the process.

There is no action required from members at this stage.

We will continue to share updates as more information becomes available. If you have any further questions, please contact uss@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:uss@admin.ox.ac.uk?subject=USS%202026%20valuation%20> .

Kind regards,

Clive Poole
Head of Pensions and Payroll
 

 	 
 	 
 	 

 	 
 <https://newsletter.admin.ox.ac.uk/t/11qqL47l8NsaqRIyY.png>  <https://newsletter.admin.ox.ac.uk/r/11qqL47l8NsaqRIyY.png>

emails.subject: FW: Health Surveillance Review
emails.body:
Hi Kevin, 

 

Just for your awareness, Sharon has asked for an in‑person session on 9th September to review Health Surveillance issues in detail. She’s keen to use this as a session for a more open and structured discussion around the ongoing challenges and potential solutions.

 

I completely understand her frustrations and agree that this has now reached a point where a more formal review is needed, given the volume and complexity of issues we’re seeing.

 

From my side, I also wanted to flag that with the DSE system now in BAU, alongside the other systems I’m currently supporting, this is starting to impact the amount of time I can dedicate to each area. With multiple systems requiring ongoing support (and the potential addition of another H&S system), it does raise some considerations around long‑term sustainability and capacity.

 

Happy to discuss this further ahead of the session if helpful.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting> 

 

Whatfix: Discover our self-help tool for PeopleXD <https://hrsystems.admin.ox.ac.uk/whatfix> 

 

             <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>  

 

From: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 24 June 2026 12:06
To: Sonya Zyssemilch <sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Angie Pullinger <angie.pullinger@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Helena Uddin <helena.uddin@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Christi Prokou <christi.prokou@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Gillian Wilson <gillian.wilson@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Angela Fountain <angela.fountain@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Karen Ralph <karen.ralph@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Health Surveillance Review 

 

Hi all

 

The amount of difficulties that are being experienced regarding health surveillance continue. 

 

In my opinion the whole issue needs a review, and frank and robust discussion. 

 

As such I feel that a day dedicated to this discussion/finding solutions would be of benefit – I have identified 9th September 2026 for this discussion (which I know feels a long time away but there is limited availability before Sonya departs for the summer, and I acknowledge that Gill W is on leave) and would like to invite Chris and James to attend in the afternoon to try and identify some solutions. 

 

Prior to this date, there is some work going on regarding training etc, and I am likely to be asking you for an account of your concerns/difficulties

 

Regards

 

 

Sharon Naylor

Head of Occupational Health 

University of Oxford Occupational Health Department

10 Parks Road

Oxford OX1 3PD

Telephone (direct line): 01865 282686

Email: sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 

Web: Occupational Health Services <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OxIntranet-health-safety-wellbeing/SitePages/occupational-health-services.aspx?web=1>  

 

 

Occupational Health Service Provision

Please note that we are in a position of high demand and as such there may be a backlog of requests to process, waiting time for appointments/responses will be longer than usual. 

 

Further information and guidance can be found on Occupational Health Services <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OxIntranet-health-safety-wellbeing/SitePages/occupational-health-services.aspx?web=1>

emails.subject: RE: Minutes – Cority Product User Group (PUG), 16 June 2026
emails.body:
Hi Kevin, 

 

Thanks for sharing this. 

 

I still think Health Surveillance should remain a priority at this stage, particularly given the recent tickets coming through. In a number of cases, previously agreed changes are having to be revisited, and these are increasingly appearing less like quick updates and more like enhancements or change requests rather than simple amendments to existing business rules and sub‑queries. 

 

I’ve also arranged an in‑person meeting with Angela next Wednesday to review the immunisation pages. I’m not yet sure of the scale of work involved, but I’ve set aside time to go through this in detail. I’ll keep you updated, and will ensure any work is aligned with our wider H&S priorities.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 24 June 2026 08:51
To: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Minutes – Cority Product User Group (PUG), 16 June 2026

 

Hi Chris,

Please find below the minutes from the first Cority Product User Group (PUG) meeting held on Tuesday 16 June 2026.

Minutes

1. Purpose of the Group

*	This was the first Cority Product User Group (PUG) meeting. The group has been set up to bridge the gap between project go-live and longer-term governance, and to provide a forum for users to raise issues, review backlog items, and agree priorities collaboratively.
*	It was clarified that the PUG is intended to be an operational, short-term, system-focused group, distinct from a future POG, which would be more strategic and aligned to PFST workstreams.

2. Membership

*	Membership covers the main operational user groups across Occupational Health and HR Systems, including representation from administration, nursing, advisors, operational management, and HR Systems.

3. Terms of Reference

*	The group agreed the following broad principles: 

	*	collective responsibility for identifying issues and solutions
	*	active participation from members
	*	collective decision-making on priorities
	*	members should highlight impacts on their own operational areas

*	It was also noted that while user priorities should drive the work, HR Systems will continue to advise on feasibility, effort, dependencies, and sequencing.

4. Enhancement Lifecycle

*	The enhancement lifecycle was reviewed as: Enhancement Request → Backlog → Roadmap → Development → Go Live.
*	Enhancement requests are normally raised through OSM self-service, although this is currently experiencing issues.
*	Prioritisation is based on four drivers: 

	1.	statutory compliance
	2.	university policy compliance
	3.	supplier-driven functionality
	4.	internal process improvement

5. Update on Delivered / Current Items

*	The following delivered items were noted: 

	*	bulk merge tool for duplicate client records
	*	centralised spirometry database on the G drive
	*	in-system widgets for audio and lung function tests
	*	200+ email template updates
	*	vaccine records history letter updated to include medications, blood tests and immunisations

*	Current in-progress / on-hold items include: 

	*	clearing duplicate client profile backlog
	*	primary job position flag fix
	*	health surveillance compliance reporting
	*	Teams/Outlook integration
	*	SMS appointment reminders
	*	smart notes draft/save functionality escalation with Cority

6. Backlog Review and Priorities

*	The highest priority backlog item remains archiving and retention rules, due to statutory requirements.

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Subject: #External# Escalation Request – Case 69001655

 

⚠️ External Email Warning ⚠️

This message is from an EXTERNAL source. Do not trust links or attachments. Verify sender authenticity before taking action.

Hi Sarah,

I would like to request your assistance in escalating Case 69001655.

The issue was initially raised on 11 June regarding an error within Recruitment E-Form Maintenance. When attempting to save changes within the Search Appointments Version 4 configuration, the system redirects to a "Sorry But Page Not Found" error and the changes are not saved.

Maura McGlynn has been helpful throughout the case and was able to reproduce the issue before raising it with Development on 12 June. Since then, I have continued to seek updates as the issue is preventing us from making required configuration changes.

On 15 June, I requested an update and was advised that the changes could potentially be applied manually. On 16 June, I provided the requirements document and details of the specific changes required. I also confirmed that the same changes had already been successfully implemented elsewhere, demonstrating that the configuration itself is valid and that the issue appears to be specific to saving changes within this section of Recruitment.

On 18 June, I advised that the matter had become time-critical as we have a delivery deadline and are unable to complete testing until the issue is resolved. I also requested that a Teams call be arranged so that the issue could be reviewed directly. The latest update advises that Development has been given the requirements, however we have not received any information regarding the investigation, root cause, ownership, workaround, or expected resolution date.

The issue affects Production, OXU and OXZ, and we remain unable to make the required configuration changes. Given the elapsed time since the issue was reproduced and passed to Development, together with the increasing business impact, I believe this now warrants e

emails.subject: Minutes – Cority Product User Group (PUG), 16 June 2026
emails.body:
Hi Chris,
Please find below the minutes from the first Cority Product User Group (PUG) meeting held on Tuesday 16 June 2026.
Minutes
1. Purpose of the Group

*	This was the first Cority Product User Group (PUG) meeting. The group has been set up to bridge the gap between project go-live and longer-term governance, and to provide a forum for users to raise issues, review backlog items, and agree priorities collaboratively.
*	It was clarified that the PUG is intended to be an operational, short-term, system-focused group, distinct from a future POG, which would be more strategic and aligned to PFST workstreams.

2. Membership

*	Membership covers the main operational user groups across Occupational Health and HR Systems, including representation from administration, nursing, advisors, operational management, and HR Systems.

3. Terms of Reference

*	The group agreed the following broad principles: 

	*	collective responsibility for identifying issues and solutions
	*	active participation from members
	*	collective decision-making on priorities
	*	members should highlight impacts on their own operational areas

*	It was also noted that while user priorities should drive the work, HR Systems will continue to advise on feasibility, effort, dependencies, and sequencing.

4. Enhancement Lifecycle

*	The enhancement lifecycle was reviewed as: Enhancement Request → Backlog → Roadmap → Development → Go Live.
*	Enhancement requests are normally raised through OSM self-service, although this is currently experiencing issues.
*	Prioritisation is based on four drivers: 

	1.	statutory compliance
	2.	university policy compliance
	3.	supplier-driven functionality
	4.	internal process improvement

5. Update on Delivered / Current Items

*	The following delivered items were noted: 

	*	bulk merge tool for duplicate client records
	*	centralised spirometry database on the G drive
	*	in-system widgets for audio and lung function tests
	*	200+ email template updates
	*	vaccine records history letter updated to include medications, blood tests and immunisations

*	Current in-progress / on-hold items include: 

	*	clearing duplicate client profile backlog
	*	primary job position flag fix
	*	health surveillance compliance reporting
	*	Teams/Outlook integration
	*	SMS appointment reminders
	*	smart notes draft/save functionality escalation with Cority

6. Backlog Review and Priorities

*	The highest priority backlog item remains archiving and retention rules, due to statutory requirements.
*	Of the process improvement items discussed, the group leaned towards prioritising auto-population of the clinician field from the booked appointment into the case screen, subject to confirmation that it is technically feasible.
*	The issue of supervisor CCs on letters needs further definition before it can be progressed, as the impact differs by letter type and some templates may be supplier-controlled.
*	It was agreed that support tickets should also be visible to the group in future so they can be reviewed alongside enhancement priorities.

7. Other Issues Raised

*	Repeated reminder emails for respiratory surveillance questionnaires are causing complaints where questionnaires have been submitted but remain pending review. A request has already been raised to explore a “pending” status or similar workaround.
*	Some surveillance letters are still going out with “Dear ?”. This remains an intermittent issue and examples should continue to be sent to the support mailbox.
*	The Cority training manual is now outdated and needs review. There was also discussion about creating short e-learning modules for key system processes.

Actions

*	Chris to arrange the next PUG meeting for early August, ideally on a Tuesday via Teams.
*	Kevin to escalate the smart notes draft/save issue with Cority.
*	
	James / Chris to pull together and circulate a list of open support tickets ahead of the next meeting.
*	Ji

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Hi Conor,

 

Just to update you, we met today and made a proposal for the post design. The project team would ideally like to set up a number of bucket posts, with hierarchy information completed down to level 4, post title completed and potentially category and/or sub-category completed, with the other fields left to fill in based on specific records being set up. Is it possible to set up a bucket post without specifying the Post Type?

 

We tend to use auto-authorised posts but I see there’s also an auto-commenced post as an option – I am assuming that this would just cut out the need to commence the appointment when the bucket post is used?

 

Are you able to confirm if there’s any restrictions with using bucket posts when it comes to loading the data into the system? Would we have to manually assign post sequence numbers when loading the employee records or is it enough to just include the post number and the data load will automatically populate the sequences and appointment IDs?

 

The business team are going to go away and confirm the structure of the bucket posts they want, but some of the decisions may depend on what the answers in this email are!

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com> 
Sent: 15 June 2026 12:54
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup

 

Good Afternoon Simon & Kevin,

 

I hope you are both well.

 

Thank you for your time on Friday. Apologies again for the error which we encountered which derailed us for a period.

I spent some time troubleshooting the issue with my colleagues on Friday and there was some data missing from database for Company 30. One of the expected params was blank which was not visible from the front end.

 

We checked all other companies, and this was the only effected one.

 

I have now shared the recordings from Friday with you both.

 

Please find below a breakdown of our call Friday:

 

Key Outcomes

1. Company & Pay Group Setup

*	A new non-payroll company (Company 90) was created to support Colleges and Halls.
*	Approach agreed: 

	*	Copy an existing company and remove unnecessary payroll elements (simpler than building from scratch).

*	A corresponding non-payroll pay group (Pay Group 90) was created: 

	*	Duplicated from a non-employee pay group template.
	*	Linked to Company 90.
	*	Configured with placeholder values (as it will not be used for payroll processing).

________________________________

2. Organisational Hierarchy Build

*	The hierarchy structure was successfully defined and built across: 

	*	Company
	*	Division (e.g. 901 – Colleges & Halls)
	*	Subdivision / Level 4
	*	Management Units (e.g. Permanent Private Halls)
	*	Departments (existing departments reused)

*	Departments were linked using“include linked items” where required.

________________________________

3. Reference Data & Data Migration

*	Review of reference data creation process, including:

________________________________

4. Access & Visibility Controls

*	Highlighted the importance of assigning: 

	*	Pay group access → for salary/pay visibility
	*	HR user access → for company/post

Cc: Mike West <mike.west@theaccessgroup.com>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: #External# Escalation Request – Case 69001655

 

⚠️ External Email Warning ⚠️

This message is from an EXTERNAL source. Do not trust links or attachments. Verify sender authenticity before taking action.

Hi Sarah,

I would like to request your assistance in escalating Case 69001655.

The issue was initially raised on 11 June regarding an error within Recruitment E-Form Maintenance. When attempting to save changes within the Search Appointments Version 4 configuration, the system redirects to a "Sorry But Page Not Found" error and the changes are not saved.

Maura McGlynn has been helpful throughout the case and was able to reproduce the issue before raising it with Development on 12 June. Since then, I have continued to seek updates as the issue is preventing us from making required configuration changes.

On 15 June, I requested an update and was advised that the changes could potentially be applied manually. On 16 June, I provided the requirements document and details of the specific changes required. I also confirmed that the same changes had already been successfully implemented elsewhere, demonstrating that the configuration itself is valid and that the issue appears to be specific to saving changes within this section of Recruitment.

On 18 June, I advised that the matter had become time-critical as we have a delivery deadline and are unable to complete testing until the issue is resolved. I also requested that a Teams call be arranged so that the issue could be reviewed directly. The latest update advises that Development has been given the requirements, however we have not received any information regarding the investigation, root cause, ownership, workaround, or expected resolution date.

The issue affects Production, OXU and OXZ, and we remain unable to make the required configuration changes. Given the elapsed time since the issue was reproduced and passed to Development, together with the increasing business impact, I believe this now warrants escalation.

I would be grateful if you could help obtain:

*	The current Development status.
*	Confirmation of any defect reference associated with the issue.
*	Any findings from the investigation to date.
*	An estimated timeframe for resolution.
*	Confirmation of any available workaround.

In addition, I would appreciate it if a member of the Development team could contact me directly. I believe a short Teams call and screen share would be a far more effective way to progress this issue than continuing communication solely through case updates, allowing questions to be answered and testing to be undertaken in real time.

Thank you for your assistance, and I look forward to hearing from you.

Best,
Kevin

 

 

Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analy

emails.subject: Business rules for Data Warehouse
emails.body:
Hi Ewan,

 

In our meeting this morning we agreed that I would confirm the rules that would need to be put in place to ensure that only University data is being processed in the current data warehouse and the future Fabric solution. Separately we will tackle the best way of getting Company 90 data into a separate area of the warehouse.

 

It was proposed that we would likely need to pull all of the data into the data warehouse and then use logic within the bronze layer to ensure we’re only surfacing and processing further data from within the University domain. The logic for how we can approach this is as follows:

 

Employee & Appointment Related Tables

In order to ensure only University data in passed into our data warehouse solution we need to restrict appointment level data to any appointment IDs which meet the following rules:

*	Company = 10 
*	The sum of days a company 10 appointment held, by person_reference > 1

 

We can then use this group of employees and appointments to restrict other tables which aren’t directly linked to appointment data (for example, person level information, UDF information, etc).

 

Examples of tables which will need to link appointment_id to the logic table in order to filter on company 10 appointments only:

[PEOPLEHR].[CT_BALANCE_ADJUSTMENT]

[PEOPLEHR].[CTBI_PERSON_CALCULATED_HOURS]

[PEOPLEHR].[PABI_PERSON_PENSION_SCHEME]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_PERSON_USER_DATA_ATITL]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_PERSON_COST_ALLOCATIONS]

 

For employee level information we will need to again link back to this logic table to check which employee records count as University records (we must not use “Company” to filter on any of these tables, even if it exists due to the way that data can be processed within PeopleXD causing errors).

 

Examples of tables which don’t contain appointment ID but do contain Person Reference, so we will need to link person_reference back to the logic table:

[PEOPLEHR].[ONBOARDING_PERS_COMPLETE_ITEMS]

[PEOPLEHR].[PABI_PERSON_MASTER]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_HR_PERSON_MASTER]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_PERSON_CONTACT]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_PERSON_QUALIFICATIONS]

 

Payroll Related Tables

Within Payroll tables we can use the Pay Group to restrict to the main University. Where Pay Group is available within the payroll tables we can restrict on Pay Groups 20, 21 and 99. 

 

Examples of tables which rely on Pay Group to filter on pay group 20, 21 and 99 only:

[PEOPLEHR].[PABI_PAY_HISTORY]

[PEOPLEHR].[PABI_PAY_HISTORY_CAS]

[PEOPLEHR].[PAY_GROUP]

 

Recruitment and post appointment data

Where data is related to post management we can safely utilise the Company Code held against these records to filter without any complex logic being required. We also don’t expect any recruitment data outside of company 10 to exist in the system so this is a low risk area.

 

Examples of tables where using Company Code to filter Company = 10 only:

[PEOPLEHR].[RTBI_STAFF_REQUESTS]

[PEOPLEHR].[RTBI_COMPETITION_MASTER]

 

Reference / Other Data

 

There’s also a number of tables which are loaded with reference data or externally derived data which won’t need any filtering, some of which we may wish to replicate in whatever the college data area looks like, and some we definitely won’t want to replicate.

Examples of tables which are reference data or not related to individuals or specific companies which won’t need any filtering (some of which may need duplicating in whatever the college area looks like):

[PEOPLEHR].[CTBI_BALANCE_HOLIDAY_SCHEME_MAPPING]

[PEOPLEHR].[EARNING_LIMITS]

[PEOPLEHR].[EXT_LOAD_UCEA_BROAD_SICKNESS_ABSENCE_REASONS]

[PEOPLEHR].[HR_HIERARCHY_DATA_VIEW]

[PEOPLEHR].[PEBI_PENSION_SCHEME_DETAILS]

[PEOPLEHR].[PRBI_APPOINTMENT_REASON_LOV]

 

Examples of external load files which won’t need any filtering and definitely won’t need to exist in the college area:

[PEOPLEHR].[EXT_LOAD_HEIDI_PL

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11688462: Ash VEA team
emails.to: Marie Cooksey; HR Systems Support; Kevin Lelitte
emails.body:
Hi Marie

 

I have had a hunt through my emails and will look through the files, however, as I wasn’t involved in the Rostering side of the project, I have limited info but will see what I can find. This is what I have so far:

 

In October, they had an issue with work group managers and access this was escalated to Sharon and Tracy, who replied it was in hand. 

 

In March, they emailed asking how to manage variable hours staff through the rostering with leave and absence function, especially in recording sickness for variable hours staff. This very well could be one of the issues, and I recently emailed a proposal to Michael on how we might be able to manage sickness for variable hours staff. 

 

The request to find a solution on this was raised to Tracy in 2025 and investigated by Sonhal (I believe ) but am unsure what the outcome was. However, the launch of leave and absence with rostering comms stated the following:

 

‘Detailed guidance outlining what each role can see and do within the system is available here <https://staff.web.ox.ac.uk/employee-self-service-view-roster-and-set-availability#collapse5699126> .

For any variable hour’s staff that won't be recording absence through the leave and absence functionality, the roster managers can continue to input this via the roster as per the previous process.’

 

I recently came across a work around using unrostered booking function within the system, which seemed to enable absence to be recorded for VH staff, and that absence would appear in reporting. If this is one of their issues, we may now have a potential solution that is with Michael on the FA roadmap. 

 

In April it came to light that the rostering staff records were not being kept up to date by the GLAM HR team, they were not adding work schedules, team areas etc to the new appointments for rostered staff. This was escalated to Cathy and Sarah on 14 April by Tracy. I believe this was resolved at this point. 

 

Hope this initial background info helps. 

 

I will happily join the call if you want me to as I may be able to provide some context and insight into project approach and whether the system is being used correctly. 

 

Kind regards

 

Anna

 

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 22 June 2026 15:41

Hi Emma

 

I have copied in Anna and Kevin.

Advise Cathy we will review and then as a team we can agree a way forward, before we go and meet with Cathy formally. I would like this to move quite quickly, as we really cannot afford for them to leave WFM.

 

@Anna Carter-Windle <mailto:anna.carter-windle@it.ox.ac.uk>  – whilst you are still within the business are you able to give any insight you may have on the issues they have previously raised? If you could share any documentation you may have with Kevin that would be really helpful.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

emails.subject: Escalation Request – Case 69001655
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I would like to request your assistance in escalating Case 69001655.
The issue was initially raised on 11 June regarding an error within Recruitment E-Form Maintenance. When attempting to save changes within the Search Appointments Version 4 configuration, the system redirects to a "Sorry But Page Not Found" error and the changes are not saved.
Maura McGlynn has been helpful throughout the case and was able to reproduce the issue before raising it with Development on 12 June. Since then, I have continued to seek updates as the issue is preventing us from making required configuration changes.
On 15 June, I requested an update and was advised that the changes could potentially be applied manually. On 16 June, I provided the requirements document and details of the specific changes required. I also confirmed that the same changes had already been successfully implemented elsewhere, demonstrating that the configuration itself is valid and that the issue appears to be specific to saving changes within this section of Recruitment.
On 18 June, I advised that the matter had become time-critical as we have a delivery deadline and are unable to complete testing until the issue is resolved. I also requested that a Teams call be arranged so that the issue could be reviewed directly. The latest update advises that Development has been given the requirements, however we have not received any information regarding the investigation, root cause, ownership, workaround, or expected resolution date.
The issue affects Production, OXU and OXZ, and we remain unable to make the required configuration changes. Given the elapsed time since the issue was reproduced and passed to Development, together with the increasing business impact, I believe this now warrants escalation.
I would be grateful if you could help obtain:

*	
	The current Development status.
*	
	Confirmation of any defect reference associated with the issue.
*	
	Any findings from the investigation to date.
*	
	An estimated timeframe for resolution.
*	
	Confirmation of any available workaround.

In addition, I would appreciate it if a member of the Development team could contact me directly. I believe a short Teams call and screen share would be a far more effective way to progress this issue than continuing communication solely through case updates, allowing questions to be answered and testing to be undertaken in real time.
Thank you for your assistance, and I look forward to hearing from you.
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: RE: Cority Security Notice: Third-Party Data Incident
emails.to: Sharon Naylor; Rob Green; James Salas Guillen
emails.body:
Hi Sharon,

 

Yes, we got sent this too.

 

As per the advice in the email, if you receive any messages seemingly from Cority, please forward them to us. We will then follow up with Cority to check their veracity. The likelihood is we will also receive the same emails but if in doubt, forward to us as you have done here.

 

Kind Regards,

 

Chris Sanders

Business Change Analyst

HR Systems <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx>  | People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

  

    <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>  <https://oxforduni-remoteforms.info-exchange.com/Incident> 

  <https://www.credly.com/badges/3e879e66-f8ce-4137-b2d6-846a0ad552af/public_url>     <https://www.credly.com/badges/6a28598f-a6f1-46d2-9f7f-43275b13fca7/public_url> 

 

From: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 18 June 2026 17:43
To: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Cority Security Notice: Third-Party Data Incident

 

You seen this? @Christopher Sanders <mailto:christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>  @James Salas Guillen <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> , I dare say you have

 

Regards

 

Sharon

 

From: Daphne Reed <daphne.reed@cority.com <mailto:daphne.reed@cority.com> > 
Sent: 18 June 2026 16:22
To: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Cority Security Notice: Third-Party Data Incident

 

Dear Sharon⁠,

 

We are writing to inform you of a security incident taking the form of a supply chain attack related to a competitive intelligence platform named Klue, which is used by Cority and connected to information stored in Salesforce, our customer relationship management tool (CRM). Based on public reports, the attack appears to have indiscriminately targeted and impacted companies around the world.

 

From the outset, we want to be clear: although the incident has impacted Cority’s Salesforce environment, the source of the incident has been contained and it appears strictly limited to data processed within Salesforce. The incident has no known impact on the CorityOne platform, Meddbase platform or customer data stored within them.

 

What happened

 

We were recently advised that access tokens for Klue were compromised. These tokens can be used to reach data held in Salesforce through an application programming interface and, as part of our investigation, we confirmed that Salesforce information was indeed accessed without authorization.

 

Although our investigation remains active and is progressing with the support of Salesforce, we want to clarify that the environments for Cority, Meddbase and their affiliates are architecturally separate from Salesforce, and our Salesforce instance is not connected to our Platforms. As a result, the incident has no known impact on CorityOne, Meddbase or any other proprietary affiliate platform.


What data was affected


 

The data affected includes ordinary information processed through Salesforce such as business relationships, sales opportunities, support ticket information and professional contact information: names, work email addresses, work telephone numbers, and job titles for Cority and its affiliates including Meddbase.


What we have done


 

As soon as we were notified, we contained the incident: we disabled the affected integration and revoked its access to Salesforce. Our security team is undertaking a forensic review, with the assistance of our CRM provider, to establish exactly which data was involved and who was affected. This work is active and is our immediate priority.


What we recommend you do


 

Due to the fact that exposed contact details can make phishing and impersonation attempts mo

emails.subject: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM) and process change for managing service request approvals
emails.body:
From: Louise Piper, ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist

To: All OSM users

 

Please disregard this email if you do not manage Service Requests.

Note that any previous recommendations have been updated.

 

For Information: 

•                        Access to IT Self Service has been partially restored for staff and students

	*	Can log their own requests
	*	‘My Items’ is currently unavailable

•                        A temporary change to the Service Request Approval process is in place

•                        Internal users of OSM can no longer log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

 

Dear All,

 

Access to IT Self Service has been partially restored for staff and students. Staff and students can log their own Service and Support Requests (SR) within IT Self Service as normal, however ‘My Items’ is not yet available.

 

As a result, Service Request Approvals cannot currently be progressed through the usual approach, and the alternative process below should be used.

 

To ensure that SRs can continue to progress without delay, approval notifications have been updated to include full details of the request, and to ask approvers to reply directly with their approval vote. Once a response has been received, the SR will turn yellow to indicate it has been updated.

It is no longer necessary to send a manual email requesting approval.

 

It is no longer possible for internal users of OSM to log SRs on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’.

 

To manage SR approvals:

1.	Monitor customer updates to active SRs.

	a.	Tip: Use the saved search ‘My Team’s SRs Updated by Customer’, available as an individual search, or via the ‘Service Desk Incident/Task/SR alternative’ dashboard.

2.	Review the response from the approver.
3.	‘Confirm Customer Updates’ to remove yellow highlight from the SR.
4.	Assign a Task to the OSM Administration team from the SR, requesting that the Approval be approved or denied, as applicable.

 

The OSM Administration team will also monitor responses and actively approve or deny approvals. Once the approval is actioned, the SR will progress as usual.

 

Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

 

 

Thanks / Regards

Louise

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Louise Piper
ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist
IT Services | University of Oxford
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

T: 01865 616283
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

 

 

 

From: Sarah Warne <sarah.warne@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 02 June 2026 16:36
To: it-services-osm-users-all@maillist.ox.ac.uk
Subject: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM), logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals

 

Dear all,

 

For Information:  

*	Note that IT self-service is currently unavailable for some users

•    Internal users of OSM can log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

•    Suggested workaround for management of approvals for Service Requests

 

Further to my email on Friday 22 May about the ongoing security issue affecting IT Self-Service via Oxford Service Manager (OSM), the University is actively working with the technical team at Ivanti to secure a resolution that will enable full restoration of service. This may take some time, given Ivanti is supporting impacted organisations around the world. An update on timelines for further engagement is expected from Ivanti’s support team in the next couple of days. 

 

Ian Teasdale, Director of Digital Assurance, is representing the University on a sector group convened by the HE IT sector body, UCISA, to expediate a resolution at a national level. This has already opened additional technical support routes.

 

Please refer to Louise’s email below for guidance on how to log a Service Request (SR) on behalf of a customer, and

Subject: UCEA Clinical pay uplift - April 2026
Importance: High

 

Hi all,

 

The clinical pay uplift rates have now been mapped to the University’s pay structure (for grades) and allowance codes (for allowances/pay premia). My colleague @Susan <mailto:susan.pratt@admin.ox.ac.uk>  has performed data assurance on the rates vs the UCEA communications and confirmed that they align. 

 

To recap what’s happening in this uplift: 

*	All clinical salary scales: 3.5% uplift
*	Flexible pay premia (Clinical Academia & Clinical Speciality allowances): 3.5% uplift 
*	National Clinical Impact Awards: 5.0% uplift backdated to 01-APR-2025

 

We are looking to implement the changes in PeopleXD’s live environment on 27-JUL-2026 so that the uplifted rates are present in time for the August payroll. 

 

*	@Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> – the uplift files for both grade and allowances are now available for the FA team to process in a test environment at: Payroll\Pay uplifts\1 Clinical Uplifts\Clinical pay uplift APRIL 2025 <file://connect.ox.ac.uk/ADMN/MIS/HR%20Systems/Payroll/Pay%20uplifts/1%20Clinical%20Uplifts/Clinical%20pay%20uplift%20APRIL%202025> . Please let me know if there are any issues with the data.
*	@Jasmin <mailto:jasmin.khokhra@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – the pay uplift information pack is attached in both Excel and PDF format, in case one or the other is better for you. 

 

Best wishes,

Athena

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Emma Fitz-Gibbon <emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: OSM Change Enablement: temporary approval process change

 

Hi Marie,

 

Just to flag the below related to change approvals…

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

 

From: Sarah Warne <sarah.warne@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.warne@it.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 17 June 2026 11:18
To: it-services-osm-ea-users@maillist.ox.ac.uk <mailto:it-services-osm-ea-users@maillist.ox.ac.uk> 
Subject: OSM Change Enablement: temporary approval process change

 

Good morning,

 

From: Sarah Warne, ITSM Systems Specialist

For: All OSM Change Coordinators

 

For Information: A temporary change to the OSM Change Approval process is in place.

 

We have made temporary configuration changes to how My Items are displayed and handled within IT Self Service.  As a result, Change Approvals cannot currently be progressed through the usual approach, and the alternative process below should be used.

 

Changes should be logged and submitted for approval as normal.

 

To ensure changes can continue to progress without delay, approval notifications have been updated to ask approvers to reply directly with their approval vote. Once a response has been received, the change will turn yellow to indicate it has been updated. If approved, the Change Coordinator can record the approval using the standard approval override process within 7 days.

 

To record an approval override

 

1.         Update the Approval Expiry Date to a future date.

2.         Save the record.

3.         Select Approve Change.

4.         Choose Yes from the Proceed with Approval Override? drop-down menu.

5.         Save the record.

6.         Enter the approver's response and any relevant details in the Reason for Approval Override field.

7.         Save the record to complete the process.

 

If the approver denies their approval, the OSM Administration team will pick these up and update them.

 

Many thanks,
Sarah.

 

 <https://www.ox.ac.uk/> 

 

Sarah Warne
ITSM Systems Specialist
IT Services | University of Oxford
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, OX1 2JD

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 17 June 2026 11:05
To: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: P5 Task assigned to you - Task# 50960484 for Incident# 11687334

 

Just because I can't resist adding a thought...

Wasn't the original scope intended to include a Talent Admin function sitting within POD (or a similar central team)? I seem to recall discussions that this type of administration wouldn't sit within the HR Systems remit.

Whilst the number of cases is currently low, I wonder whether it's worth revisiting the ownership question before this becomes established as a BAU activity. If there is an expectation that these exceptions will continue to arise, it may be worth considering where responsibility is best placed from a process and resourcing perspective, rather than defaulting it to Support or FA.  

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 17 June 2026 10:16
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: FW: P5 Task assigned to you - Task# 50960484 for Incident# 11687334

 

Hi all,

 

Just wanted to check in with you regarding the below query from Julie, and the below response I am planning to send. 

 

The security model in My Development only allows an HR user to override a Reviewer where that Reviewer has an appointment within the HR user's People Management access. Where the Reviewer is in a different department/division and falls outside the HR user's security permissions, the override cannot be completed. Ilona already has access to PeopleXD records across all Humanities departments. If the Reviewer only holds appointments outside Humanities, then it is appropriate that the system prevents the assignment. I don’t think we need to follow up with AG on this.

 

I would not recommend creating additional non-employee appointments purely to facilitate this process.

 

Ideally, there would be a central Talent Admin role with Uni-wide access to manage this type of admin. However, given the relatively small number of cases identified so far, I think it would be more appropriate for the Support Team or a System Administrator if 1st line is not confident to manage these exceptions as needed.

 

Do you agree?

 

 

Many thanks,

Asta

 

Asta Palmer

Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University

From: tasknotifications@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:tasknotifications@it.ox.ac.uk>  <tasknotifications@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:tasknotifications@it.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 17 June 2026 05:00
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Owner Required - Task# 50959264 for ServiceReq# 30412906

 

 

Dear Marie Cooksey

The following Task has been in a status of Logged with HRIS Management Team since 09/06/2026 10:53:41 (UTC) - Etc/UTC:

Task: 50959264
Summary: IAM request for access to HR data from Core User Directory for Groupstore
Parent ID: ServiceReq 30412906

Please ensure a member of the team takes ownership and progresses to completion as soon as possible.

Open:Task <https://oxford.saasiteu.com/Login.aspx?Scope=ObjectWorkspace&CommandId=Search&ObjectType=Task%23Assignment&CommandData=AssignmentId,%3D,0,50959264,1,AND>  

 

Open:Parent <https://oxford.saasiteu.com/Login.aspx?Scope=ObjectWorkspace&CommandId=Search&ObjectType=ServiceReq%23&CommandData=ServiceReqNumber,%3D,0,30412906,1,AND>  

Task Details:

Dear HRIS Management Team,

 

In order to resolve OSM Problem P#40000852, the Identity and Access Management Team (IAM) are adding additional Groupstore groups for each department to distinguish types of HRIS staff (casual and non-casual) from people with University Staff cards.  This requires access to the following two attributes from Core User Directory (CUD) for all employees:

 

New attributes required:

cud:uas_hris:primary_post_department_code

cud:uas_hris:primary_post_appointment_id

 

The reason for access is:

"These two attributes will be used to determine casual vs. non-casual staff within Groupstore, allowing us to publish groups of users for access control purposes.

Currently, people are classed as University Staff if they have a Card with US status or have a live HRIS affiliation for the department. Unfortunately for some departments, it is necessary to distinguish between casual staff and normal staff as casual staff may well be students working over the summer. Groupstore needs to check if the post's primary department matches the current department being processed, hence primary_post_department_code."

 

A previous request covered the use of these attributes in a test environment.  However, this request is for use of live HRIS data in the production environment.

 

As data custodians for HRIS, do you approve of this use case and the release of attributes for this purpose?

Hi Laura,

Thanks for checking in — my hand is on the mend, thank you.

A quick update on the URL changes. We identified an issue within the Recruitment system which prevented us from updating the relevant URLs and has caused a delay to the planned work. The issue has been raised with our supplier and they have now offered to perform manual workaround for us while the underlying problem is investigated.

I'm currently providing the details required for the workaround and am still aiming to make the changes on or around 22 June, ahead of the old page being retired.

I'll let you know once the links have been updated across the three pages. If anything changes regarding the retirement date in the meantime, please let me know.

Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: UCEA Clinical pay uplift - April 2026
emails.to: Jasmin Khokhra; Tracy Roberts; Michelle Williams; Michael O'Sullivan; Susan Pratt
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

The clinical pay uplift rates have now been mapped to the University’s pay structure (for grades) and allowance codes (for allowances/pay premia). My colleague @Susan <mailto:susan.pratt@admin.ox.ac.uk>  has performed data assurance on the rates vs the UCEA communications and confirmed that they align. 

 

To recap what’s happening in this uplift: 



*	All clinical salary scales: 3.5% uplift
*	Flexible pay premia (Clinical Academia & Clinical Speciality allowances): 3.5% uplift 
*	National Clinical Impact Awards: 5.0% uplift backdated to 01-APR-2025

 

We are looking to implement the changes in PeopleXD’s live environment on 27-JUL-2026 so that the uplifted rates are present in time for the August payroll. 

 

*	@Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> – the uplift files for both grade and allowances are now available for the FA team to process in a test environment at: Payroll\Pay uplifts\1 Clinical Uplifts\Clinical pay uplift APRIL 2025 <file://connect.ox.ac.uk/ADMN/MIS/HR%20Systems/Payroll/Pay%20uplifts/1%20Clinical%20Uplifts/Clinical%20pay%20uplift%20APRIL%202025> . Please let me know if there are any issues with the data.
*	@Jasmin <mailto:jasmin.khokhra@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – the pay uplift information pack is attached in both Excel and PDF format, in case one or the other is better for you. 

 

Best wishes,

Athena

 

Athena Artuso (she/her)

People Analytics Team Lead

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

emails.subject: RE: Holiday Records Reporting – FlexPoints Approval
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

 

Thank you for giving me this overview, I have gone in and approved based on the understanding these reports will support our duty requirements. Can I be kept in the loop once they are developed as I would like to have a look at them.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 15 June 2026 17:32
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Holiday Records Reporting – FlexPoints Approval

 

Hi Marie,

 

I wanted to give you an update on the Holiday Records reporting work and seek approval to proceed with the associated FlexPoints request.

 

Following discussions with Athena and Simon, the requirement has been broken down into three Insight reports:

*	Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year

*	Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements

*	Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination

 

The reports are intended to support our Holiday Records Duty requirements and cover the majority of the reporting requirements that have been identified.

 

As we were approaching the end of June and the expiry of our remaining FlexPoints, I worked with Athena to pull together the report specifications and sent these to Access for review. Mike was on annual leave at the time, so I copied in Ann-Marie Topliss to see if she could help progress the request or direct it to the appropriate team whilst he was away.

 

Ann-Marie subsequently raised FlexPoints case 69001638 and advised that, if the work was deemed feasible, approval would be needed before the FlexPoints expire so that the remaining allocation could be safeguarded for this work.

 

Access have now completed their review and provided a quotation under case 69001638. The quotation has been raised as FlexPoints request FPT-062315 for 2,800 FlexPoints and is currently awaiting approval.

The quotation totals 2,800 FlexPoints in full, made up of 1,750 for the Payroll Consultant element and 1,050 for the WFM Consultant element. Against our current available balance of 2,935 FlexPoints, this would leave 135 FlexPoints remaining.

I've attached the quotation and a screenshot of the current FlexPoints position for reference.

Could you let me know if you are happy to approve and proceed with the work?

 

Best,

Kevin

 

 

Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 

 



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Good Afternoon Simon & Kevin,

 

I hope you are both well.

 

Thank you for your time on Friday. Apologies again for the error which we encountered which derailed us for a period.

I spent some time troubleshooting the issue with my colleagues on Friday and there was some data missing from database for Company 30. One of the expected params was blank which was not visible from the front end.

 

We checked all other companies, and this was the only effected one.

 

I have now shared the recordings from Friday with you both.

 

Please find below a breakdown of our call Friday:

 

Key Outcomes

1. Company & Pay Group Setup

*	A new non-payroll company (Company 90) was created to support Colleges and Halls.
*	Approach agreed: 

	*	Copy an existing company and remove unnecessary payroll elements (simpler than building from scratch).

*	A corresponding non-payroll pay group (Pay Group 90) was created: 

	*	Duplicated from a non-employee pay group template.
	*	Linked to Company 90.
	*	Configured with placeholder values (as it will not be used for payroll processing).

________________________________

2. Organisational Hierarchy Build

*	The hierarchy structure was successfully defined and built across: 

	*	Company
	*	Division (e.g. 901 – Colleges & Halls)
	*	Subdivision / Level 4
	*	Management Units (e.g. Permanent Private Halls)
	*	Departments (existing departments reused)

*	Departments were linked using“include linked items” where required.

________________________________

3. Reference Data & Data Migration

*	Review of reference data creation process, including: 

	*	Pay administered by (User Code 5)
	*	Other hierarchy reference points

*	Demonstrated use of the Data Migration Tool to: 

	*	Bulk upload reference data efficiently
	*	Validate and process entries successfully

*	Confirmed file structure and formatting requirements for uploads.

________________________________

4. Access & Visibility Controls

*	Highlighted the importance of assigning: 

	*	Pay group access → for salary/pay visibility
	*	HR user access → for company/post visibility

*	Confirmed that without correct access: 

	*	Users may lose visibility of companies, posts, or salary data.

________________________________

5. Hierarchy Management Guidance

*	Best practices discussed: 

	*	Use unlink (not delete) within the structure where needed.
	*	Deactivate reference data rather than deleting it (to preserve historical data).
	*	Be cautious when removing higher-level hierarchy items, as this removes all dependent levels.

________________________________

6. Employee Self-Service (ESS) Configuration

*	Confirmed: 

	*	ESS menus can be partially customised for Company 90 users.
	*	Unnecessary items (e.g. payslips) can be removed where applicable.

*	A follow-up session can be arranged to: 

	*	Review menu options
	*	Define appropriate configuration for end users

________________________________

7. Post Creation & Data Migration Strategy

*	Agreed approach: 

	*	Use Data Migration (DM) for initial post creation.
	*	Follow up with manual or bulk updates as needed.

*	Post design (granular vs. “bucket” posts) remains under consideration and is dependent on internal administrative processes.

 


Outstanding Actions / Open Items


For Conor


*	Complete remaining hierarchy linking (departments + pay administered by). - DONE
*	Share: 

	*	Documentation for company, pay group, and hierarchy setup - ATTACHED
	*	Menu/profile options for ESS configuration – Working on this currently

*	Follow up on: 

	*	Access query raised by Kevin – Can I ask you to confirm the areas on LIVE which you do not have access and if the same on UAT(UOXC)?
	*	Confirmation of pay group/company setup validation testing  - DONE

	*	
________________________________



For Customer Team


*	Review completed hierarchy and confirm structure me

emails.subject: Re: Menu Profile for hierarchy administration
emails.to: Asta Palmer; Michael O'Sullivan; Simon Burford
emails.body:
Thanks, Asta.
When you get a chance, could you run the comparison and send over the details of the menu options and any associated configuration required?
I'll pick this up and set up some time to review the wider position and determine the best way to take this forward.
Given the work already in UOXU, it would make sense to use that as a proof of concept before progressing any wider rollout across the remaining environments.
I'll also start pulling together the relevant guidance and supporting documentation so we can include this process as part of this work.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Friday, June 12, 2026 12:52 PM
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Menu Profile for hierarchy administration 


Yes that’s correct - Helen was working on this at the time, but due to some limitations we were unable to fully migrate the functionality into the Portal.

 

The COREPORTAL_ADMIN menu profile will need to be updated across all environments. Simon, if these are all the menu options you need, then I can run a comparison and extract what exactly has been enabled against this menu profile and send it to you @Kevin Lelitte <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>  for the change request.

 

Many thanks

Asta

 

 

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 12 June 2026 12:06
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Menu Profile for hierarchy administration

 

If it helps, I don't believe we had migrated the management of the organisational structure from Back Office into the Portal at this stage. That is likely the reason why those options have not yet been enabled.

We are also unlikely to have any Portal-specific ‘How To’ type guidance documented for this functionality, so documenting the process and producing supporting guidance would need to be captured as a follow-up action.

In terms of post management, some of the functionality is likely covered by the COREPORTAL_SUPPORT responsibility, with other elements potentially falling under the standard COREPORTAL_HR_ADMIN_WEB. However, if any of the functionality relates to the organisational structure migration, that would also explain why the corresponding options were not enabled.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

It might be that there’s a separate menu profile that I just don’t have assigned to me, but in order to get the hierarchy access needed I had to add the following items in UOXU:

Simon
