emails.subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus
emails.to: Ralph Watson; James Salas Guillen; Rachel Midgley
emails.body:
Hi Ralph

 

Thank you for your update. 

 

@James Salas Guillen <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – could you raise a Change request please, so that we can have indicative costs.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Ralph Watson <ralph.watson@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 15 June 2026 08:12
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Rachel Midgley <rachel.midgley@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie,

 

The last I heard about the SBS exclusion was from Tony Boydell saying it would be a change request:

 

Send us an email formally requesting the business requirement (e.g. exclude Said Biz School from DSE extract and include the rules), confirm you have the budget for it and let us put a ticket together and estimate it 

 

I imagine it’s quite a quick thing for them to do but it needs approval from the Safety Office because of the budget.  In the meantime, SBS are in Cardinus but excluded from the emails that it sends out. 

 

Officially, I’m not on the project anymore but I’m happy to answer any questions to help embed this in BAU as now the integration is set up, I don’t think this would be too time consuming.

 

Best wishes,

 

Ralph.

Marie

Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie, 

 

Yes, the automated data upload from PXD to Cardinus is now live and working as expected. Based on initial spot checks, the data appears to be accurate.

 

However, there are a small number of issues that we are addressing as part of BAU:

 

*	SBS users – These users are currently included in the data upload but should not be, as they use a separate DSE system. There is an email rule currently in place to exclude them from DSE emails but ideally we would want them to be excluded from the data upload. *	Contractors and other non‑PXD users with DSE requirements

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 05 June 2026 10:59
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie, 

 

Yes, the automated data upload from PXD to Cardinus is now live and working as expected. Based on initial spot checks, the data appears to be accurate.

 

However, there are a small number of issues that we are addressing as part of BAU:

 

*	SBS users – These users are currently included in the data upload but should not be, as they use a separate DSE system. There is an email rule currently in place to exclude them from DSE emails but ideally we would want them to be excluded from the data upload. We may need to raise a service request to exclude SBS users from the feed. I believe Ralph has raised this with the Azure team. Ralph is currently on annual leave and not back until the 15th June. 
*	Contractors and other non‑PXD users with DSE requirements – As these users are not present in PXD, they are being automatically archived during the weekly upload. As a temporary workaround, these users can be manually tagged as ‘Do Not Archive’ to prevent this. Any new users not in PXD who require DSE access will also need to be created manually. 
*	Users without an ox.ac.uk email address – There are a small number of users without an Oxford email address. We are monitoring these and keeping an eye on them, as the numbers are currently low.

 

Process documentation is currently being updated and will be uploaded to the H&S folder ahead of the handover session with Kevin and Chris this afternoon.

 

The next scheduled data upload is Wednesday 10th June at 4:00am. 

 

There should not be a need for further technical input, but could you confirm whether we can call on Ralph for any technical questions that may arise over the next few weeks, just in case?

 

Best wishes,

emails.subject: RE: #External# PeopleXD Insight Reporting – Quote Request for Holiday Records Reports
emails.body:
Hi Kevin,

 

Thank you for your patience whilst we reviewed your requirements. I'm pleased to confirm that we've prepared a quotation for your project, which you'll find attached to your case and included with this email.

 

Next Steps: 

To proceed with this work, please: 

*	Authorise the quote through your FlexPoints Redemption case on the Support Portal 
*	Confirm your acceptance by replying to this email, which will allow me to progress your request immediately

 

Important Timescales: 

*	Quote validity: This quotation is valid until 26th June 2026 (14 days from today) 
*	Case closure: If we don't receive approval by this date, your FlexPoints Redemption case will be closed 
*	Project scheduling: Please note that once approved, there will be lead times for scheduling and commencing the work

 

Questions or Support: 

If you need any clarification about the quotation or have questions about the proposed approach, I'm here to help.

 

We look forward to working with you on this project.

Kind regards,

 

 

Ann-Marie Topliss

Optimisation Services Solution Delivery Manager – People XD

Access People Division

 

Upcoming Annual Leave: 20th, 30th & 31st July, 3rd – 5th Aug

_____________________________

 

Access UK Ltd l The Armstrong Building l 10 Oakwood Drive l Loughborough l LE11 3QF 

T: 0845 345 3300 l M: 07384 251744 l www.theaccessgroup.com <http://www.theaccessgroup.com/>  

 

From: Ann-Marie Topliss 
Sent: 11 June 2026 15:01
To: 'Kevin Lelitte' <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Amanda Mcdonnell <Amanda.Mcdonnell@theaccessgroup.com>
Subject: RE: #External# PeopleXD Insight Reporting – Quote Request for Holiday Records Reports

 

Hi Kevin,

 

In Mike’s absence I have created a FlexPoints case for you - #69001638.

 

You FlexPoints are due to expire by 29th June, however if we cannot scope before then we will look to you to approve the FlexPoints before the 29th June so that they are safeguarded for this work.

 

In the meantime:

 

What happens next: 

*	Our specialist team is currently reviewing your requirements 
*	A dedicated Consultant will be assigned to your case and will assess the scope of work required 
*	You'll receive a quotation once the review is complete 
*	If additional clarification is needed, your assigned Consultant will arrange a brief scoping call with you

 

Timescales: 

Please be aware that there are standard lead times for both the scoping and quotation process. Once you've approved any quotation, additional lead times will apply for project completion. We'll keep you informed of expected timescales throughout the process.

 

Your next update: 

We'll be in touch shortly with either your quotation or to arrange a scoping discussion if required.

 

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to contact me.

Kind regards,

 

 

Ann-Marie Topliss

Access People Division

Sent: 11 June 2026 12:03
To: Mike West <mike.west@theaccessgroup.com>; Ann-Marie Topliss <annmarie.topliss@theaccessgroup.com>; Amanda Mcdonnell <amanda.mcdonnell@theaccessgroup.com>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: #External# PeopleXD Insight Reporting – Quote Request for Holiday Records Reports
Importance: High

 

⚠️ External Email Warning ⚠️

This message is from an EXTERNAL source. Do not trust links or attachments. Verify sender authenticity before taking action.

Hi Mike,

I hope you're well.

We've identified a requirement for a number of new PeopleXD Insight reports to support our holiday records reporting requirements and have developed an initial specification for review.

The attached documents include:

*	Oxford Holiday Records Revised Three Report Specification
*	Oxford Holiday Records Report Field Lists

The proposed solution consists of three reports:

*	Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year
*	Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements
*	Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination

We would be grateful if you could review the attached requirements and advise whether the proposed reports are achievable within Insight Reporting, together with an indication of any custom development that may be required.

I appreciate you are currently out of office, so I have copied Amanda McDonnell and Anne-Marie Topliss in case either is able to assist or direct this to the appropriate team while you are away.

We are working to a tight deadline, as we have flex points available within the current year that must be utilised before month end. We would therefore appreciate a quotation or indicative estimate at the earliest opportunity, as our intention is to use the remaining flex point allocation to fund this work if it is deemed feasible.

If a quotation cannot be provided from the attached documentation alone, would it be possible to arrange a scoping session as a priority, ideally within the next week, so that the requirements can be reviewed and an estimate produced in time for us to progress the work through the current funding allocation.

Please let me know if any further information would be helpful.

Best,
Kevin

 

 

Kevin Lelitte

emails.subject: DTP1092 - Team Meeting Notes - 10th June 2026
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Simon Burford; Kevin Lelitte; Nathan Kirwan; Nicholas Chandler; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Dear All

 

Some notes from our team meeting held on 10th June.

 

HR Systems Proposal Document

 

*	The ‘College Staff in PXD Proposal’ was shared by Simon on 29th May. This was discussed. Only a few comments were made by Anne regarding:

	*	A question about Research Services staff needing self-service access – this will not be needed. SS will however be needed for college staff
	*	Any additional fields needed from college census data – only field additional field is date of birth – which will be requested and provided.
	*	A question about HESA ID - this may exist in Company 10 and can be referenced there but will not be collected for Company 90. 
	*	Risks – it was pointed out that some of the risks are REF risks and not project risks – the project should not be responsible for REF risks. Action: AM to review the risks in the  DTP1092 Research Staff into PXD - RAID Log 290526-1.xlsx <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/ITServices-CollegeStaffintoPXD-ProjectDocuments/Shared%20Documents/Project%20%20Management%20Documents/RAID%20Log/DTP1092%20Research%20Staff%20into%20PXD%20-%20RAID%20Log%20290526-1.xlsx?d=w22279fa4bd7140a492bc79ff56c2b730&csf=1&web=1&e=rZi8Fa> 

*	An additional point was picked up by Marie on Page 8 – Training materials for college staff. Anne explained that the responsibility probably rests with Research Services but RS would benefit from referencing existing guidance which can be found at: https://staff.admin.ox.ac.uk/hr-self-service-user-guides-0. Simon pointed out that there is very limited capacity currently available in the HR teams for supporting this activity. This ties in with the menu profile – something which needs support from Access Group.
*	The proposal is with Marie for signature.

 

Downstream impacts

 

*	The integrations team have been consulted regarding any possibility of ‘data leakage’ between environments. An assessment has been undertaken by Guy Williams who has recommended some code changes. There is an outstanding question which Action: SB will respond to. Assuming no more questions then we should accept Guy’s recommendation. Action: CM to follow-up.
*	David Hickman has also been consulted on the new interface platform being developed. David has confirmed no impact.
*	With regards the DWH there is a meeting on 22nd June between HR Systems and Ewan Kilgour. It was proposed that testing should be done on uoxu environment which is not connected to the DWH. 

 

The Org Structure and Reference Data

*	The reference data document still needs some refinement – particularly around ‘posts’.
*	The Org Structure reference data has been agreed, although Simon will undertake a final review before the Friday meeting with Access Group.

emails.subject: PeopleXD Insight Reporting – Quote Request for Holiday Records Reports
emails.to: Mike West; Ann-Marie Topliss; Amanda Mcdonnell
emails.body:
Hi Mike,
I hope you're well.
We've identified a requirement for a number of new PeopleXD Insight reports to support our holiday records reporting requirements and have developed an initial specification for review.
The attached documents include:

*	Oxford Holiday Records Revised Three Report Specification
*	Oxford Holiday Records Report Field Lists

The proposed solution consists of three reports:

*	Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year
*	Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements
*	Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination

We would be grateful if you could review the attached requirements and advise whether the proposed reports are achievable within Insight Reporting, together with an indication of any custom development that may be required.
I appreciate you are currently out of office, so I have copied Amanda McDonnell and Anne-Marie Topliss in case either is able to assist or direct this to the appropriate team while you are away.
We are working to a tight deadline, as we have flex points available within the current year that must be utilised before month end. We would therefore appreciate a quotation or indicative estimate at the earliest opportunity, as our intention is to use the remaining flex point allocation to fund this work if it is deemed feasible.
If a quotation cannot be provided from the attached documentation alone, would it be possible to arrange a scoping session as a priority, ideally within the next week, so that the requirements can be reviewed and an estimate produced in time for us to progress the work through the current funding allocation.
Please let me know if any further information would be helpful.
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports
emails.body:
Hi Athena,
Thank you for sending this through.
I've reviewed the revised spec. The three-report approach provides a much clearer structure than the original proposal, particularly in separating leave entitlement, accrual and holiday pay, and termination payments into distinct reporting areas.
I'll send the specification and field list to Access Group and ask them to review the requirements. Given our current flex point position and the need to utilise any remaining funding before month end, I'll also request an indicative quote as soon as possible. If they are unable to provide this from the documentation alone, I'll ask that a scoping session is arranged as a priority.
I'll let you know once I've heard back from them.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

Hi both,

 

Following discussion in today’s management team meeting, I’ve pivoted from attempting to join everything together into a single report and split out the requirements into three separate reports: 

 

1.	Annual Leave Record by Person and Leave Year
2.	Holiday Pay and Accrual Record for Variable-Hours and Casual Arrangements
3.	Holiday Pay in Lieu on Termination

 

See attached for the proposed report specifications, as well as how these reports support the holiday records duty. A handful of material gaps remain to be addressed, but these reports should get us most of the way towards being able to demonstrate full compliance with the duty.  

 

Could you send this document (and the associated Report Field List spreadsheet) to Access Group, to see whether the proposed reports might be buildable in Insight? 

 

Thanks!!

Athena

emails.subject: Re: #External# Re: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up
emails.body:
Hi Conor,
I've attached a draft REF2029 load file based on the format in the test HR Reference File you sent through.
Would you mind taking a quick look and confirming that the file structure and contents are correct for the HR Reference File load?
I'm also still waiting to understand where the upload process is run from within PeopleXD and whether there are any additional steps required after loading, such as hierarchy linking.
Any documentation covering the upload process would also be appreciated, as I'd like to document the full process for our internal knowledge base.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com>
Sent: 11 June 2026 10:03 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>
Subject: RE: #External# Re: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up
 

Good Morning Kevin,

 

I hope you are well.

 

Sorry, I didn’t see your email as I am working down through them, but to cover what I said on Teams:

 

“Yes that is correct in terms of where possible to load, but just to confirm firstly, you do wish for the "REF2029" codes to appear on the employee appointment record similar to what shows for other employees currently., it is user field 5 which can be located on the Data Centre under the Reference Data area.

Yes this would be the correct place to create the codes.

As a test you could create one called "Test" and assign a random code and you should then upon saving be able to see this in the Hierarchy as an available item which to link at 

If you have access on "Hr User Access" for "Personnel" access at any level above "Pay Administered By", you should inherit the access to this area so long as you have the access to the department, level, division etc above it which it is linked to on the Hierarchy.

In terms of Data Migrating the data, yes this is possible, if you use the "Reference - HR Reference File" it is possible to load the reference data en masse. I am blocked from attaching a document to this chat but will send across in an email shortly.”

 

Including above a test file which I just loaded on my own internal site to confirm all worked as expected. In theory you should be able to able to use this file on your UAT site and it should work if you wished to use it as a quick test.

 

If there is any other questions ahead of tomorrow, please let me know. I do see two emails from Simon but just haven’t yet had a chance to review but will do so today.

 

Thank you,

 

Conor O'Brien

Senior Consultant

The Access Group Unit 2302, 3rd Floor Building 2000, Citygate,

Mahon, Cork, T12 K06Y

www.theaccessgroup.com <http://www.theaccessgroup.com/> 

 <https://www.theaccessgroup.com/>

When: Fri 2026-06-12 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM
Where: Microsoft Teams Meeting 


Good Morning All,

 

I hope you are all well.

 

Can I please ask you to join me on the 12th of June for a session to cover the below items:

 

Training Agenda: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up

1. Overview & Objectives

*	Purpose of the session
*	Key dependencies between hierarchy and payroll configuration
*	Pre-requisites (completed in advance):

	*	Hierarchy placement agreed
	*	Reference data created (e.g. Management Units, Departments)

2. Payroll Company Set-Up

*	Creating the company in Payroll (copy vs new)
*	Key company parameters and required fields
*	Overview of payroll configuration

3. Pay Group Configuration

*	Creating a dummy/non-payroll pay group
*	Linking pay group to the new company
*	Importance for employee onboarding

4. Building the Hierarchy

*	Adding the company to the hierarchy structure
*	Linking levels and validating structure
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy integrity

5. Summary & Next Steps

*	Recap of configuration order:

	1.	Payroll company setup
	2.	Pay group creation
	3.	Build organisational hierarchy

*	Q&A

 

 

Thank you,

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: 09 June 2026 15:48
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports

 

Hi Simon,

 

Please see attached for the two .SQL reports that Codex helped me to create, based on existing Time and Payroll reports. There is also a ‘Buildability Matrix’ which indicates how easy (or not!) it will be to recreate the SQL logic in Insight Reporting, based on Codex’s generic understanding of that tool. Lastly, there is the Gap Analysis, which shows which duty requirements will remain unmet by the proposed reports.

In brief, the material gaps remaining are:

 

*	transparent holiday pay calculation basis
*	direct leave-to-pay linkage
*	full payment-in-lieu methodology
*	full irregular-hours / part-year accrual audit trail
*	six-year retention evidence, which sits outside the report outputs themselves

 

I don’t know whether we are capable of meeting these requirements with any of our existing reports, nor do I know whether these gaps are best closed with a report or with a detailed business rules document indicating the methodology and rules for calculation, accrual, payment, and retention of holiday pay and related records. 

 

I hope this review is useful in at least starting a discussion with Access Group abou

Please see the attached requirements for our new org structure. We will go ahead and set up the codes in UOXU tomorrow. 

 

In terms of the decisions, just to confirm, I think we’ll need to set up the Payroll Company from scratch as we’ve never had a non-payroll company before so I’d want to be sure that the config is correct, but the pay group can be a copy of the existing pay group 99.

Subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup

 

Good Afternoon Simon,

 

I hope you are well. Apologies, I was on site training.

 

Can I ask if the below agenda suits what you wish to achieve on the 12th or if there is anything else which you would like to cover:

 

Training Agenda: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up

1. Overview & Objectives

*	Purpose of the session
*	Key dependencies between hierarchy and payroll configuration
*	Pre-requisites (completed in advance): 

	*	Hierarchy placement agreed
	*	Reference data created (e.g. Management Units, Departments)

2. Payroll Company Set-Up

*	Creating the company in Payroll (copy vs new)
*	Key company parameters and required fields
*	Overview of payroll configuration

3. Pay Group Configuration

*	Creating a dummy/non-payroll pay group
*	Linking pay group to the new company
*	Importance for employee onboarding

4. Building the Hierarchy

*	Adding the company to the hierarchy structure
*	Linking levels and validating structure
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy integrity

5. Summary & Next Steps

*	Recap of configuration order: 

	1.	Payroll company setup
	2.	Pay group creation
	3.	Build organisational hierarchy

*	Q&A

 

Just also with the above, some choices made by yourselves will dictate what we would need to cover, so for example, we need to decide with the Company, do we want to copy a preexisting Company or create from scratch. Similarly, we also need to decide the same for Pay Group. 

 

For the Pay Groups, on UOXU for example, there is Pay Groups 99(Non Employees) and 2221(Test PG), if these were suitable, we can use one of these as the template to create our new non payroll

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 09 June 2026 15:48
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports

 

Hi Simon,

 

Please see attached for the two .SQL reports that Codex helped me to create, based on existing Time and Payroll reports. There is also a ‘Buildability Matrix’ which indicates how easy (or not!) it will be to recreate the SQL logic in Insight Reporting, based on Codex’s generic understanding of that tool. Lastly, there is the Gap Analysis, which shows which duty requirements will remain unmet by the proposed reports.

In brief, the material gaps remaining are:

 

*	transparent holiday pay calculation basis
*	direct leave-to-pay linkage
*	full payment-in-lieu methodology
*	full irregular-hours / part-year accrual audit trail
*	six-year retention evidence, which sits outside the report outputs themselves

 

I don’t know whether we are capable of meeting these requirements with any of our existing reports, nor do I know whether these gaps are best closed with a report or with a detailed business rules document indicating the methodology and rules for calculation, accrual, payment, and retention of holiday pay and related records. 

 

I hope this review is useful in at least starting a discussion with Access Group about what’s possible in Insight – do let me know if you’d like a chat about this just with me to talk through our options 😊

 

Best,

Athena

 

Athena Artuso (she/her)

People Analytics Team Lead

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Hi Conor,

 

Please see the attached requirements for our new org structure. We will go ahead and set up the codes in UOXU tomorrow. 

 

In terms of the decisions, just to confirm, I think we’ll need to set up the Payroll Company from scratch as we’ve never had a non-payroll company before so I’d want to be sure that the config is correct, but the pay group can be a copy of the existing pay group 99.

 

If there’s anything else you need us to confirm before our session on Friday please let me know.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com> 
Sent: 21 May 2026 16:08
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup

 

Good Afternoon Simon,

 

I hope you are well. Apologies, I was on site training.

 

Can I ask if the below agenda suits what you wish to achieve on the 12th or if there is anything else which you would like to cover:

 

Training Agenda: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up

1. Overview & Objectives

*	Purpose of the session
*	Key dependencies between hierarchy and payroll configuration
*	Pre-requisites (completed in advance): 

	*	Hierarchy placement agreed
	*	Reference data created (e.g. Management Units, Departments)

2. Payroll Company Set-Up

*	Creating the company in Payroll (copy vs new)
*	Key company parameters and required fields
*	Overview of payroll configuration

3. Pay Group Configuration

*	Creating a dummy/non-payroll pay group
*	Linking pay group to the new company
*	Importance for employee onboarding

4. Building the Hierarchy

*	Adding the company to the hierarchy structure
*	Linking levels and validating structure
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy integrity

5. Summary & Next Steps

*	Recap of configuration order: 

	1.	Payroll company setup
	2.	Pay group creation
	3.	Build organisational hierarchy

*	Q&A

 

Just also with the above, some choices made by yourselves will dictate what we would need to cover, so for example, we need to decide with the Company, do we want to copy a preexisting Company or create from scratch. Similarly, we also need to decide the same for Pay Group. 

 

For the Pay Groups, on UOXU for example, there is Pay Groups 99(Non Employees) and 2221(Test PG), if these were suitable, we can use one of these as the template to create our new non payroll pay group so that we can link this to the new Organisation. If not, we can of course create from scratch.

 


1. Payroll Company – Create New vs Copy


Copy Existing Company (Recommended)


When to use:

*	New company is similar to an existing one
*	Same country, payroll rules, or configuration structure

Benefits:

*	Faster setup – most fields pre-populated
*	Reduces risk of missing key payroll parameters
*	Ensures consistency across companies

What to review after copying:

*	Company Code and Name
*	Contact and registration details
*	Currency, payroll provider, and bank details
*	Payroll parameters (e.g. pay codes, cost centres) 

Key consideration:
Copying is best practice as it “cuts down processing time” and only requires minor updates rather than full configuration 

________________________________


Create New Company (From Scratch)


When to use:

*	Company has different payroll requirements
*	Different country, legislation, or setup
*	No suitable template exists

Considerations:

*	All fields must be manually configured
*	Higher risk of missing mandatory payrol

From: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 21 May 2026 14:24
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Michael,

I reran the Insight reports for all LEVELS and SOC values in UOXP (after refreshing the data to ensure the latest version was used).

After noticing that there were fewer SOC records than LEVELS records, I compared the two populations in Excel to identify records present in LEVELS but missing from SOC. I was then able to locate the corresponding SOC values in an older SOC extract. From the investigation, it appears the issue is likely related to the Comments field not having been updated to reflect inclusion in the 2024/25 return. This resulted in the records not appearing as expected, since they only had 2023/24 comments.

I also realised that I previously provided the wrong Appointment IDs — apologies for the confusion.

The records currently missing SOC values for 2024/25 are:

Person Reference           Appointment ID               HESA Code         HESA Code Description

2361315                           266215-1                          211                      Natural and Social Science Professionals

2201600                           217565-1                          231                      Teaching Professionals

For both records, the SOC entries do exist historically and can be evidenced in prior extracts. In each case, there is:

*	a record marked as “Returned 23-24”
*	a corresponding record included in the 2024/25 return extract downloaded from HESA

This suggests the records themselves were not missing, but that the Comments field may not have been overwritten to reflect 2024/25 inclusion during processing. Based on this, would AG be able to reinsert the missing records and include the 2024/25 comments values so that we have a complete record for 2024/25?

I am also adding an additional validation step to our process notes for future returns so we can identify this type of issue before the module rollover takes place and ensure we’ve corrected it ourselves.

Please let me know if any further detail or extracts would be helpful.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Data in PXD - Integrations
emails.to: Simon Burford; Anne Mortimer; Kevin Lelitte; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Hi Simon

 

Thank you very much for this. Look forward to discussing further tomorrow. In the meantime I will contact David Hickman.

 

Crispin

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 08 June 2026 18:25
To: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Steve McBrearty <steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 College Data in PXD - Integrations

 

Hi Crispin,

 

I’ve responded to Guy – he sent me this (and the context) in an OSM task.

 

For transparency, the full text from Guy is:

 

Working on impact analysis (Person and/or Appt records for a 'new company' 90).

Assumed: current integrations (Biztalk and int-stg) are supposed to only transfer company=10 info (main univ payroll appts).

 

However.. Biztalk currently filters only on appt dept code not starting with 'Z'.

i.e. no explicit `and p.company=10` and/or `and a.company=10` in its query.

Thus company!=10 info may be leaking into subscribing systems.

Further there may be data quality issues around the person and appt tables in pxd.

For both issues the following query shows extent of problem, as at june2026.

 

For Interface-staging pxd flows - they DO restrict on  prbi_hr_person_master.company = 10. 

BUT NOT ON prbi_appointment_history.company_code

(atm there are no appt records with company!=10).

 

Questions: 

1. Given results below, is BT sending appt info to subscribing systems?

 particularly emp 2384116 is a recently ended appt.

2. For all flows, should they be checking person.company only, or appt also (assuming not appt as that appears to be 10 always, though I haven't reviewed the pxd BI layer views to see if they are filtering out rows).

3. Similar to q2, but specifically for the new company 90 - is that going to work like companies 50, 60, 63, 70?

 

And my response to him was:

 

 

Hi Guy,

 

Thank you for this analysis. It may help to have this context:

 

During administrative processing in PeopleXD, Company!=10 employees should be kept separate to Company=10 employees, however there's not actually anything stopping administrators processing data incorrectly resulting in poor data quality in the system.

 

I have reviewed a couple of the examples provided and they appear to be where a person has a record in a company other than Company 10, and in error an appointment has been added within Company 10. Where this happens the company on the appointment overwrites the company on the person. 

 

In these instances the company 10 appointment has been ended the same day is it started, indicating an error, but as a result of the data quality error the company at person level has been set as Company 10.

 

The safest coding to use would be to ensure a.company=10. The appointment record is the primary source of information about a person's association with a company, the company held at person level is a by product of the appointment.

 

Preventing appointments with a department code starting with a Z will prevent the existing !10 records from being passed downstream, however I do not think this will work for the new structure we are setting up because we are planning on setting up colleges using their PACS structure codes. e.g. 'JN' = Wolfson College.

 

The fact that there are currently no appointment records with company!=10 makes sense given the above, but we would need the logic to be adjusted to explicitly restrict on a.company=10.

 

In answer to your questions:

1. Currently it looks like !co10 records are not being passed, however the current rules are not going to prevent company 90 data being passed to subscribing systems.

 

2. All flows should be checking company = 10 against the appointment

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Data in PXD - Integrations
emails.to: Crispin Muncaster; Anne Mortimer; Kevin Lelitte; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

 

I’ve responded to Guy – he sent me this (and the context) in an OSM task.

 

For transparency, the full text from Guy is:

 

Working on impact analysis (Person and/or Appt records for a 'new company' 90).

Assumed: current integrations (Biztalk and int-stg) are supposed to only transfer company=10 info (main univ payroll appts).

 

However.. Biztalk currently filters only on appt dept code not starting with 'Z'.

i.e. no explicit `and p.company=10` and/or `and a.company=10` in its query.

Thus company!=10 info may be leaking into subscribing systems.

Further there may be data quality issues around the person and appt tables in pxd.

For both issues the following query shows extent of problem, as at june2026.

 

For Interface-staging pxd flows - they DO restrict on  prbi_hr_person_master.company = 10. 

BUT NOT ON prbi_appointment_history.company_code

(atm there are no appt records with company!=10).

 

Questions: 

1. Given results below, is BT sending appt info to subscribing systems?

 particularly emp 2384116 is a recently ended appt.

2. For all flows, should they be checking person.company only, or appt also (assuming not appt as that appears to be 10 always, though I haven't reviewed the pxd BI layer views to see if they are filtering out rows).

3. Similar to q2, but specifically for the new company 90 - is that going to work like companies 50, 60, 63, 70?

 

And my response to him was:

 

 

Hi Guy,

 

Thank you for this analysis. It may help to have this context:

 

During administrative processing in PeopleXD, Company!=10 employees should be kept separate to Company=10 employees, however there's not actually anything stopping administrators processing data incorrectly resulting in poor data quality in the system.

 

I have reviewed a couple of the examples provided and they appear to be where a person has a record in a company other than Company 10, and in error an appointment has been added within Company 10. Where this happens the company on the appointment overwrites the company on the person. 

 

In these instances the company 10 appointment has been ended the same day is it started, indicating an error, but as a result of the data quality error the company at person level has been set as Company 10.

 

The safest coding to use would be to ensure a.company=10. The appointment record is the primary source of information about a person's association with a company, the company held at person level is a by product of the appointment.

 

Preventing appointments with a department code starting with a Z will prevent the existing !10 records from being passed downstream, however I do not think this will work for the new structure we are setting up because we are planning on setting up colleges using their PACS structure codes. e.g. 'JN' = Wolfson College.

 

The fact that there are currently no appointment records with company!=10 makes sense given the above, but we would need the logic to be adjusted to explicitly restrict on a.company=10.

 

In answer to your questions:

1. Currently it looks like !co10 records are not being passed, however the current rules are not going to prevent company 90 data being passed to subscribing systems.

 

2. All flows should be checking company = 10 against the appointment ONLY. Checking company on the person record is prone to inaccuracies and data quality issues and could cause issues and conflicts with the data held at appointment level so best to just ignore the person level data.

 

Employee 2384116 is a perfect example of the data quality issue described above, this person held an appointment record in the linacre payroll, where the department is listed as "ZZ - LINACRE COLLEGE PAYROLL" so would have been excluded from the interface, however, they have had an appointment created in Law, in error, which was likely passed to downstream systems.

Cc: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Emma Fitz-Gibbon <emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Major Incident - Priority 1 Support Call# TBC - a Security vulnerability with Ivanti.

 

Hello all

 

There is not a lot to update you on regards finding a resolution to the OSM issue.

 

*	Both support teams in HR Systems and Safety Office have workarounds, that whilst add additional manual activity on them thus making the service slower, does mean we can still deliver a service and keep details within OSM for audit.

 

*	Communications have been sent out University-wide and also to all our local channels, so hopefully most users are aware, or will be when they return from leave.

 

*	UCISA (independent association) have agreed to act as an intermediary between Ivanti and the 10+ HEIs affected in UK. We have been advised that they are actively working on a solution, but that is not expected this week, so the next Incident meeting is convened for Tuesday 2nd June.

 

Once again thank you to everyone who has developed our workarounds and allowed us to continue to deliver a service 😊

 

I’ll let you know if I hear anything else in the interim.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

emails.body:
Hi all,

Thank you again for attending the workshop on Sustaining Adoption of Leave & Absence.

Please find attached a summary of the key findings and proposed next steps.

Marie reviewed the summary before going on leave and asked me to confirm that she will work with the team to review the recommendations, agree priorities, and determine how and when any agreed actions should be taken forward as part of the roadmap.

If you have any questions or additional comments, please do let me know.

Thanks,

Lyndsey

emails.subject: Marie Handover 8-12 June
emails.to: Simon Burford; Michelle Williams; Christopher Sanders; Kevin Lelitte; Julie Hickman
emails.body:
Hi all

 

A few notes for handover, whilst I’m off:

 

*	H&S – Chris and I have met with colleagues from the Fire team, and have agreed to support them to document their reporting/dashboard needs. I will work with Kate Vickers to try and secure a BI Analyst to take the development forward. In the meantime, we are seeking access to RiskBase to have a look at it (the system that holds the data), any data outputs they currently have (Excel) and examples of dashboards they want created. This is considered a high priority for the University, hence why I’m supporting to actually move it forward.
*	PFST

	*	Simon will be attending the next talent acquisition workshop on Thur 11th, I would like to have a summary of this session and thoughts/ideas/considerations that I may need to take onboard for “potential” Eploy implementation.
	*	Data assessment, if you are invited to attend a workshop by a supplier (possibly via Tom Harlos), please do priortise as this is feeding into the data preparedness for migration to a new HR/Payroll and Finance system as part of PFST programme. If invited, could you let me know for info please?

*	OSM – Emma is scheduled to attend the “Major Incident” updates, next one due Tuesday, but I have asked her to let Simon know if she is unable to attend so he can go in her place. I always want a People representative, so should both be unavailable please reach out to Saffron Greenwood, to have a team member’s name added to the meeting list so they can join.
*	Access Requests – Simon you will have to pick these up in my absence. @Asta Palmer <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – just flagging this change for your info.
*	Escalation – in the first instance, please speak to Simon, but should he not be available Sarah/Athena. If you need to discuss an issue then in the first instance can you reach out to my colleagues in Talent Management SMT ie Phil, Laura Brown, Sarah Clarke, Sarah Kilgour – they are on standby should you need them. I am contactable Thu/Fri – so if anything urgent arises, please do feel free to WhatsApp me and I’ll call you back as soon as I can.

 

See you on 15th!

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

Marie Cooksey

Head of HR Systems

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx>  | Contact the team <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/contact-details.aspx> 

 

 



I may send emails outside of your usual working hours. Please be assured there is no expectation for you to respond to this outside of your own working hours.

emails.subject: FW: Preventing archiving of users not included in HR data upload
emails.body:
Hi Team,

 

The automated data upload from PeopleXD to the DSE system (Healthy Working Plus) went live on 3rd June, and overall the dataset looks correct. However, I’ve identified a small number of issues that I’m currently investigating.

 

One of these relates to some users being automatically archived, as they are not present in the PeopleXD dataset. This is typically the case for contractors or users who have university credentials but are not directly employed by the University.

 

Jill Farmer is one of the affected users.

 

Could you please check her account on Wednesday and ensure that it remains active (or reactivate it if needed)?

 

I’m currently in contact with Cardinus to put a more permanent solution in place, but in the meantime your support would be really appreciated.

 

If any similar queries come through, please do let me know so I can investigate further.

 

Thanks very much for your help.

 

Best wishes,

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting> 

 

Whatfix: Discover our self-help tool for PeopleXD <https://hrsystems.admin.ox.ac.uk/whatfix> 

 

             <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>  

 

From: James Salas Guillen 
Sent: 05 June 2026 15:04
To: 'Matt Walford' <Matt.Walford@cardinus.com>
Cc: Gail Miller <gail.miller@safety.ox.ac.uk>; support <support@cardinus.com>
Subject: RE: Preventing archiving of users not included in HR data upload

 

Hi Matt, 

 

Thank you for this. However, the user in question is a DSE Assessor. Could you please confirm whether they can hold both roles? When I attempted this, I could only select one or the other.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James

 

 

From: Matt Walford <Matt.Walford@cardinus.com <mailto:Matt.Walford@cardinus.com> > 
Sent: 04 June 2026 13:56
To: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Gail Miller <gail.miller@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:gail.miller@safety.ox.ac.uk> >; support <support@cardinus.com <mailto:support@cardinus.com> >
Subject: RE: Preventing archiving of users not included in HR data upload

 

Last one from me for the moment!

 

We have the ability to mark someone on PACE as do not archive currently – your account for example is on the data import so we leave things just as course user or a type of admin account:

 



 

This gets updated/archived with the data import as normal.

 

One of my test accounts which would never be on the auto import I’ve marked as Do not archive:

 



 

So that means when the import runs it ignores my account and leaves things as they are.

 

This is manually done in the users module – modifying the account required.

 

In practical terms, you could add a contractor on the system that wouldn’t appear on the data import and the system would leave the account active until it was manually archived when not needed.

 

Couple of things to think about – the account set like this won’t be on any of the reports or email schedules (we look for active users – we would need to change things if this was required).  The accounts never get archived unless someone does it manually – so it will take a licence.  More importantly they probably won’t be able to logon via the SSO link, if they don’t have the UserID used by SSO they will see an error message (there are things that might help with this though).

 

Hope that makes sense, if you need a call to discuss let me know and we can get something in the diary.

 

If you need anything explaining just let me know.

 

 

 

Matt Walford

Customer Services Executive

 

Matt.Walford@Cardinus.com <mailto:Matt.Walford@Cardinus.com> 

T: +44 (0) 207 469 0200

M: +44 (0) 7813 310690

cardinus.com <http://

Sent: 05 June 2026 11:36
To: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Rachel Midgley <rachel.midgley@it.ox.ac.uk>; Ralph Watson <ralph.watson@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi James

 

Thank you for update. Could you please confirm who is creating the manual records for contractors etc? 

 

I have copied in @Ralph Watson <mailto:ralph.watson@it.ox.ac.uk>  and @Rachel Midgley <mailto:rachel.midgley@it.ox.ac.uk> , to:

*	Is there any update on the call raised with Azure team re excluding SBS from feed.
*	about Ralph’s availability support over the next few months.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 05 June 2026 10:59
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie, 

 

Yes, the automated data upload from PXD to Cardinus is now live and working as expected. Based on initial spot checks, the data appears to be accurate.

 

However, there are a small number of issues that we are addressing as part of BAU:

 

*	SBS users – These users are currently included in the data upload but should not be, as they use a separate DSE system. There is an email rule currently in place to exclude them from DSE emails but ideally we would want them to be excluded from the data upload. We may need to raise a service request to exclude SBS users from the feed. I believe Ralph has raised this with the Azure team. Ralph is currently on annual leave and not back until the 15th June. 
*	Contractors and other non‑PXD users with DSE requirements – As these users are not present in PXD, they are being automatically archived during the weekly upload. As a temporary workaround, these users can be manually tagged as ‘Do Not Archive’ to prevent this. Any new users not in PXD who require DSE access will also need to be created manually. 
*	Users without an ox.ac.uk email address – There are a small number of users without an Oxford email address. We are monitoring these and keeping an eye on them, as the numbers are currently low.

 

Process documentation is currently being updated and will be uploaded to the H&S folder ahead of the handover session with Kevin and Chris this afternoon.

 

The next scheduled data upload is Wednesday 10th June at 4:00am. 

 

There should not be a need for further technical input, but could you confirm whether we can call on Ralph for any technical questions that may arise over the next few weeks, just in case?

 

Best wishes,  

 

James Salas

Senior Functi

emails.subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus
emails.to: James Salas Guillen; Rachel Midgley; Ralph Watson
emails.body:
Hi James

 

Thank you for update. Could you please confirm who is creating the manual records for contractors etc? 

 

I have copied in @Ralph Watson <mailto:ralph.watson@it.ox.ac.uk>  and @Rachel Midgley <mailto:rachel.midgley@it.ox.ac.uk> , to:

*	Is there any update on the call raised with Azure team re excluding SBS from feed.
*	about Ralph’s availability support over the next few months.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 05 June 2026 10:59
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie, 

 

Yes, the automated data upload from PXD to Cardinus is now live and working as expected. Based on initial spot checks, the data appears to be accurate.

 

However, there are a small number of issues that we are addressing as part of BAU:

 

*	SBS users – These users are currently included in the data upload but should not be, as they use a separate DSE system. There is an email rule currently in place to exclude them from DSE emails but ideally we would want them to be excluded from the data upload. We may need to raise a service request to exclude SBS users from the feed. I believe Ralph has raised this with the Azure team. Ralph is currently on annual leave and not back until the 15th June. 
*	Contractors and other non‑PXD users with DSE requirements – As these users are not present in PXD, they are being automatically archived during the weekly upload. As a temporary workaround, these users can be manually tagged as ‘Do Not Archive’ to prevent this. Any new users not in PXD who require DSE access will also need to be created manually. 
*	Users without an ox.ac.uk email address – There are a small number of users without an Oxford email address. We are monitoring these and keeping an eye on them, as the numbers are currently low.

 

Process documentation is currently being updated and will be uploaded to the H&S folder ahead of the handover session with Kevin and Chris this afternoon.

 

The next scheduled data upload is Wednesday 10th June at 4:00am. 

 

There should not be a need for further technical input, but could you confirm whether we can call on Ralph for any technical questions that may arise over the next few weeks, just in case?

 

Best wishes,  

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

Marie

 	Marie Cooksey left a comment

Not 100% sure what this risk means, could this be further clarified please?


 	You left a comment

My thinking was that we haven't yet assessed whether Company 90 records could be picked up by any existing talent management functionality, PDR or CDR processes, reporting or interfaces that are currently configured for Company 10 employees.
I don't know whether this is actually a risk, but I couldn't see it being addressed elsewhere in the design, so I wanted to capture it for investigation and testing. It may ultimately be mitigated by the proposed ESS menu profile and other access controls, but I think we should confirm this as part of the wider review of what functionality Company 90 users can access and what data they may be included in.


 	Marie Cooksey replied

thanks for explanation Kevin, and yes glad you have identified that will need testing.


…y the Research project team, this may include staff training for employee self-service

7. Risks and Issues

The risk register and the issue registerheld by the DTP 1092 Project 

A copy of the risk registeras at 29/05/26is listed below for convenience

…

Inconsistent admin activity	Inconsistent admin activity - Errors and inconsistent data handling	Define roles; mandatory training; guidance materials	
Company 90-only access needs	Company 90-only access needs - Missing personal data	Ensure access + onboarding guidance.also need to add a mitigation around data guardianship - need to define who is the guardian for college data – i.e. who signs off on who gets access to college data and a solid technical process for how this is controlled	
Validating record changes	There is risk that record changes could be made without any validation in place or without the necessary access governance in place.		
Talent data	There is a risk that talent data may be automatically assigned to college staff which could end up in reporting etc. 	This would need mitigating and testing	
College data in main DWH	There is risk that college data could enter the main data warehouse in an unintended way. 	IT Services resource will need to be involved in designing and implementing the data warehouse solution to make sure college data is segregated completely from the main University records.	
Person reference numbering	Risk of disrupting the numbering system for Company 10 users, or potentially a number being taken by a Company 10 user during the upload process making it unavailable for Company 90 usage. 		
…

@Kevin Lelitte - is this meant to say HESA fields?

Yes amended




•This provides a reliable join key for data warehouse and reporting purposes.
Additional UDFs
The following additional UDFs will be required on college staff records to support REF and HESA reporting:
•Academic title
•Fellowship designation
HESA REF fields

Subject: POC for HALO data work?

 

Dear Heidi, Marie and Simon

 

I hope this finds you well. The data architecture team is getting more involved with HALO - especially around reference data standardisation - and it would be helpful to have specific points of contact to liaise with for that piece of work on both Education and People Data.

Daphne

emails.subject: Re: Reference request - Lee Strudwick
emails.body:
Hi Claire,
Thank you for your email.
I can confirm the following information in relation to Lee Strudwick:
Dates of employment
February 2025 – 30 January 2026
Job title and key responsibilities
Senior Functional Analyst
Key responsibilities included:

*	
	Providing second-line support for HR and payroll systems, investigating and resolving complex technical issues.
*	
	Analysing business requirements and implementing system enhancements and configuration changes.
*	
	Coordinating system changes, testing, software releases, and quality assurance activities.
*	
	Working closely with stakeholders across HR, Payroll, Pensions, IT Services, and external suppliers.
*	
	Supporting system compliance with legislative and policy requirements.
*	
	Producing and developing reports to meet business needs.
*	
	Managing incidents, problem resolution, and supplier escalations.
*	
	Contributing subject matter expertise to projects, governance groups, and continuous system improvement initiatives.
*	
	Maintaining system documentation, testing procedures, and user guidance materials.
*	
	Supporting the delivery and ongoing development of the HR Systems service.

Reason for leaving
Contract end.
Key skills, achievements, and attributes in their position held
Lee demonstrated strong analytical and problem-solving skills and was able to investigate and resolve complex system issues effectively. He developed a good understanding of HR business processes and system functionality and worked collaboratively with colleagues, stakeholders, and suppliers to deliver system improvements and support services. Lee was reliable, professional, and adaptable, with a strong attention to detail and a willingness to take ownership of tasks and see them through to completion. He communicated effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences and made a positive contribution to the HR Systems team.
Would you re-employ them?
Yes.
Additional comments
Lee was a valued member of the team and made a positive contribution during his time with us.
Please let me know if you require any further information.
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 









________________________________

From: Claire Scrase <claire@s4labour.co.uk>
Sent: Thursday, May 28, 2026 10:42 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Human Resources <HR@s4labour.co.uk>
Subject: Reference request - Lee Strudwick 


Good morning Kevin,

 

I hope you’re well.

Lee Strudwick has recently applied for a position with us at S4Labour Limited and has provided your details as a referee.

We would be grateful if you could provide a reference to support us in assessing their suitability for the role of Senior Service Management Analyst by kindly providing the following details and responding at your earliest convenience.

 

*	Dates of employment
*	Job title and key responsibilities
*	Reason for leaving (if applicable)
*	Key skills, achievements, and attributes in their position held
*	Whether you would re-employ them
*	Any additional comments you feel are relevant

 

We appreciate that you may be limited by your organisation’s reference policy, and any information you are able to share would be very helpful.

Please feel free to reply directly to this email or let me know if you would prefer to discuss this via phone.

 

Thank you in advance for your time.

Claire Scrase
​​​​
Head of Operational Finance
	 
t: +44 01295 267400 <tel:+44%2001295%20267400>

emails.subject: Re: Halo ER Case Management Support Operating Model - Approval Confirmation
emails.to: Natasha Heaton; Samantha Bateman; Carla Thornley; Stuart Robeson; Andrew Fuller; Marie Cooksey; Kevin Lelitte; Dawn McNish; Beata Krzysztan; Isobel Stokoe; Matthew Castle; Benjamin Hill; Chris Mackie; David Hickman; Roy McKee; Djena Heaton; Sarah Warne; Louise Piper
emails.body:
Dear all,
Thank you, @Natasha Heaton <mailto:natasha.heaton@digital.ox.ac.uk> ,for letting us know. I am sorry to hear that the Product Owner is still off sick, and I hope they make a full recovery soon. While it would certainly be beneficial to have the Product Owner involved in the review, I believe the discussion can still be productive with the team representatives currently invited.
The purpose of the session is to review the temporary support arrangements that were put in place earlier this year (end of January), discuss any concerns or required changes, and agree the approach going forward. As the review has been scheduled well in advance and the relevant teams have already been invited, I would prefer to keep the current date. My main consideration would be ensuring that the relevant teams are represented by the appropriate authority to agree any changes.
The objective is to conclude the review with a clear direction and agreed next steps, rather than defer decisions for further rounds of discussion afterwards. For that reason, I suggest we keep the 30 July meeting in the diary unless there is a significant risk that we would be unable to agree and confirm the way forward on the day.


Kind regards,
 
Viktoriia Vasyliuta



IT Support Architect

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square

Oxford OX1 2JD

Home | IT Help <https://help.it.ox.ac.uk/> 


________________________________

emails.subject: FW: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

 

As you can see I’m rather behind on emails!

 

I am not sure if anyone has reached out direct, but could you please action this for me as soon as possible please re the option to select volunteering pilot?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Matt Thomas <matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 06 May 2026 11:37
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD

 

Hi Marie,

 

Just following on from this email I was wondering whether the ‘volunteering pilot’ drop-down in People XD can be paused now that the pilot has officially concluded and while we’re awaiting approval from People Committee to extend the pilot in the next academic year? I want to ensure that hours aren’t being recorded during this interim period if possible.

 

Many thanks,

 

Matthew Thomas

Project Delivery Manager - Pay and Conditions

People Department | University of Oxford

matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 



 

 

From: Matt Thomas 
Sent: 28 April 2026 13:30
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD

 

Hi Marie,

 

I hope that all’s well with you.

 

The Volunteering Pilot paper was well received at PCOB yesterday but concerns around the operational complexity associated with logging issues were highlighted as an issue that would need fixing before a wider University roll-out would be possible. 

 

I believe that the main difficulties during the pilot phase were due to a combination of the below:

 

a.	Line managers having to log the hours on behalf of their staff, rather than staff being able to log them and line manager’s approving the hours (as is the case with annual leave);
b.	Some departments still not being fully transitioned onto People XD;
c.	Some departmental administrators still not being fully trained in using People XD.

 

In light of this - I was wondering whether yourself or a member of your team could please kindly provide an update on:

 

1.	The status of departmental migration over to People XD and which departments are still yet to migrate;
2.	The status of training for departmental administrators in using People XD;
3.	The possibility to amend the module so that participants can request their volunteer hours for approval by their manager’s, as opposed to the manager’s having to log the hours.

 

Many thanks in advance for any help that you’re able to offer on the above queries.

 

Kind regards,

 

Matthew Thomas

Project Delivery Manager - Pay and Conditions

People Department | University of Oxford

matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Hi all,

Thank you for your feedback on the Halo ER Case Management Support Operating Model.

All comments received to date have been reviewed and addressed. As no further feedback has been received, the current version of the Support Operating Model should be considered the agreed working version. This version remains in effect until it is superseded by a subsequent revision. The current version of the Support Operating Model and its supporting documentation can be found in the ​Halo ER Case Management document Library <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OUIT-ITSupportArchitecture/Halo%20ER%20Case%20Management/Forms/AllItems.aspx> ​.

emails.subject: FW: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM), logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals
emails.body:
Hi All

 

Please see centralised communication update for your information regarding the ongoing OSM issue.

 

The next meeting is on Thursday, hopefully we will be given some resolution dates.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Sarah Warne <sarah.warne@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 02 June 2026 16:36
To: it-services-osm-users-all@maillist.ox.ac.uk
Subject: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM), logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals

 

Dear all,

 

For Information:  

*	Note that IT self-service is currently unavailable for some users

•    Internal users of OSM can log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

•    Suggested workaround for management of approvals for Service Requests

 

Further to my email on Friday 22 May about the ongoing security issue affecting IT Self-Service via Oxford Service Manager (OSM), the University is actively working with the technical team at Ivanti to secure a resolution that will enable full restoration of service. This may take some time, given Ivanti is supporting impacted organisations around the world. An update on timelines for further engagement is expected from Ivanti’s support team in the next couple of days. 

 

Ian Teasdale, Director of Digital Assurance, is representing the University on a sector group convened by the HE IT sector body, UCISA, to expediate a resolution at a national level. This has already opened additional technical support routes.

 

Please refer to Louise’s email below for guidance on how to log a Service Request (SR) on behalf of a customer, and how to manage approvals whilst IT Self-Service is unavailable to many colleagues.

Please note the attached guidance document has been updated to clarify some minor details, however the process remains unchanged.

 

Please feel free to reach out if you have any further questions. 

 

Kind regards, 

Sarah

 

 

From: Louise Piper <louise.piper@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:louise.piper@it.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 27 May 2026 11:34
To: it-services-osm-users-all@maillist.ox.ac.uk <mailto:it-services-osm-users-all@maillist.ox.ac.uk> 
Subject: For Information: Logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals

 

From: Louise Piper, ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist

To: All OSM users

 

Please disregard this email if you do not need to log or manage Service Requests on behalf of customers.

Note that any previous recommendations have been updated as a result of further testing and feedback.

 

For Information: 

•              Internal users of OSM can log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

•              Suggested workaround for management of approvals for Service Requests

 

Dear All,

 

Please find attached guidance on how to log a Service Request (SR) on behalf of a customer, and how to manage SR Approvals whilst IT Self Service is unavailable to many colleagues.

 

*	Log Service Requests on behalf of customers in the Service Request workspace in OSM, in your usual Service Desk Analyst or Enhanced Access role, not through IT Self Service.
*	Follow the guidance relating to the Service Request Customer and Reported by fields.
*	Check whether approval is required and review the guidance for how to manage this by email in the interim.
*	If appropriate, change the owner and set the status to Submitted after logging.
*	Review steps for managing older requests with stuck approvals
*	In the event of an issue, assign a Task to the OSM Administration team from the SR

 

Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

 

Thanks / Regards

Louise

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Louise Piper
ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist
IT Services | University of Oxford
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

T: 01865 616283
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk

emails.subject: HR Systems support activities in May
emails.to: Brian Jenkins; Sharon Naylor; Chris Williams; Andy Bryson; Paul Kayente
emails.body:
Hi All,

 

See below summary of the activities that have taken place in May in support of your systems. Included is also the latest update in relation to the loss of access to the self-service module of OSM and what that means to us.

 

As always, please feel free to share with your teams.

 

Highlights

 

*	No user access to self-service module of OSM – following the discovery of a potential security vulnerability, IT removed access to the self-service module of OSM for all users. This means that system users cannot submit or check the status of self-service requests they have submitted. Specifically for us, this means that requests to grant access to IRIS, Cority and Healthy Working Plus cannot be submitted in the normal way using the relevant self-service request. Instead, these requests must be submitted by email to our support mailbox hssystemssupport@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:hssystemssupport@safety.ox.ac.uk> . Other IRIS self-service requests are also affected. Odyssey does not currently utilise self-service requests, so there are no changes to the support provided.

At present, there is no defined resolution timeline for this issue. Workarounds are in place to ensure all support requests are recorded on OSM to ensure no request is missed – thanks to Rob his help supporting this.

 

*	Cority email issue – The Occupational Health identified that emails were not being received by clients from the Cority system in early May. This was caused by an update to the email security protocols by the University’s Nexus team. This meant that approximately 1,000 emails that were sent by the Cority system to ox.ac.uk email addresses were being held in a quarantine area as they did not satisfy the new security rules. This has now been resolved with Cority and all emails from the system now meet the updated University security protocols.

 

Support Tickets

 

A total of 49 support tickets were raised in May with 41 of those being now resolved:

System

Total Raised

Resolved

Open

Waiting for Customer

Waiting for Supplier

Cority

14

14

0

0

0

Healthy Working Plus

6

2

2

0

2

IRIS

23

22

1

0

0

Odyssey

6

3

1

2

0

Total

49

41

4

2

2

 

The average resolution time for these incidents in May is 4 days.

 

Enhancement Work

 

The following enhancement items have been delivered in May:

*	Cority – G-Drive location provided for storing of database for use with spirometers.
*	Cority – Bulk merge tool now available in the Live environment – this will allow us to merge applicant data with data from the People feed. Work to merge records has started.

 

 

Future Enhancement Work

 

All enhancement items, both those in progress and planned for the future, can be found on the Health & Safety Roadmap – Available here <https://app.powerbi.com/groups/me/apps/c00c2bb4-2038-4318-bc17-5e700b6a3927/reports/314ba831-839f-4f53-846d-36acabb3f703/69e9b07a2ba77205059d?ctid=cc95de1b-97f5-4f93-b4ba-fe68b852cf91&experience=power-bi> 

 

 

One new enhancement request has been submitted for IRIS looking at the information recorded on the system for potential exposures to ATMPs and Challenge Agents following sharps injuries.

 

There are also two requests for funding for enhancement work with OH&S to approve – these are:

 

1.	Cority:

*	Fix for Primary Job Position flag business rules – Issue preventing client details appearing in banner and inaccuracies in organisation structure data in reporting
*	Health surveillance compliance reporting – Data field missing in build from Cority preventing a consistent link between the clearance certificate and the specific surveillance group membership
*	SMS appointment reminders – New supplier functionality to send automatic SMS reminders to clients 24 hours before their appointment
*	Outlook and MS Teams integration with Cority – New functionalit

I’ve reviewed this agenda and I think it will cover what we need in this first session. I will forward on the reference data once it’s been signed off.

 

In terms of these decisions I think we’d want to create a new Payroll Company as we would want this one to behave very differently to anything we have set up currently and we would want to avoid any overlooked reference data which related to an existing company in case we were audited on this.

 

However, I think we’d be safe to copy pay group 99 into a new pay group.

Subject: GLAM- Botanic Gardens Ticket Office and Ashmolean Security- Rostering requirement 

 

Hello, all,

 

On Tuesday, GLAM HR asked if we could meet with a manager in Botanic Gardens who was struggling with using the system. Today myself and Tracey met with Heidi, and also Harriet Webb from Botanic Gardens Ticket Office. 

 

During the call, it was established that the Botanic Gardens had not been very forthcoming with staffing data required to set their staff up correctly to use leave and absence in PeopleXD. We were able to identify that the Ticket Office team, have similar requirements and ways of working to the Ashmolean Security team, and would therefore benefit from Rostering implementation.

 

Unfortunately, we are no longer in a position to support this, and therefore, any future roll outs of Rostering, will need to be picked up by yourselves.

Anna
