emails.body:
Hi Conor,

 

I’ve reviewed this agenda and I think it will cover what we need in this first session. I will forward on the reference data once it’s been signed off.

 

In terms of these decisions I think we’d want to create a new Payroll Company as we would want this one to behave very differently to anything we have set up currently and we would want to avoid any overlooked reference data which related to an existing company in case we were audited on this.

 

However, I think we’d be safe to copy pay group 99 into a new pay group.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com> 
Sent: 21 May 2026 16:08
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup

 

Good Afternoon Simon,

 

I hope you are well. Apologies, I was on site training.

 

Can I ask if the below agenda suits what you wish to achieve on the 12th or if there is anything else which you would like to cover:

 

Training Agenda: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up

1. Overview & Objectives

*	Purpose of the session
*	Key dependencies between hierarchy and payroll configuration
*	Pre-requisites (completed in advance): 

	*	Hierarchy placement agreed
	*	Reference data created (e.g. Management Units, Departments)

2. Payroll Company Set-Up

*	Creating the company in Payroll (copy vs new)
*	Key company parameters and required fields
*	Overview of payroll configuration

3. Pay Group Configuration

*	Creating a dummy/non-payroll pay group
*	Linking pay group to the new company
*	Importance for employee onboarding

4. Building the Hierarchy

*	Adding the company to the hierarchy structure
*	Linking levels and validating structure
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy integrity

5. Summary & Next Steps

*	Recap of configuration order: 

	1.	Payroll company setup
	2.	Pay group creation
	3.	Build organisational hierarchy

*	Q&A

 

Just also with the above, some choices made by yourselves will dictate what we would need to cover, so for example, we need to decide with the Company, do we want to copy a preexisting Company or create from scratch. Similarly, we also need to decide the same for Pay Group. 

 

For the Pay Groups, on UOXU for example, there is Pay Groups 99(Non Employees) and 2221(Test PG), if these were suitable, we can use one of these as the template to create our new non payroll pay group so that we can link this to the new Organisation. If not, we can of course create from scratch.

 


1. Payroll Company – Create New vs Copy


Copy Existing Company (Recommended)


When to use:

*	New company is similar to an existing one
*	Same country, payroll rules, or configuration structure

Benefits:

*	Faster setup – most fields pre-populated
*	Reduces risk of missing key payroll parameters
*	Ensures consistency across companies

What to review after copying:

*	Company Code and Name
*	Contact and registration details
*	Currency, payroll provider, and bank details
*	Payroll parameters (e.g. pay codes, cost centres) 

Key consideration:
Copying is best practice as it “cuts down processing time” and only requires minor updates rather than full configuration 

________________________________


Create New Company (From Scratch)


When to use:

*	Company has different payroll requirements
*	Different country, legislation, or setup
*	No suitable template exists

Considerations:

*	All fields must be manually configured
*	High

Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Clive Poole <clive.poole@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Confirming ERs pension rates for annual Financial Statements -- FINAL

 

Athena

 

I am not sure if you are the right person to say this to, but you should be aware that OSPS contribution rates will be changing from 1 October 2026, so the payroll system will need to be updated. The changes are as follows:

 

*	DB employer contributions going down to 11.8% for all cost plans (schemes 12-17)
*	DC employer contributions going up by 1.5% for all tiers (43 to 48)

 

I know we do payroll for Linacre so it will need to change them and any subsidiary companies for whom we have equivalent scheme codes (if that is what happens).

 

From 1 April 2027 DB employee contributions will go down by 0.5% for all cost plans.

 

Many thanks

 

David

emails.subject: For Information: Logging requests on behalf of a customer and managing approvals
emails.body:
From: Louise Piper, ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist

To: All OSM users

 

Please disregard this email if you do not need to log or manage Service Requests on behalf of customers.

Note that any previous recommendations have been updated as a result of further testing and feedback.

 

For Information: 

•              Internal users of OSM can log requests on behalf of customers in ‘Service Request’

•              Suggested workaround for management of approvals for Service Requests

 

Dear All,

 

Please find attached guidance on how to log a Service Request (SR) on behalf of a customer, and how to manage SR Approvals whilst IT Self Service is unavailable to many colleagues.

 

*	Log Service Requests on behalf of customers in the Service Request workspace in OSM, in your usual Service Desk Analyst or Enhanced Access role, not through IT Self Service.
*	Follow the guidance relating to the Service Request Customer and Reported by fields.
*	Check whether approval is required and review the guidance for how to manage this by email in the interim.
*	If appropriate, change the owner and set the status to Submitted after logging.
*	Review steps for managing older requests with stuck approvals
*	In the event of an issue, assign a Task to the OSM Administration team from the SR

Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

 

Thanks / Regards

Louise

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Louise Piper
ITSM Self-Service and Reporting Specialist
IT Services | University of Oxford
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

T: 01865 616283
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

 

 

 

From: Sarah Warne <sarah.warne@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 22 May 2026 14:33
To: it-services-osm-users-all@maillist.ox.ac.uk
Subject: For Information: IT Self-Service access to Oxford Service Manager (OSM) 

 

From: Sarah Warne, ITSM Systems Specialist 

To: All OSM users 

  

For Information:  

*	Note that IT self-service is currently unavailable for some users

*	Note measures being put in place to support continuity of access to IT help and support, and alternative ways to access IT help and support at Oxford 

 

Dear all, 

 

You may be aware that access to IT Self-Service via Oxford Service Manager (OSM) is currently unavailable (including access to Service Requests, My Items or Request for Help functionality). This follows notification by Ivanti, the third-party supplier of OSM software, of a security vulnerability. 

 

Please note, that as an OSM user (i.e, access to the ‘Service Desk Analyst’ or ‘Enhanced Access’ role when you log in) your access — including access to IT Self-Service — remains unchanged. 

Incoming and outgoing email services are unaffected. Some system-generated emails may still contain links to IT Self-Service. These are currently being reviewed and updated where necessary. 

OSM approvals relating to Service Requests, Changes, and Knowledge Articles are also under review to determine the most appropriate way for these processes to be managed. The appropriate approach may vary depending on the requirements of the individual support team involved. 

We are putting additional arrangements in place to ensure that support centres and service desks can raise Service Requests on behalf of customers. 

 

Many universities and organisations using the Ivanti Service Management platform around the world are impacted. There is currently no evidence of unauthorised access to Ivanti’s platform. 

We are sorry for any inconvenience this causes. We are continuing to work closely with our supplier, Ivanti, to resolve the issue. However, at this stage, we do not yet have a confirmed timescale for the reinstatement of IT Self-Service. Updates will be shared on the University’s Service Status site <https://status.ox.ac.uk/#/> .  

 

Please feel free to reach out if you have any further quest

emails.subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Good Afternoon Simon,

 

I hope you are well. Apologies, I was on site training.

 

Can I ask if the below agenda suits what you wish to achieve on the 12th or if there is anything else which you would like to cover:

 

Training Agenda: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up

1. Overview & Objectives

*	Purpose of the session
*	Key dependencies between hierarchy and payroll configuration
*	Pre-requisites (completed in advance): 

	*	Hierarchy placement agreed
	*	Reference data created (e.g. Management Units, Departments)

2. Payroll Company Set-Up

*	Creating the company in Payroll (copy vs new)
*	Key company parameters and required fields
*	Overview of payroll configuration

3. Pay Group Configuration

*	Creating a dummy/non-payroll pay group
*	Linking pay group to the new company
*	Importance for employee onboarding

4. Building the Hierarchy

*	Adding the company to the hierarchy structure
*	Linking levels and validating structure
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy integrity

5. Summary & Next Steps

*	Recap of configuration order: 

	1.	Payroll company setup
	2.	Pay group creation
	3.	Build organisational hierarchy

*	Q&A

 

Just also with the above, some choices made by yourselves will dictate what we would need to cover, so for example, we need to decide with the Company, do we want to copy a preexisting Company or create from scratch. Similarly, we also need to decide the same for Pay Group. 

 

For the Pay Groups, on UOXU for example, there is Pay Groups 99(Non Employees) and 2221(Test PG), if these were suitable, we can use one of these as the template to create our new non payroll pay group so that we can link this to the new Organisation. If not, we can of course create from scratch.

 


1. Payroll Company – Create New vs Copy


Copy Existing Company (Recommended)


When to use:

*	New company is similar to an existing one
*	Same country, payroll rules, or configuration structure

Benefits:

*	Faster setup – most fields pre-populated
*	Reduces risk of missing key payroll parameters
*	Ensures consistency across companies

What to review after copying:

*	Company Code and Name
*	Contact and registration details
*	Currency, payroll provider, and bank details
*	Payroll parameters (e.g. pay codes, cost centres) 

Key consideration:
Copying is best practice as it “cuts down processing time” and only requires minor updates rather than full configuration 

________________________________


Create New Company (From Scratch)


When to use:

*	Company has different payroll requirements
*	Different country, legislation, or setup
*	No suitable template exists

Considerations:

*	All fields must be manually configured
*	Higher risk of missing mandatory payroll settings
*	More time-consuming

Key takeaway:
Use only when structure or payroll rules differ significantly from existing companies.

With the Pay Group it is as below:

 


2. Pay Group – Create New vs Copy


Copy / Duplicate Pay Group (Recommended)


When to use:

*	New pay group follows same structure (even if non-payroll)
*	Same pay frequency or similar setup

Benefits:

*	Saves time vs building from scratch
*	Retains correct configuration (periods, indicators, thresholds)
*	Reduces configuration errors

What to update:

*	Pay Group Code and Description
*	Pay frequency (if different)
*	Key indicators (e.g. timesheet, salary settings) 

Key consideration:
Duplication is specifically designed to “save time when configuring multiple new pay groups” 

________________________________


Create New Pay Group (From Scratch)


When to use:

*	Unique pay setup required (e.g. different frequency or behaviour)
*	No existing template matches requirements

Considerations:

*	Must define all mandatory fields (periods, frequency, indicators)
*	Requires correct linkage to company
*	More manual effort and val

emails.subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

I reran the Insight reports for all LEVELS and SOC values in UOXP (after refreshing the data to ensure the latest version was used).

After noticing that there were fewer SOC records than LEVELS records, I compared the two populations in Excel to identify records present in LEVELS but missing from SOC. I was then able to locate the corresponding SOC values in an older SOC extract. From the investigation, it appears the issue is likely related to the Comments field not having been updated to reflect inclusion in the 2024/25 return. This resulted in the records not appearing as expected, since they only had 2023/24 comments.

I also realised that I previously provided the wrong Appointment IDs — apologies for the confusion.

The records currently missing SOC values for 2024/25 are:

Person Reference           Appointment ID               HESA Code         HESA Code Description

2361315                           266215-1                          211                      Natural and Social Science Professionals

2201600                           217565-1                          231                      Teaching Professionals

For both records, the SOC entries do exist historically and can be evidenced in prior extracts. In each case, there is:

*	a record marked as “Returned 23-24”
*	a corresponding record included in the 2024/25 return extract downloaded from HESA

This suggests the records themselves were not missing, but that the Comments field may not have been overwritten to reflect 2024/25 inclusion during processing. Based on this, would AG be able to reinsert the missing records and include the 2024/25 comments values so that we have a complete record for 2024/25?

I am also adding an additional validation step to our process notes for future returns so we can identify this type of issue before the module rollover takes place and ensure we’ve corrected it ourselves.

Please let me know if any further detail or extracts would be helpful.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

People Analytics Team Lead for BAU, HR Systems

People Department | University of Oxford

www.oxford.ox.ac.uk <http://www.oxford.ox.ac.uk/> 

 





 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Best wishes,

Micha

emails.subject: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD - Team Meeting Notes - 19th May 2026
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Kevin Lelitte; Nathan Kirwan; Nicholas Chandler; Simon Burford; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Dear All

 

Some notes from our team meeting on 19th May.

 

Impact Analysis

*	It was agreed that the impact analysis done by Mel Furniss is sufficient for the time-being. Simon highlighted the importance of the downstream impacts – into the DWH and other integrations. Action: CM to confirm back to Mel.
*	An HR Systems ‘internal’ meeting has been setup to discuss DWH and interfaces (22nd June). Other members of the project are not invited but can be available if required.
*	With regards reports, there are three approaches: direct queries through self-service, HR Reports and Insight. The expectation is that Nathan would be in the best position to confirm if ‘90’ and ‘10’ data are being mixed. 

 

Organisation Structure 

*	Anne shared an updated Org structure on 15th May and this was considered by the team as ‘agreed’.

 

Reference Data

*	Almost complete – needs a final review. Role types and status and sub-status were discussed. Simon confirmed that he is happy with the assumptions made.

Engagement with Access Group

*	The question was raised about next steps and getting our next engagements with Access Group in the diaries. Simon explained that we need the proposal signed off first which has the dependency on the org chart and reference data.
*	Action: SB will talk to Conor regarding the next meeting. It was noted that Conor was away 1st – 10th June so the next meeting will be shortly after then. 
*	We also need a meeting with Conor to get advice on the menu profiles – which also needs steer from the project team. 

 

GDPR

*	Anne shared the College Data Sharing Agreement with the project team. Simon raised the point that we should wait until the agreement is signed by the colleges before we load their data into live. Working in the test areas however should be OK. Action: AM to confirm when signed,
*	DPIA – with Anne and in progress.

 

 

 

Open actions from earlier meetings

 

Company Code and Naming Convention

*	It is understood that the new company would be ‘90’ but SB/KL will share a more formal proposal on this. Action: SB to share proposal with rest of team before getting Marie to sign-off.
*	Action: SB – on sign-off, SB to add to initiate a Change Request. Note that the CR will include multiple changes relating to this initiative. The CR needs to be signed-off before we go live.

 

User access – visibility of Portal structure and reference data 

*	A question was raised about who would be the guardian for the data and for access. It was assumed that this would rest with AM.
*	Action: AM to confirm how to formalize the role of data guardian being mindful of any college approval needed.

 

 

 

Present: AM,CM,NC,NK,SB, SM

Apologies: KL

 

 

 

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Crispin Muncaster
PROJECT MANAGER
IT Services | University of Oxford

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11676510: PERDEP02 List of FTCs Due To End report - parameters for target end date issue
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

 

I was pretty close with August then! I couldn’t look any further back as I got a new laptop around the same time so all my downloads were gone.

 

I did 2 tests for Biology and 4 for Undergraduate Admissions and it showed up to the end of the report date as expected.

 

I did a quick couple of runs on division as well as and it only pulled through rows for that division, although I didn’t check to see if anything was missing. One was an MPLS one and it looked like it was only the MPLS departments which is good.

 

Let me know if anything else would be valuable to look at and I can run a few reports – it doesn’t take me long.

 

Best wishes,

Beth

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 19 May 2026 13:36
To: Beth Gray <beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Support Call# 11676510: PERDEP02 List of FTCs Due To End report - parameters for target end date issue

 

Hi Beth,

 

Thanks for flagging the issue with the FTC report. I’ve had to go back through several earlier versions — and a few changes made by departed colleagues — to get to the bottom of this one.

 

It looks like a version was deployed in July 2025 (change # 20019074). That update added the “End of FTC Reason” fields as intended, but in the process it appears to have removed the end‑date filtering and the MAX_TARGET_END logic. That explains why the report no longer responds to the end‑date parameter.

 

I also noticed in QA that Lee had been working on the report (open change # 20017394) , but hadn’t completed his changes. Appears he was part‑way through adding a Division parameter, but his version wasn’t passing tests.

 

To resolve this, I reverted back to an earlier version Simon created in 2021, before the above two changes. I copied that version, reapplied the updates to include the “End of FTC Reason” fields, and tested to confirm everything worked correctly. I then added the Division parameter that Lee had started, and tested that too.

 

As you mentioned you’d also spotted this issue from your Biology experience, I wondered whether you might have time and be willing to test my latest version. It would be really helpful to have another set of eyes on it.

 

If so, here’s a direct link to report in the QA Reporting Server:

 

PERDEP02 List of FTCs Due To End_v5 - Power BI Report Server <https://hr-reporting-qa.admin.ox.ac.uk/Reports/report/_Deployment/PERDEP02%20List%20of%20FTCs%20Due%20To%20End_v5> 

 

If I know this is working as expected, I then still need to review the other reports in the PERDEP02 suit, to see if they should also have a Division parameter for consistency, as well as checking and testing to ensure they weren’t also impacted by previous changes.  

 

Best wishes,

Michael

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com> 
Sent: 15 May 2026 09:44
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>; Ann-Marie Topliss <AnnMarie.Topliss@theaccessgroup.com>
Subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup

 

Good Morning Simon,

 

I hope you are well.

 

Thanks for the update, and no problem at all—happy to work with you on this while Kevin is out.

 

In terms of next steps, you’re absolutely on the right track.

 

What we need from you

Before we begin configuration, the key dependencies are:

*	Finalised hierarchy structure
*	Confirmed organisation structure / company
*	Details for: 

	*	Payroll Company
	*	Non-payroll Pay Group
	*	Required reference data updates

Once those are agreed, you can share them with me and we can move into the build phase.

________________________________

What I will cover

Based on the plan we agreed, my role is to support and guide you through both configuration and enablement:

*	Review of your data/templates (UAT & Live) to make sure everything is ready before loading
*	Working sessions to walk through: 

	*	Loading templates
	*	Building and validating the hierarchy

*	Support configuring key items, including: 

	*	Non-pay pay group (if required, this was to be checked to see if the existing non Payroll pay group could be used)
	*	Menu profile for non-pay employees

*	UAT support, including: 

	*	Test script provision
	*	Review sessions
	*	Ongoing check-ins

*	Post go-live support

________________________________

On configuration in UAT

Rather than a full “black box” build by me, the approach is collaborative:

*	We will configure together in UAT during working sessions, so your team is enabled to maintain this going forward
*	I’ll guide best practice and support where needed, but you’ll have visibility and ownership of the setup

________________________________

Menu profile

For the menu profile:

*	I will need to meet with you to define what exactly is required for the new staff
*	We can review and refine it together
*	This will be built in UAT for validation and testing before being moved to Live

________________________________

Suggested next step

Once you have the hierarchy and reference data confirmed, I suggest we schedule:

1.	A finalisation session (to review and agree structure and data)
2.	A configuration session (to start building in UAT)

________________________________

 

Hopefully that helps clarify things, but happy to walk through it all together if useful.

 

Can I please just add also that I am on Annual Leave between the 01st & 10th of June, but if there is a requirement to have some work complete during this period, I can request a colleague does so here.

 

Thank you,

 

Conor O'Brien

Senior Consultant

The Access Group Unit 2302, 3rd Floor Building 2000, Citygate, 

Mahon, Cork, T12 K06Y

Subject: DSE Update - user data import and upload to Cardinus

 

Hi Marie, 

 

Just a quick update with DSE.

 

I completed testing for the automated user data import into Cardinus on Friday, and carried out additional log checks and further test scenarios this morning. I’ve shared the testing confirmation and the document outlining the test criteria with Tony Boydell, confirming that everything is working as expected.

emails.subject: Java JRE 8 update 491 - testing request [Document Attached]
emails.to: Belinda Birch; Bridget Midwinter; Caroline Afford; Cathy Gannon; Fiona Belardo; Helen Hall; James Boon; Kevin Lelitte; Michael O'Sullivan; Olga Balcerzyk; Simon Burford
emails.body:
[Apologies – test guidance notes now attached]

 

Good afternoon,

 

Please find information regarding your testing for Java updates.

 

Oracle have released Java JRE 8 update 491 as part of their Critical Patch Updates for April 2026: https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/cpuapr2026.html 

 

It is important we test against our business systems before they are rolled out to all managed workstations in your units.  The Endpoint Device Management Services team (EDMS) are currently preparing a packaged install for Windows 11 computers.

 

As per the previous test cycle, JRE 8 update (481), for those of you who are not currently using a CONNECT computer, a CONNECT Windows Virtual Machine will be made available for testing via the Remote Desktop Connection.  EDMS have prepared guidance notes for testers to follow the remote desktop process (attached).

 

Notable Dates:

*	Thursday 21st May - Java Runtime Environment 8 Update 481 will be deployed to the test group.
*	4pm on Wednesday 27th May - please report back any issues by this time. 

 

The proposed rollout to all managed CONNECT computers is:

*	28/05/2026 10:00 - Initiate pilot to a subset of CONNECT Windows 11 computers with an earlier version of JRE 8 installed.
*	04/06/2026 10:00 - If the pilot is successful, rollout to all CONNECT Windows 11 computers with an earlier version of JRE 8 installed.
*	11/06/2026 10:00 - The progress of the rollout will be reviewed, and if all is well, this ticket will be closed.

 

Please let me know if you are not able to take part in this testing cycle.  Also, if there are members of your team(s) who you feel should be involved in testing Java JRE updates then please get in touch.  Please also cc in Philip White to any responses to this email.

 

Thank you in advance for feeding back on this round of testing. 

 

Kind regards,

Simon

 

Simon Hamerton

Senior Services Relationship Manager
IT Services | University of Oxford
Dartington House, Oxford. OX1 2HS


www.it.ox.ac.uk <http://www.it.ox.ac.uk>

emails.body:
Hi all,

 

Looking forward to speaking to you all at the online workshop on Wednesday.

Just a reminder that if you have some time, it would be great if you could start adding your thoughts to the “What’s working well?” and “What’s not working well” sections. I’ve already included a few initial ideas from the lessons learned session to get things started. No worries if not, there will still be an opportunity to add more details on Wednesday.

Thanks,

Lyndsey

 

 

-----Original Appointment-----
From: Lyndsey Middleton 
Sent: 29 April 2026 12:39
To: Marie Cooksey; Maria Morris; Rachel Midgley; Emma Fitz-Gibbon; Kevin Lelitte; Asta Palmer; Simon Burford; Tracy Barlow-Bacon; Anna Carter-Windle; Tracey Holland; Melanie Furniss; Julie Hickman; Michael O'Sullivan
Subject: Workshop: Sustaining Adoption of Leave & Absence
When: 20 May 2026 14:00-15:30 (UTC+00:00) Dublin, Edinburgh, Lisbon, London.
Where: Microsoft Teams Meeting

 

Hi all,

I hope you can all make this rearranged workshop to look at Sustaining Adoption of Leave & Absence.

Please find below the agenda.

We’ll be using the following Miro board during the session:
https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGvJIqfs=/

If you have some time ahead of the workshop, it would be great if you could start adding your thoughts to the “What’s working well?” and “What’s not working well” sections. I’ve already included a few initial ideas from the lessons learned session to get things started.

When adding your ideas, please keep the focus on sustaining the adoption of leave and absence, rather than reflecting on what has or hasn’t worked well within the project itself.

We’ll also spend time building on this together during the workshop.

Thanks everyone, and see you on 20th May!

emails.subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference
emails.to: Marie Cooksey; Simon Burford; Sarah Rowles
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

 

I think referencing FinStats/HESA improvements within the workshop to bring the examples to life is a valid approach, but I was intending for the focus to be on the high level story that we’re trying to tell which departments will be inspired by and take away from the session – i.e. we had inefficient, labour-intensive processes -->  we quantified the amount of time spent on them with Clockify -->  we made targeted improvements to the processes using Alteryx -->  we quantified the improvements using Clockify. We don’t need to go into any detail about the processes themselves (lest our audience fall asleep), but these two things are useful case-studies in how Clockify and Alteryx can be used for continuous improvement. Is that the type of session you are wanting to run? If so we can have a think about who would be best placed to feed in – when would you need to know actual names by?

 

Best,

Athena 

 

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 18 May 2026 11:47
To: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference

 

Hi All

 

Thanks for feedback. I have to disagree, I don’t think FinStats and HESA are appropriate for this workshop, we can reference them, but this is about how can departments utilise these tools. 

 

Regarding the webpage, I’m already having discussions with Finance who have a great example of this plus case studies, which is what I want to add to our People Hub.

 

Can you check diaries to ensure they are not on holiday and then give me 1 name for Alteryx demo please? And Clockify demo please? (this can be 2 people or 1 person for both).

 

Thanks

 

Marie

 

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 18 May 2026 10:31
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference

 

Hi Simon,

 

I agree that the FinStats are a showcase example of Continuous Improvement In Action – so are the HESA process improvements Sarah has implemented, which have hugely reduced the man-hours spent on HESA and mitigated the risk of the staff return by spreading responsibility across the team and ensuring even greater accuracy of data. Either of these could be talked through with a simple “this is how it used to take and this is how long it takes now” as the cherry on top? 

 

Also happy to quickly run through how I’ve used Power Automate to refine and formalise the ad-hoc data request process, although Pete Stanton might have better ideas! 

 

I like the idea of a web-page with more details on what we’ve done – it’d probably be a good exercise to write punchy summaries of our CI work in preparation for the conference anyway, so doing it for a dedicated web-page is a good thought. 

 

Best,

Athena 

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 18 May 2026 10:13
To: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference

 

Hi all,

 

I agree with Athena. And I also wonder whether the financial statements would be a really good ex

Apologies, I got sucked into sickness absence reporting and didn’t realise the time.

 

Agree with what Simon and Athena have said. The ms forms based ad hoc log would be a good general example of automation. Really any of our workflows would be good exemplars of how Alteryx can speed things along. Aside from the FinStats DQ, there are the MGPGR workflows, and the module configuration workflows are nice examples of an audit-ready process (although HESA!).

 

None of the team (apart from me in the afternoon) have booked leave the day of the People Conference.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

Either of these could be talked through with a simple “this is how it used to take and this is how long it takes now” as the cherry on top? 

 

Also happy to quickly run through how I’ve used Power Automate to refine and formalise the ad-hoc data request process, although Pete Stanton might have better ideas! 

 

I like the idea of a web-page with more details on what we’ve done – it’d probably be a good exercise to write punchy summaries of our CI work in preparation for the conference anyway, so doing it for a dedicated web-page is a good thought. 

 

Best,

Athena

Subject: RE: Response required by 11am: People Conference

 

Hi all,

 

I agree with Athena. And I also wonder whether the financial statements would be a really good example to demonstrate? Without getting into any technical details it’s very easy to see the benefits, not just to our team, but to the process as a whole, by being able to produce more advanced outputs which satisfy auditors and keeping the process visibly simple to follow (you could flash up what the R code looked like and then how pretty and simple the alteryx workflow is)?

 

I like the idea of bringing in another team to demonstrate PowerBI, especially if you could bring in someone like Annette Sandrawich who is doing good things for her division using People Data. We could also feed into our longer term vision of being able to release PowerBI datasets for local PowerBI report writers to use, once we get to that point of maturity.

 

It would be really great if we could get someone to demonstrate how we or someone else uses power automate to improve productivity. Again, Athena maybe your use of PA and Teams Forms to collect consistent data? Or maybe what Pete’s team is doing using their booking system and sending out surveys. Although they might have their own slot? 

 

We’d obviously need to be careful how we frame clockify but I think that could be one of the most beneficial tools for everyone to use as it’s free and easy to use, and really good at helping you identify where to concentrate efforts for continuous improvement when you don’t know where to start. Best example of this is the HESA improvements. 

 

Maybe we could just do a teaser of what we’ve done but then pull together a web page or something that people could go to read more information or maybe use cases so people who are inspired could take things forward after the session?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 18 May 2026 09:21
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Response required by 11am: People Conference

 

Hi All

 

I have been asked to pull together a session at the People Conference. It is still very much a work in progress, but I need to advise today on the initial session description.

 

My brief was:   The conference theme is: Shifting the Dial: Strategy to Impact, with a focus on reflecting on the progress since the launch of the People Strategy.  I was asked to consider how we can build capability across the People profession to deliver impact in a changing landscape such as insights driven decision making. The initial thought is that the session could focus on showcasing how tools like Power Automate and PowerBI can deliver insights to improve decision making, processes, and efficiencies.

 

I don’t want it to be a technical session, I would like it to have more of a workshop, demonstration approach. Primarily demonstrating some of the activity, tools used and achievements within the People Department to give the audience ideas, inspiration, confidence that they can do it to. I also don’t want it to just be me talking at people for 40 minutes.

 

So I am enquiring whether you agree with my suggestions below and names you would propose for demo. Please note this will not just be members of HR Systems I am keen to bring others in from other teams.  

 

I think HR Systems could give a demo of:

*	Clockify – key message that it allows us to identify time-consuming task to drive our focus of attention and demonstrate benefit 
*	Alteryx – workflows, recognise this may not be for all, but some depts have more money and analysts so they may be interested
*	PowerBI – simple dashboards. Note that I am going to try and find other teams to do this as I think ours would scare most people off due to complexity. But we may need to be back-up!

 

We can obviously discuss in more detail, content, timings etc but initially I would want you to follow a format of below for 5-10 mins (timings to be confirmed) :

1.	Explaining the problem
2.	Demonstrating your solution
3.	Tying this back to the benefit for the business (team, department, PLT etc as relevant)
4.	Lessons learned (what would you do differently, who did you contact for advise etc)

 

If you could provide feedback and a name to do the demo for Clockify and Alteryx please and let me know by 11am today, so that I can ensure your name is included on the session information form, we can work out detail later.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

 

Marie Cooksey

Head of HR Systems

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx>  | Contact the team <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/contact-details.aspx> 

 

 



I may send emails outside of your usual working hours. Please be assured there is no expectation for you to respond to this outside of your own working hours.

emails.body:
Hi Simon

 

Thank you for explaining, and yes that seems a logical approach, especially as you can change if administrative responsibilities change.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 15 May 2026 17:53
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Marie,

 

This field indicates who holds responsibility for maintaining the records, which as it stands is the research services teams for REF2029. I wanted to have an indication that it is not the colleges themselves who hold responsibility for the administration of records within the system.

 

It’s only reference data so in 2029 or as and when the administrative responsibility for these records change we can reflect that in the descriptions of the codes. It’s the codes that are actually the most important aspect of it. We wouldn’t report on level 7 / pay admin by either unless we were targeting those responsible for the administration.

 

Hope that clarifies my thinking. I will be running all of the proposed config past you before doing anything with it once I’ve pulled it all together.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

I’m not pretending I understand UOX mappings, but can I ask why the Payroll L7 admin code description have REF2029? Does this not limit/date it for future use? Is there a reason behind this naming convention?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

emails.subject: FW: Thank you for joining Codex for non-Coders
emails.body:
Hi team,

 

Anyone who’s interested in using the Codex agent within ChatGPT may find the linked resources below quite useful! 

 

Best,

Athena

 

From: AI and ML Competency Centre <aimlcompetencycentre@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 01 May 2026 10:24
To: AI and ML Competency Centre <aimlcompetencycentre@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Thank you for joining Codex for non-Coders

 

Dear colleagues,

Thank you for joining us for Codex for non-Coders with OpenAI on 22 April at the Oxford e-Research Centre.

We hope the session offered a useful introduction to what AI agents are, how tools such as Codex can support work across the University, and how non-coders can begin exploring them in a practical and supportive way.

As a follow-up, we are pleased to share two Canvas resources that are now live:

Getting Started with Codex <https://canvas.ox.ac.uk/courses/289256/pages/getting-started-with-codex> 

OpenAI Codex for non-Coders <https://canvas.ox.ac.uk/courses/289256/pages/openai-codex-for-non-coders> 

Together, these pages provide both a practical starting point and a way to revisit the material from the session. They include guidance, examples and follow-up resources to help you continue exploring Codex in your own work. These support our new Codex webpage <https://oerc.ox.ac.uk/ai-centre/generative-ai-tools/codex>  which gives a full overview of using Codex at Oxford.

We would also be very grateful for your feedback on the session. Your comments will help us improve future events and shape further support for colleagues across the University.

Submit your feedback <https://www.coursecheck.com/event/UQYRXM> 

Thank you again for taking part, and for helping us build a thoughtful and practical conversation around AI across Oxford.

Best wishes,

Zoe Case

 



 

Zoe Case (she/her)
Community and Channels Officer

AI Competency Centre 

LinkedIn <https://www.linkedin.com/in/zoe-case-63712874/> 

 

 

All staff and students with an SSO now have access to Oxford's workspace in ChatGPT Edu - all resources including access to Oxford-specific training can be found on ox.ac.uk/gen-ai <https://www.ox.ac.uk/gen-ai>

emails.subject: DTP1092 College Staff in PXD - Team Meeting Notes - 12th May 2026
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Nicholas Chandler; Nathan Kirwan; Simon Burford; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Dear All

 

Some notes from our team meeting on 12th May: 

 

Sensitive Units

*	Decision: It was agreed that we do not need any sensitive units.

 

Organisation Structure 

*	AM/NK confirmed that they are now happy with the structure and Action: NK will pass the final version to SB.
*	Action: SB – to organize formal sign-off from Marie and then to raise a task with Conor to take forward.

 

Company Code and Naming Convention

*	It is understood that the new company would be ‘90’ but SB/KL will share a more formal proposal on this. Action: SB to share proposal with rest of team before getting Marie to sign-off.
*	Action: SB – on sign-off, SB to add to initiate a Change Request. Note that the CR will include multiple changes relating to this initiative. The CR needs to be signed-off before we go live.

 

User access – visibility of Portal structure and reference data 

*	A question was raised about who would be the guardian for the data and for access. It was assumed that this would rest with AM.
*	Action: AM to confirm how to formalize the role of data guardian being mindful of any college approval needed.

 

Impact Analysis 

*	The impact analysis report (done by Mel Furniss) was shared with the team. Simon has given feedback. It was agreed that we should amend, taking on board feedback, particularly on the risks having appropriate mitigations. Action: CM to ask if Mel can undertake further work.
*	The comment was also made that we need more analysis on the downstream impacts – into other systems and especially reports. An example scenario is that someone, such as NK, who is likely to have access to all areas may find that reports default to an expanded set of results.
*	It was recognized that we need some additional IT Services support to investigate the downstream impacts: Action: CM to take forward.

 

DPIA

*	It was accepted that a new DPIA will be needed to cover college data in PXD. Action: AM will make a start on the DPIA. Action: CM/AM to workout timing for first draft. 
*	Action: AM to help provide assurance that there are appropriate data sharing agreements in place with the colleges.

 

Reference Data query

*	A query was raised about Position Code/Point(?) which is a mandated field. This is thought to relate to salary grade/point which we do not need to know. It was suggested that we consider having a default entry for this.

 

 

 

Present: AM,CM,NC,NK,SB, SM

Apologies: KL

 

 

 

 

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Crispin Muncaster
PROJECT MANAGER
IT Services | University of Oxford

T: 01865 Â 616324
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/>

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 13 May 2026 09:40
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Update on Kevin

 

Hi Simon, 

 

Regarding OSM task 50947670 – it’s been a while, but from memory, I think this might be related to two things: the deletion of active reviews, and the bulk deletion of forms assigned to reviewees once the review has been activated.

 

Since the go-live, we’ve encountered cases where departments have mistakenly created and activated reviews, which has caused unnecessary clutter in the system. There have also been instances where incorrect individuals were assigned in large numbers because departments did not select a Department when creating criteria groups.

 

At the moment, it is:

• not possible to delete an active review

• not possible to reset a review to inactive once activated

• not possible to bulk delete assigned reviews (must be done individually)

 

Ilona sent me the attached document – I think Ilona/Kevin were having discussions with AG around these issues.

 

I hope this provides some background info.

 

 

Many thanks,

Asta

 

Asta Palmer

Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

 



 

Many thanks

Asta

Kevin’s planned surgery was more complex than first thought, he was told that he should recover within a week or two but he has been signed off as not fit for work for four weeks following surgery, so we won’t expect him to be back until 2nd June at least.

There is a change to the cority applicant data report which Kevin was expecting to pick up on his return, so this may also need to be something I pick up now, depending on what it entails. @James <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>  do you have any background you can share to get me up to speed on what needs to happen?

 

@James <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> , @Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  & @Asta <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – is there anything else that you were expecting Kevin to take forward? 

 

Meetings

emails.subject: Expressions of interest for HR Systems Support Analyst role
emails.body:
Good morning team,

 

Hopefully you saw my post on the People Teams channel yesterday, we now have permission to replace Otter on a permanent and full-time basis and we are seeking expressions of interest.

 

Please see details below and the attached JD.

 

Julie

 

Expressions of interest for HR Systems Support Analyst role

 

We are seeking expressions of interest for a HR Systems Support Analyst within the HR Systems team.

 

About the role

As an HR Systems Support Analyst, you will engage directly with our diverse user base in a key customer-facing role, addressing technical queries, troubleshooting issues, and providing effective solutions.

 

Key responsibilities

In this role, you will:

*	Provide first-line technical support to HR teams across the University.
*	Work in a fast-paced environment, often under tight deadlines, particularly around payroll deadlines.
*	Collaborate with colleagues to assess, prioritise, and resolve user queries.
*	Escalate complex queries in an efficient manner and to support knowledge enhancement.
*	Identify opportunities to enhance the user experience by reviewing training and guidance materials to encourage self-service, ideally utilising emerging technologies such as AI-powered tools.

For a full list of responsibilities and selection criteria, please reach out to Ffion Fry <mailto:ffion.fry@admin.ox.ac.uk>  to request a copy of the Job Description.

 

Who we’re looking for

We are looking for someone with proven experience in:

*	A customer-facing or technical support role within HR or IT.
*	Working in a fast-paced environment and managing multiple priorities effectively.
*	Excellent written and confident verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with a range of stakeholders.
*	Handling confidential information appropriately and professionally.

 

How to apply

If you are interested in this role and feel you meet the criteria, please send a brief expression of interest outlining how you meet the selection criteria, along with an up-to-date CV, to Ffion Fry <mailto:ffion.fry@admin.ox.ac.uk>  in the Talent Acquisition team by 12pm on 19 May 2026.

If you have any questions about the role or would like further information, please feel free to contact Emma Fitz-Gibbon <mailto:emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>  in HR Systems team.

I’ve now confirmed with AG that this type of scripting work can be covered by flex points. Please see the attached quote.

Based on the quote received, the effort would be 0.75 days (525 flex points), equivalent to £97

emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

 

Attached is the current draft of the PeopleXD DPIA that Kevin has been working on. There is a separate DPIA for the WFM module which is references in this main DPIA (see references to “B2 Data Protection Impact Assessment v1.3 WFM-ICT”) – I imagine that we would have similar references between a DPIA for the college staff and the main DPIA.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 12 May 2026 09:11
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Steve McBrearty <steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk>; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Crispin

 

I agree with Simon, in that I think we should try and keep the separation of this data, so a standalone DPIA that references the PXD DPIA, which I have yet to review and sign-off. @Simon Burford <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>  do we at least have a draft that we can share with Crispin, for him to reference?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

Sent: 11 May 2026 17:31
To: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Steve McBrearty <steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk> >; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Crispin,

 

There is no current DPIA that covers PeopleXD, we are working on one (and have been for about a year) but it’s not been signed off by Marie or information security yet.

 

However, considering this is work that is essentially temporary (data will presumably be kept and updated for a specific and time limited purpose) I would have expected to have a separate DPIA which may refer to the main one but very much focus on the processing of data for this purpose, as the flow of data, the access and maintenance arrangements etc are all different and purposefully separate from the way we process and administer the rest of the system.

 

I’m copying Marie in to this conversation to see if she agrees with me on this one?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

Sent: 30 April 2026 17:35
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Steve McBrearty <steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk> >; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Kevin

 

With regards the DPIA, then we have now taken compliance advice on this.

 

There is a DPA that covers the data processing element of the existing college census and we are looking to amend this to cover the transfer of data into PXD. The data being loaded into PXD is not sensitive data and therefore does not warrant a DPIA and so will be covered by the amended DPA.

 

Once the data is in PXD however, there will indeed be a need for DPIA coverage in PXD. We were wondering therefore if there is a PXD DPIA which by default covers the processing of the new records we are putting into PXD, or whether an existing PXD DPIA would need amending (most likely)? 

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

emails.subject: Update on Kevin
emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; James Salas Guillen; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

Just wanted to drop you a quick email to update you on Kevin now that I’ve caught up on emails etc. 

 

Kevin’s planned surgery was more complex than first thought, he was told that he should recover within a week or two but he has been signed off as not fit for work for four weeks following surgery, so we won’t expect him to be back until 2nd June at least.

 

Work initiatives

I will cover as much of his role as I can. We had been working towards this surgery and so cleared a lot of his workload before he went off but the items I’m aware of as being handed over to me are:

*	the project to bring College staff into PeopleXD
*	the Applicant Successfully Hired change
*	the DPIA for PeopleXD 
*	the ORCID onboarding change (if this comes back to us). 

 

There is a change to the cority applicant data report which Kevin was expecting to pick up on his return, so this may also need to be something I pick up now, depending on what it entails. @James <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>  do you have any background you can share to get me up to speed on what needs to happen?

 

@James <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> , @Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  & @Asta <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – is there anything else that you were expecting Kevin to take forward? 

 

Meetings

In terms of daily catch ups it would be good for me to attend these on Mondays and Thursdays, on Tuesday and Wednesday I need to lead the BI Developer huddle, so if we could all make sure we can attend at least those two meetings so I can touch base that would be really useful.

 

In terms of 121s, I’m very happy to meet with any of you on a 121 basis while Kevin is away, so please pop in a meeting in my diary if you see a space! If there’s ever anything you would normally expect to go to Kevin with please just come to me instead.

 

@Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  I understand that Kevin prepared KPIs and agreed with you to present them this week? Marie Athena and I will be at PMB at the same time as the meeting anyway so hopefully no pressure but I’m hoping you’ve got everything you need.

 

OSM items

I’ve taken a look at all of Kevin’s change requests and tasks. I’ve picked up what I need to take forward.

 

@Michael <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  you were going to contact the data compliance team and check regarding tasks 50944471 & 50944466. It looks like we’ve had around 11 subject access requests relating to this same person over the last 6 months (based on the emails I’ve seen) so it might be that Kevin has responded but not closed the tasks. But I’ll leave these two tasks for you to sort out if that’s ok.

 

@Asta Palmer <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>  there’s a task 50947670 relating to a talent management query which I think Kevin was due to pick up on his return, but we might need to take forward now. Are you able to take a look and see if you understand what’s being asked?

 

I’ve seen that there’s an enormous number of changes assigned to Lee still, which I’m hoping have all actually been implemented but just not updated, so we might have to go through these at some point, but unless there’s anything that anyone is aware is pressing I’m happy to de-prioritise these for now, but if someone could take a quick look through and see if there are any we need to be concerned about that would be really helpful.

 

Please shout if there’s anything else you need me to be thinking about!

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Many thanks,

Simon

Subject: Surgery update and medical leave

 

Hi Simon,

 

I wanted to give you an update on my surgery and recovery.

 

The procedure was more complicated than initially anticipated due to issues involving the tendons and scar tissue from the previous joint replacement in the same area, so the surgeon has signed me off for four weeks, I’ve attached the fit note for documentation.

 

I’m due to meet again with the surgeon on May 21 and he has agreed to reassess if I can return to work sooner.

 

I’ll keep you informed of any updates, please feel free to contact me if needed.

 

Best,

Kevin

emails.body:
Hi all,

 

Can I also just ask whether the end to end process including all of the considerations raised in this email thread are covered in a knowledge article and/or how to guide for this process? It’s unclear if the process isn’t documented or if it just wasn’t followed?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 07 May 2026 16:21
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: P5 Task assigned to your team - Task# 50951232 for Incident# 11677930

 

Thanks for flagging. I’ll make sure all payroll and central team access has been updated accordingly, also for those who have Uni-Wide recruitment access. 

 

I’ll also check in with Ant to see whether he’ll be processing any restructuring for IT Services, and then I’ll take it from there re: user access.

 

I’d also like to echo that it would probably help if we coordinated these changes a bit more closely upfront, just to make sure the user access and approval permissions are picked up as part of the same process.

emails.body:
Hi all

 

I have a simple question, I have a raft of items on the dashboard to deal with, do I instruct the team to process as per standard practise now as the emails will go/get received? I have previously advised them to hang fire until we knew what we were dealing with

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 08 May 2026 17:02
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk>; Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Delayed delivery of outgoing emails to ox.ac.uk addresses [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFY33x3:ref ] 00502238.

 

Hi Marie,

 

Based on the information we now have from Central IT and Cority, this does not appear to have been an ongoing issue. When Cority was initially implemented, it inherited the existing email configuration that had been migrated from Cohort, which was working at the time.

 

Since that implementation, Microsoft and the University have continued to tighten email security controls, particularly around authentication and anti‑fraud (phishing emails) measures. While our outbound emails continued to send successfully from the system, they were not fully authenticated, and once the security checks became stricter, those messages began to be quarantined.

 

In that sense, this wasn’t caused by a functional change within Cority itself, but by evolving Microsoft and University email security standards, combined with the legacy configuration that had previously been sufficient.

 

To prevent this happening again, both Central IT and Cority have confirmed that the correct long‑term solution is to update our configuration so that outgoing emails are properly authenticated (using DKIM). Once this is in place, the University’s email security checks should pass successfully and emails should no longer be quarantined.

 

We’re now progressing the DKIM configuration as a priority, and once implemented this should provide a stable, long‑term solution.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

Hi Rob and Sharon, 

 

We’ve just finished a call with Cority’s technical contact and a member of the University’s Outlook/Nexus team.

 

The issue has now been identified. Outgoing emails from the system were being quarantined by the University’s email security controls. The outgoing emails did not meet the University’s email policy requirements and were automatically held in quarantine rather than delivered to recipients. This explains why the system showed emails as “sent” while recipients did not received them.

 

A total of 1,106 emails sent from enquiries@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:enquiries@uohs.ox.ac.uk>  were placed into quarantine, dating from 23 April. All of these emails have now been released and the University will check quarantine again on Monday morning in case any additional emails are identified and need releasing. While this isn’t ideal, it does mean that affected clients should start receiving reminder emails over the weekend. Please note that this only affected ox.ac.uk email addresses.

 

There may be some user/client confusion due to the delated delivery of older emails and we may need to support in handling these queries. More than likely, the majority of these emails wi

In addition, I have just received a notification from Infosec as one of our automated emails has been reported “Outlook has alerted this as a suspicious sender despite the email address being one that looks legitimate.”

 

Is this a new thing? Outlook identifying our emails as looking suspicious?

 

Regards

 

Sharon

Sent: 08 May 2026 16:11

Hi James and Chris

 

Thank you for pursing this and finally giving us an answer. 

 

I would also like to understand if this has been an ongoing issue, if not why has it occurred now? Because if it is due to a recent change then we also need to ensure that Cority, IT and ourselves have a process to ensure testing takes place if security protocols are amended.

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 08 May 2026 16:02
To: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk> >; Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Delayed delivery of outgoing emails to ox.ac.uk addresses [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFY33x3:ref ] 00502238.

 

Hi Rob and Sharon, 

 

We’ve just finished a call with Cority’s technical contact and a member of the University’s Outlook/Nexus team.

 

The issue has now been identified. Outgoing emails from the system were being quarantined by the University’s email security controls. The outgoing emails did not meet the University’s email policy requirements and were automatically held in quarantine rather than delivered to recipients. This explains why the system showed emails as “sent” while recipients did not received them.

 

A total of 1,106 emails sent from enquiries@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:enquiries@uohs.ox.ac.uk>  were placed into quarantine, dating from 23 April. All of these emails have now been released and the University will check quarantine again on Monday morning in case any additional emails are identified and need releasing. While this isn’t ideal, it does mean that affected clients should start receiving reminder emails over the weekend. Please note that this only affected ox.ac.uk email addresses.

 

There may be some user/client confusion due to the delated delivery of older emails and we may need to support in handling these queries. More than likely, the majority of these emails will be appointment reminders.

 

Most importantly, to prevent this happening again, we need to update our email configuration so that outgoing emails are properly authenticated (using DKIM). This will ensure emails are accepted by the University’s email security protocols. Both Central IT and Cority have confirmed that this is the correct and recommended solution to ensure emails are not quarantined or blocked in the future. This requirement wasn’t identified during the original project but will now be implemented.

 

I’ll raise a Service Request with the University’s Networks team as soon as we receive the remaining technical details required from Cority to make these changes.

 

We’ll continue to keep you updated as this progresses.

 

Best wishes,

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: ht

emails.subject: Surgery update and medical leave
emails.body:
Hi Simon,

I wanted to give you an update on my surgery and recovery.

The procedure was more complicated than initially anticipated due to issues involving the tendons and scar tissue from the previous joint replacement in the same area, so the surgeon has signed me off for four weeks, I’ve attached the fit note for documentation.

I’m due to meet again with the surgeon on May 21 and he has agreed to reassess if I can return to work sooner.

I’ll keep you informed of any updates, please feel free to contact me if needed.

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Thank you for chasing Michael!

 

Henry is about to start working on the off-system part of the process so it would be good to have UOXP ready to load the values to but I absolutely understand the wisdom of a back-up before anything is deleted from the live system!

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 07 May 2026 12:27
To: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Sarah,

 

I’ve just come out of our support review meeting with AG. I raised the HESA UDF case, which is currently our only open case with them.

 

The delay in implementing the bulk deletion in Live is due to Development requesting that their DBAs take a backup of the data before the deletion is carried out. This additional requirement has caused the hold‑up. They are chasing internally and expect progress soon.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan 
Sent: 24 April 2026 06:51
To: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Morning,

 

I’ve heard back from AG who will go ahead with the deletion activities in live today, so just waiting to hear once complete now.  

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 23 April 2026 15:37
To: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >

Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Sarah,

 

That’s amazing news.  Thanks so much for all the effort that’s gone into reviewing this, really appreciate it.

 

I’ll update AG and confirm we are ready to proceed into live, unless you have any concerns with that.  

 

Best wishes,

Michael

Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Michael,

 

I made sure the Insight report was refreshing data, re-extracted the data and compared to the records for deletion file again:

 

Insight:



 

Extracted UOXU data comments and count:



 

Deletion list comments and counts:



 

So now I can see that all the records for deletion no longer appear, and this is confirmed by the data extracted from Insight showing nulls for all the records for deletion:



 

So I’m not sure what happened yesterday, it might have been something with my laptop since I had that weird excel error I’ve not experienced with appointment id before and I had other laptop issues too, or the Insight report needed refreshing (I thought I had done this!). Anyway, it’s all good now and I’ve set the report to refresh on every change to avoid this happening again.

 

Be

emails.subject: RE: Delayed delivery of outgoing emails to ox.ac.uk addresses [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFY33x3:ref ] 00502238.
emails.body:
Hi James

 

Thank you for this update. I have just had my monthly meeting with Sharon and she raised it, so it was good to be able to give her an update. I note that you are keeping Rob updated, could I ask you to include Sharon too.

 

Regards IT or Cority, please let me know if you need to escalate. It would be good to understand where the actual is. Do you have a network with Cority users that you can reach out to, to see if any of them are experiencing problems?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 07 May 2026 10:11
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Delayed delivery of outgoing emails to ox.ac.uk addresses [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFY33x3:ref ] 00502238.

 

Hi Marie and Kevin, 

 

Just to make you aware that we’re currently experiencing an issue where emails are not being sent from the Cority system, which appears to have been occurring since the weekend.

 

This was flagged by Rob yesterday. I’ve raised the issue with both Cority Support and IT Services, as it’s not yet clear whether the root cause sits with the Nexus email relay or Cority’s email configuration.

 

Rob has been informed, and I’ll continue to update him as soon as I receive further information from Cority.

 

At present, the supplier has re‑prioritised the case to a lower priority, which we’re not satisfied with given the impact. I’ve asked for the priority to be reassessed and requested an earlier Teams call, as emails are currently not being sent from the system (last email to the Cority support team below).

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting> 

 

Whatfix: Discover our self-help tool for PeopleXD <https://hrsystems.admin.ox.ac.uk/whatfix> 

 

             <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>  

 

From: James Salas Guillen 
Sent: 07 May 2026 10:00
To: 'Cority Customer Support' <helpdesk@cority.com <mailto:helpdesk@cority.com> >
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Delayed delivery of outgoing emails to ox.ac.uk addresses [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFY33x3:ref ] 00502238.

 

Dear Cority Customer Support,

 

Thank you for your response. I can see that the case is currently being investigated and that a Teams call has been offered. Unfortunately, the earliest available slot is Monday 11th, and while we’re willing to proceed with this if it helps progress the issue, the timing is a concern.

 

Given the active and ongoing impact on users, we’re particularly concerned that no emails will be sent during this period.

 

This issue is having a direct impact on operational delivery, specifically affecting:

 

*	Appointment confirmation emails and reminders, resulting in missed appointments
*	Health Surveillance questionnaires and compliance-related emails, which are not being received due to missing reminders

 

This represents a partial loss of critical functionality in our production environment, with clear user and compliance implications.

 

Could we please have this case reassessed as a matter of priority? An earlier Teams call would also be appreciated so we can mitigate the issue over the next few days.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting> 

 

Whatfix: Discover our self-help tool for PeopleXD <https://hrsystems.admin.ox.ac.uk/whatfix> 

 

             <https://safety.admin.ox.a
