emails.subject: RE: AD groups for PowerBI reports
emails.body:
Hi Asta,

 

All good questions.

 

This is essentially to replace outlook groups with entra ID security groups, which is the way the University is moving under the identity programme.

 

For context, this is only talking about how we provide security access to view PowerBI reports / dashboards. Similar to the AD groups that control access to Tableau reports but Tableau is being transitioned away from so we should get to the point where it’s only PowerBI reports that we will need to grant access to.

 

So Entra ID Security Groups will control access to view the dashboards, and we’ll still need the CONNECT AD groups to control report developer access to PowerBI (because PowerBI is a connect remove service app). 

 

In the example of the reward team, I imagine that the University Administration and Services - HR Reward PowerBI Admin Outlook group will be replaced by an Entra ID group, which will theoretically make administration of membership and auditing easier. But I’m keeping the total reward team out of scope for the time being because I would imagine they’ll want to set up their own groups to control access to the PowerBI dashboards as Jasmine is taking over ownership of them. 

 

We will continue to control who gets access to the CONNECT\BI-RS-PeopleHR-TotalReward_Report_Writers AD group because this controls access to our area of the data warehouse. 

 

For the teams / areas I’ve outlined in my email we will be setting up Entra ID groups to control access to view our PowerBI dashboards only at this stage. I can imagine that over time Entra IDs will be the thing that everyone uses, and therefore could probably merge access to PowerBI with access to HR Reporting reports, for example, but the identity programme are a way off this at the moment!

 

Does this answer your questions?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 06 May 2026 11:29
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: AD groups for PowerBI reports

 

Hi Simon, 

Just wanted to check my understanding of what you propose, if that’s OK as I can’t quite get my head around how users would actually connect and view the dashboards, basically I want to understand the permissions model in place. Is it via Tableau or through Microsoft 365 account, or something else.

If we take the Reward team as an example, their access to PowerBI is currently managed through a bit of a mixed approach using both Outlook groups and one AD super group.

 

The ‘University Administration and Services - HR Reward Team’ Outlook group grants access to the Staff Requests Dashboard to all graders/managers, but there’s one user within their team – Jasmine - who is also added to the ‘University Administration and Services - HR Reward PowerBI Admin’ Outlook group, whilst additionally being a member of the CONNECT\BI-RS-PeopleHR-TotalReward_Report_Writers AD group.

 

Do you know why Jasmine has been added to both? I’m assuming one (Outlook group) is controlling general dashboard access and the other (Ad Group) is providing elevated report writer permissions.

 

Outlook groups work fine for smaller teams because I can add and remove people individually, but they aren’t really a workable solution for larger user groups such as HR Admins. Obviously, if we grant access to PowerBI dashboards via AD groups that would be perfect, as it would align neatly with existing PeopleXD system roles through our

emails.subject: RE: Leave Handover — w/c 4 May 2026
emails.body:
Hi both,

 

I have reviewed the College Staff in PeopleXD Implementation Proposal which looks good. I have consulted with payroll to make sure there’s no rules we have to adhere to when setting up a payroll company and I’ve also reviewed the existing company and pay group codes. 

 

I propose that we use the code 90 for the company and pay group. There is already a pay group 80 for the OSPS pensioners payroll, so it would be easier to have the company and pay group codes match for this new college area of the system, the existing non-payroll pay group code for the main University is 99 so it will align with keeping non-payroll pay groups in the 90s. 

 

I have updated all references in the proposal document with this code and added reference to the proposed pay group code as well. 

 

I am still waiting to see the document that Nathan and Anna Mortimer are pulling together but I’d like to have sight of this before Marie signs anything off. I will ask Nathan for a progress update today.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 01 May 2026 21:08
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Leave Handover — w/c 4 May 2026

 

Hi Simon,

 

As discussed at our handover meeting, here’s a summary of the active workstreams for the week. I’ve also linked the full handover document and the DTP1092 proposal below for reference.

 

DTP1092 — College Staff in PeopleXD

The org structure design session was completed on 27 April. Key decisions are made — we’re reusing the existing management unit structure from Company 10, with Colleges and Halls as two separate units at management unit level. Configuration is estimated at around half a day once the proposal is signed off with Connor O’Brien. Proposal target is w/c 11 May, with sign-off by 15 May.

 

Actions during my leave:

*	Confirm the Company (80) code or choose another to feed into the proposal

*	Confirm the pay group name and payroll calendar for Company 80

*	Create the new payroll company input in PXD

*	Act as point of contact for Melanie Furness (BA) on questions around downstream impact

*	Review Nathan and Anne Mortimer’s Excel hierarchy draft when ready

 

Applicant Successfully Hired Status — PeopleXD (Support Call #11675276)

You’re already leading the Access Group work on this. I’ve raised the change request. On my return I’ll deactivated the status in UOXU and confirm everything is working as expected.

 

Cority Applicant Data Report

James is managing the manual weekly applicant upload. The date-filtered report still needs rebuilding — happy to pick this up on my return unless you’d like to progress it during the week.

 

PXD - DPIA

Simon's review comments addressed as tracked changes (replies under each thread, marked resolved). @Marie Cooksey <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>  — please sanity-check the wording on C21, C28, C30 and C32. They all tie together: C21 cites the Applicant Data Erasure — Process & Legal Statement (currently with you to approve), and C28/C30/C32 cover the retention / destruction / monitoring rewrites in light of the fact that systematic deletion isn't yet in place.

 

ORCIDs in PeopleXD

Screenshots of the current tile have been sent to the team. If they respond with text requirements or staff group specifications during the week, please note and pass to me on my return.

 

Documents

​Kevin - Leave Handover.loop <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/:fl:/g/contentstorage/CSP_fe4d9486-a863-4c5a-bd26-e6d4dcaccd31/IQChMkB-FjCSR5Dxu76BGCrNAZ2Iisvt0dx5g8_

emails.subject: HR Systems support activities in April
emails.to: Brian Jenkins; Sharon Naylor; Chris Williams; Andy Bryson; Paul Kayente
emails.body:
Hi All,

 

As we are now in May, see the update below of support activities that took place in April. You’ll note the lower levels of development or enhancement activity this month – this is due to two sizeable pieces of work (details below) that we are waiting to be funded.

 

As always, please feel free to share with your teams. 

 

Highlights

 

*	We have started to receive enhancement requests being submitted using the Enhancement Request Form on OSM – Available on OSM here <https://oxford.saasiteu.com/Modules/SelfService/#serviceCatalog/filter/AC6143F9458A44F9B3B86C45FF1E571F> 
*	Quote received from Eco-Online (the supplier of the IRIS system) for a number of changes requested by the Incident Reporting POG
*	Have had discussions with the University’s accessibility lead to discuss the accessibility of our systems and how we might go about getting assessments done

 

 

Support Tickets

 

A total of 44 support tickets were raised in April with 34 of those being now resolved:

System

Total Raised

Resolved

Open

Waiting for Customer

Waiting for Supplier

Cority

18

10

5

2

1

Healthy Working Plus

5

5

0

0

0

IRIS

19

18

1

0

0

Odyssey

2

1

0

1

0

Total

44

34

6

3

1

 

The average resolution time for these incidents in April is 4 days.

 

Enhancement Work

 

The following enhancement items have been delivered in April:

*	IRIS – Updates to fields on the DSO Review form allowing users to highlight an incident as a Serious Incident of Fatality incident (SIF) and have this trigger a formal investigation on IRIS
*	IRIS – OSM requests updated with new URLs to the IRIS training pages on OxIntranet

 

 

Future Enhancement Work

 

All enhancement items, both those in progress and planned for the future, can be found on the Health & Safety Roadmap – Available here <https://app.powerbi.com/groups/me/apps/c00c2bb4-2038-4318-bc17-5e700b6a3927/reports/314ba831-839f-4f53-846d-36acabb3f703/69e9b07a2ba77205059d?ctid=cc95de1b-97f5-4f93-b4ba-fe68b852cf91&experience=power-bi> 

Two new enhancement requests have been submitted by system users – both for IRIS.

 

There are also two requests for funding for enhancement work with OH&S to approve – these are:

 

Cority:

*	Fix for Primary Job Position flag business rules – Issue preventing client details appearing in banner and inaccuracies in organisation structure data in reporting
*	Health surveillance compliance reporting – Data field missing in build from Cority preventing a consistent link between the clearance certificate and the specific surveillance group membership
*	SMS appointment reminders – New supplier functionality to send automatic SMS reminders to clients 24 hours before their appointment
*	Outlook and MS Teams integration with Cority – New functionality to integrate links for MS Teams meetings with appointments created on Cority

 

IRIS:

*	Relocation of Supervisor fields to DSO Review – Allows a supervisor to be notified for every incident
*	Updates to incident classification options – Bring in-line with best practice
*	Look and feel updates to support the above changes

 

Outside of those two funding requests, the following items are in progress or planned for the near future:

 

Cority:

*	Drafting notes templates – Ability to draft smart note templates to allow a user to complete them at a later time – Fix expected in June 2026 release by the supplier
*	Bulk merging tool – Ability to merge applicant and live client data from the People data feed – Solution has been tested and working in production environment – Work has started to merge these records and will take place over the coming months

 

Healthy Working Plus:

*	Data import automation – Work being conducted by the Azure Integrations Team in IT Services to automate the feed of people data into the system – Expected del

This sounds like a sensible thing to turn on in my opinion, but we’d just need to include something in guidance and gives users a heads up I imagine. 

 

Is there scope to change what the error message says? “Gender” is the field name but we have it language packed as “Sex” so I would have expected the error message to adopt the language packed phrase. From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 05 May 2026 09:07
To: Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Decision Required – New “Gender Must Be Selected” Client Parameter

 

Morning,

 

Access Group have introduced a new client parameter in the portal: “Gender of Male or Female must be selected.”

 

If enabled, this parameter prevents users from selecting and saving “Unknown” as a gender when creating a new starter record or updating Diversity Details. It does not prevent users from changing an existing employee’s gender to “Unknown.”

 

Tracy (Payroll) has confirmed that HMRC does not allow us to report “Unknown” via RTI. At present, she runs a report and manually corrects any records where “Unknown” has been selected.

 

I wanted to bring this new parameter to your attention so that a decision can be made on whether we should enable it.

 

For validation, I enabled the parameter in UAT. When attempting to save a new starter or update Diversity Details with “Unknown” selected, the system displays a warning message stating “Gender of Male or Female must be selected” and prevents the user from continuing. I also confirmed that changing an existing employee’s gender to “Unknown” is still permitted.

 

A screenshot of the warning message is included below for reference.



 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

emails.subject: Leave Handover — w/c 4 May 2026
emails.body:
Hi Simon,

As discussed at our handover meeting, here’s a summary of the active workstreams for the week. I’ve also linked the full handover document and the DTP1092 proposal below for reference.

DTP1092 — College Staff in PeopleXD
The org structure design session was completed on 27 April. Key decisions are made — we’re reusing the existing management unit structure from Company 10, with Colleges and Halls as two separate units at management unit level. Configuration is estimated at around half a day once the proposal is signed off with Connor O’Brien. Proposal target is w/c 11 May, with sign-off by 15 May.

Actions during my leave:

*	
	Confirm the Company (80) code or choose another to feed into the proposal
*	
	Confirm the pay group name and payroll calendar for Company 80
*	
	Create the new payroll company input in PXD
*	
	Act as point of contact for Melanie Furness (BA) on questions around downstream impact
*	
	Review Nathan and Anne Mortimer’s Excel hierarchy draft when ready


Applicant Successfully Hired Status — PeopleXD (Support Call #11675276)
You’re already leading the Access Group work on this. I’ve raised the change request. On my return I’ll deactivated the status in UOXU and confirm everything is working as expected.

Cority Applicant Data Report
James is managing the manual weekly applicant upload. The date-filtered report still needs rebuilding — happy to pick this up on my return unless you’d like to progress it during the week.

PXD - DPIA
Simon's review comments addressed as tracked changes (replies under each thread, marked resolved). @Marie Cooksey <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>  — please sanity-check the wording on C21, C28, C30 and C32. They all tie together: C21 cites the Applicant Data Erasure — Process & Legal Statement (currently with you to approve), and C28/C30/C32 cover the retention / destruction / monitoring rewrites in light of the fact that systematic deletion isn't yet in place.

ORCIDs in PeopleXD
Screenshots of the current tile have been sent to the team. If they respond with text requirements or staff group specifications during the week, please note and pass to me on my return.

Documents

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

 

With regards the DPIA, then we have now taken compliance advice on this.

 

There is a DPA that covers the data processing element of the existing college census and we are looking to amend this to cover the transfer of data into PXD. The data being loaded into PXD is not sensitive data and therefore does not warrant a DPIA and so will be covered by the amended DPA.

 

Once the data is in PXD however, there will indeed be a need for DPIA coverage in PXD. We were wondering therefore if there is a PXD DPIA which by default covers the processing of the new records we are putting into PXD, or whether an existing PXD DPIA would need amending (most likely)? 

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin  

 

 

 

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 30 April 2026 16:56
To: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Steve McBrearty <steve.mcbrearty@it.ox.ac.uk>; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Melanie Furniss <melanie.furniss@it.ox.ac.uk>

Hi Crispin,

Thank you for sharing.

I have included @Simon Burford <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>  in this email for visibility.

Could you also advise on the status of the DPIA to be initiated in parallel with the proposal approval - Is completion required as a pre-condition to any live data load?

Best,
Kevin

emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; Michelle Williams; Julie Hickman; Simon Burford; Kevin Lelitte; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi All

 

As we know SBS have deferred using WFM until October 2026. As the project is coming to a close Tracy BB has shared the following actions required to support SBS go-live.

 

Can I ask that you review and if necessary amend this document:  SBS detailed actions to launch in Oct 2026.docx <https://unioxfordnexus-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/personal/admn5561_ox_ac_uk/Documents/Projects%20info/Workforce%20Management/SBS%20detailed%20actions%20to%20launch%20in%20Oct%202026.docx?d=wf7a61df4ce3349b6872b6b3ae599f4f0&csf=1&web=1&e=NwPd7d>  

 

*	Ensure it lists all the activity you would expect. 
*	What resource do we need to onboard them ie can we manage it within the team with existing resource, or do we need to bring in additional resource if so what role?
*	Can you give me an indication of time required to onboard them?
*	When would you envisage starting this activity?

 

Tracy is going to arrange a meeting to discuss this further, so I will ensure the correct people are invited.

 

Please forward to anyone else who you feel should contribute.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

Marie Cooksey

Head of HR Systems

emails.subject: AD groups for PowerBI reports
emails.body:
Hi Asta,

 

I have been involved in discussions around different types of AD groups as part of the Identity Management programme and I think we need to consider setting up some groups for PeopleXD users so that we can automate the provisioning of access to dashboards such as the Fixed Term Contract dashboard which will need to be locked down to PeopleXD users only.

 

I’d like to make sure we’re aligned with the existing persona’s / roles that we manage at the moment so that we can map any new AD groups to the security matrix to make maintenance as easy as possible, so I just wanted to confirm what I was thinking with you before I took it anywhere.

 

I am thinking we should set up the following…

 

1.	HR Admin PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*         Personnel Administrator 

*         Personnel Administrator Read-Only

*         Staff Request Creator 

*         Staff Request Approver

*         Reporting Only – Personnel

 

2.	Recruitment PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*	Recruitment Administrator
*	Recruitment Administrator Read-Only
*	Reporting Only – Recruitment

 

3.	Payroll PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*	Payroll Administrator
*	Payroll Manager
*	Payroll Manager + ability to generate Timesheets

 

4.	EDU PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*	Diversity&Equal Opps Administrator

 

5.	HR Systems PowerBI user

And assign this to anyone with the following system roles:

*	HESA Administrator (HR Analytics team) 
*	HR Systems Administrator (1st line support team, G5)
*	HR Systems Administrator (2nd line support team - Sys Admin and G7+)

 

6.	HR Systems PowerBI workspace owners

We may need to administer these separately to PowerBI developers who also need to be workspace owners.

 

I’m not sure what to do just yet with the Total Reward Team as they have a PowerBI developer as part of their team so I suspect we will not be creating many dashboards for them, and they may take forward the creation of AD groups for their team which we can piggy back onto.

 

Can you think of any other groups we might need to share PowerBI reports with who we already manage system access for?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: RE: Project to get College staff onto peoplexd
emails.body:
Hi Mel,

 

We haven’t got to the level of detail of workgroups yet, and I don’t know if we need to record managers for college staff, but if we do need to record who manages who then we’ll need separate work groups for each team according to the management structure in each college. I also think there’s a risk that using one work group will expose data between different colleges which could be a concern.

 

We do know at this stage that we will have a separate company code for colleges, so any filtering of college data can be done at this level, with the University of Oxford data being held under company 10 and college data being held under a different code (e.g. company 80).

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Melanie Furniss <melanie.furniss@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 30 April 2026 09:23
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Project to get College staff onto peoplexd

 

Hello Kevin,

 

I believe Crispin has spoken with you regarding the impact assessment I am producing, for the project looking at putting college staff into PeopleXD.

 

I just had a quick question and was hoping you might be able to help.  I can see that you are looking to create a separate entity on PXD to put the college staff records into.  Was the option of adding the staff into PXD and putting them into one single workgroup discussed?  I don't have much background of this project or the technical information of how the system is fully configured, so I'm unaware if this was discussed as an option.

 

If they were added and placed in a single workgroup, I believe they could be in/excluded from reporting where needed?  This would simplify the process and eliminate the need for a separate entity.

 

I may/probably have missed some of the requirements/technical background which would mean this would not be an option, but if you could let me know if this was looked at as an option, or the reasons why this isn't an option that would be great so I can include it in my report.

 

 

Thanks 

Mel

 

Melanie Furniss

Business Analyst

emails.subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration
emails.to: Marie Cooksey; Michael O'Sullivan; Michelle Williams
emails.body:
Thank you for the full explanations and recommendations Michael, this is very thorough and helpful to know!

 

I think we will need to be mindful of what the WFM project team has already communicated, I know they were very much in favour of enabling the automatic carry over.   I also think we need to consider when we communicate this.   

 

We have time in the diary to discuss this tomorrow, so I’m going to suggest we use some of this time to discuss the comms.

 

Many thanks

Julie

 

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 29 April 2026 08:46
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration

 

Hi Michael

 

Thanks for the ratio. I think having this understanding allows us to have a robust response if challenged why we have opted not to proceed and we can include in our communications.

 

Michelle, Julie we need to work on this to include in the Bulletin and Briefing.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 29 April 2026 08:15
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration

 

Hi Marie,

 

Just taking a very simple look at this using the existing ‘PERDEP01 Staff in Post Full Data Set’ report and filtering on the ‘Employee Sub Status’ for full or part time, so not including term-time, variable hours. This returns:

 

Full‑time vs Part‑time Breakdown

 

•             Full‑time: 18,452 (77.2%)

•             Part‑time: 5,437 (22.8%)

 

So roughly 1 in 4 employees is part‑time.

 

At first glance, Option B looks attractive because it automates carry forward for the majority (full‑time staff). But the critical point is this:

 

The entire complexity and risk sits with the part‑time group, and that group is still very large.

 

5,437 part‑time staff is not a small exception group when you look at the required actions:

 

•             Manual corrections every year

•             Opportunities for incorrect balances

•             Cases where staff will temporarily see the wrong entitlement

•             Adjustments that must be completed quickly to avoid confusion and complaints

 

Even if automation helps with full‑time staff, the operational risk and policy compliance risk are entirely concentrated in the part‑time population.

 

Option B creates a two‑tier experience:

 

•             Full‑time staff see correct balances immediately

•             Part‑time staff see incorrect balances until HR fixes them

 

That inconsistency is visible, confusing, and likely to generate queries.

 

Option A avoids all of this.

 

It treats all staff consistently, ensures policy compliance, and avoids the reputational risk of staff seeing fluctuating or incorrect balances.

 

 

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

emails.body:
Good morning Kevin and Marie,

 

I’m sorry I feel I keep picking this up and down based on my workload, but I think we should now ask AG to carry out this work to unpick the error created by Chemistry and start this cycle with clean data.  We have already have one department contact Support about this.

 

Marie – I know we discussed this in a 121 a few months ago, but are you still happy to request that AG goes ahead using our flex points (see quote details below in Kevin’s email)?  For now, I think we should see this as one off and manage what we can with comms and guidance to hopefully prevent it happening again.

 

Kevin – If Marie gives us the go ahead, are you ok to arrange what’s needed with AG?

 

Many thanks both for your help!

Julie

April 2026	 
Welcome to the IT and Digital Digest, the bi-monthly newsletter bringing you the latest news from across our digital teams and portfolios. 

Your feedback is very welcome. Please get in touch via the Office of the CDIO (OCDIO) Communications team <mailto:communications@it.ox.ac.uk?subject=IT%20and%20Digital%20Digest> .
 

 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/i/amf_ouc/workspace_44/IT_and_Digital_Digest/Your_paragraph_text_1_.png?size=600x0&w=BtJDs2Ne5igNWIDy3IfuAv1cfdyo> 	
  	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7U2gzz1xSGiJBlRitMThuctku> 	
We’re pleased to share the first of two editions for Trinity term. Headline items below include new measures taking effect from June to ensure that all staff complete our Information Security & Data Protection training annually, and the shift to more agile delivery in our IT Services teams and digital portfolios. You can also read about the launch of the redeveloped Oxweb, the University’s main website. 

You’ll find information about AI-enabled phishing scams targeting universities, news of enhanced training data reports, and updates from the project teams supporting our ongoing investment in digital transformation. Please note the reminder from the Telephony Project that all Chorus phone lines need to be retired by July. 

All of this sits within the broader digital strategy I'm developing with colleagues across the divisions and colleges. Do come and hear more at the Digital Insights Open Forum on 21 May <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7U51RpvHBFokRt6Gzoo4CHTD6>  — and bring your questions. 

David White

  	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/i/amf_ouc/workspace_44/Highlights_2026.jpg?size=600x0&w=Btra6FGdEK3K7C0VBJKBXUgsO0wk> 	
  	
	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7U9aNbeWc91JKDYLHNDgkt251> 	

A new website for the University

Oxford’s main public website has been overhauled, and new developments made to the University-wide intranet, as part of the ongoing programme to enhance Oxford’s digital communications.

Discover enhancements to Oxweb and OxIntranet <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7UbW51J5V87l0Le9Np83sYsnD>  	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7UeHmSdfE7cWgStxT0CQBtSGf> 	

Keeping the University's people and data safe

All University and College staff must complete Information Security & Data Protection training annually. From 1 June, you will risk losing access to your University mailbox until this is completed.   

Read more and complete our online training (SSO) <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7UiQiDWueAQla3lD1pS2jf18a>  	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7UlBAuqDXzVWqaB171mPrKrqM> 	

Training data - new report launched

From raw data to rapid insight: enhanced training data reports are now available for managers across the University, making it faster and easier to monitor mandatory training data.

Find out about the mandatory training data reports  <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7UomSkUNGz1xGhQpcCRCAfRJo>  	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7Ur8aboXpy78Wp5NiempILi20> 	

Microsoft licence features and what's available 

The Microsoft A5 licensing tier for Office (Nexus) 365 includes a larger mailbox, access to services such as Microsoft Bookings and Power Automate, as well as access to paid-for subscriptions.

Features available with your licence <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7UtTs1T78xcKcwlbnPRcRgIkC>  	
 <https://news.it.ox.ac.uk/c/117z7Uzq1IRqAvnWIKPXz2QN8hyVQ> 	

Quiet technologies enabling better work

Across the University, we rely on Microsoft tools every day to teach, study, research, and run services. Read about our vision for developing these technologies and why they matter.

From: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 15 April 2026 8:59 AM
To: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Eugenio Barrio <eugenio.barrio@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: ORCIDs in PXD - 14th Apr 26 meeting notes 
 

Dear All

 

I just want to be absolutely sure that I have correctly understood what we agreed with regards referencing ORCID in PXD. Please can you confirm – is my understanding correct?

 

There are two areas to consider: onboarding and self-service. Currently instructions for ORCID appear only in onboarding and only to a select groups (researchers). There is currently no reference to ORCID (tile or task) in self-service.

It was accepted that capturing ORCIDs directly into PXD, either by free-text inputs or a dynamic connection to ORCID.org, would not be feasible, at least not in the immediate term. It was therefore agreed that the DTP1092 team would:

*	Review/update the current text appearing in the onboarding pages
*	Review/update the links (e.g. adding  https://register.it.ox.ac.uk/self/orcid)
*	Review/update the select groups – most likely expand the groups.

 

Making the changes would be relatively straight forward and is estimated to take 1-2 weeks.

 

Current Onboarding 

 

There is a tile visible to selected groups with instructions and links – see attached example.

 

Currently open to selected groups (are these the correct ones? This list comes from an earlier meeting we had on ORCIDs):

 

*	AC  Academic (teaching & research) 
*	AP  Research Staff (including E grades) 
*	AR  Research Fellow 
*	AS  Research Support 
*	AT  Teaching Support 
*	AO  Senior University Officer 

 

Current wording: 

If you carry out research and do not yet have an ORCID (unique research ID) [https://orcid.org] then please visit our ORCID webpage [https://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home] to get one and affiliate it to the University. An ORCID is important to prevent similar name confusion, meeting funder requirements and help us to identify your research outputs for our current research information system.

 

If you already have an ORCID but have not yet affiliated it to Oxford University please visit our ORCID at Oxford webpage [https://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home] to do so. This will verify your affiliation to Oxford at ORCID.org,

 

Current Links given:

 

ORCID:  http://orcid.org <http://orcid.org> 

ORCID Oxford Homepage: http://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home <http://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home> 

 

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

 

 

 

 

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Crispin Muncaster
PROJECT MANAGER
IT Services | University of Oxford

T: 01865 Â 616324
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: RE: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD
emails.body:
Thanks Michael, a comprehensive, clear response as always 😊

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 29 April 2026 07:53
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD

 

Hi Marie,

 

In PeopleXD, any absence type that employees can request through self‑service must be linked to a entitlement balance. This is because the system needs a measurable unit (days or hours) to validate requests, apply caps, and route them through the standard approval workflow.

 

If Volunteering Days were moved into self‑service, they would therefore require their own dedicated balance and entitlement configuration. The system cannot separate them from annual leave unless a new balance is created and maintained.

 

Because Volunteering Days typically have a fixed allowance, this balance would need its own entitlement rules, its own annual refresh, and its own monitoring. It would also need to be allocated to every relevant employee appointment so that the option appears in self‑service.

 

We have already faced this issue with TOIL.

When reviewing whether TOIL could be booked through self‑service, we reached the same conclusion: enabling self‑service would require creating and maintaining a separate TOIL balance for every employee. The administrative overhead and risk of confusion outweighed the benefit, so we deliberately chose not to introduce a TOIL balance. That decision has worked well in practice and has kept the system clean and manageable.

 

The same logic applies here. Introducing a Volunteering Day balance would mean:

 

•             Every employee would carry an additional entitlement balance alongside annual leave

•             HR teams would need to maintain, adjust, and troubleshoot two balances per employee

•             Annual resets or entitlement changes would need to be configured and monitored

•             Employees would see multiple balances in self‑service, increasing queries and errors

 

This creates ongoing workload for HR as well as HR Systems without delivering meaningful value, especially given how infrequently Volunteering Days are used.

 

The current approach, HR/Manager recording Volunteering Days directly, in line with other non‑entitlement absences such as jury service, study leave, sabbatical, and TOIL, is simple, controlled, and low‑risk. It avoids unnecessary configuration and prevents setting a precedent where other absence types might also be expected to have their own self‑service balance and entitlement setup.

 

In summary:

 

•             The system can support self‑service Volunteering Days, but only by introducing a new balance type.

•             We have already rejected this approach for TOIL for the same reasons.

•             The administrative cost and complexity fall entirely on HR/HR Systems teams.

•             The current HR‑administered process is consistent, efficient, and avoids creating future expectations for other absence types.

 

For these reasons, retaining the existing process remains the most sustainable and operationally sound approach.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

 

Whatfix – use our self-help tool for PeopleXD <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/whatfix.aspx>

emails.subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration
emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; Michelle Williams; Julie Hickman
emails.body:
Hi Michael

 

Thanks for the ratio. I think having this understanding allows us to have a robust response if challenged why we have opted not to proceed and we can include in our communications.

 

Michelle, Julie we need to work on this to include in the Bulletin and Briefing.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 29 April 2026 08:15
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration

 

Hi Marie,

 

Just taking a very simple look at this using the existing ‘PERDEP01 Staff in Post Full Data Set’ report and filtering on the ‘Employee Sub Status’ for full or part time, so not including term-time, variable hours. This returns:

 

Full‑time vs Part‑time Breakdown

 

•             Full‑time: 18,452 (77.2%)

•             Part‑time: 5,437 (22.8%)

 

So roughly 1 in 4 employees is part‑time.

 

At first glance, Option B looks attractive because it automates carry forward for the majority (full‑time staff). But the critical point is this:

 

The entire complexity and risk sits with the part‑time group, and that group is still very large.

 

5,437 part‑time staff is not a small exception group when you look at the required actions:

 

•             Manual corrections every year

•             Opportunities for incorrect balances

•             Cases where staff will temporarily see the wrong entitlement

•             Adjustments that must be completed quickly to avoid confusion and complaints

 

Even if automation helps with full‑time staff, the operational risk and policy compliance risk are entirely concentrated in the part‑time population.

 

Option B creates a two‑tier experience:

 

•             Full‑time staff see correct balances immediately

•             Part‑time staff see incorrect balances until HR fixes them

 

That inconsistency is visible, confusing, and likely to generate queries.

 

Option A avoids all of this.

 

It treats all staff consistently, ensures policy compliance, and avoids the reputational risk of staff seeing fluctuating or incorrect balances.

 

 

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

emails.subject: People data problems identified as part of REF2029 (DTP1092)
emails.to: Sarah Rowles; Athena Artuso; Simon Burford
emails.body:
Hi All

 

I would like to discuss this table below at our meeting tomorrow.

*	This is a list of “Problems” identified as part of DTP1092 (which is ending v. soon) and people data. I have met with Anne M a few times to discuss and would like to give you an update of the situation and work together to agree how we can approach this, and on the whole support Nathan to deliver. Please note the table below is not all sitting within our remit, it does go across the People Department and the relevant contacts are being engaged.

 

I will also give a brief update on the Tableau withdrawal, so we are all up-to-date.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

 

People data problems identified as REF2029

Ref

Problem

Solution

Status

People Department involved

P1.1

Some PXD records to not have associated SSO for staff member, so data sets cannot be linked

'DTP1092 team identified and validated missing SSOs for 67% of problem records and established corrections process

HR systems to do regular repeat of the process developed by the DTP1092 team in spring 2026 to identify SSOs for these records and arrange for PXD to be updated. Review of controls to ensure new personnel records' SSO data complete

Resolution of records for the same individual to be addressed by the Identity programme, and potentially replacement of SSO as the default identifier

Green

 

Correction of historic records

Protocol created

Regular checks by Nathan

In progress

 

P1.2

P5.1

Problem: College Staff - HR Data about College staff who undertake research is not readily available. Data can generally be supplied by the x40 colleges and PPHs and their respective systems. Data is devolved and therefore unqueryable. The process to collate researcher data from each college (inc SSO) is manual and time consuming.

EDI data for college staff

RoD data for college staff

RS implemented college census

HR systems  to create appointment records for College Staff with research duties in PXD. This data will be managed by Research Services and will be imported from the College Census that Research Services undertake. This has been agreed in principle

Green

 

Project underway

In progress

HR Systems

P1.12

P1.2

P1.3

NHS-only employees - there is no identifier data available in grants data for researchers who are currently NHS-only employees

HR only records added to PXD alongside College researchers, to provide systemised records of honorary positions for those with significant research responsibilities

Red

 

No implementation ongoing

 

P8.2

P8.3

Staff classification issues (a) Classification Clashes (e.g. impossible combinations of category and subcategory); (b) Classification values not appropriate to a particular Job Title Description; (c) Some JTD values are either vague (e.g. “Researcher”) or no longer in sector use (e.g. “Postdoctoral Research Assistant”) 

Report consumers misunderstand data related to categories of researcher

Long term: replace PXD with system that uses controlled vocabularies and other system controls to enforce consistent and accurate classifications.

Until then, support HRIS users to use the Decision Tree Tool in PXD which is designed to ensure more accurate classification of posts is entered.

Work with the HRIS team to produce a reduced list of Job Title Descriptions and the corresponding classifications that each JTD should have - linked to job family development

Provide report users with enhanced definitions of reporting categories

Report on HESA classifications

Amber

 

Partial solution in progress

HR briefing for classification values issues + bespoke written guidance planned

ACRF - Rachel

P2.2

P5.7

P5.5

Missing identifiers in grants data for fellowship holders

No EDI data for fellowship scheme applicants

Cannot identify fellowship holders in HR data

Standardise processes acro

emails.subject: FW: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD
emails.body:
Hi Kevin, Michael

 

Could one of you let me know the feasibility of the query below (highlighted)?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Matt Thomas <matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 28 April 2026 13:30
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Logging of volunteering hours in People XD

 

Hi Marie,

 

I hope that all’s well with you.

 

The Volunteering Pilot paper was well received at PCOB yesterday but concerns around the operational complexity associated with logging issues were highlighted as an issue that would need fixing before a wider University roll-out would be possible. 

 

I believe that the main difficulties during the pilot phase were due to a combination of the below:

 

a.	Line managers having to log the hours on behalf of their staff, rather than staff being able to log them and line manager’s approving the hours (as is the case with annual leave);
b.	Some departments still not being fully transitioned onto People XD;
c.	Some departmental administrators still not being fully trained in using People XD.

 

In light of this - I was wondering whether yourself or a member of your team could please kindly provide an update on:

 

1.	The status of departmental migration over to People XD and which departments are still yet to migrate;
2.	The status of training for departmental administrators in using People XD;
3.	The possibility to amend the module so that participants can request their volunteer hours for approval by their manager’s, as opposed to the manager’s having to log the hours.

 

Many thanks in advance for any help that you’re able to offer on the above queries.

 

Kind regards,

 

Matthew Thomas

Project Delivery Manager - Pay and Conditions

People Department | University of Oxford

matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:matt.thomas@admin.ox.ac.uk>

emails.subject: RE: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration
emails.to: Michael O'Sullivan; Michelle Williams; Julie Hickman
emails.body:
Hi Michael

 

Thank you for pulling this together in this easy to digest format.

 

I agree with your recommendation, based on the facts you have presented, but I would like to understand the ratio of FT : PT staff.

 

Michelle & Julie if we go with Michael’s recommendation, how would you propose we communicate this and engage with departments, given that we are aware the WFM project have raised this as a solution going forward?

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 27 April 2026 14:14
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Proposed Approach for Annual Leave Carry‑Forward Configuration

 

Afternoon, 

 

I’m writing to share the outcome of my review of the PeopleXD carry‑forward functionality, particularly in relation to the pro‑rating of carry‑forward, and to present the recommended approach for this year’s annual leave process. Based on the system’s capabilities and the University’s policy requirements, especially the need to pro‑rate carry‑forward for part‑time staff, my recommendation is to proceed with Option A, continuing with manual HR adjustments. I would welcome your input on this approach before we move forward.

 

I’ll take a look at our calendars and setup a meeting to discuss further.  

 

Background:

 

The University’s annual leave policy allows staff, in exceptional circumstances and with Head of Department approval, to carry forward up to 5 days, pro-rated for part-time staff.  

 

Testing in PeopleXD confirms that the system cannot pro-rata the carry-forward limit based on FTE.  The system applies a single maximum value (e.g., 5 days or equivalent hours) to all staff, regardless of working pattern.

 

This creates a gap between policy requirements and system capability.

 

 

System Behaviour Identified:

 

If an employee has more than 5 days remaining, the system carries forward 5 days and removes the rest.

 

If an employee has less than 5 days, the system carries forward the full remaining balance.

 

No pro‑rating is applied for part‑time staff.

 

Configuration allows only a single minimum and maximum value for all staff.

 

 

Access Group Support have confirmed the following:

There is nothing in the standard system setup that supports automatic pro‑rating of the carry‑forward maximum specifically for part‑time staff.

There is no standard configuration option that allows pro‑rated carry‑forward caps; this functionality sits outside the current leave setup.

The Terms & Conditions profiles, where annual leave entitlement rules are defined, do not include any logic that would allow the system to pro‑rate a carry‑forward maximum.

 

 

Options for Implementation:

 

Option A – (Recommended) Continue with Manual HR Adjustments (Current Method).

 

HR manually enters carry‑forward adjustments for all approved cases.

 

Advantages

•             Ensures full compliance with University policy, including pro‑rata rules.

•             HR retains control and can check accuracy before applying adjustments.

•             No risk of over‑crediting part‑time staff.

 

Disadvantages

•             Entirely manual process; time‑consuming for HR teams.

•             Higher administrative burden during year‑end.

•             Potential for human error if volumes are high.

 

Option B – Enable Automated Carry‑Forward in PeopleXD, with HR Adjustments for Part‑Time Staff

 

The system automatically carries forward up to 5 days for all staff. HR then manually adjusts part‑time staff to apply the correct pro‑rata entitlement.

 

Advantages

•             Reduces manual work for full‑time staff. 

•             Provides a consistent, auto

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11675276: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi Kevin,

 

Thanks for this.

 

I’m aware of the issue with records being cleared if they are moved onto a status without the Applicant Acceptance checkbox enabled, this is actually as a result of my testing around identifying records where the this has happened, so I am currently working with the Access Group to identify records where this has occurred so we can script a fix. I would like to get the applicant successfully hired status deactivated before we run that script in LIVE to make sure that we catch all of them and no future ones are introduced.

 

Yes please do raise a change, it doesn’t make sure difference to me which environment you do it in, but UOXU is the most recently cloned so would be sensible to test in there I would think.

 

I don’t believe that there will be any need to provide an alternative status as this is a status that no one should be using. The correct “Final Status” should be applicant accepted, in the scenario where the applicant has been hired.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

Subject: Support Call# 11675276: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status

 

Hi Simon,

Quick update on the Successfully Hired status — Access Group has confirmed deactivation is the right route. There is an at-risk group to flag, along with a couple of points I need your steer on.

What Access Group confirmed
The retention mechanism is driven by the Applicant Acceptance checkbox on the status setup. If this is unticked, applying the status strips retention. They confirmed the Active toggle is the supported method to deactivate. As an alternative, they suggested ticking Applicant Acceptance on the existing status instead of removing it, which would fix retention while keeping the status usable.

On deactivation

*	Existing records, the Successful Applicants screen, and RECDEP41 are unaffected — deactivation only prevents new use
*	Fully reversible — re-ticking Active restores the status
*	Does not retroactively restore retention

Access Group advised reviewing existing records before cleardown. The at-risk group is anyone on Successfully Hired without a linked HR record (those with a linked record are excluded from cleardown).

Could you pull the count for this group based on your data? We can decide next steps once we have the number. Will departments currently using the status need an alternative? — flagging this for Michelle’s cleardown comms.

Testing plan

*	Apply the status to a test applicant and confirm retention behaviour pre-deactivation
*	Deactivate via the Ac

emails.subject: Support Call# 11675276: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Quick update on the Successfully Hired status — Access Group has confirmed deactivation is the right route. There is an at-risk group to flag, along with a couple of points I need your steer on.
What Access Group confirmed
The retention mechanism is driven by the Applicant Acceptance checkbox on the status setup. If this is unticked, applying the status strips retention. They confirmed the Active toggle is the supported method to deactivate. As an alternative, they suggested ticking Applicant Acceptance on the existing status instead of removing it, which would fix retention while keeping the status usable.
On deactivation

*	Existing records, the Successful Applicants screen, and RECDEP41 are unaffected — deactivation only prevents new use
*	Fully reversible — re-ticking Active restores the status
*	Does not retroactively restore retention

Access Group advised reviewing existing records before cleardown. The at-risk group is anyone on Successfully Hired without a linked HR record (those with a linked record are excluded from cleardown).
Could you pull the count for this group based on your data? We can decide next steps once we have the number. Will departments currently using the status need an alternative? — flagging this for Michelle’s cleardown comms.
Testing plan

*	Apply the status to a test applicant and confirm retention behaviour pre-deactivation
*	Deactivate via the Active toggle and confirm it no longer appears for new use
*	Confirm existing records, Successful Applicants, and RECDEP41 are unchanged
*	Re-tick Active to confirm reversibility, then deactivate again

Points for confirmation

*	Which environment do you want this done in?
*	Would you like me to raise a change request?

We have an Applicant Statuses article, but it only covers the navigation path — it doesn't actually explain how to remove or deactivate a status. Happy to update the article once we've completed this work.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, April 23, 2026 10:45 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Subject: Re: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status 

Hi Simon,
Thanks for flagging this.
I’ll take this forward and review the deactivation approach, I’ll also check for any existing knowledge articles on removing applicant statuses.
I’ll come back with an update and next steps shortly.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 4:03 PM
To: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status


Hi all,

 

Could I request that you look into de-activating the “Applicant Successfully Hired” applicant status in PeopleXD?

 

From a very quick look it seems that we can untick the “Active” indicator against this status and this prevents the status from being selectable, but doesn’t seem to error when you view the applicant or anything. However, full testing would be needed, I’m not sure if we have a knowledge article outlining removing applicant statuses?

 

The reason for this is that I have found that departments are fairly regularly using this status but when they do so it removes one of the flags in the background of the database which indicates that the applicant should be retained. When I spoke to the access group recently they asked why we had that status active if it causes us issues. I think it was introduced during an upgrade but I don’t think we have

emails.subject: H&S management system
emails.to: Kevin Lelitte; James Salas Guillen; Christopher Sanders
emails.body:
Hi All

 

The Strategic Health and Safety Modular system project is progressing well and at the stage of pulling together the questionnaire to send out to prospective suppliers to complete.

 

I am very keen for HR Systems to approach this project differently to other recent H&S projects and I want to ensure that we can feed in at every stage.

 

With that said I have asked for you all to be invited to review the questionnaire before it is issued. We will then discuss the review of responses and attendance at shortlisted presentations.

 

As mentioned there are further key dates over the next few months, that I want you involved with. I am awaiting the timelines so that I can share them with you and we can agree who will be attending what. Can I ask for you to establish a shared site, we can add any information regarding this project, so it is in a central location.

 

I would like to discuss this project in further detail with you, and how I envisage us working. But I just wanted to give you a heads up that you may start receiving invites.

 

Chris – I have asked Maria Morris to reach out to you to discuss our approach to PUGs.  As part of this project she will be establishing a Working Group, that may naturally morph into a PUG at the end of the project.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

Marie Cooksey

Head of HR Systems

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

emails.subject: Organizational Hierarchy Discussion
emails.body:
Good Afternoon Kevin,

 

Thank you for both your own and your colleagues time today.

 

Please find below a summary of the call:

 

1. Executive Summary

*	The session focused on how to incorporate a new non‑payroll company into the existing PeopleXD organisational structure using best practices.
*	Strong guidance was given to manage hierarchy links via the Portal, while continuing to manage hierarchy design via Back Office.
*	Most required reference data already exists; the only potential addition discussed is splitting “Colleges and Halls” at an intermediate hierarchy level.
*	Reuse of existing reference data across multiple companies was confirmed as valid, with reporting implications highlighted.
*	Clear next steps were outlined: confirm reference data decisions, create company/pay group, build hierarchy, then load posts and employees.

________________________________

2. Key Topics Discussed

*	Overview of the current organisational hierarchy in PeopleXD
*	Best practice for maintaining hierarchy (Portal vs Back Office)
*	Adding a new non‑payroll company into an existing structure
*	Hierarchy levels, indicators, defaults, visibility, and display modes
*	Reference data management (companies, divisions, departments, management units, user fields)
*	Reusing reference data across multiple companies
*	Reporting implications when reference data is shared
*	Payroll-side setup required for a non‑payroll company
*	Pay group creation for non‑payroll employees
*	Security, access control, organisational roles, and workflows
*	Sequencing for hierarchy setup, post profiles, and employee data migration
*	Access issues to Portal structure setup and reference data screens

________________________________

3. Decisions Made (Explicit or Implicit)

*	New company will be incorporated into the existing organisational structure, not a separate structure.
*	Hierarchy items should be linked using the Portal, not both Portal and Back Office.
*	Hierarchy structure/layout changes should remain managed in Back Office.
*	Existing hierarchy design (levels and order) will be retained for the new company.
*	Existing reference data (e.g. divisions, departments, management units) will largely be reused rather than recreated.
*	A new non‑payroll pay group will be created rather than reusing an existing one.
*	Reference data should generally be inactivated, not deleted.
*	Reports should be filtered by company to avoid cross‑company data contamination when reference data is reused.

________________________________

4. Open Questions / Unresolved Issues

*	Final decision on whether to split “Colleges” and “Halls” into separate management units at an intermediate hierarchy level.
*	Confirmation of: 

	*	Company code to be used for the new non‑payroll company
	*	Company naming convention

*	Whether any additional reference data beyond the Colleges/Halls split is required.
*	Which organisational roles and access permissions must be replicated for the new company.
*	Confirmation of which users require Portal access to structure and reference data in live.

________________________________

5. Action Items

*	Action: Confirm reference data changes, specifically whether to split Colleges vs Halls at management unit level
	Owner: Client team (Anne / Kevin / Nathan / stakeholders)
	Due date: Not specified (implied: next project step)
*	Action: Confirm new company code and naming convention
	Owner: Client team
	Due date: Not specified (required before company setup)
*	Action: Provide list of available company codes - Attached
	Owner: Conor O’Brien
	Due date: Post‑call
*	Action: Send menu profile / Portal access items required for Structure Setup and Reference Data – Attached(You can just import this menu profile into LIVE which should allow you to see the Structure in full)
	Owner: Conor O’Brien
	Due date: Post‑call
*	Action: Review and update user access so required users can see Por

emails.subject: Bittersweet farewell, with a heart full of gratitude
emails.body:
Dear lovely team,

I can’t quite believe this is my last day—definitely a mix of emotions! It’s really hard to say goodbye to such an amazing group of people.

It’s been an absolute pleasure working here. Thank you for all your patience, guidance and coaching along the way—you are all so knowledgeable and always willing to share, which has helped me grow so much. I’ve always felt incredibly supported—I’ll never forget how, whenever first line needed help, so many of you reached out with kind messages, checked in on my workload and wellbeing, and offered a helping hand. It always made me feel so warm. Honestly, this is the best team I’ve ever been part of in my entire working life.

There have been so many memorable moments, but what I’ll treasure most are the friendships we’ve built—that’s truly the biggest reward for me.

Thank you all for the incredibly generous gifts and for every heartfelt word on the Kudoboard—I’m truly touched.

I’ll really miss you all, but I’m sure our paths will cross again. Please do count me in for any gatherings! 😊

Take care!

Love,

Otter

 

 

From: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 23 April 2026 16:59
To: Otter Ho <otter.ho@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: A message from the Team

 

https://www.kudoboard.com/boards/piLd54ow#view

Subject: Re: Implementation of EPLOY

 

Hi Marie,

 

Simon and Julie have covered the key roles. Based on what we already know about the Eploy/PeopleXD integration, I'd flag a few additions:

 

*	Middleware / Integration development — prior scoping confirmed that Eploy would not develop or host the middleware required to integrate with PeopleXD, meaning external development resource would be needed. However, given that Access Group has since acquired Eploy, it's worth clarifying whether this is now something AG would handle as part of a supported native integration.
*	Information Security / Compliance — the middleware setup will require TPIAs, DPIAs and a SaaS PCSC assessment, so some dedicated compliance input will be needed
*	Integration Tester — end-to-end testing of the middleware and PeopleXD interaction is a distinct piece of work, separate from the general UAT testing Julie has flagged

 

Best,

Kevin

emails.subject: DTP1092 College Staff in PXD - Meeting with Conor Notes - 24th Apr-26
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Nathan Kirwan; Kevin Lelitte; Simon Burford; Nicholas Chandler; Steve McBrearty
emails.body:
Dear All – please see below the synopsis of the call with Conor:

 

1. Executive Summary 

*	The preferred approach is to incorporate the new non‑payroll company into the existing organisational structure, rather than creating a separate structure.
*	This approach simplifies reporting, security, configuration effort, and use of existing reference data.
*	A new company (e.g. Company 80) will be created within the current structure to hold college/non‑payroll records.
*	Single Sign-On (SSO) and HR self‑service access for individuals with multiple records (company 10 and company 80) remains the key unresolved complexity.
*	Further internal (Oxford-specific) discussion is required on authentication, access precedence, and data maintenance processes.

________________________________

2. Key Discussion Themes and Decisions Made

Organisational Structure Options

*	Two options were assessed: 

	*	Creating a new separate organisational structure
	*	Incorporating a new non‑payroll company into the existing structure

*	Decision direction: 

	*	Strong consensus that incorporation into the existing structure is the best option.
	*	Separate structures introduce complexity for: 

		*	Staff with multiple concurrent appointments
		*	Reporting and analytics
		*	EMS emails, org charts, and manager relationships

Reporting and Analytics

*	Unified structure allows: 

	*	Single, consolidated reporting (Insights / People Analytics)
	*	Avoidance of structure‑by‑structure reporting

*	Org chart functionality only works against one structure, reinforcing the need to stay within the existing one.

Company Setup

*	A new company code (illustratively “80”) will be created: 

	*	Used for college / non‑payroll / REF‑related data
	*	Not treated as part of Company 10 (University payroll entity)

*	Existing reference data (job titles, structure levels, cost centres, etc.) can be reused.

Employee Records & Duplication

*	Agreement that: 

	*	No duplicates within the same company
	*	Duplicates across companies (10 and 80) are acceptable and sometimes required

*	Separate employee numbers must be used for separate records to avoid payroll/HMRC conflicts.

HR Self‑Service & SSO

*	A major discussion point: 

	*	Individuals may have records in both company 10 and company 80.
	*	SSO can only authenticate to one record at a time.
	*	Company 10 (payroll) access must always take precedence.

*	No out‑of‑the‑box mechanism identified to automatically force this precedence.

Manager Relationships

*	“Reports to manager” configuration is global, not company‑specific.
*	Use of workgroups/workgroup managers would apply equally to the new company.

________________________________

3. Risks, Issues, or Open Questions Identified

*	Single Sign‑On limitations: 

	*	Users cannot easily access two records via one SSO account.
	*	Risk of users being authenticated into the “wrong” record.

*	HR self‑service access for Company 80‑only individuals: 

	*	Must allow access for EDI / protected characteristics data collection.

*	Data maintenance effort: 

	*	Manual identification of individuals who already exist in Company 10.

*	Automatic precedence logic: 

	*	No confirmed way to make Company 10 automatically “win” over Company 80 without manual intervention.

*	Email address constraint: 

	*	Same email address cannot exist on two XD records, causing linkage challenges.

________________________________

4. Action Items

Action Description

Owner

Deadline

Provide a written synopsis of the discussion and recommendations

Conor O’Brien

Complete

Internally review SSO and authentication behaviour for users with records in both company 10 and company 80

Oxford HR / IT (Simon, Anne, team)

Not specified

Determine operational process for switching HR self‑service access when individuals move between companies

Oxford HR / IT

Not specified

Consider use o

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11675188: WFM - TUPE Balance - Plymouth University
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

 

I asked Engineering for clarification and they responded:

 

I have been in contact with Helen Burton who was involved in the transfer from Plymouth:

 

The Department will honour the 35-day annual leave and 6 closure days that they have at the University of Plymouth.

 

Whilst transferring staff will retain their existing contractual annual leave entitlements as above, the fixed closure dates during which staff are required to use some of this annual leave for the Department of Engineering will differ from any fixed closure days at the University of Plymouth. The dates of the fixed closure days are available at  https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/fixed-closure-dates <https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/fixed-closure-dates> . These include 5 days at Christmas and 1 day at Easter.

 

So, 35 days of annual leave plus 6 closure days, and, in addition, the bank holidays. Plymouth staff follow the engineering fixed closed days.

 

Have a super sunny weekend!

 

I had a look and it seems their department follows 24Days+8BH+5Xmas+1MThur like UAS.

 

I hope you indeed do have a sunny weekend 😊

 

Best wishes,

Beth

 

Beth Gray

HR Systems Support Team

A-Z of user guides <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/a-z-hr-systems-guidance.aspx>  | Workforce Management resources <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/workforce-management-resources-hr-staff-managers.aspx> 

 

We aim to respond within two working days

Times may vary where we have a reduced team. Queries are handled in order via our ticketing system, with payroll-related issues prioritised near cut-off dates. To avoid delays, please refrain from sending follow-ups, as these create duplicate tickets. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

 

HR Systems |People Department

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx>  | Contact the team <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/contact-details.aspx> 

 

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 24 April 2026 09:31
To: Beth Gray <beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Support Call# 11675188: WFM - TUPE Balance - Plymouth University

 

Hi Beth,

 

Please can I get a sense‑check on the TUPE holiday entitlements for the Plymouth staff, particularly regarding fixed closure days.

 

My understanding from the information copied in below is that Plymouth provided 3 additional fixed‑closure days, not 6. I appreciate that contractual terms may differ for the individuals transferring, and we will of course set them up according to the agreements in place. I just want to ensure I am working from the correct baseline information.

 

If the entitlement is 6 closure days, could you confirm that these are:

 

•             5 Christmas closure days – ordinarily 2 days before Christmas and 3 days between Christmas and New Year

•             1 Maundy Thursday

 

(noting that for the 2025 leave year, in Oxford one of the pre‑Christmas days was moved to Friday 2 January as a one‑off adjustment)

 

Please can you confirm whether the above is correct so we can ensure the setup is accurate from the outset.

 



 

Best wishes,

Michael

emails.subject: RE: Implementation of EPLOY
emails.to: Kevin Lelitte; Julie Hickman; Simon Burford; Sarah Rowles; Athena Artuso; Michelle Williams
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

 

Can I check this ‘prior scoping re Middleware’, is this post purchase of Eploy by Access Group? They have successfully integrated PXD with Evo, which will be the platform all future modules will connect.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 24 April 2026 11:26
To: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: Implementation of EPLOY

 

Hi Marie,

 

Simon and Julie have covered the key roles. Based on what we already know about the Eploy/PeopleXD integration, I'd flag a few additions:

 

*	Middleware / Integration development — prior scoping confirmed that Eploy would not develop or host the middleware required to integrate with PeopleXD, meaning external development resource would be needed. However, given that Access Group has since acquired Eploy, it's worth clarifying whether this is now something AG would handle as part of a supported native integration.

*	Information Security / Compliance — the middleware setup will require TPIAs, DPIAs and a SaaS PCSC assessment, so some dedicated compliance input will be needed

*	Integration Tester — end-to-end testing of the middleware and PeopleXD interaction is a distinct piece of work, separate from the general UAT testing Julie has flagged

 

Best, 

Kevin

 

 

________________________________

From: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: Thursday, April 23, 2026 8:19 PM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Implementation of EPLOY 

 

Hello Marie,

 

In addition to what Simon has already covered, we will need a dedicated tester later in the project to ensure that all scenarios supported by the current system works as expected in the new system.

 

Thank you for including training and comms, this often gets overlooked!

 

Many thanks

Julie

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: 23 April 2026 16:49
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Implementation of EPLOY

 

Hi Marie,

 

We would effectively be putting in a new recruitment system – so thinking back to when we first implemented the current system we probably also need to ask for:

 

*	Project Manager or Scrum Master
*	Business Analyst (unless we’re expecting implementation officers to cover this role)
*	BI Developer (We will need to review all of our recruitment reports as well as all of the dashboards)
*	Data and Integrations Architect (there will be an impact to integrations and things like the link to the website as well as needing to map the data going into the data warehouse and downstream into systems such as Cority – probably
