emails.body:
Morning all,

Please find below the agenda for next week’s workshop on Sustaining Leave & Absence Adoption. I’m looking forward to seeing you all online.

We’ll be using the following Miro board during the session:
https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGvJIqfs=/

If you have some time ahead of the workshop, it would be great if you could start adding your thoughts to the “What’s working well?” and “What’s not working well” sections. I’ve already included a few initial ideas from the lessons learned session to get things started.

When adding your ideas, please keep the focus on sustaining the adoption of leave and absence, rather than reflecting on what has or hasn’t worked well within the project itself.

We’ll also spend time building on this together during the workshop.

Thanks everyone, and see you next week.

 

Time

Item

Who?

14:00 – 14:05

Introduction 

 

Lyndsey Middleton

14:05 – 14:15

Context & Alignment

Marie Cooksey

14:15 - 14:20

Lyndsey Middleton

ALL

ALL

14:35 – 14:50

ALL

ALL

Discussion, ownership & next steps

 

Rachel Midgely 

ALL

15:25 – 15:30

Lyndsey Middleton

Susan considers it but doesn’t rely on it, if that makes sense. I have just been informed that there is currently an issue that is affecting our imaging system, this issue has been escalated to our technical team who are currently investigating the problem.

 

Unfortunately, this does mean that there may be a delay getting your device back to you, but we will do I best to meet your 12:30 deadline, but it may be worth considering retaining the loan laptop for a day or two, to give us the time to clone your original drive to the new drive.

emails.subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Hi Michael,

 

I made sure the Insight report was refreshing data, re-extracted the data and compared to the records for deletion file again:

 

Insight:



 

Extracted UOXU data comments and count:



 

Deletion list comments and counts:



 

So now I can see that all the records for deletion no longer appear, and this is confirmed by the data extracted from Insight showing nulls for all the records for deletion:



 

So I’m not sure what happened yesterday, it might have been something with my laptop since I had that weird excel error I’ve not experienced with appointment id before and I had other laptop issues too, or the Insight report needed refreshing (I thought I had done this!). Anyway, it’s all good now and I’ve set the report to refresh on every change to avoid this happening again.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

 

From: Sarah Rowles 
Sent: 23 April 2026 14:24
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Michael,

 

To confirm the Alteryx workflow wasn’t caching anything because I only created it from scratch when excel was having issues with the datatype of appointment id in the lookup calculation comparing the extract from Insight against the original list for deletion. 

 

I will have another go using a fresh extract from Insight and a new Alteryx workflow, hopefully the records are gone!

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 23 April 2026 14:00
To: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: FW: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

Hi Sarah,

 

Following our conversations in the office yesterday regarding the HESA LEVEL and SOC UDFs, I thought it would be helpful to follow up on this email chain used when escalating the case with AG, so that Simon/Kevin are also updated at the same time.  

 

Background to bring everyone up to speed:

AG successfully ran the script against one test employee record from our list. We then agreed for the full bulk script to be run in UOXU, which AG completed on Tuesday evening.

 

When reviewing the Insight report that Sarah created, the deleted records were not returned, which aligns with what AG expected. However, when Sarah ran the same dataset through Alteryx, the records that should have been deleted were still appearing.

 

I went back to AG to query this, but they remain confident that the data has been deleted, as the same script was used and Insight is no longer returning the records. This makes the situation a little awkward, as Insight does appear to support their position.

 

It has been suggested that Alteryx may be returning cached data, as it is known to cache inputs silently. To rule this out, could we try clearing all Alteryx caches and re‑running the workflow using a fresh link to the latest Insight return?

 

The recommended steps are:

•             Right‑click the Input tool → Clear Cache

•             Disable any Cache and Run settings

•             Check whether any temporary yxdb files are being referenced

•             Re‑run the workflow from scratch with caching disabled, ensuring it is pointing to the latest Insight data.

 

 

This may help us confirm whether the discrepancy is due to cached data rather than an issue with the deletion itself.

 

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

emails.subject: RE: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

 

Susan considers it but doesn’t rely on it, if that makes sense. She combines people on this status with people on the offer accepted statuses, so there would be no impact if we prevented people from moving applicants from the offer accepted status onto the successfully hired status, as they would remain in the same bucket that Susan uses.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 4:03 PM
To: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status 

 

Hi all,

 

Could I request that you look into de-activating the “Applicant Successfully Hired” applicant status in PeopleXD?

 

From a very quick look it seems that we can untick the “Active” indicator against this status and this prevents the status from being selectable, but doesn’t seem to error when you view the applicant or anything. However, full testing would be needed, I’m not sure if we have a knowledge article outlining removing applicant statuses?

 

The reason for this is that I have found that departments are fairly regularly using this status but when they do so it removes one of the flags in the background of the database which indicates that the applicant should be retained. When I spoke to the access group recently they asked why we had that status active if it causes us issues. I think it was introduced during an upgrade but I don’t think we have ever asked people to use it and definitely didn’t identify any impact until now, so I’d like to remove it before we switch on the applicant cleardown process.

 

Hopefully it won’t be a large amount of work. I will mention it to Michelle when we talk about applicant cleardown

emails.subject: RE: Request for funding for Cority
emails.body:
Hi Chris,

 

I couldn’t find anything specific either, but I do remember them sharing a link to their page in the Teams chat (https://www.twilio.com/en-us/sms/pricing/gb). I’ve had a quick look, and it appears Twilio works on a pay‑as‑you‑go model, with no contracts, so we only pay for the number of outbound messages we send.

 

There are a few variables (for example, message length and whether two‑way responses are required, such as Yes/No replies), but as a rough estimate, for around 800 messages per month we would be looking at just over 500 USD per year.

 

We would also need to complete a TPSA (Third‑Party Security Assessment) before implementing this alongside our system.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting>

From: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 23 April 2026 11:35
To: Brian Jenkins <brian.jenkins@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:brian.jenkins@safety.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk> >; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: FW: Request for funding for Cority

 

Hi Brian,

 

I’ve just had a chat with Chris so I can better understand the issues he raised as points 1-4 in his document.  

 

In summary, there is a genuine need for Cority to develop some solutions and paying them £4,100/£4,500 seems like a good investment.

 

1. Essential.  Cority comes with some ‘out-of-the-box’ business rules over which we have no editing control.  We have created many business rules of our own, some of which then get over-ridden by the Cority rules. We need some local rules to cover Oxford scenarios eg 2 jobs with 2 different parts of the University.  Cority needs to identify a ‘primary’ role to which all future medical records are attached, and we are unable to do this for all clients.  This could affect reports we need to produce eg surveillance compliance.  

 

2. Essential.  Where this is most needed is for clients who are registered in multiple SEGs for surveillance eg respiratory and dermal.  Annual review for respiratory may be now, and for dermal it may be in 6 months.  When we load the certificate for respiratory now, the system assumes it applies to dermal as well and we miss the review in 6 months.

 

3. This will be a time saving for t

  <https://www.credly.com/badges/3e879e66-f8ce-4137-b2d6-846a0ad552af/public_url>     <https://www.credly.com/badges/6a28598f-a6f1-46d2-9f7f-43275b13fca7/public_url> 

 

From: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 23 April 2026 11:35
To: Brian Jenkins <brian.jenkins@safety.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Request for funding for Cority

 

Hi Brian,

 

I’ve just had a chat with Chris so I can better understand the issues he raised as points 1-4 in his document.  

 

In summary, there is a genuine need for Cority to develop some solutions and paying them £4,100/£4,500 seems like a good investment.

 

1. Essential.  Cority comes with some ‘out-of-the-box’ business rules over which we have no editing control.  We have created many business rules of our own, some of which then get over-ridden by the Cority rules. We need some local rules to cover Oxford scenarios eg 2 jobs with 2 different parts of the University.  Cority needs to identify a ‘primary’ role to which all future medical records are attached, and we are unable to do this for all clients.  This could affect reports we need to produce eg surveillance compliance.  

 

2. Essential.  Where this is most needed is for clients who are registered in multiple SEGs for surveillance eg respiratory and dermal.  Annual review for respiratory may be now, and for dermal it may be in 6 months.  When we load the certificate for respiratory now, the system assumes it applies to dermal as well and we miss the review in 6 months.

 

3. This will be a time saving for the administration team – currently booking an appointment in Cority, then having to send out an email with a Teams link for most of the OHA/OHP appointments.  Cority can create a rule that allows 90 minutes for a management referral in the Cority diary (60 minutes consultation, 30 minutes to write up), and creates a 60 minute appointment in Teams.  Less essential than 1 and 2, but nice to have and if we have to pay for a minimum number of hours development work then we’ll take this.

 

4. We have introduced additional measures to improve our DNA rate.  We have a 72-hr email reminder in addition to the original 24-hr reminder, and for our OHP appointments we also make a phone call the day before the appointment.  As with 3, if there is time within the 20 hours we’re paying for it would be good to have the SMS option, although we need to consider the cost of annual subscription as this service is provided by a 3rd party.  It would be ok if it were hundreds, maybe not worth paying thousands for.  Chris S will make enquiries about this.  We’re confident that Cority and Twilio will have a DP agreement that would be appropriate, obviously this would need to be verified.

 

Happy to chat if you want more information.

 

Rob 

 

From: Brian Jenkins <brian.jenkins@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:brian.jenkins@safety.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 20 April 2026 09:36
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk> >

emails.subject: Potential Applicant Retention changes
emails.body:
Hi all

 

It has been flagged that there may be a forthcoming change to the retention period of applicant data driven by recent changes to the Employment Rights Act (ERA).

 

Good news: Policy agreed with our request to amend retention period for applicant data from 6-months to 12-months…yay!  But…LSO have since advised that there may be a requirement to review this , due to changes in the length of time for tribunal claim to be raised from 6-12 months.  

 

The ERA will extend the time limit for making an employment tribunal claim from 3 to 6 months.  Together with the elongated period for ACAS early conciliation (extended to 12 weeks in December 2025), this means that a candidate could be issuing a claim 9 months after the conclusion of the applicant process they took part in.  To add to this, the claim could sit in the Employment Tribunal for a number of months before being served on the University. It is unclear if the consideration of offering 14-15 months is driven internally, Oxford being cautious, or if this a new requirement. Kathryn FJ is engaging with LSO for more detail and I will speak to James Bristow Head of DP, for his take on this (he often is the voice of reason on these matters).

 

So, as discussed today we should reign back our engagement campaign until we have better understanding of what the actual ask is for the system and the departments. 

 

Question:

1. If we need to extend retention period of applicant data is that possible to manage locally or do we need to ask AG? – Simon has advised this is an AG activity.

2. Can we reach out to Access Group to find out if they can adjust or add an additional option of retention, say 15 or 18-months on the back of the ERA. Note: this can initially be raised at monthly UOX/AG meeting

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

 

Marie Cooksey

Head of HR Systems

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx>  | Contact the team <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/contact-details.aspx> 

 

 



I may send emails outside of your usual working hours. Please be assured there is no expectation for you to respond to this outside of your own working hours.

Sent: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 4:03 PM
To: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status 

 

Hi all,

 

Could I request that you look into de-activating the “Applicant Successfully Hired” applicant status in PeopleXD?

 

From a very quick look it seems that we can untick the “Active” indicator against this status and this prevents the status from being selectable, but doesn’t seem to error when you view the applicant or anything. However, full testing would be needed, I’m not sure if we have a knowledge article outlining removing applicant statuses?

 

The reason for this is that I have found that departments are fairly regularly using this status but when they do so it removes one of the flags in the background of the database which indicates that the applicant should be retained. When I spoke to the access group recently they asked why we had that status active if it causes us issues. I think it was introduced during an upgrade but I don’t think we have ever asked people to use it and definitely didn’t identify any impact until now, so I’d like to remove it before we switch on the applicant cleardown process.

 

Hopefully it won’t be a large amount of work. I will mention it to Michelle when we talk about applicant cleardown go live comms as it would be good to line these pieces of work up.

 

Please keep me in the loop on who’s going to take this forward.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

emails.subject: Applicant Successfully Hired Applicant Status
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

Could I request that you look into de-activating the “Applicant Successfully Hired” applicant status in PeopleXD?

 

From a very quick look it seems that we can untick the “Active” indicator against this status and this prevents the status from being selectable, but doesn’t seem to error when you view the applicant or anything. However, full testing would be needed, I’m not sure if we have a knowledge article outlining removing applicant statuses?

 

The reason for this is that I have found that departments are fairly regularly using this status but when they do so it removes one of the flags in the background of the database which indicates that the applicant should be retained. When I spoke to the access group recently they asked why we had that status active if it causes us issues. I think it was introduced during an upgrade but I don’t think we have ever asked people to use it and definitely didn’t identify any impact until now, so I’d like to remove it before we switch on the applicant cleardown process.

 

Hopefully it won’t be a large amount of work. I will mention it to Michelle when we talk about applicant cleardown go live comms as it would be good to line these pieces of work up.

 

Please keep me in the loop on who’s going to take this forward.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Emma Fitz-Gibbon <emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Applicant support inbox cover plans going forward 
 

Hello all,

 

With Otter’s leaving date quickly approaching, I am reaching out to ask for support for Emma and Beth. 

 

I have caught up with Marie who supports the idea that the Applicant inbox is primarily covered by non-Support team members, with Emma and Beth covering the remaining gaps. As things stand, we have no cover for the applicant inbox next week and this is likely to be one of the first areas impacted by the current reduction in resource.

 

Please can I ask that where your team can cover the Applicant inbox they update the rota <https://loop.cloud.> . 

 

I will continue to process all training checks for user access and cover the main support inbox (mainly the WFM queries).

 

Any help your teams can offer will be incredibly appreciated, but I’m fully aware everyone is stretched. Can I also add that Ant and Henry have been so supportive during this whole period, we do appreciate them.

 

Many thanks

Julie

 

Julie Hickman

HR Systems Trainer

emails.subject: FW: Cority Known Issues Page - Phase 1 Launch April 27, 2026
emails.body:
Forwarding in case you didn’t receive this

 

Kind Regards,

 

Chris Sanders

Business Change Analyst

HR Systems <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx>  | People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

  

    <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>  <https://oxforduni-remoteforms.info-exchange.com/Incident> 

  <https://www.credly.com/badges/3e879e66-f8ce-4137-b2d6-846a0ad552af/public_url>     <https://www.credly.com/badges/6a28598f-a6f1-46d2-9f7f-43275b13fca7/public_url> 

 

From: Andre Russo <Andre.Russo@cority.com> 
Sent: 21 April 2026 22:46
To: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Cority Known Issues Page - Phase 1 Launch April 27, 2026

 

Dear Chris Sanders,

You are receiving this email because you have been designated as a CorityOne solution admin responsible for submitting customer support tickets.

We are excited to announce the Phase 1 launch of the Cority Known Issues page on the Cority Community on April 27, 2026!  This page will serve as a central location for information on known issues the Cority Technical and Support teams are addressing, and that may affect your organization.

The Cority Known Issues page will help you manage your CorityOne solution more effectively and keep your users informed.  This new resource will enable self-service access, reducing the need to contact the Cority Customer Support Team for status updates.

The Cority Known Issues page will be managed by the Customer Support team and will include the following:

*	A list of active known issues related to the Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) application   
*	The option to follow the known issues and receive timely email notifications when the issues are updated
*	Easy filtering by Component and Status 
*	A handy search function for the Known Issues ID and Issue
*	A personalized “My Known Issues” for quick access to the known issues you’re following

 

FAQs

1.	How do I access the Known Issues page?

Simply log in to the Cority Community, click on the “Contact Support” option and then select “Known Issues”.

 

2.	How do I view the details of a Known Issue?

On the Known Issue list, locate the Known Issue you’re curious about and click on its Known Issue ID.

 

3.	How do I follow a Known Issue?

On the Known Issue list, click on the Known Issue ID to display the details.  Then, click on the “Follow” button at the bottom of the details screen.  

 

4.	How do I view all the Known Issues I am following?

Simply click on the “My Known Issues” checkbox located above the list on the right side.

 

5.	Do I need to submit a support ticket for the Known Issue I am following?

No, submitting a support ticket for a Known Issue you are following is not necessary unless you have a specific question related to it.  By following the Known Issue, you indicate that your organization is affected by it.

 

6.	Will the list contain Known Issues related to other CorityOne solutions?

For Phase 1, the Known Issue list will contain only CoreEHS+ (GX2)-related known issues.  In Phase 2, we will add Known Issues related to all other CorityOne solutions.

 

7.	I am a Cority admin but don’t have access to the Cority Community. How do I get access?

Ask a fellow Cority admin within your organization to submit a request on your behalf to provide you with access to the Cority Community and the ability to submit support tickets.  They can do this via a support ticket or by emailing community@cority.com <mailto:community@cority.com> .

 

Thank you for your attention!

 

Andre Russo

Sr. Manager, Customer Support Operations

W cority.com <http://www.cority.com/>

We urgently need an update on the outstanding request to bulk delete the SOC and LVEL HESA module UDFs.

 

The case was raised on 13 February, and on 14 April we were advised that Development were ready to proceed, starting with a single test deletion before completing the full bulk deletion. emails.subject: Re: Bi-monthly CDR/PD working group
emails.body:
Hi Lindsey,

Thank you for sharing those use cases — they're really helpful in understanding what you're looking to achieve.

I can confirm that automated reminder functionality is available within the Insight module via a feature called iCues. In principle, it would be possible to build reminders along the lines you've described, provided the relevant CDR data is in Insight in a way that can drive the triggers.

That said, this would need to be raised as a formal development request with Access Group, so it can be properly scoped and prioritised alongside our other commitments — my team doesn't currently have the capacity to take it on informally.

Happy to discuss further if that would be useful.

Best, 
Kevin


________________________________

From: Lindsey Spriggs <lindsey.spriggs@socsci.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 15 April 2026 1:41 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mary Muers <mary.muers@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Bi-monthly CDR/PD working group
 

Hi Kevin

 

Thank you for following up on this.

 

In theory, it would be things such as:

 

*	Reminder that a CDR needs to be scheduled (reviewee/reviewer/HR)
*	Reminder that the CDR form needs to be completed (reviewee)
*	Reminder that a CDR form needs to be signed off (reviewee/reviewer/HR)

 

I hope that makes sense – I actually don’t have access to the CDR functionality in My Development, so I can only describe the requirements in the abstract.

 

Best wishes

 

Lindsey

Hi Lindsey,

 

Apologies for the delay on this. I've reached out to the Access Group to find out whether automated reminder functionality can be enabled within the Insight module — I'll come back to you as soon as I hear from them.

 

In the meantime, it would be really helpful if you could share any examples or use cases you have in mind, so I can make sure I'm asking the right questions.

 

Best,

Kevin

________________________________

emails.subject: Outstanding Flexpoints Activities – Payroll Management Training & Payroll Module Health Check
emails.body:
Hi Amanda,

I hope you're well.

I'm writing to follow up on two outstanding items from our agreed Flexpoints package that we are keen to progress as a priority:


1.	
	Payroll Module Health Check (Case No: 67285209 – 2,450 pts) Following our initial Process Review session on 20 October 2025, a follow-up review meeting is still required to complete the review, formalise the findings, confirm conclusions on current behaviour versus best practice, and agree any recommendations or next steps.


2.	
	Payroll Management Training (Case No: 67285342 – 1,050 pts) This training has not yet been scheduled or delivered. We would welcome an initial call to discuss scope and agree dates at your earliest convenience.


When we last spoke, you mentioned being very busy with the Irish payroll - However, both of these items have been outstanding for some time now and we would really like to get them moving.

Could you please let me know your availability over the coming weeks so we can get both sessions in the diary?

I have copied in Mike West for awareness.

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

emails.subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Good Morning, Michael,

 

I hope you are keeping well.

 

Myself, Mike, and Maura (who is currently assigned to the ticket) have all been actively following up on this for you this morning, and throughout the past week. We fully appreciate the time sensitivity involved.

 

The matter has now been escalated to the relevant leadership within Development, and we are hopeful to have a further update shortly.

 

I will provide an update via this thread, and Maura will also ensure the case is updated accordingly.

 

Kind regards,
Sarah

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 21 April 2026 07:22
To: Sarah Murphy <sarah.murphy@theaccessgroup.com>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

⚠️ External Email Warning ⚠️

This message is from an EXTERNAL source. Do not trust links or attachments. Verify sender authenticity before taking action.

Hi Sarah,

 

We urgently need an update on the outstanding request to bulk delete the SOC and LVEL HESA module UDFs.

 

The case was raised on 13 February, and on 14 April we were advised that Development were ready to proceed, starting with a single test deletion before completing the full bulk deletion. Since then, despite follow‑ups on 17 April, we have still not received any update on when the test deletion will take place.

 

This is now time‑critical. The HESA module will be updated with the 2026 legislation changes in software patch 28.0.149, which is scheduled to go into production on Wednesday evening. We need confirmation as soon as possible of when the test deletion will be carried out and when the full deletion will follow.

 

Please provide an immediate update on the status and planned timeline.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Some notes from our meeting – please note any actions:

 

*	Discussed alternative dates for an introductory meeting with Access Group – this is to align expectations and agree timelines. It is not a formal kick-off meeting. We agreed on the Morning Tuesday 31st March. Action: KL to arrange with Access Group -a two slot (I hour) meeting.

This information would most likely come from Access Group. Action: KL offered to approach Conor (Access Group) for information ahead of our meeting on 31st March.

With day one SSP and new day one rights for paternity leave and parental leave now in play, businesses really need to make sure their setup can actually stand up in practice. 

 

Luckily, we’ve created our ERA Readiness Checklist <https://www2.moorepay.co.uk/e3t/Ctc/DO+113/d30F6004/VWfKLT7d7Z1GW8YS40h2ZmMYBW5l3Nw35M-KZTN7jDJvK3dh8WW7lCdLW6lZ3mRV_b56F3F97W-W7j9F8P1JQhHjW254C9J5hgttcW7sN3db7pY5hcVFpFKq8dDlFXW7SkJQn5Ky5YMW44t7K23BYCYzW1qp5Fb9hhPzGW2p4_Zv6T-H4KW27Sqyr7jGF-_W4m96F-5z7MhxW2kjLB25VCYVcW3nHG798bR_TzW4flN_-2yd5G9W6_9JZz7r9YFlW6kyzpz2_97hKW5C2jfF2zyY9bW1XZD6Z4gD7RTW6VfYB23yr3whN7NRzGNKykWZW8WR02N7Z3sLgVRRxcF94CNWmW91wjSd8C7Y5qW38QGFh8Sxx02f4xJZhY04>  to help you do just that!

emails.subject: Re: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,

This is great, thank you — exactly what I needed. I'll incorporate it into the DPIA.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 4:47 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording 


Hi Kevin,

 

How is this?

 

Data quality is fundamental to the HESA Staff Return, as it underpins the funding, regulation, analysis, and public representation of both the University of Oxford and the UK higher education sector. Accurate staff data informs funding allocations, official statistics, sector league tables, regulatory oversight, and policy development, while also supporting institutional planning and benchmarking. Poor data quality can lead to misallocated resources, distorted metrics, flawed decision-making, and increased regulatory scrutiny, as well as reputational damage. As a core dataset linking to wider sector information, the integrity of the Staff Return is essential to ensuring that decisions across the sector are reliable, transparent, and defensible.

 

In REF 2029, HESA Staff data moves from a supporting dataset to a central mechanism: it determines the scale of submissions will serve as the sector’s authoritative record of research capacity. As a result, staff data accuracy and governance have a direct and material impact on institutional REF outcomes such as how institutions are funded, perceived, and strategically developed, with long-term consequences for research direction, workforce, and competitiveness.

 

Athena Swan accreditation helps institutions attract talent by providing externally validated evidence of a strong commitment to equality, diversity, and inclusion. It signals that robust policies, transparent processes, and targeted interventions are in place to support career progression and foster an inclusive working environment, enhancing institutional reputation and appeal to a diverse talent pool. Central to this is the effective use of high-quality staff data, which enables institutions to identify inequalities, monitor progress, and demonstrate measurable impact, reinforcing the credibility of their commitment.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

 

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 26 March 2026 15:57
To: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording

 

Hi Sarah,

 

I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and want to strengthen the section on why we process HR data.

 

Simon suggested linking data quality to strategic outcomes — specifically how accurate HR data supports things like HESA returns, REF submissions, and Athena Swan accreditation, and how these directly affect funding and our ability to attract talent.

 

Could you give me a couple of sentences I can include that ties this together? Something along the lines of how accurate data underpins external reporting which influences funding and institutional reputation.

 

Happy to discuss if easier.

 

Best,

Kevin

 

 

Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: Re: Freedom of Information requests - DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Athena,

This is really helpful, thank you — I'll incorporate it into the DPIA.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 1:55 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Freedom of Information requests - DPIA wording 


HI Kevin,

 

The short version:

 

The University is obliged to comply with an FOI request only if we already hold the information requested and are able to locate and retrieve it in less than 18 hours and for a cost of <= £450. In addition, there are exemptions from disclosure, which allow a public authority to withhold information in certain circumstances, usually where disclosure is likely to result in some form of harm, which would outweigh the public interest that is presumed to exist in favour of disclosure.

 

The full version:

Personal data held within the University’s HR system (PeopleXD) is subject to the Freedom of Information Act 2000 (FOIA). While the University is required to confirm whether requested information is held and disclose it where appropriate, the majority of HR data engages exemptions under FOIA, particularly where it constitutes personal data.

*	Section 40 (Personal Data) is most commonly applicable, as disclosure must comply with UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018; therefore, identifiable employee information (e.g. names, salaries, sickness records, or performance data) is typically exempt from disclosure unless it can be lawfully shared.
*	Other relevant exemptions may include Section 41 (Information Provided in Confidence), where disclosure would breach an obligation of confidence;
*	Section 36 (Prejudice to the Effective Conduct of Public Affairs), relating to internal deliberations and decision-making;
*	and Section 43 (Commercial Interests), where disclosure could prejudice the University’s commercial position.

Some exemptions are absolute (e.g. Sections 40 and 41), while others are qualified and require a public interest test. Where possible, the University may fulfil FOIA obligations through anonymised or aggregated data, or by applying redactions to protect sensitive information.

I hope this is helpful.

 

Best,

Athena

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 26 March 2026 15:51
To: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Freedom of Information requests - DPIA wording

 

Hi Athena,

 

I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and need to include a reference to data shared under Freedom of Information requests.

Currently the data sharing section mentions statutory bodies and regulatory requirements, but doesn't explicitly cover FOI.

 

Could you provide some standard wording I can include that covers:

*	How FOI applies to data held in HR systems
*	Any relevant exemptions (e.g. personal data protections)

 

Just a sentence or two would be fine — happy to discuss if easier.

 

Best,

Kevin

 

 

Kevin Lelitte

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

To answer your question, the role of HRBPs was discussed and confirmed across several meetings. HRBPs were agreed as the first point of contact for access-related requests, performing the initial review and approval before passing to the CI Squad for implementation. PeopleXD access is assumed as a prerequisite, with any queries raised via the existing route to HR Systems.

Regarding Super Users, this was raised briefly in the November 2025 meeting. The discussion clarified that a Super User is a technical system role for end-to-end testing and is not equivalent to an HRBP role. No agreement was reached on who Super Users would be or how the role would operate in this context.

The Superuser role is referenced in the product overview but has no corresponding entry in the support structure, enablement requirements, or routing model, unlike the other roles defined in this document.

I wanted to flag this for your awareness.

Kevin

emails.subject: RE: Support Call# 11673291: itss - problem with macros in Word
emails.body:
Thank you Michael,

 

In Emma’s absence I’ll go back to Andrew.

 

Thank you for the suggested text, I have updated it this morning.

 



 

Many thanks

Julie

 

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 16 April 2026 09:05
To: Emma Fitz-Gibbon <emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Support Call# 11673291: itss - problem with macros in Word

 

Morning Emma,

 

 

Here’s what I think is a suitable response to send back to the Andrew at PCHS, but I’ll leave you to read through and decide whats best.  

 

Thanks for the investigation. Office LTSC 2024 includes Microsoft’s newer macro security model, which blocks macros in files that have a Mark of the Web unless they are stored in a Trusted Location or digitally signed.

The previous guidance (“Disable all macros with notification”) is no longer sufficient for LTSC 2024.

Your workaround of creating a Trusted Location is the correct approach under the new security model. We will update our documentation to reflect this for users on LTSC 2024 or later.

 

 

 

@Julie Hickman <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – At the bottom of this email I have provided a suggested update for the Macro Settings in Word section of IT set-up information <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/information-for-local-it-officers.aspx> .  Could you please review and if happy make the changes.  

 

 

 

Background

 

The IT Officer is right, Office LTSC 2024 introduced stricter macro security, and the old guidance (“Disable all macros with notification”) is no longer enough for files downloaded from the internet or stored in untrusted locations.

This is why the workaround (trusted folder + forcing downloads to a chosen location) works, and why the older LTSC 2021 machine didn’t have the same issue.

 

What changed between Office LTSC 2021 and LTSC 2024L

 

Microsoft hardened macro security across all supported Office versions starting in 2022–2023. LTSC 2021 received some of these changes, but LTSC 2024 enforces them fully.

 

The key change:

 

Files downloaded from the internet (or received via email/Teams/SharePoint) are automatically blocked, even if Word is set to “Disable all macros with notification.”

 

Word now checks:

 

•             Whether the file has a Mark of the Web (MOTW)

•             Whether the file is stored in a Trusted Location

•             Whether the macro is digitally signed by a trusted publisher

 

If a file has MOTW and is not in a trusted location, Word will block macros outright — no prompt, no override.

 

This is exactly the behaviour the IT Officer observed.

 

Why the workaround works

 

The workaround they used is completely consistent with Microsoft’s new security model:

 

1. Adding a Trusted Location

Word will always allow macros from:

 

•             Trusted Locations

•             Trusted Publishers (digitally signed macros)

 

Since templates are not digitally signed, a trusted folder is the correct workaround.

 

2. Changing browser settings to “Ask where to save downloads”

If a user downloads a template directly into a trusted folder, the file:

 

•             Does not inherit MOTW

•             Or, if it does, Word still allows macros because the folder is trusted

 

This bypasses the new macro-blocking behaviour.

 

 

What this means for your PeopleXD templates

 

The current guidance “Disable all macros with notifications”…was correct for older versions of Office, but is no longer sufficient for LTSC 2024.

 

The tem

Hi Kevin,

 

Thanks for your message, all well here thanks for asking and hope the same for you.

 

Based on the latest information I have, I believe Maura has re‑engaged on this case and has picked it back up with Development. I appreciate the length of time this has been open and understand the urgency. We will continue to update the ticket as soon as we receive confirmation that the initial deletion has been completed by Development.

 

Kind regards,
Sarah

 

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 14 April 2026 10:19
To: Sarah Murphy <sarah.murphy@theaccessgroup.com>
Cc: Mike West <mike.west@theaccessgroup.com>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: #External# Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues

 

⚠️ External Email Warning ⚠️

This message is from an EXTERNAL source. Do not trust links or attachments. Verify sender authenticity before taking action.

Hi Sarah,

I hope you're well.

I am writing to escalate Case 68351079 (HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues), as we have not received any confirmed resolution.

This was raised by Michael O'Sullivan on 13 February 2026 and relates to two issues within the HESA Module. Data load updates with NULL values are being rejected for records not active in the current HESA return (2024/25), with errors stating the records do not exist in the current year, which is unexpected given the LEVELS UDF operates as a static repository. In addition, there is no clear method to bulk delete UDF entries via data load where the requirement is to remove rows entirely rather than overwrite with NULL.

The case has been followed up multiple times by Michael and progressed via Maura McGlynn, and I understand it has been raised with Development. However, there is no indication of progress or timeframe, and the issue remains unresolved as of today.

As this has now been ongoing for approximately two months, this is becoming urgent. Could you provide an update on the current status, and confirm what progress has been made with Development, including an expected timeframe for resolution?

Best,
Kevin

 

Kevin Lelitte

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: ORCIDs in PXD - 14th Apr 26 meeting notes
emails.to: Anne Mortimer; Eugenio Barrio; Kevin Lelitte; Simon Burford
emails.body:
Dear All

 

I just want to be absolutely sure that I have correctly understood what we agreed with regards referencing ORCID in PXD. Please can you confirm – is my understanding correct?

 

There are two areas to consider: onboarding and self-service. Currently instructions for ORCID appear only in onboarding and only to a select groups (researchers). There is currently no reference to ORCID (tile or task) in self-service.

It was accepted that capturing ORCIDs directly into PXD, either by free-text inputs or a dynamic connection to ORCID.org, would not be feasible, at least not in the immediate term. It was therefore agreed that the DTP1092 team would:

*	Review/update the current text appearing in the onboarding pages
*	Review/update the links (e.g. adding  https://register.it.ox.ac.uk/self/orcid)
*	Review/update the select groups – most likely expand the groups.

 

Making the changes would be relatively straight forward and is estimated to take 1-2 weeks.

 

Current Onboarding 

 

There is a tile visible to selected groups with instructions and links – see attached example.

 

Currently open to selected groups (are these the correct ones? This list comes from an earlier meeting we had on ORCIDs):

 

*	AC  Academic (teaching & research) 
*	AP  Research Staff (including E grades) 
*	AR  Research Fellow 
*	AS  Research Support 
*	AT  Teaching Support 
*	AO  Senior University Officer 

 

Current wording: 

If you carry out research and do not yet have an ORCID (unique research ID) [https://orcid.org] then please visit our ORCID webpage [https://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home] to get one and affiliate it to the University. An ORCID is important to prevent similar name confusion, meeting funder requirements and help us to identify your research outputs for our current research information system.

 

If you already have an ORCID but have not yet affiliated it to Oxford University please visit our ORCID at Oxford webpage [https://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home] to do so. This will verify your affiliation to Oxford at ORCID.org,

 

Current Links given:

 

ORCID:  http://orcid.org <http://orcid.org> 

ORCID Oxford Homepage: http://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home <http://orcid.web.ox.ac.uk/home> 

 

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

 

 

 

 

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

Crispin Muncaster
PROJECT MANAGER
IT Services | University of Oxford

T: 01865 Â 616324
www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: RE: Update
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

 

As discussed yesterday, please see my response below. I wanted to provide some additional context and an update of where we currently are with these items. 

 

Health Surveillance – Automated Emails

The Health Surveillance items listed are all related and sit within the same automated email configuration. This is an area of the system that was not available out‑of‑the‑box and was implemented using a significant amount of custom logic to meet the UOHS’ requirements.

 

To give an indication of the complexity involved:

*	We currently have around 44 business rules and 123 sub‑queries (reports) supporting the automated email functionality.
*	These rules cover 26 different SEG groups, each with their own conditions and email templates.
*	The logic needs to remain consistent across all SEGs, with the exception of Baseline and Recall SEGs, which adds another layer of complexity.
*	Even relatively small changes can have knock‑on effects across multiple business rules using the same sub-queries, so any updates require careful review, documentation, and thorough testing across all scenarios.

 

It’s also important to note that, while we have tried to cover the majority of scenarios in which a client should receive an automated email, there may be a very small number of scenarios that were not captured. In these instances, the email would not be sent and the business rule conditions would need to be reviewed and updated accordingly.

 

I’ve already shared an update with Rob and Karen via OSM regarding the SEG reminder emails. This work is currently being reviewed, updated, documented, and tested, with the aim of resolving the issues consistently rather than introducing further workarounds that could increase complexity or risk.

 

Spirometry

The Spirometry‑related points are helpful, as these issues were not previously known to us. As discussed yesterday, initial review suggests most of these are scenarios where the system is working as designed, with certain workflows and user interface behaviour being hard‑coded by Cority and not configurable.

 

For these items, the focus will likely be:

*	Reviewing and updating training materials
*	Agreeing consistent working practices
*	Walking through the workflows with the appropriate super‑users, to ensure expectations align with how the system operates

 

These are items we can discuss further at the next PUG.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting>

             <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>

emails.body:
Hi Anne,
Thank you for putting this together.
I’ve reviewed both proposals and would suggest proceeding with Proposal 1, as it follows the same structure as PACS.
I’ve noted Simon’s points on data warehouse code mapping and potential padding levels. If anyone has any further input before we close this, please let me know.
I noticed Mansfield and Harris Manchester College appear to be missing the L2 full name in the Proposal 1 sheet. Does this need to be corrected?
If there are no further comments, are you happy to take this forward directly with Conor?

Kevin

Best wishes

Anne

emails.subject: Escalation Request – Case 68351079 | HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I hope you're well.
I am writing to escalate Case 68351079 (HESA Module LEVELS UDF – Data Cleardown & Update Issues), as we have not received any confirmed resolution.
This was raised by Michael O'Sullivan on 13 February 2026 and relates to two issues within the HESA Module. Data load updates with NULL values are being rejected for records not active in the current HESA return (2024/25), with errors stating the records do not exist in the current year, which is unexpected given the LEVELS UDF operates as a static repository. In addition, there is no clear method to bulk delete UDF entries via data load where the requirement is to remove rows entirely rather than overwrite with NULL.
The case has been followed up multiple times by Michael and progressed via Maura McGlynn, and I understand it has been raised with Development. However, there is no indication of progress or timeframe, and the issue remains unresolved as of today.
As this has now been ongoing for approximately two months, this is becoming urgent. Could you provide an update on the current status, and confirm what progress has been made with Development, including an expected timeframe for resolution?
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: #External# Accepted: Data Migration Training
emails.body:

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com>
Sent: Monday, April 13, 2026 5:37 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Accepted: Data Migration Training 


Good Afternoon Kevin,

 

Thank you for taking the time to attend the Data Migration training session earlier today.

As a follow‑up, I have outlined below a summary of what we covered, along with recommended next steps for both yourselves and me.

 

________________________________

Session Summary

During the session, we covered the following areas in detail:

*	Data Migration overview

	*	Difference between core Data Migration, Employee Bulk Updates, and the Update Facility
	*	When each approach should be used

*	Dashboard configuration

	*	Where Data Migration and Bulk Update widgets can be displayed
	*	Options to place widgets on the Administrator and/or People Management dashboards

*	Security & menu profile access

	*	Required menu profile permissions
	*	Controlling access to:

		*	Specific templates
		*	Upload, edit, archive and clear‑down actions

	*	Risks associated with “Clear Down” permissions and recommended restrictions

*	Templates & helper files

	*	Importance of downloading both template and helper files
	*	How helpers explain:

		*	Mandatory vs optional fields
		*	Conditional logic
		*	Valid formats and reference values
		*	Where data exists within the system

*	Person File (Complete) walkthrough

	*	Mandatory data requirements
	*	HR‑only staff setup and use of a dummy pay group
	*	Handling reference data (code vs description behaviour)
	*	Dates, hierarchy, contract, and post requirements
	*	Best practice regarding leavers and start/end dates

*	Post Profile data migration

	*	Creating posts before loading people where required
	*	Differences between authorised, auto‑authorised, and auto‑commenced posts
	*	Using generic vs more tightly defined posts (pros and cons)

*	Hierarchy & reference data

	*	Population of hierarchy levels based on your structure
	*	Recommendation to have reference data in place prior to loading

*	Loading, validation & error handling

	*	File upload process and pre‑run validation screen
	*	Understanding partial successes vs failures
	*	Editing data in‑screen vs correcting offline and re‑uploading
	*	Downloading and reviewing error files

*	Data Migration Update Facility

	*	Making mass updates without creating new contract sequences
	*	Use‑cases for retrospective corrections

*	Employee Bulk Updates

	*	Bulk salary and allowance updates
	*	Creating custom bulk contract templates
	*	Current limitation around clearing down bulk update files

________________________________

Suggested Next Steps – UOX Actions

It would be helpful for you to consider and confirm the following:

*	Post strategy

	*	Whether posts should be generic (shared) or more specifically tied to hierarchy levels
	*	Which approach best supports your reporting needs

*	Hierarchy & reference data

	*	Review and confirm hierarchy levels to be used
	*	Ensure required reference data (job titles, categories, work groups, etc.) is in place before migration

*	Pay group approach

	*	Creation of new Pay Group is the agreed approach?

*	Testing

	*	Begin test uploads in the test environment to validate:

		*	Templates
		*	Reference data
		*	Post and person setup

	*	Note any issues or questions arising during testing

*	Further training

	*	Confirm whether follow‑up sessions are required (e.g. hierarchy or reference data setup) I have checked my calendar and have availability on the below times and dates:

 

Wednesday 22nd between 15:00 and 17:00

Thursday 23rd between 10:00 and 13:00

Friday 24th

Best wishes, 

 

James

 

From: Sonya Zyssemilch <sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 13 April 2026 09:12
To: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Spirometry - EasyOne Software

 

Dear James,

 

Please accept my apology for the delay in responses. I have been away from work for the last 3 weeks due to illness and holiday.

We need to have access to this information. I deem this to be an important part of a continuous programme carried out by each of us. We do have a shared folder on the G: drive, which should be visible to us all, but it would not show the history of each individual and tests to compare and contrast. I have copied Sharon in to ensure I am not stepping on her toes in conveying my opinion.

 

Kind Regards

 

Sonya Zyssemilch

Occupational Health Advisor

University of Oxford Occupational Health Department

10 Parks Road

Oxford OX1 3PD

Working Days: Monday-Wednesday

Email: sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 

Web: https://occupationalhealth.admin.ox.ac.uk/ <https://occupationalhealth.admin.ox.ac.uk/> 

 

 

     <https://staff.admin.ox.ac.uk/thriving-at-oxford> 

 

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 09 April 2026 15:14
To: Sonya Zyssemilch <sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Spirometry - EasyOne Software

 

Hi Sonya, 

 

I hope you had a good easter break. I wanted to check something with you regarding the EasyOne setup.

 

Could you let me know how important it is for multiple UOHS staff to be able to use the EasyOne software at the same time? I’m trying to understand whether there is a need for a central database to support this.

 

If each user has their own local copy of the database stored on their laptop, this can lead to a number of challenges, including GDPR considerations, data inconsistency, duplicate records, and a higher risk of errors. However, if a central database stored in a central location exists, how is this currently working? 

 

Your insight on how the team currently uses the software will really help us decide whether centralising the database using SharePoint Online is something we should look into.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 13 April 2026 12:08
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >

Subject: RE: Payroll Validation Failing_pay140

 

Hi Marie,

 

In terms of timelines, the supplementary payroll run is scheduled for 22 April and the final run for 23 April. To complete these successfully, Payroll will need to be running nightly validations in the lead‑up to both runs.

 

With that in mind, we ideally need the fix in place and verified no later than mid‑week. If we reach Wednesday 15 April without a confirmed resolution in UAT, the situation becomes more concerning, as it reduces the available time for testing and for Payroll to complete their normal pre‑run validation cycles.

 

I’ll continue to monitor progress closely and will escalate if we don’t see movement within that timeframe.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

emails.subject: FW: Spirometry - EasyOne Software
emails.body:
Response from Sonya below (regarding a centralised database for the EasyOne software) awaiting confirmation from Sharon.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James

 

From: Sonya Zyssemilch <sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 13 April 2026 09:12
To: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Sharon Naylor <sharon.naylor@uohs.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Spirometry - EasyOne Software

 

Dear James,

 

Please accept my apology for the delay in responses. I have been away from work for the last 3 weeks due to illness and holiday.

We need to have access to this information. I deem this to be an important part of a continuous programme carried out by each of us. We do have a shared folder on the G: drive, which should be visible to us all, but it would not show the history of each individual and tests to compare and contrast. I have copied Sharon in to ensure I am not stepping on her toes in conveying my opinion.

 

Kind Regards

 

Sonya Zyssemilch

Occupational Health Advisor

University of Oxford Occupational Health Department

10 Parks Road

Oxford OX1 3PD

Working Days: Monday-Wednesday

Email: sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk> 

Web: https://occupationalhealth.admin.ox.ac.uk/

 

 

     <https://staff.admin.ox.ac.uk/thriving-at-oxford> 

 

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 09 April 2026 15:14
To: Sonya Zyssemilch <sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sonya.zyssemilch@uohs.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Spirometry - EasyOne Software

 

Hi Sonya, 

 

I hope you had a good easter break. I wanted to check something with you regarding the EasyOne setup.

 

Could you let me know how important it is for multiple UOHS staff to be able to use the EasyOne software at the same time? I’m trying to understand whether there is a need for a central database to support this.

 

If each user has their own local copy of the database stored on their laptop, this can lead to a number of challenges, including GDPR considerations, data inconsistency, duplicate records, and a higher risk of errors. However, if a central database stored in a central location exists, how is this currently working? 

 

Your insight on how the team currently uses the software will really help us decide whether centralising the database using SharePoint Online is something we should look into.

 

Best wishes, 

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting>

             <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>

Marie

Subject: RE: Payroll Validation Failing_pay140

 

Hi Marie,

 

In terms of timelines, the supplementary payroll run is scheduled for 22 April and the final run for 23 April. To complete these successfully, Payroll will need to be running nightly validations in the lead‑up to both runs.

 

With that in mind, we ideally need the fix in place and verified no later than mid‑week. If we reach Wednesday 15 April without a confirmed resolution in UAT, the situation becomes more concerning, as it reduces the available time for testing and for Payroll to complete their normal pre‑run validation cycles.

 

I’ll continue to monitor progress closely and will escalate if we don’t see movement within that timeframe.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

Subject: RE: Payroll Validation Failing_pay140

 

We’ve received the latest update from Access Group’s development team regarding the ongoing pay140 payroll validation failure.

 

Access have confirmed that a recent change relating to output messages for tiered pension schemes is generating an unexpectedly high volume of background messages. Because Oxford has particularly large pay groups, this volume is exceeding system limits and causing payroll validation to fail.

 

To resolve this, Access will:

 

•             Deploy a fix to our UAT environment first so we can verify the solution

•             Once confirmed, deploy the same fix to LIVE

 

Their developers are actively working on this, and Access will provide a further update — including any hotfix scheduling — as soon as they have more information.

 

I’ll continue to keep everyone updated as this progresses.

 

Best wishes,

Michael

To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >

Subject: Payroll Validation Failing_pay140
Importance: High

 

Just a quick heads‑up:

 

Payroll Validation (pay140) has been failing since the end of last week with the following error:

 

ERROR at line 1: ORA‑20000: ORU‑10027: buffer overflow, limit of 1,000,000 bytes

ORA‑06512: at "COREHR.PAY300", line 9199

ORA‑06512: at "COREHR.PAY300", line 63756

ORA‑06512: at line 3

 

Access Group have confirmed this is a system bug and have raised a high‑priority internal development case (Ref: 2236415) to address it.

 

 

From the error detail, the issue appears to be caused by PAY300 generating more than 1MB of DBMS_OUTPUT text, which exceeds Oracle’s buffer limit. This is a system‑side issue within COREHR.PAY300, so it isn’t something we can’t resolve from the UI or through configuration. It requires a fix from Access/their development team.

 

 

I’ll keep you updated as soon as we hear more from Access.

 

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

Michael O’Sullivan

Senior Functional Analyst

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Hi Anne,

 

Thank you for this. From my point of view proposal 1 is workable and I can see the benefits of it. It would be good to get everyone’s opinion though.

 

We may need to assess the code mapping rules that we have set up in the data warehouse and may need to recode the level 2 codes slightly and we may find we need to bulk the structure out with ‘padding’ levels, depending on how much flexibility PeopleXD gives us, but I assume we’ll find out more as we engage with Access Group.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

Subject: RE: DTP1092 - How colleges are represented in the new entity

 

Hi All

Following our meeting this morning, we have a couple of proposals for the structure of colleges entity which try to mirror the PACS org structure as far as possible, but put the colleges at L3, so we would be reporting consistently at the same org structure level for the whole population. My preference is for proposal 1. Any further thoughts? I have also asked Katherine Corr, who administers the org structure in PACS, if this causes any issues. This, or course, assumes that we do not have to set each College up as a separate company within the multi-company structure.

Best wishes

Anne

emails.subject: Urgent request for an SQL report please to help identify balance code
emails.body:
Hi Kevin, Michael,

I am hoping someone from your team could help us please with an SQL report, it is quite urgent. 

We have three very large areas left to go live and we need to complete a balance adjustment for these areas to ensure their leave is correct for their staff from go live. We need to complete this within the next couple of weeks. 

In order to ease the manual piece of this work, and speed up the process, we wanted to know if it was possible/ you had capacity to create an SQL report that pulled the name, personnel ID, appt ID and balance code from PeopleXD for these depts (two of them are divisions).

We have reports that pull the balance description but then we need to manually match that to the balance matrix in order to get the balance code to use in the data upload files.

I have spoken to Simon who said this is possible using SQL developer and to reach out to you both to take forward. Simon is happy to advise if you need any assistance. 

Please let us know ASAP, if this is possible and when it could be achieved by. Your help is greatly appreciated to get these last depts over this final hurdle. 

Many thanks,

Anna



Anna Carter-Windle

Business Change Manager

Contractor of AL Training Solutions Ltd

 

Workforce Management resources for HR, staff and managers <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/workforce-management-resources-hr-staff-managers.aspx> 

 

Programme and Project Delivery Group

IT Services | University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices,  Oxford OX1 2JD

 

Please note my working hours typically are: 

Tuesday- Thursday 8am-5:30pm, 

Friday 8am-12:30pm

From: Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 09 April 2026 11:05
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Elena Jarii <elena.jarii@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Beth Gray <beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Proposed security model for annual leave tool

 

HI Simon,

 

My initial feeling is that if seeing a staff member’s total appointments in-year would give a workgroup manager a better understanding of how that staff member’s leave is calculated, then we should be giving managers access to that information. It would forestall potential questions if the information was already available, making the annual leave tool more useful in general. 

 

Is there a compelling reason to hide appointments in other work groups? The only thing I can think of is sensitive departments.

 

Best,

Athena

Sent: 09 April 2026 11:05

Subject: RE: Proposed security model for annual leave tool

 

HI Simon,

 

My initial feeling is that if seeing a staff member’s total appointments in-year would give a workgroup manager a better understanding of how that staff member’s leave is calculated, then we should be giving managers access to that information. It would forestall potential questions if the information was already available, making the annual leave tool more useful in general.

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 25 March 2026 10:23
To: Elena Jarii <elena.jarii@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:elena.jarii@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Beth Gray <beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk <mailto:beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Proposed security model for annual leave tool

 

Hi both,

 

Attached is the security model that I’m proposing we use to control access to data in the annual leave tool.

 

There’s just one question about what we want to do about manager access – we could restrict to appointments which exist within the manager’s workgroup, or we could give access to all appointments that the employee in their work group holds. 

 

The first obviously restricts the manager’s view to only see appointments held under their remit of responsibility, so I am tempted to say that’s all a manager would need to know, but we could also show details of all the appointments that the employee holds or has held within the current annual leave year, to give the manager more context on how their total leave is calculated.

 

I don’t really know if there’s going to be a scenario where option 2, giving access to all appointments, is necessary, but this is a decision which needs to be made. I’ve copied in the other managers in the hope that we can come to a firm consensus between us.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: RE: Proposed security model for annual leave tool
emails.to: Simon Burford; Elena Jarii; Beth Gray
emails.body:
HI Simon,

 

My initial feeling is that if seeing a staff member’s total appointments in-year would give a workgroup manager a better understanding of how that staff member’s leave is calculated, then we should be giving managers access to that information. It would forestall potential questions if the information was already available, making the annual leave tool more useful in general. 

 

Is there a compelling reason to hide appointments in other work groups? The only thing I can think of is sensitive departments.

 

Best,

Athena

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 25 March 2026 10:23
To: Elena Jarii <elena.jarii@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Beth Gray <beth.gray@biology.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Proposed security model for annual leave tool

 

Hi both,

 

Attached is the security model that I’m proposing we use to control access to data in the annual leave tool.

 

There’s just one question about what we want to do about manager access – we could restrict to appointments which exist within the manager’s workgroup, or we could give access to all appointments that the employee in their work group holds. 

 

The first obviously restricts the manager’s view to only see appointments held under their remit of responsibility, so I am tempted to say that’s all a manager would need to know, but we could also show details of all the appointments that the employee holds or has held within the current annual leave year, to give the manager more context on how their total leave is calculated.

 

I don’t really know if there’s going to be a scenario where option 2, giving access to all appointments, is necessary, but this is a decision which needs to be made. I’ve copied in the other managers in the hope that we can come to a firm consensus between us.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

From: David Hickman <david.hickman@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 10 February 2026 10:54
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Tony Boydell <tony.boydell@it.ox.ac.uk>; Erika Braverman <erika.braverman@it.ox.ac.uk>; Andrew Goff (IT Services) <andrew.goff@it.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.

 

Hi Kevin,

 

Unless we do something specifically as a manual step, the initial go live of the ESS SBI will not cause large scale data updates to provision or deprovision users.  SBIs that work in the way that the ESS one does effectively react to changes that are detected in the data that they are interested in, and these changes will be picked up either from the date of go live, or from a date close to it as agreed between ourselves.

 

The ESS SBI is only interested in changes to a staff members active appointments and the logic of the SBI will only deprovision records where they have no active appointments.  The combination of these two things will mean that the only* users that will be deprovisioned are those that have active appointments on the date when changes start to be picked up by the SBI, and then leave the University (meaning that they then have no active appointments).  If changes are made to the inactive appointments then these will not be sent to the SBI and the user will not be deprovisioned. 

 

*Note that there are two edge cases to this which would result in data on an existing “leaver” being sent to the SBI and causing the deprovisioning logic to run for that person.  Both of these are very rare but I thought that it would be sensible to mention for completeness:

* If the SSO for the record is changed or removed in People XD.

* Some automated error handling routines within the platform itself can cause updates to be sent to “all consumers” where the platform can no longer be completely sure of the consumers that should be informed.  This usually happens because of a data error that triggers a resend.  Typically there may be 1 or 2 records a month that would be sent to the SBI in this way, however because they are for records that are changing, they are most often active staff rather than those that have left.
