From: John Ireland <john.ireland@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 07 April 2026 09:30
To: Elliot Falvert-Martin <elliot.martin@dae.ox.ac.uk>; John Nicholas <john.nicholas@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Martin Kendall <martin.kendall@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marion Manton <marion.manton@ctl.ox.ac.uk>; Robin Stevens <robin.stevens@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Chris Mortlock <chris.mortlock@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Action required ahead of retirement of P:\PROJECTS

 

To: All IT Services and DGU staff; Heads of Central Application Support Teams and Information Security; Centre for Teaching and Learning (CTL) representatives

 

Action: 

*	Review any remaining use of P:\PROJECTS and copy / move required files to alternative service / project storage by May

*	Cascade this information within your team as appropriate, if they haven't received this directly

 

Note: If you don't use files under P:\PROJECTS, or that folder doesn't appear on your device, you don't need to take any action.

 

We're retiring legacy files under the P:\PROJECTS folder on managed staff PCs. This area contains old project files (mainly pre-2014) and has been superseded twice, with the current preferred location for project files being the Change and Projects Hub, and associated sites, in SharePoint Online. We've previously consulted the Programme and Projects Delivery Group (PPDG), Student Systems, HR Systems, Financial Systems, and DARS teams, with advice to identify and migrate any remaining project files that need to be retained.

 

Following a delay in the staged removal of access to files in P:\PROJECTS, this will now take place on the following schedule:

*	4 May 2026: P:\PROJECTS files will become read-only. You will still be able to access the folder to move any critical files to an alternative location.
*	3 August 2026: Access will be removed and P:\PROJECTS will no longer show on staff devices. Data will still exist and will be made available on request, for moving to a more suitable location.
*	1 February 2027: P:\PROJECTS data will be removed from live servers.
*	1 May 2027: Final data backup will expire, after which nothing can be retrieved.

 

If your product, service or project has a critical dependency on files within P:\PROJECTS, and you're not aware of a relocation plan for these, please contact the relevant manager / head of service to ensure that files are migrated to a suitable alternative location by the start of May.

 

If you've got any questions or concerns about this, please contact me by reply.

 

John

 

 

 

JOHN IRELAND

Director, Software Solutions (interim)

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

Teams: Start Chat - Web: https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/ <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

I work flexible hours and am sending this email at a time that works for me. Please do not feel any pressure to read, act or respond outside of your own work schedule.

Sent: 01 April 2026 09:26
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Philip Jukes <philip.jukes@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Appeal Panel (Support Staff)

 

Good morning,

 

Pam Rodgerson suggested that one of you may be able to help: I need a panel to hear an appeal against a final written warning (support staff, Estates); I have a Chair and one other panel member, but need a third, and to ensure gender balance, that member needs to be male. 

 

In view of the Easter break, timings are a bit awkward, and the Chair is away from 13th April for a week, so we are looking at w/c 20th, or, in the last resort, w/c 27th April.

 

If one of you is available, and can let me know any dates in those two weeks to avoid, I would be grateful.

 

Many thanks.

 

Appeal panel constitution regarding a formal warning

Appeals notified in respect of a formal warning will be heard by a panel consisting of three members of staff, at grade 8 or above, (the panel chair will be at an equivalent level to or more senior than the original decision maker, and should not be in a position of reporting to the original decision maker). They will have a balance of role types and one, if appropriate, may have experience relevant to the case being heard. The panel members will be eligible for membership of Congregation, external to the department concerned, and nominated by the Director of Human Resources. One will be asked to act as chair.

 


Phillip Purves

He / Him

 

Phillip Purves, Chartered MCIPD

HR Business Partner, UAS

University HR | University of Oxford

5, Worcester Street

Oxford OX1 2BX

 <https://www.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 ORCIDs in Onboarding
emails.to: Crispin Muncaster; Kevin Lelitte; Eugenio Barrio; Anne Mortimer
emails.body:
Hi all,

 

I’m very confused now, because is this not the solution we already have in place? Apologies if this has already been discussed at the meeting, I’m just catching up on my emails from last week.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 27 March 2026 10:16
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Eugenio Barrio <eugenio.barrio@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Martha Buckley <martha.buckley@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Martin McGovern <martin.mcgovern@it.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 ORCIDs in Onboarding

 

Thanks Simon

 

Eugenio has helped to further clarify – see below. We are not proposing there are any free text fields and so I am sorry if that impression came through. I have sent an invite to meet with Kevin next week, but I will extend the invite to you too. 

 

*	PXD

	*	Self service

		*	All staff
		*	A card (existing) that points to IT registration orcid management (does not collect)

	*	Onboarding

		*	Selected staff groups
		*	A page/set that points to IT registration orcid management (does not collect)

*	Governance

	*	A paper to RPC or RIC about mandating or not, single system or multiple 

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

 

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 27 March 2026 09:43
To: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk> >; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Eugenio Barrio <eugenio.barrio@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:eugenio.barrio@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Martha Buckley <martha.buckley@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:martha.buckley@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Martin McGovern <martin.mcgovern@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:martin.mcgovern@it.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: DTP1092 ORCIDs in Onboarding

 

Hi all,

 

Can I also please ask you to assess the risk of individuals recording their ORCID on PeopleXD and not affiliating their ORCID with Oxford via https://register.it.ox.ac.uk/self/orcid? 

 

What is the overall aim here? Do we still need people to link via registration? 

 

Can we also do some analysis to see if adding the existing link to onboarding has improved the number of affiliations via the above route?

 

Updating the onboarding software in PeopleXD is actually very high risk for us – the last time we did it there was a major incident called due to data loss, so for us to amend it again would need a very robust benefit justification and I don’t feel that Kevin’s point (highlighted) has been responded to at this point.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Hi Kevin,

I know it’s a crazy busy time, so your April to‑do list must be feeling relentless?! But 'yes, yes, I'm in Bank holiday weekend mode, so that's a future Kevin problem!'.

 

Unfortunately, the April Employment Rights Act changes are still very much next week’s problem too. But don’t worry, we’ve got your back!

 

Next Wednesday (8 April) I’m running a free webinar <https://www2.moorepay.co.uk/e3t/Ctc/DO+113/d30F6004/VVtqly3khV1FVTNgwV2dkVxTW2QY3Nh5MnqXKN3Lg8983dh8WW8wLKSR6lZ3lmMQF3XLlZ9gyW67y9s-23PCwJW3HpwgC985zpLW3nbgnY4HhyCsW1pmQ-B8P0LFJW5TGP2v2nWSC4W7l7_Md6DJS46W51r_Q25HtHVqW48LfkR7LGmKgW3h-JWf4D1x_wW4SRfp41mnkfQW3qPkKX6X3ms9W2ScSzZ6hd5-xN58T501xWGffN2h1M1V4KRGmW7_Z_fH5Cm6FPW8CbB-m2dvLS2W2rB6wJ5ZB-x9W33Ky1F9cLys3Vc5vr527lVScW1MGnNR2nxGzZW2Yk_Jv7VGntyW18y0lv6Lsm0dW28G7r441FJgVW7CcRCJ20Pl0KW2rMFVZ9jQjJPW3R9FDv4m57nHW5HzdcK6pGBRfdnjg3K04>  on exactly what you need in place for the April changes. I’ll cover what’s changing across absence, pay, family leave and documentation, plus the Fair Work Agency’s role and what solid audit trails look like.

 

And because we all love a freebie, you’ll also get a handy operational checklist to run against your current set‑up, so nothing slips through the cracks at 4:59pm on a Friday…

 

6,000+ professionals just like you have already signed up to the Moorepay ERA Readiness Series. So I’d say this one’s well worth having on your radar.

 

👉 Pls click here <https://www2.moorepay.co.uk/e3t/Ctc/DO+113/d30F6004/VVtqly3khV1FVTNgwV2dkVxTW2QY3Nh5MnqXKN3Lg8983dh8WW8wLKSR6lZ3kLW40D6NV4SYvDGN2K_VYwgyfzxW5mB67s4RT3HLW36CFb85cBFhDW92VZ8k5Bd5Y4N3wKjNTrVScgW9hkxRj6Bq-nfW98SCRn7-zgs1W1W1KB-8RmYd6N36NW3C9B2W3W8dRNSK6x8bZtW3WW3Rj669N68W8TXs_68XRb59W2gTHD12wN0pwW8Jj9PC6TPXw_VfnQ5b7zC7PDW1CD4C67Jt7L4W73WjFj8vh7SqW4YwQV63y_JrHW7hwPtn8mC_pSMCcVjhsChLBW5Vf_9c6TJ4msW1zT80R5nC878W2wDPLf5tpvxbN8r0-rKGZqH2Vkp2sx4F-HqgW8JG_n32GH82gW3qgRRS8gmvpff525LhF04>  to sign up.

 

And if you can't make it, don't panic. Just sign up anyway and we’ll send you the recording, slides and checklist after 😊.

Michelle

emails.subject: RE: #External# RE: Follow up call
emails.body:
Good Afternoon Anne,

 

I hope you are well.

 

When you are back in office, can I ask you to review the CSV file attached to case: 68261303

I have created 3 employees on UOXZ as a test(now cleared down) and they are in that file on the case, I have created these with the least amount of information, the 2nd and 3rd employees showing the least amount you need but showing how for items like the structure, you can either enter the code or the description as opposed to having to enter both, which might save some time.

 

With the person details, you can see that name and DOB as well as first 2 lines of address and the country code are the requirements. If you have the work emails for the people, these will go in the “Email” column. But you don’t need either the Mobile Number or Email address.

 

Any issues or questions, please let me know.

 

Thank you,

 

Conor O'Brien

Senior Consultant

The Access Group Unit 2302, 3rd Floor Building 2000, Citygate, 

Mahon, Cork, T12 K06Y

www.theaccessgroup.com <http://www.theaccessgroup.com/> 

 <https://www.theaccessgroup.com/>

emails.subject: Re: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

Thanks for arranging the org structure conversation on Friday 10 April — I am on annual leave this week and unable to attend. Please do go ahead with the internal meeting if others are able to progress it without me.

It's great to hear that Mel is on board for the PeopleXD impact assessment work. I would ask that we hold off on starting that workstream until we've had a chance to speak on my return, so that we can agree the right approach before things get underway.

If you need any PeopleXD input in the interim, please do reach out to Michael O'Sullivan or Simon Burford, who are well placed to help.

Best, 
Kevin

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 01 April 2026 11:56

Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Crispin,

 

Thanks - glad it was a useful session. I have copied you into my email to Conor — he will be in touch to confirm proposed session dates, and I've asked him to direct scheduling coordination to you.

 

A few things to pick up ahead of the kick-off.

 

Hierarchy Setup — Internal Review Required

Conor has confirmed that a separate organisation entity will be created for the college staff group and is currently checking internally on best practice for the hierarchy structure. Before configuration begins, we need to complete our own internal review to determine how colleges should be represented within that structure and what reference data and hierarchy elements will need to be created. Could you arrange a session to work through this before the kick-off?

 

A meeting invite sent for this – but just to note that I am on annual leave next week and so will need another team member to lead.

 

Menu Profile Access Levels

Before Conor can configure the menu profile, we need to define what access levels are required. Could you work with Anne, Nathan, and Nick to pull this together and feed back to me so I can brief Conor ahead of that session?

 

Hi Anne, Nathan, Nick – since I am off next week on annual leave – can I leave you to work through this one? From what I understand, we need admin access for viewing and amending records (assumed Nathan and Anne). Will college HR staff need any access at all?

 

Downstream Systems — BizTalk, CUD and other identity/HR integrations.

It was flagged in the meeting that the new non-payroll entity will need to be safely excluded from downstream systems and other identity/HR integrations. Could you investigate what's needed on our side to ensure the new entity doesn't get unintentionally picked up by any existing integrations, and feed back to me? This needs to be resolved before configuration begins.

 

Hi Kevin – I sent an email on this earlier last week. I am trying to source a BA but that is proving difficult, but as explained in my earlier note, I was hoping that the HR Systems would be able to offer some guidance on this. Is there anyone in HR Systems who knows for example how CUD links to an Entity, or what other systems c

Good Afternoon Kevin & Crispin,

 

Thank you both for the below.

 

Apologies @Crispin <mailto:crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>  but I am on a customer call on the 15th between 11 & 12 and am unable to move this specific meeting but can move the Monday meetings to suit, alternatively if we could amend the time of the meeting on the 15th, I could accommodate? I would suggest if possible also that we try to have a two hour training session, just as there is a lot to cover and I would prefer to allow time for questions as we move through.

 

13th of April, 12:00 until 17:00(Data Migration Training)

15th of April, 14:00 until 17:00(Data Migration Training)

16th of April, 14:00 until 17:00(Data Migration Training)

27th of April, 09:30 until 17:00(New reference data and hierarchy items Training)

The New reference data and hierarchy items Training should only take one and a half hours at most.

Dependent on when we complete the above training for Data Migration, it will be a case of loading the data into the Data Migration files, I will then need to review the files to ensure data is meeting requirements and that all data on files exists and meets requirements.

This will dictate when we are able to move forward with the other tasks then also. Once I receive the data for the Data Migration files to review, I will be better placed to advise on specific timelines for loading the data etc.

However, some of the below can be started now:

- Configuration of Non-Pay Pay Group – I can start this now if you can confirm you are happy for me to do so? 

- Set up of new menu profile for new non-pay employees – If you have an outline of what you want removed, I can also begin work on this.

- Script in new employee user accounts and roll out support – I can start preparing the scripts in the coming days and complete once I have the staff numbers.

 

I have also spoken with colleagues regards the Organisational layout and my colleagues have advised that they in fact believe it would be better to maintain the current structure and add a new branch in under this for the Non Payroll Employees. They said in terms of reporting and with EMS notifications and org roles and workflows etc it is more straight forward to maintain within the one structure.

 

Thank you,

 

 

Conor O'Brien

Apologies @Crispin <mailto:crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>  but I am on a customer call on the 15th between 11 & 12 and am unable to move this specific meeting but can move the Monday meetings to suit, alternatively if we could amend the time of the meeting on the 15th, I could accommodate? I would suggest if possible also that we try to have a two hour training session, just as there is a lot to cover and I would prefer to allow time for questions as we move through.

 

13th of April, 12:00 until 17:00(Data Migration Training)

14th of April, 14:30 until 17:30(Data Migration Training)

15th of April, 14:00 until 17:00(Data Migration Training)

16th of April, 14:00 until 17:00(Data Migration Training)

17th of April, 14:00 until 17:00(If Data Migration Training is complete, we could look at arranging the “new reference data and hierarchy items” training)

22nd of April, 09:30 until 13:00(New reference data and hierarchy items Training)

24th of April, 09:30 until 17:00(New reference data and hierarchy items Training)

27th of April, 09:30 until 17:00(New reference data and hierarchy items Training)

The New reference data and hierarchy items Training should only take one and a half hours at most.

Dependent on when we complete the above training for Data Migration, it will be a case of loading the data into the Data Migration files, I will then need to review the files to ensure data is meeting requirements and that all data on files exists and meets requirements.

This will dictate when we are able to move forward with the other tasks then also. Once I receive the data for the Data Migration files to review, I will be better placed to advise on specific timelines for loading the data etc.

However, some of the below can be started now:

- Configuration of Non-Pay Pay Group – I can start this now if you can confirm you are happy for me to do so? 

- Set up of new menu profile for new non-pay employees – If you have an outline of what you want removed, I can also begin work on this.

- Script in new employee user accounts and roll out support – I can start preparing the scripts in the coming days and complete once I have the staff numbers.

 

I have also spoken with colleagues regards the Organisational layout and my colleagues have advised that they in fact believe it would be better to maintain the current structure and add a new branch in under this for the Non Payroll Employees. They said in terms of reporting and with EMS notifications and org roles and workflows etc it is more straight forward to maintain within the one structure.

 

Thank you,

 

 

Conor O'Brien

Senior Consultant

The Access Group Unit 2302, 3rd Floor Building 2000, Citygate, 

Mahon, Cork, T12 K06Y

www.theaccessgroup.com <http://www.theaccessgroup.com/>

emails.subject: RE: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Kevin

 

Some feedback in red. 

 

Kind regards


Crispin

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> 
Sent: 01 April 2026 11:56
To: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nicholas Chandler <nicholas.chandler@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD

 

Hi Crispin,

 

Thanks - glad it was a useful session. I have copied you into my email to Conor — he will be in touch to confirm proposed session dates, and I've asked him to direct scheduling coordination to you.

 

A few things to pick up ahead of the kick-off.

 

Hierarchy Setup — Internal Review Required

Conor has confirmed that a separate organisation entity will be created for the college staff group and is currently checking internally on best practice for the hierarchy structure. Before configuration begins, we need to complete our own internal review to determine how colleges should be represented within that structure and what reference data and hierarchy elements will need to be created. Could you arrange a session to work through this before the kick-off?

 

A meeting invite sent for this – but just to note that I am on annual leave next week and so will need another team member to lead. 

 

Menu Profile Access Levels

Before Conor can configure the menu profile, we need to define what access levels are required. Could you work with Anne, Nathan, and Nick to pull this together and feed back to me so I can brief Conor ahead of that session?

 

Hi Anne, Nathan, Nick – since I am off next week on annual leave – can I leave you to work through this one? From what I understand, we need admin access for viewing and amending records (assumed Nathan and Anne). Will college HR staff need any access at all?

 

Downstream Systems — BizTalk, CUD and other identity/HR integrations.

It was flagged in the meeting that the new non-payroll entity will need to be safely excluded from downstream systems and other identity/HR integrations. Could you investigate what's needed on our side to ensure the new entity doesn't get unintentionally picked up by any existing integrations, and feed back to me? This needs to be resolved before configuration begins.

 

Hi Kevin – I sent an email on this earlier last week. I am trying to source a BA but that is proving difficult, but as explained in my earlier note, I was hoping that the HR Systems would be able to offer some guidance on this. Is there anyone in HR Systems who knows for example how CUD links to an Entity, or what other systems could be or need to be linked.

 

Data Sharing Agreement with Colleges

Could you confirm the status of this with Anne and let me know where it stands? It needs to be in place before we approach colleges for data.

 

Hi Anne – again I will need your steer on this.

 

Let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

 

Best, 

Kevin

 

 

________________________________

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com <mailto:Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com> >
Sent: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 5:32 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com <mailto:Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com> >
Subject: The University Of Oxford - Requirement to load new group of (NP)employees - Planning Session - Post Call Notes 

 

Good Afternoon Kevin,

 

Thank you again for your time today. As discussed, I’ve pulled together a summary of the key points, decisions, and next steps from our planning session regarding the loading of the new group of non‑payroll (NP) employees into PeopleXD.

 

Summary of Discussion

 

Key Topics Covered

 

Overview of the project scope, including loading NP employees, creation of a new non‑pay pay group, and required data migration activities.

Review of the data migration process, including templates, mandatory vs conditional fields, and preparation steps.

Considerations around organisation/hierarchy structure for these NP employees.

Portal access and SSO implications for individuals who already exist within University systems.

Scheduling, timelines, and required resources.

Potential downstream impacts on CUD, BizTalk and other identity/HR integrations.

 

 

Decisions Made

 

A separate organisation entity will be created for this group of NP employees to allow for future-proofing and to avoid cross‑contamination with existing structures. – I am checking this internally to ensure best practise is used.

Project kickoff is planned for 13 April, aiming for completion by late April to mid‑May, depending on resource availability.

Data migration training to take place early—provisionally during the week commencing 13 April.

UAT environment UOXZ will be used

A small test batch of employees will be loaded first before the full load.

 

 

Open Questions / Items Requiring Clarification

 

Final approach for avoiding duplicate SSO/portal accounts for individuals who already have an existing staff record.

Final shape of the hierarchy structure, including how colleges should be represented within the new entity.

Confirmation of which reference data items/hierarchy elements will need to be created.

Internal decisions concerning downstream systems (BizTalk, CUD, identity management) and how to safely exclude the new NP entity where necessary.

Agreement on which mandatory data fields will be supplied for

emails.body:
Hi Chris

 

Many thanks for producing and sharing this update.

 

It is a useful step forward and I am sure will help us understand what is happening & when.

 

I’ll let others pick up on any urgent questions, but I suggest we discuss this at our OHS Management Team meeting.  I had planned for that to happen on April 15th, but I need to reorganise our meeting OHSMT agendas, so this will now take place in May.  

 

I’ll resend the OHSMT meeting invite for May and hope you, Marie & James can make it to discuss this further.

 

Until then, thanks again.

Brian.

---------------------------------------------------------------

Brian Jenkins

Interim Director of Occupational Health & Safety

University of Oxford

T:   01865 (2)70814  |   07940 769947

W:  https://safety.web.ox.ac.uk/our-offices <https://safety.web.ox.ac.uk/our-offices>

Subject: HR Systems support activities in March

As we move into April, I wanted to give you an update of some of the work that has been going on in the background in support of your systems. Please feel free to share this with your teams for awareness.

 

Highlights

 

*	Enhancement lifecycle launched – framework presented to key contacts within OH&S and the Incident Reporting POG
*	Launch of the Enhancement Request Form on OSM to allow users to propose new or updates to existing system functionality – Available on OSM here <https://oxford.saasiteu.com/Modules/SelfService/#serviceCatalog/filter/AC6143F9458A44F9B3B86C45FF1E571F> 
*	Launch of the Health & Safety Roadmap dashboard to allow all staff to have visibility of enhancement requests that have been delivered, those in progress and planned for the future – Available here <https://app.powerbi.com/groups/me/apps/c00c2bb4-2038-4318-bc17-5e700b6a3927/reports/314ba831-839f-4f53-846d-36acabb3f703/69e9b07a2ba77205059d?ctid=cc95de1b-97f5-4f93-b4ba-fe68b852cf91&experience=power-bi>

*	IRIS – Updates to the secondary incident type field to include an option for laser incidents to trigger the notification

 

Future enhancement work

 

Planned work is shown on the Health & Safety Roadmap:

 

Cority:

*	Fix for Primary Job Position flag business rules – Issue preventing client details appearing in banner and inaccuracies in organisation structure data in reporting – Fix funding decision with OH&S for approval
*	Health surveillance compliance reporting – Data field missing in build from Cority preventing a consistent link between the clearance certificate and the specific surveillance group membership – Fix funding decision with OH&S for approval
*	SMS appointment reminders – New supplier functionality to send automatic SMS reminders to clients 24 hours before their appointment – Funding decision with OH&S for approval
*	Outlook and MS Teams integration with Cority – New functionality to integrate links for MS Teams meetings with appointments created on Cority – Funding decision with OH&S for approval
*	Drafting notes templates – Ability to draft smart note templates to allow a user to complete them at a later time – Fix expected in June 2026 release by the supplier
*	Bulk merging tool – Ability to merge applicant and live client data from the People data feed – Solution has been tested and working in production environment – Functionality to be rolled out after Easter

 

Healthy Working Plus:

*	Data import automation – Work being conducted by the Azure Integrations Team in IT Services to automate the feed of people data into the system – Expected delivery in May 2026

 

IRIS:

*	Relocation of Supervisor fields to DSO Review – Allows a supervisor to be notified for every incident – Aw

emails.subject: Re: DTP1092 College Staff into PXD
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

Thanks - glad it was a useful session. I have copied you into my email to Conor — he will be in touch to confirm proposed session dates, and I've asked him to direct scheduling coordination to you.

A few things to pick up ahead of the kick-off.

Hierarchy Setup — Internal Review Required
Conor has confirmed that a separate organisation entity will be created for the college staff group and is currently checking internally on best practice for the hierarchy structure. Before configuration begins, we need to complete our own internal review to determine how colleges should be represented within that structure and what reference data and hierarchy elements will need to be created. Could you arrange a session to work through this before the kick-off?

Menu Profile Access Levels
Before Conor can configure the menu profile, we need to define what access levels are required. Could you work with Anne, Nathan, and Nick to pull this together and feed back to me so I can brief Conor ahead of that session?

Downstream Systems — BizTalk, CUD and other identity/HR integrations.
It was flagged in the meeting that the new non-payroll entity will need to be safely excluded from downstream systems and other identity/HR integrations. Could you investigate what's needed on our side to ensure the new entity doesn't get unintentionally picked up by any existing integrations, and feed back to me? This needs to be resolved before configuration begins.

Data Sharing Agreement with Colleges
Could you confirm the status of this with Anne and let me know where it stands? It needs to be in place before we approach colleges for data.

Let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

Best, 
Kevin


________________________________

I can help look for some slots as before.

 

Kind regards

 

Crispin

emails.body:
Hi Conor,

Thank you for the post-call notes and your availability — both are really helpful in moving things forward.

I note you're planning to reach out to Anne to arrange a session on the Person File Complete template and minimum required fields. Please could you ensure that myself and @Michael O'Sullivan <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  is included in that session. 

On scheduling — could you confirm which dates from your availability you'd propose for each session, including the kick-off, and how long you'd suggest we need for each? I have copied in Crispin, who will be coordinating the diary slots for the sessions on our side.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Conor O'Brien <Conor.OBrien@theaccessgroup.com>
Sent: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 5:32 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>
Subject: The University Of Oxford - Requirement to load new group of (NP)employees - Planning Session - Post Call Notes 


Good Afternoon Kevin,

 

Thank you again for your time today. As discussed, I’ve pulled together a summary of the key points, decisions, and next steps from our planning session regarding the loading of the new group of non‑payroll (NP) employees into PeopleXD.

 

Summary of Discussion

 

Key Topics Covered

 

Overview of the project scope, including loading NP employees, creation of a new non‑pay pay group, and required data migration activities.

Review of the data migration process, including templates, mandatory vs conditional fields, and preparation steps.

Considerations around organisation/hierarchy structure for these NP employees.

Portal access and SSO implications for individuals who already exist within University systems.

Scheduling, timelines, and required resources.

Potential downstream impacts on CUD, BizTalk and other identity/HR integrations.

 

 

Decisions Made

 

A separate organisation entity will be created for this group of NP employees to allow for future-proofing and to avoid cross‑contamination with existing structures. – I am checking this internally to ensure best practise is used.

Project kickoff is planned for 13 April, aiming for completion by late April to mid‑May, depending on resource availability.

Data migration training to take place early—provisionally during the week commencing 13 April.

UAT environment UOXZ will be used

A small test batch of employees will be loaded first before the full load.

 

 

Open Questions / Items Requiring Clarification

 

Final approach for avoiding duplicate SSO/portal accounts for individuals who already have an existing staff record.

Final shape of the hierarchy structure, including how colleges should be represented within the new entity.

Confirmation of which reference data items/hierarchy elements will need to be created.

Internal decisions concerning downstream systems (BizTalk, CUD, identity management) and how to safely exclude the new NP entity where necessary.

Agreement on which mandatory data fields will be supplied for NP staff and where “unknown” values will be used.

 

Action Items

 

Action

Owner

Timeline

Send availability and proposed session dates

Conor

ASAP (pre‑13 April)

Confirm selected training & UAT dates

Kevin

Before project kickoff

Arrange session to review mandatory fields & data templates

Conor & Anne

Next few days

Internal review of hierarchy setup options

Marie, Anne, Michael, Kevin

Before configuration begins

Internal review of SSO/portal handling for multi‑appointment staff

HR/IT Identity Teams

Before account‑creation steps

Review data migration templates and identify needed files

Project Team

Before 13 April training

Conor to confirm hierarchy guidance internally & provide follow‑up summary

Conor

Currently doing so

 

 

Risks & Considerations

 

Risk o

From: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 27 March 2026 11:50
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Query Regarding Employee‑Initiated Sickness Absence Entry in PeopleXD

 

Hi All,

 

A quick update on the PeopleXD sickness‑absence work.

 

Following my initial contact with Policy, where they raised a few concerns.  I provided mitigations, which you both agreed addressed all points. Policy have now confirmed they accept the mitigations and will treat the full email chain as the “risk assessment”.

 

One valid point Simon raised was the risk of duplicate sickness entries. This can happen if an employee initiates sickness and, while it’s still pending approval, the manager also enters the absence. This mirrors how annual leave already behaves. Once an employee‑initiated entry is approved, the system will warn of overlaps, but managers can still continue if needed. Simon suggested a report to flag duplicate sickness periods for HR teams to correct — that’s still on the to‑do list.

 

I’ve documented draft guidance, as well as comms requirements and will pick this up with Julie separately, with a suggestion we work through them when we are both in office together. 

 

Best wishes,

Michael

 

 

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >

To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Query Regarding Employee‑Initiated Sickness Absence Entry in PeopleXD

 

Hi Michael

 

I believe you have address all the concerns raised by policy, and as you clearly stated, this is an alternative, not a replacement, so this functionality need not be used if departments make that decision.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: 19 March 2026 09:17
To: Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Query Regarding Employee‑Initiated Sickness Absence Entry in PeopleXD

 

Hi Michael,

 

I’ve read through all of this email chain and I think your response reflects a balanced mitigation of their concerns. I assume that you’ve worked with Julie / Business Change on the response, particularly the mitigations of what will go into guidance.

 

The only thing I wonder is whether we should suggest that the policy reflects the system guidance – i.e. also stating in the

emails.subject: Children's data in pension payments - PXD DPIA query
emails.body:
Hi David,

I hope you're well.

Tracy Roberts in Payroll mentioned you'd be able to help with this.
I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and need to understand what children's data we process for pension payments (e.g. orphan pensions following death in service).
Specifically:

*	
	What type of pension payments involve children?
*	
	What information do we hold about them (name, DOB, bank details, etc.)?
*	
	Is this held in PeopleXD or processed separately?


Just need a brief answer so I can update the DPIA accurately.

Best, 
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Retention (applicant vs personnel) - PXD DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and, need your sign-off on the retention wording. There are two separate areas:
Applicant data — Clear-down process being implemented (covered in my separate erasure document). DPIA states records cleared per retention framework. 
Personnel/leaver data — The DPIA currently states we delete records 7 years after employment ends, but PeopleXD has no functionality to do this. Access Group has developed data minimisation scripts with Glasgow University (clearing bank details, next of kin, photos, etc.) which will eventually become a configurable module — but there's no confirmed timeline for availability, and we'd need to define our retention rules once it exists.
Proposed DPIA wording:
"Personnel records are retained in accordance with University of Oxford retention policy. Automated deletion functionality for leaver records is not currently available within PeopleXD. Access Group is developing data minimisation tooling which, once available, will enable systematic data retention processes to be implemented."
Please can you confirm you are happy with this approach?
Best, 
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Benefits of HR data processing - PXD DPIA wording
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,

I'm finalising the PeopleXD DPIA and want to strengthen the section on why we process HR data.

Simon suggested linking data quality to strategic outcomes — specifically how accurate HR data supports things like HESA returns, REF submissions, and Athena Swan accreditation, and how these directly affect funding and our ability to attract talent.

Could you give me a couple of sentences I can include that ties this together? Something along the lines of how accurate data underpins external reporting which influences funding and institutional reputation.

Happy to discuss if easier.

Best, 
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: ESS API - Go Live
emails.body:
Hi Michelle,
Are you happy with the draft comms below? As there will be minimal impact on existing users, it may be more appropriate to include this in the monthly bulletin rather than a standalone message.
Let me know your thoughts.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 2:37 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Anna Carter-Windle <anna.carter-windle@it.ox.ac.uk>; Tracey Holland <tracey.holland@it.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: GLAM Go Live 

Hi Kevin

Here is a draft.  However, as the current project team haven't had that much involvement in the development side, there may be other details that need including which I will leave for the functional analyst team to add in.

Dear colleagues,
We’re writing to let you know about an upcoming improvement to Employee Self Service, which will go live on 13 April.
From this date, access to Employee Self Service will be automatically provisioned for all employees via a newly developed system integration (API). As part of this change, all staff will have access to the Workforce Management (WFM) functionality within the system.
For those of you who already use Employee Self Service, there will be no change to your current experience. This update is designed to simplify and streamline how access is provided, removing the need for manual setup for new starters and ensuring consistent access for everyone.
What this means:

*	
	Automatic access to Employee Self Service for all employees from 13 April
*	
	No impact to existing users or current functionality
*	
	Faster, more efficient onboarding for new employees

If you have any questions or need support, please contact the HR Systems Support team.
Thank you for your continued support.
Kind regards,



________________________________

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 25 March 2026 13:52
To: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Anna Carter-Windle <anna.carter-windle@it.ox.ac.uk>; Tracey Holland <tracey.holland@it.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: GLAM Go Live
 
Hi Tracy,
Thanks for confirming.
Michelle is picking this up with Marie on ownership of the comms, as this will need her steer. She will confirm the outcome shortly.
In the meantime, it would be helpful to proceed on the basis that WFM lead on drafting the comms, with HRIS providing input and review.
Happy to adjust once we have confirmation from Marie.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 12:39 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Tracy

Subject: Re: GLAM Go Live
 
Hi Tracy,

Thanks for the update. Good to know we're still pushing forward with the 13th for the API go-live.

We had a catch-up this morning and wanted to clarify the comms approach for the go-live. Could you let us know if there's an existing plan, or if we need to put something together, particularly for TSS?

I've copied in Michelle for awareness.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 09, 2026 9:10 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Tony Boydell <tony.boydell@it.ox.ac.uk>; David Hickman <david.hickman@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Anna Carter-Windle <anna.carter-windle@it.ox.ac.uk>; Tracey Holland <tracey.holland@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: GLAM Go Live

Hello all

I wanted to update you on our conversations with GLAM regarding their go live date and the impact this will have on the ESS API.

We were hoping to have them live before the 13th April as per our last conversations with you, unfortunately they have pushed to now go live after Easter.  They are fully aware of the implications and that all of their staff will have WFM access from the 13th but they feel they can manage that through their comms.  This is something I wanted to avoid but they just don't feel ready to live.

However, I haven't said we will move the API go live so I think we push forward with the 13th and GLAM can manage it through their comms.  If there are other implications that I'm not aware of, please let me know.

We also have SBS who haven't got a confirmed go live but should have an answer on that this week.

Many thanks
Tracy

emails.body:
Hi Julie,

That's great news — thanks for sorting that out with Tracy, and updating the new user email in OSM.

Much appreciated.

Best,
Kevin
________________________________

From: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 24 March 2026 3:26 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Asta managing HR chat members 
 

Hello Kevin,

 

I’m pleased to report that Tracy has informed me that she has updated the channel so anyone can now join without us having to manage members 😊.

 

I will raise a change in OSM so that new users are made aware of the channel in their “new user” email.

 

Many thanks

Julie

 

From: Julie Hickman 
Sent: 04 March 2026 10:11
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Asta managing HR chat members 

 

Good morning Kevin,

 

Thank you for contacting Asta and the update below!

 

We discussed this in our Business Change catch up this morning and Marie suggested that we pause and go back to the WFM project team to see if they can configure the channel so that it’s open to all (but aimed at HR teams) and eliminate the management from HR Systems altogether.  

 

I’m still keen to update the PeopleXD new user access email with a link to the channel, and will send a change request when it’s appropriate.

 

I’m going to contact Tracy Barlow-Bacon shortly and I will keep everyone updated.

 

Many thanks

Julie

 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> > 
Sent: 03 March 2026 14:40
To: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Re: Asta managing HR chat members 

 

Hi Julie,

 

I’ve been added to the Teams channel to understand the context. It appears this is not a Teams chat, but a channel primarily used to access the WFM weekly rotating schedule finder and some historic Mobile App - go live announcements. Given this, I don’t think overall ownership should sit with the FA team. Would it be more appropriate for this to sit with Business Change?

 

Of the two options, the second approach, providing users with the option to request access – sounds reasonable. Asta is comfortable supporting this from a membership management perspective. We would need to agree a clear process, defined eligibility, and ensure there is more than one approver to avoid a single point of failure, with this managed by the owner.

 

It would be helpful to arrange a short meeting to confirm ownership, responsibilities, and the agreed approach so we are all aligned before moving forward.

 

Best,
Kevin

 

 

________________________________

From: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: Tuesday, March 03, 2026 10:17 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: Asta managing HR chat members 

 

Good morning Kevin,

 

The WFM project team is currently managing the membership. My understanding is that they receive ad hoc requests and approve them as they come in.

 

Asta was suggested for this responsibility, as the work is similar to her existing tasks in maintaining the mailing list, and it wouldn’t require any changes (eg automatic task creation) to the user access service request.

 

Marie – if Asta isn’t the right person to take this on, if/ when it’s appropriate, please could you advise on the next steps?

 

Many thanks

Julie

emails.subject: Customer Success Review Tomorrow – Cases 68351079 & 68388326
emails.body:
Hi both,

Ahead of your customer success review with Access Group tomorrow, I wanted to flag two outstanding cases that need raising.

Case 68351079 – HESA Module LEVELS UDF (Case Owner: Maura McGlynn): We raised data cleardown and update issues on 17 February. The data output was shared with the dev team on 4 March by Maura. Her last update was on 19 March, apologising for the delay and confirming she was chasing. We've had nothing back since. This case is now over five weeks old, and we still need clarity on whether a script and quote are required to expedite the deletion.

Case 68388326 – Recruitment Vacancy Alert Email (Case Owner: Iulian Rosia): We've been trying to resolve a configuration issue with the vacancy alert email since 27 January (original Ref 68285965). Changes applied in our test environments didn't take effect, so a follow-up case was raised on 25 February and passed to the dev team on 2 March. I chased Sarah Murphy on 19 March (cc Mike West) but have had no update. This is now almost two months outstanding.


Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

emails.subject: Re: People XD data question
emails.body:
Hi Roy,

Unfortunately, the Country field in PeopleXD is mandatory and must be populated from the drop-down list — there is no option to set it to Null/blank. The only available choices are specific countries or "Not Known." So, it maybe in this case, "Not Known" is a legitimate value rather than a data-entry error. That said, I appreciate most records will have an actual country selected.

Given this constraint, I think the best route would be a mapping adjustment on the CRM side to handle "Not Known", even if it only applies to a handful of records.

Happy to discuss further if that would help.

Best,
Kevin



________________________________

From: Roy McKee <roy.mckee@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 1:10 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: People XD data question 

Hi Kevin, 

I spoke to a couple of staff from the central HR team. 

They suggested that it was unusual for a staff record to have the country field in their address populated with 'Country Unknown'. 

Would you be able to assist us by asking the owner of this data to change the value in this field from "Not Known" to Null, to match the other address fields? 

We feel this would be an appropriate solution given that this record appears to be an outlier. 


Many thanks, 


Roy McKee
Dynamics Functional Analyst - CRM

Software Solutions | IT Services | University of Oxford

+44 01865 111716
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD
 

________________________________

From: Roy McKee
Sent: Thursday, March 5, 2026 3:45 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: People XD data question
 
Hi there Kevin,

Are you the right person to approach about PeopleXD data related issues? If not, please let me know who I can refer this to.

The team who manages the integration between CRM and PeopleXD told us today that one of the incoming staff records from PeopleXD failed to load into CRM due to an unrecognised value in the "Home Country" field. 

This individual (2418170) has their Home country set to "Not Known" in PeopleXD. Because this value doesn't exist in CRM, the integration is failing. 

This is the first time this has happened, which suggests that staff records in PeopleXD don't normally have their home country set to "Not Known". 

My question for you is: is "Not Known" an acceptable value for users to put in the "Home Country" field when they are creating staff records? Or has someone used this value in error? 

Before we pursue any possible data mapping exercise on our end to prevent this issue occurring in future, it would be helpful to know if we can expect to see more staff records in future that also have this value as their Home Country. If not, then I think simply adjusting the data on this record in PeopleXD would be the simplest solution. The integration will be able to run successfully if the Home country field is set to blank instead. 


Kind regards,


Roy McKee
Dynamics Functional Analyst - CRM

Software Solutions | IT Services | University of Oxford

+44 01865 111716
Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

emails.subject: Re: GLAM Go Live
emails.to: Tracy Barlow-Bacon; Simon Burford; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi Tracy,

Thanks for the update. Good to know we're still pushing forward with the 13th for the API go-live.

We had a catch-up this morning and wanted to clarify the comms approach for the go-live. Could you let us know if there's an existing plan, or if we need to put something together, particularly for TSS?

I've copied in Michelle for awareness.

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 09, 2026 9:10 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Tony Boydell <tony.boydell@it.ox.ac.uk>; David Hickman <david.hickman@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Anna Carter-Windle <anna.carter-windle@it.ox.ac.uk>; Tracey Holland <tracey.holland@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: GLAM Go Live 

Hello all

I wanted to update you on our conversations with GLAM regarding their go live date and the impact this will have on the ESS API.

We were hoping to have them live before the 13th April as per our last conversations with you, unfortunately they have pushed to now go live after Easter.  They are fully aware of the implications and that all of their staff will have WFM access from the 13th but they feel they can manage that through their comms.  This is something I wanted to avoid but they just don't feel ready to live.

However, I haven't said we will move the API go live so I think we push forward with the 13th and GLAM can manage it through their comms.  If there are other implications that I'm not aware of, please let me know.

We also have SBS who haven't got a confirmed go live but should have an answer on that this week.

Many thanks
Tracy

Hi Kevin,

 

At EcoOnline we are continuing to invest in building a consistent, best-in-class customer experience. I’m writing to let you know about an upcoming change to how we support your EcoOnline account. 

 

The most important thing to know: you will continue to have access to expert help, product guidance, and best practices. 

 

We’re introducing a new way to access to expert guidance and best-practice support. We’ve done this by simplifying how customers access our Customer Success team, co-locating it with our Customer Support portal. Our goal is to resolve your request in the best way possible.  

 

In practice, many customers find this model delivers faster response times and more direct access to the right expertise, rather than relying on a single point of contact. 

 

What’s changing 

From Friday March 13th, your account will no longer have a single dedicated Customer Success Manager. Instead, you will have access to a pool of Customer Success managers in addition to your existing access to Customer Support. 

 

Requests for Customer Success support will be submitted through one central portal: 

*	Requests will be handled by a specialist team and prioritised by urgency and impact, ensuring your request is routed to the right expert as quickly as possible.  
*	You’ll continue to receive access to product guidance, best practices, and updates 

 

What’s not changing 

*	Our commitment to helping you successfully use EcoOnline   
*	Your access to expert help when you need it 

 

What you need to do

emails.subject: Re: Rollout of Automatic Annual Leave Carryover
emails.to: Tracy Barlow-Bacon; Simon Burford; Michelle Williams; Michael O'Sullivan
emails.body:
Hi all,

Thanks for today's session, here's a summary of what we covered and the agreed next steps.

Approach
We'll clone the existing holiday scheme and keep the majority of departments on the original, which will have carryover automation enabled (max 5 days). Any departments that opt out will be moved to the cloned scheme with no automation. This avoids moving the majority and keeps manual adjustment work to a minimum. Carryover is set against the balance calendar, not by department. So, this will effectively be a universal setting on the original scheme. Departments that opt out can still manage carryover manually as they do now — just in reverse.

Policy and Process Implications
Melanie (business analyst) discussed this with the policy / wellbeing team, and they did not raise any concerns about automating carryover. Given that policy guidance emphasises using leave and supporting wellbeing, and this would be a universal change, it is recommended we do a follow-up check-in with Kate Butler to confirm before proceeding.

Going forward, we need to involve Julie to define central processes and year-end comms. This includes defining how managers agree the number of days to carry, how they monitor and report on carryover, and providing guidance for departments that want tighter control.

Testing and Timelines
We need a new balance/calendar set up purely for testing. Julie normally starts rollover comms in July, so all configuration, testing, policy sign-off, and comms preparation need to be completed by the 2nd week of June.

Next Steps

1.	
	Tracy – Send over the WFM contact list from the project tracker.
2.	
	Michelle – Discuss monitoring and reporting processes with Julie.
3.	
	Kevin – Draft a test plan for the new balance calendar setup and carryover configuration.
4.	
	Michael – Execute testing in PeopleXD test environment with Kevin's support.
5.	
	Follow-up with non-responding departments on the opt-in/opt-out survey.
6.	
	All configuration and testing complete by 2nd week of June, ready for live.



Timeline

*	
	w/c 6 April: Test plan ready
*	
	w/c 13 April: Testing begins – pre-implementation checks and baseline capture
*	
	w/c 20 April: Scheme cloning and verification
*	
	Late April: Michelle and Julie discuss monitoring, reporting processes, and manager guidance; follow-up with non-responding departments
*	
	w/c 27 April: Department assignments and carryover rule configuration
*	
	May: Functional testing (automated and manual schemes) and less common scenarios testing

*	

*	
	w/c 8 June: Sign-off and go-live ready
*	
	July: Julie begins year-end comms



Let me know if I've missed anything.

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Tracy Barlow-Bacon <tracy.barlow-bacon@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 23, 2026 2:20 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michelle Williams <michelle.williams@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Carry Forward Leave Info

Please find below the links to key documents as discussed:

From: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 4:32 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Email for Marie 


Hi Kevin,

 

James and I are happy with the explanation below of the quotes and enhancement items to be sent to Marie to approach OH&S for funding.

 

Kind Regards,

 

Chris Sanders

Business Change Analyst

​HR Systems <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/hr-systems.aspx> ​ | People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

  

    <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>  <https://oxforduni-remoteforms.info-exchange.com/Incident> 

  <https://www.credly.com/badges/3e879e66-f8ce-4137-b2d6-846a0ad552af/public_url>     <https://www.credly.com/badges/6a28598f-a6f1-46d2-9f7f-43275b13fca7/public_url> 

 

~~~~~~~~~~~~

 

Hi Marie,

 

See attached email from Sophie Levy at Cority with quotes for CAP time and a one-off charge option for the enhancement items we have identified.

 

The quote for 20 hours of CAP time to cover the enhancement items comes to ?4,100 for the first year and would be for a commitment to 2 years of this charge. If agreed before 31st March 2026, the second year could be cancelled meaning this ?4,100 charge would effectively be a one-off charge.

 

The attachment in the email outlines there flat-rate one-off charge to cover this work of ?4,500.

 

The enhancement items this covers are as follows – I have provided some additional information for context:

 

1.      “Primary Job Position” business rule fix

MoSCoW Rating = Must Have

 

Issue:

Business rules set up to flag one employment record for a client as the “Primary Job Position (PJP)” are not working as expected. The rules in place are a combination of out of the box rules and ones created by us to cover the different scenarios and logic to determine which employment record is the primary one e.g. scenarios like a client with more than one active employment record. This is resulting in clients not having an employment record associated with their interactions with UOHS and the banner at the top of the Cority screen not populating with their employment information.

 

Consequence if not resolved:

This in turn means any reports that look at these interactions cannot be associated with the organisational structure (as this is showing blank for the client). This means accurate billing by UOHS to Colleges and any statistical reporting are not accurate. This will also likely affect dashboard reporting in the future if not resolved.

 

Benefits of fix

*	Improved data quality
*	Accurate reporting
*	Accurate billing by UOHS

 

2.      Health surveillance compliance report fix

MoSCoW Rating = Must Have

 

Issue:

When data relating to health surveillance clearance certificates is explored in the data warehouse or in the query builder in Cority, there is no field that links the clearance certificate to the specific enrolment on a health surveillance programme. This means when we try to create a report to show whether a client is compliant with their health surveillance (i.e. all clearance certificates are in date), all clearance certificates for the client are applied to all of their health surveillance programme enrolments e.g. a client is enrolled in Dermal and Respiratory health surveillance programmes. When we run a query for the client, all clearance certificates for the Dermal programme are also applied for the Respiratory programme as well.

 

Consequences if not resolved:

It will be impossible to produce an accurate report to show whether a client is compliant with health surveillance programmes. This is a crucial activity

Subject: Applicant Data Erasure Process Legal Statement 


Hi Kevin

 

I have reviewed and amended. I’ve added some comments for further clarification etc.

 

Please note I have also reached out to policy to update the https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/retention-periods-for-university-personnel-records

As PeopleXD data is automatically deleted 12-months after the closing of the vacancy, but currently we state 6-months on the schedule. I have suggested we either:

Best wishes

 

Marie

emails.body:
Hi Marie,
Thanks for the update — that all sounds sensible.
I’m happy to act as the SME for this and link into the project directly, as the rest of the team are currently at capacity. Please feel free to let Claire know.
Best,
Kevin

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 13 March 2026 9:19 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Childcare monthly report - February 
 

Hi Kevin

 

I’m sharing this status update with you to give you oversight on progress with the introduction of a new system for childcare services. They initially were going to go as part of Halo, but as they have the funidng and it is time critical they have decided to purchase a standalone system, which is the best decision in my opinion.

 

It has become apparent as more things unfold for this implementation that we are going to need to get involved, specifically around the PeopleXD integration and also around the support model (our experience with Halo being the big red flag for me here!).

 

So, can I ask you nominate someone in the team who can link into this project as SME to ensure they get these things right at the start, and I’ll inform Claire the PM.

 

I should stress that I have not had any conversations about who will support this system and what that will entail, so we need to unpick this as part of the implementation process. I will remain close to the project, so I will work with whoever you nominate. For your info, this has been identified as a priority for the People Department over the next year, so let’s try and keep it as pain-free as possible.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

emails.subject: Follow-up: Recruitment Vacancy Alert Email Configuration Issue (Refs 68285965 & 68388326)
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I hope you're well.
I wanted to follow up on the support ticket 68388326. Recruitment Vacancy Alert Email – Configuration Changes Not Applying, as I have not received any recent updates on this issue.
This relates to an original case (Ref 68285965) which was logged on 27 January to confirm whether the opening text of the system-generated vacancy alert email is configurable.
Following this, we applied the changes in our test environments (OUXU and OUXZ), but the opening text in the outgoing email did not update.
The case (Ref 68388326) was then logged on 25 February to address this, and as of 2 March the issue had been passed to the DEV team. Since then, I have not received any further updates, despite requesting this.
As this has now been ongoing since 27 January 2026, and with the current case now open for over three weeks, this has now become urgent. I wanted to check if you are able to provide any update on the progress, and whether you could reach out to the DEV team to contact me directly to help resolve this.
Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: Updates: ORCID Integration: Tasks and Next Steps
emails.body:
bump
________________________________

From: Grace Parsons <grace.parsons@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 10 December 2024 2:59 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Grace Parsons <grace.parsons@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Updates: ORCID Integration: Tasks and Next Steps 
 

Summary from 121:

 

Notes on updates from 10th December

 

Hi Grace,

Here are a few tasks to investigate and focus on regarding the ORCID integration. I’m sure some of these are already on your radar, but I wanted to ensure we’re aligned on the next steps for you. I’ve copied Lee in, as you may require his assistance with some of these items.

Please let me know if you’d like to add or adjust anything on this list - it’s very much a work in progress. 🙂

Best, Kevin

1.	Review UDF Requirements:

	*	Investigate how a new User Defined Field (UDF) for ORCID data can be created in PeopleXD.
	*	Determine whether the UDF should be configured at the person level or appointment level.
	*	Confirm whether UDF data can be automatically synced with other systems or if manual uploads will be required.

Attached are the two guidance documents I can locate, one in reference to UDFs and one in reference to onboarding.

ACTION – read the attached documents and summarise. Locate the PXD UDF knowledge article.

The good to know points: UDFs can be reported upon to see what exists.

UDF summary:
1. Complete the UDF template
2. Defining the field items
3. Defining where the UDF is to appear – to enquire as to whether this is order/space in PDX/Onboarding
4. Entering a format for numeric fields
5. Security options are optional
6. Separate section on the Editing or removing a list item.



Note that there is a maximum of 12 fields that can be recorded within a UDF.
Add these in through the data labels sections, where you can also choose the data type. For each one:



The process for creating a secure UDF is separate and separate access is required. Details of which are in the attached.



 

2.	Reporting and Maintenance:

	*	Determine the reporting needs for ORCID data stored in PeopleXD.
	*	Explore if the UDF can support robust reporting via PeopleXD.

	*	Identify the long-term maintenance requirements for the UDF, such as updates and periodic checks.

To add

3.	HR Team Dependencies:

	*	Clarify what specific actions the HR teams will need to take if the ORCID data is captured through onboarding.
	*	Investigate whether existing HR workflows will be impacted by introducing a new checklist item or tile for ORCID data.

To add

4.	Onboarding Checklist Configurations:

	*	Review current onboarding configurations in PeopleXD to determine if ORCID data can be added as a non-mandatory checklist item.
	*	Test the functionality for signposting to ensure smooth user experience.

To add

5.	Researcher Hub Coordination:

	*	Investigate whether ORCID registration and validation information are already part of the Researcher Hub.
	*	Assess if duplicating this information in PeopleXD is necessary or if simple signposting would suffice.

To add

6.	Resource Implications:

	*	Evaluate the workload implications for local HR teams if manual uploads or frequent UDF updates are required.
	*	Identify potential automation options to reduce administrative burden.

Kevin Q: is there a technical way for us to do this, or is it through the steps and time to uniquely do it for each one, i.e. ‘HR teams having a 5 minute action piece from this addition per individual that begins’?

7.	Collaborate with Julie:

	*	Work with Julie to gather examples of similar onboarding tasks and materials.
	*	Coordinate efforts to align onboarding processes with existing HR guidelines and resources.

Emailed Julie at 10am 10th Dec for these and once received will build upon these to ensure we have full knowledge on all stages we currently have.

emails.subject: Re: DTP1092 ORCIDs in Onboarding
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,
Following your email, I’ve reviewed the requirements and wanted to set out what we can support within PeopleXD.
We can capture ORCID during onboarding and allow this to be entered or amended via self-service. The value can also be passed to the data warehouse alongside SSO.
We can also control which staff groups are presented with the field during onboarding. However, this cannot be enforced beyond that point, as the field would remain available in self-service, and users or HR teams would still be able to enter or amend it outside of onboarding.
There are elements of the requirements that we cannot support. Prepopulating the field from CUD would require a new integration into PeopleXD, which is not in place. Validation via orcid.org would also require external integration and is not supported within PeopleXD.
It would be helpful to understand the benefit of capturing ORCID given that we cannot verify whether the ORCID entered is correct, meaning entries could include typos, incorrect values, or another person’s ORCID, which would not be identified within the system.
If you’re able to share any further context on priorities, timelines, or the original business case, that would help us to support next steps.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

From: Reenu Delaney <Reenu.Delaney@theaccessgroup.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 04, 2026 11:50 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: #External# Re: Case 68312192 - The University Of Oxford - reporting for vacancy alert subscriptions

Hi Kevin,

I hope you’re keeping well.

I’ve reviewed your query and, unfortunately, it isn’t possible to report on the details of applicants who are subscribed to vacancy alerts. While we do store this information in a table, extracting it would require a data import, which is a chargeable request.

Regarding internal candidates, employees will receive internal vacancy alerts. However, if they register using an external email address, they may only receive external vacancy alerts unless there is another identifier in place that flags them as an internal candidate.

If you need to update the wording visible to users, you can do so by navigating to:
Recruitment → Toolbox → Recruitment Admin → My Account.


Based on the above, are you happy for me to close this case?

Reenu

Marie

Subject: RE: Issues with Recruitment Data Validation report

 

Hi Marie,

 

Apologies for letting this one sit for a while!

You and I had a brief discussion about this late last year, but I’m happy to touch base again if you’d like! 

 

This is tricky because the stupid Vacancy Details page has five fields simply labelled ‘Closing Date’, but only the top one applies to the overall vacancy – the others refer to advertising closing dates.  I think a couple of things should happen here:

 

1.	This field should be renamed in PeopleXD to ‘Vacancy Closing Date’ to make it distinct from the advertising dates – I believe this can be done in the Language Pack, but in any event, it would be a configuration change that the Functional Analyst team would need to make, so @Marie <mailto:marie.king@admin.ox.ac.uk>  could you please reach out to Kevin and ask him whether a member of his team could take this forward? 

 



2.	If the FA team are able to relabel the field as indicated, then @Julie <mailto:julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>  we’ll need your assistance, as the guidance on this linked page <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/manage-recruitment-statuses-and-events.aspx>  should be updated for the yellow-highlighted text shown below: 

*	Vacancies which have a ‘Currently’ Open status, but have a Vacancy Closing Date
*	Vacancies that have been open for more than four months where the Vacancy Closing Date is blank (these may be inactive vacancies that should be closed)
