emails.subject: Re: Scoping Session update
emails.body:
Hi Sophie,

I hope you’re well.

I’m following up on the scoping meeting held on Thursday 26 February to check on progress with the agreed actions.
In particular, we are awaiting:

*	
	Estimated consultancy hours per item (Compliance Report, Outlook/Teams integration, SMS configuration, and the business rule/banner issue)
*	
	Confirmation whether the Compliance Report linkage is a product development item or would require consultancy
*	
	An update on whether the business rule issue can be resolved via Support or requires CAP hours
*	
	The GDPR/data protection information relating to the Twilio integration


Once we have this, we can assess the appropriate level under the Advantage Programme and progress internally.

Could you please let us know when we can expect an update?

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, February 16, 2026 10:27 AM
To: Sophie.Levy@cority.com <Sophie.Levy@cority.com>
Subject: Request to Arrange Scoping Session – Compliance Report & Enhancements 

Hi Sophie,

I hope you’re well.

We would like to arrange a scoping session to review several items that may require consultancy support.
The primary item is the Compliance Report for Health Surveillance. We would like to confirm the scope of work involved and receive a formal Statement of Works, including associated costs.

If possible, we would also like to include the following in the same session:

*	
	Outlook and Teams integration
*	
	SMS reminders for Occupational Health


The purpose of the discussion is to clarify requirements and obtain costings so we can progress this internally for a funding decision.
Could you please confirm your availability over the next week or two, and we will arrange a suitable time?

Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 






Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Hi Amy,
Thank you for the update.
We have selected the Level 2 Support option and the FlexPoints case has now been approved. I understand Mike has raised the T&M case.
On that basis, could you please confirm the next steps and when we might expect the formal discovery phase to be scheduled?
@Mike West <mailto:Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>  – are we able to bring the current 5-6 week lead time forward? We are keen to progress this as soon as possible.
Best,
Kevin

Subject: RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup 


Hi Kevin

 

Thank you for your patience whilst I get back to you. Re your initial questions

*	Whether any further discovery or formal scoping sessions would be required before delivery begins

Yes, there will be a formal discovery/scoping period to understand all requirements.

*	Your earliest indicative start date

Ideally, we’ll need to know exactly what level of support you require – once we have sign off for the project we will review the days involved and see what consultant has earliest availability.  We are currently working on approx. five-to-six-week lead time, but this can change on a week-by-week basis depending on the level of projects we have coming in.

 

Any further questions please do not hesitate to contact me.

 

Thanks

 

Amy

Senior Consultant

emails.body:
Hi Reenu,
Thank you for your update.
Regarding the wording change, could you please clarify exactly where within Recruitment → Toolbox → Recruitment Admin → My Account the amendment needs to be made? It isn’t clear which specific field or setting controls the wording visible to users.
If available, please share any supporting documentation or guidance that outlines the steps.
My preference would be to arrange a short call with screen sharing to walk through this together - please let me know you availability.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Reenu Delaney <Reenu.Delaney@theaccessgroup.com>
Sent: Wednesday, March 04, 2026 11:50 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: #External# Re: Case 68312192 - The University Of Oxford - reporting for vacancy alert subscriptions 

Hi Kevin,

I hope you’re keeping well.

I’ve reviewed your query and, unfortunately, it isn’t possible to report on the details of applicants who are subscribed to vacancy alerts. While we do store this information in a table, extracting it would require a data import, which is a chargeable request.

Regarding internal candidates, employees will receive internal vacancy alerts. However, if they register using an external email address, they may only receive external vacancy alerts unless there is another identifier in place that flags them as an internal candidate.

If you need to update the wording visible to users, you can do so by navigating to:
Recruitment → Toolbox → Recruitment Admin → My Account.


Based on the above, are you happy for me to close this case?



Regards,

Reenu




Reenu Delaney

Senior Consultant

Hi Marie,
I've reviewed the MoU and can confirm that the Support descriptions are accurate and allocated ownership is correct.
However, I did note an inconsistency in the document. The Areas of Responsibility section references imports 4x per year, whereas the narrative section states that the import routine, using Excel templates will take place once per year.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Tuesday, March 03, 2026 5:21 PM
To: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 PXD Initiative 


Hi Anne

 

If you could share your DPIA that would be great, as I suspect it may need refining, and assume this will be overseen by the Project with input from HR Systems?

 

I am still to see receive the data sharing from James Bristow, but assuming this is robust enough, could you add a row to the area of responsibility with assigned ownership to Colleges, that their Data Privacy statement is clear on data sharing with the central University.

 

In the MoU, based on previous discussions, should it read that the data import will be updated quarterly in line with the census reviews?

The SPRE (Strategy, People, and Research Environment) narrative also needs to include aggregated EDI data but there is currently no reliable mechanism for collating this EDI data. A rolling census exercise which gathers core data on relevant college staff already exists, but this excludes EDI data. The census is kept up to date with quarterly reviews.

The “College Only to PeopleXD” initiative will utilize the existing census routine. The collected data will then be imported into PeopleXD (PXD) via a file import routine, using Excel templates – once per year.

 

@Kevin Lelitte <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – can you please confirm the support descriptions are relevant and allocated correct ownership?

 

Regarding Flexpoints, I need to liaise with Kevin if we have any available and how we proceed, as I have been advised by Access Group that they cannot accept mixed payment. I will get back to you on this one.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

Subject: RE: DTP1092 PXD Initiative 
 

Hi Marie,

 

Just putting my 2c in here, our DPIA hasn’t been signed off yet and I feel like it might muddy the water to include college data in the main DPIA. 

 

We’ve already been advised to keep WFM separate so I suspect that it would be sensible to keep this separate as well, particularly as the reason and justification for processing the data is very different to the reasoning laid out in the main DPIA. I would personally feel much more comfortable if we have a separate DPIA justifying our processing of college data within PeopleXD so this can be signed off specifically.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

From: Data Protection <data.protection@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 3:06 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention 


Dear Kevin

 

Many thanks for your clear and fulsome email – that all sounds really positive.

 

I will look out for future updates regarding the erasure request.

 

Best wishes

 

Joshua Hodgson

Information Compliance Officer

 

Information Compliance Team

University of Oxford, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

https://compliance.admin.ox.ac.uk <https://compliance.admin.ox.ac.uk/> 

 



 

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 26 February 2026 14:43
To: Data Protection <data.protection@admin.ox.ac.uk>; HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention

 

Hi Joshua,

Thank you for your email. Please see our responses below.

Erasure of Unsuccessful Application Data
We are in the process of deleting Vanessa Beaumont’s unsuccessful application records that fall outside the six-month retention window. We will confirm once this has been completed.

6-Month vs 26-Month Retention
We acknowledge the misalignment and are addressing this as part of our wider applicant data retention review. Our guidance is being aligned with the University retention schedule, and we will confirm the agreed position shortly.

PeopleXD Cleardown Process
The automated cleardown process in PeopleXD is not currently live. Testing is underway and subject to business change considerations, a realistic go-live timeline is likely to be within the next few months.

In the interim, manual erasure requests will continue to be processed in line with the retention schedule.

Cross-Department Application History — Access and Safeguards
We have reviewed the  PeopleXD system access and can confirm:

*	Visibility in PeopleXD is department specific. Users can only view applications for departments to which they have access.

*	Cross-department visibility is restricted to users who hold both a recruitment administrator menu profile and recruitment system user/HR access. This is a limited group and does not apply to general departmental HR staff.

*	Access is controlled through system access groups and restricted to recruiting administrators. There are no additional configuration settings to further limit visibility beyond manual access adjustments, if required.

Based on this, departmental HR staff cannot view application history across the University unless they hold specific recruitment administrator privileges.

Please let me know if you have any further queries.

Best,
Kevin

 

Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 

 



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

 

________________________________

emails.subject: Re: Asta managing HR chat members
emails.body:
Hi Julie,

I’ve been added to the Teams channel to understand the context. It appears this is not a Teams chat, but a channel primarily used to access the WFM weekly rotating schedule finder and some historic Mobile App - go live announcements. Given this, I don’t think overall ownership should sit with the FA team. Would it be more appropriate for this to sit with Business Change?

Of the two options, the second approach, providing users with the option to request access – sounds reasonable. Asta is comfortable supporting this from a membership management perspective. We would need to agree a clear process, defined eligibility, and ensure there is more than one approver to avoid a single point of failure, with this managed by the owner.

It would be helpful to arrange a short meeting to confirm ownership, responsibilities, and the agreed approach so we are all aligned before moving forward.

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Asta managing HR chat members 


Good morning Kevin,

 

The WFM project team is currently managing the membership. My understanding is that they receive ad hoc requests and approve them as they come in.

 

Asta was suggested for this responsibility, as the work is similar to her existing tasks in maintaining the mailing list, and it wouldn’t require any changes (eg automatic task creation) to the user access service request.

 

Marie – if Asta isn’t the right person to take this on, if/ when it’s appropriate, please could you advise on the next steps?

 

Many thanks

Julie

emails.body:
Hi Asta,
That makes sense, and I agree that overall ownership of the channel should not sit with the FA team.
I’ve indicated that the opt-in approach sounds reasonable, with you supporting membership management. I’ve also noted that we would need a clearly agreed process, defined eligibility, and more than one approver to avoid a single point of failure, with oversight sitting with the confirmed owner.
I expect we will arrange a short meeting to align on ownership and responsibilities before anything is formalised. I’ll keep you updated.
Best,

Subject: RE: WFM HR Teams Chat - Membership Management 


Hi Kevin,

 

Of the two options, updating the membership alongside the mailing list would likely be the most efficient and consistent approach. I think the main ownership of the WFM HR Teams Chat should sit with Michelle, or the Business team. I’m happy to support by managing membership updates but not to take on overall ownership of the chat.

 

If we proceed, it would be helpful to confirm the agreed process and any criteria for adding people on, just to ensure consistency. The name ‘WFM HR Teams Chat’ suggest this is designed for People Management Admins with Absence Admin role assigned. I’m not part of that chat, so unsure about the nature of discussions there.

 

 

Many thanks,

Asta

These are the records for deletion (UOXP) by AG.

 

Basically its anything in either HESA Module UDF (LEVELS or ACTSOC20201) that doesn’t have the comment ‘Returned 24-25’, that way we retain last year’s returned values and nothing else; so we have a clean slate ready for this year’s records and can see where we already have a returned value and where we need one.

 

I’m changing the SOPs to make it clear that in the future any changes need to be made before the software roll-over so we aren’t in this situation again.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

From: Julie Hickman 
Sent: 10 February 2026 12:17
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Asta managing HR chat members 

 

Hello Kevin,

 

I have put all the details from my previous emails into one – so the previous two can be deleted.

 

The WFM project have created a IT Services - Workforce Management HR Network <https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3AfNw-_7sDqcPUR8KPvFqcTDQboHNLIC2tufh_6aK5kck1%40thread.tacv2/General?groupId=3600ef48-ed85-4ed3-a767-9ee16778b92a&tenantId=cc95de1b-97f5-4f93-b4ba-fe68b852cf91>  chat, to act as a shared space for support, knowledge-sharing, and collaborate during the project.  

 

The project team believe this would be useful to continue for the long term (after the project has finished), for HR teams to have peer-to-peer support.  The HR Systems team would not be members and all questions from departments would still be raised to the Support team via email.

 

Without the project team there won’t be anyone to manage members of the chat.  I therefore wondered if it would be appropriate for Asta to add/ remove members, she currently does this for the mailing list.

 

We think there are two options:

 

1.	When Asta updates the mailing list, she could update the chat membership at the same time.
2.	We could update the standard system generated email from OSM, which is sent to users when access is granted/ updated.  The updated email could include a link for them to click a link to request access to the Chat (which then Asta could potentially approve/ reject).

 

The advantage with the second approach is that it provides users with the option to join (it won’t be mandatory) and I presume less work for Asta.

 

The WFM project team would be able to advise more on their current process.  Please can you let me know your initial thoughts?  If you require more details on the current process, I could ask Tracy Barlow Bacon to get in touch with you.

 

Any questions please let me know.

 

Many thanks

Julie

 

Julie Hickman

HR Systems Trainer

I have looked at some historic quotes for previous development scripting work, and this has typically cost around 700 FlexPoints. On that basis, I am assuming a similar level for the HESA Module UDF corrections.
Assumptions:
- College Data (Level 2 shortfall): c.4,000 FlexPoints

Kevin

emails.subject: Re: Org Structure Dashboard
emails.to: Simon Burford; Michael O'Sullivan; Asta Palmer; James Salas Guillen
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Thank you for sharing this - the dashboard is very useful and will be helpful when reconciling updates between PeopleXD and PACS. I will incorporating the dashboard link into the relevant org structure guidance and process documentation.
I noticed that many of the lower match percentages appear to stem from descriptive differences caused by the short name (abbreviation) due to the 40-character limit within PeopleXD. There are also cases such as the diaeresis in “Saïd Business School”, which cannot be used in PeopleXD.
Should we accept descriptive mismatches where they are caused by known system constraints, and if so, is there a way to refine the dashboard.
Best,
Kevin
________________________________

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 27 February 2026 5:29 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael O'Sullivan <michael.osullivan@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Org Structure Dashboard 
 

Hi all,

 

Following a recent conversation with Kevin and some work I’ve been doing in the data warehouse to map PeopleXD hierarchy data with the PACS org structure in the warehouse I’ve come up with a dashboard which highlights differences between the two which I hope can be utilised to automate some of the checking that we have to do as part of the org structure updates.

 

I’ve given you all access, the link is here:

https://app.powerbi.com/groups/51d48682-2a16-41ed-b5e7-c4e4e97900a7/reports/c3add62d-f5ea-47b7-ae94-db08608705fc?ctid=cc95de1b-97f5-4f93-b4ba-fe68b852cf91&pbi_source=linkShare

 

Can someone please update any guidance or knowledge articles relating to the org structure maintenance with a link to this dashboard so it doesn’t get forgotten about? 

 

It has three tabs:

Tab 1: Matched Values

 

This tab assesses how closely the reference data names match where I’ve been able to determine a match between PeopleXD data and the PACS Org Structure in the warehouse. This can be used to identify where the PACS Structure has updated the names of codes which we already have set up in PeopleXD and decide if we want to make the change in PeopleXD. 

 

For each level, Division, Department and Management Unit the dashboard will assess how closely the descriptions match and will highlight any which don’t in order of how far away they are from matching. For example, under Departments there’s a big mismatch between Area Studies in PeopleXD and Oxford School of Global and Area Studies in the PACS Org Structure:



 

Looks like we may have missed this as an update to the structure, or PACS didn’t include it in the change schedule, but this will allow us to pick it up and fix it in PeopleXD.

 

Some of these may not match because the PACS “Short” code is actually really long still, for example Oxford Lifelong Learning International Programmes is 49 characters long, and we have a character limit of 40 in PeopleXD. Kevin has already picked up on this I think and is talking to PACS about actually providing a short name that’s short enough for us to use, so once they do this we might get a better match percentage. So this is just going to be a tool to help the analysis of the differences, rather than a descriptive list of tasks to undertake but hopefully it can replace any analysis currently done in Excel.

 

Tab 2: Codes missing from PACS Structure

 

This tab highlights any codes which are in PeopleXD but aren’t in the PACS structure. There could be lots of reasons for this, for example there’s no match for our Management Units for Kellogg College because Kellogg as a department lives at level 2 in the PACS org structure so matches at department code level but there’s an extra level added to PeopleXD. Other r

Hi Mike and Ann-Marie,
Following our internal review, we are close to confirming the preferred support level and progressing to PO/Flexpoints. I will confirm separately once we are in a position to raise this.

In the meantime, it would be helpful to understand anticipated timelines from your side. Based on the draft project plan, could you please advise:

*	
	Whether any further discovery or formal scoping sessions would be required before delivery begins
*	
	Your earliest indicative start date


Target dates for the following phases:

*	
	Scoping sessions – discovery, scoping and follow up
*	
	Training sessions – data migration, reference data, hierarchy
*	
	Configuration (UAT) – hierarchy, menu profile, pay group
*	
	Data migration (UAT) – template loads
*	
	UAT – admin and employee testing
*	
	UAT sign-off
*	
	Configuration (Live)
*	
	Data migration (Live)
*	
	Live account creation
*	
	Live updates (access, reports, alerts)
*	
	Live roll-out and sign-off

Please let me know if a short call would be easier.
Best,
Kevin

Hi Simon

We would have batches of new records where it would be far easier and less error prone to load them - and once a process had been established I don’t think this should present much work for HRS. I would envisage this would be 50- 70  records a year, most starting in October. Manually keying all new records would be a considerable burden on my team. What I thought we had agreed was any amendments would be updated manually (eg FTE changes, end dates) and thus no process would be required to load record amendments. I am particularly concerned about this as I have a recruitment hold on the admin post in my team.

Best wishes

Anne

Thank you for this explanation.

 

Just to caution that the use of the term ‘anonymised’- Arguably there may be sufficient attributes (gender, ethnicity, DOB and Postcode), which if retained at an individual applicant level, may be a sufficient to enable an identifiable profile of an individual to be constructed. Can you clarify how long these attributes are required to be retained for?

 

Best wishes
Nina

emails.subject: TIMDEP Go-Live – HR Bulletin Content
emails.body:
Hi Michelle,
We are ready to proceed with the TIMDEP and CoreTime report updates and wanted to know if we should include this in the next HR Bulletin.
There are a few key points to highlight in the communication:

*	
	Report renames:
	“TIMDEP02 Casuals Only – Leave Entitlement” will be renamed to
	“TIMDEP02 Leave Entitlement for Accruing Casual and Variable Hours Staff (v4)”.
*	
	Security model alignment:
	The reports will now align with People Management security. Users may notice differences in results compared to previous runs, as data will reflect their system security permissions.
*	
	Historic absence cut-off:
	TIMDEP03 Sickness Absence Reasons (v4) and TIMDEP04 Absence Reasons (v2) will only return absence records with a start date on or after 1 August 2021.

Could you confirm when you would like this to go live so we can align the bulletin timing with the change implementation?
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 






Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention
emails.to: Data Protection; HR Systems Support; Marie Cooksey
emails.body:
Hi Joshua,
Thank you for your email. Please see our responses below.
Erasure of Unsuccessful Application Data
We are in the process of deleting Vanessa Beaumont’s unsuccessful application records that fall outside the six-month retention window. We will confirm once this has been completed.
6-Month vs 26-Month Retention
We acknowledge the misalignment and are addressing this as part of our wider applicant data retention review. Our guidance is being aligned with the University retention schedule, and we will confirm the agreed position shortly.
PeopleXD Cleardown Process
The automated cleardown process in PeopleXD is not currently live. Testing is underway and subject to business change considerations, a realistic go-live timeline is likely to be within the next few months.
In the interim, manual erasure requests will continue to be processed in line with the retention schedule.
Cross-Department Application History — Access and Safeguards
We have reviewed the  PeopleXD system access and can confirm:

*	
	Visibility in PeopleXD is department specific. Users can only view applications for departments to which they have access.
*	
	Cross-department visibility is restricted to users who hold both a recruitment administrator menu profile and recruitment system user/HR access. This is a limited group and does not apply to general departmental HR staff.
*	
	Access is controlled through system access groups and restricted to recruiting administrators. There are no additional configuration settings to further limit visibility beyond manual access adjustments, if required.

Based on this, departmental HR staff cannot view application history across the University unless they hold specific recruitment administrator privileges.

Please let me know if you have any further queries.

Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 





________________________________

From: Data Protection <data.protection@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 4:40 PM
To: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention 


Dear All,

 

The Information Compliance Team has received a query and erasure request from an individual named Vanessa Beaumont, who has made a series of historic job applications (19) since 02-Aug-2016. She is concerned that data related to her previous unsuccessful applications is being stored for an excessive period of time. She has therefore requested these be removed from.

 

Firstly, please can you review her profile and erase any application data where the outcome was recorded as unsuccessful in accordance with the stated retention period <https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/retention-periods-for-university-personnel-records#:~:text=6%20months%20following%20the%20end%20of%20the%20recruitment%20process%20(subject%20to%20rules%20in%20respect%20of%20sponsorship%20application%20as%20set%20out%20above)>  of 6 months for unsuccessful job applicants? Please email me to confirm once this has been actioned.

 

I have two further queries related to this matter from our perspective:

 

*	I understand that there has been some misalignment over the 6 month period linked above and the 26 month period listed in the job applicant privacy notice <https://compliance.admin.ox.ac.uk/job-applicant-privacy-policy#collapse966156:~:text=If%20you%20are%20unsuccessful%2C%20your%20data%20will%20be%20deleted%20no%20more%20than%2026%20months%20after%20the%20final%20decision%20is%20recorded%20on%20the%20vacancy%2C%20unless%20it%20is%20required%20to%20be%20retained%20for%20longer%

emails.subject: Re: HESA Module UDFs - technical issues
emails.to: Sarah Rowles; Marie Cooksey; Michael O'Sullivan; Simon Burford
emails.body:
Hi Sarah,
I wanted to confirm where we are with the HESA Module UDF deletions.
I am reviewing our current Flexpoints commitments and would like to understand whether this needs to be prioritised before 1 July. Can you confirm the timeline? Do we need this completed before rollover, or is there a specific deadline driving this work?
It’s worth noting that, while no charge has been mentioned, developer-led script work is likely to be treated as chargeable.
Best,
Kevin
________________________________

I will prepare a list/some logic for the deletions then.

 

Best wishes,

 

Sarah

@Sarah Rowles <mailto:sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>   Yes, you’re still fine to load HESA records for 2024/25 up until the rollover.

On the Bulk Deletions side, there’s been no mention of any charge at this stage, just an offer to refer it to the developers to approve and run. They’ve asked for a list of the UDF entries (or the requirements on what to include/exclude) so they can prepare a script for the deletion.

Continuing Education continues to be the Level 1 entity name with Lifelong Learning being introduced as a title in level 2 and level 3 entities in the official org structure:

All of these departments have also not been updated:

Simon

emails.subject: Re: DTP1092 PXD Initiative
emails.body:
Hi Both,

Crispin has asked whether we are able to contribute FlexPoints towards the Level 2 support for the College Data initiative, so I think we should align internally before responding.

Level 2 is approximately £18,000 incl. VAT (c. £15,400 ex VAT / 9,800 FlexPoints). Assuming the project’s proposed £11,000 contribution is inclusive of VAT, we would need to cover approximately 4,000 FlexPoints to make up the remaining balance until renewal on 1 July.

We currently have 9,410 FlexPoints available. This does not take into account any upcoming HESA-related work, which may also require FlexPoint allocation.

Can we agree what level of contribution we are comfortable making, given existing commitments and anticipated demand?

Happy to discuss.

Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, February 26, 2026 8:40 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 PXD Initiative 


Thanks Simon – the staff cohort is dominated by junior research fellows and career development fellows, which tend to be fixed term contracts of ca 3 years. There are some permanent roles (e.g. All Souls senior research fellows) but they are in the minority.

Best wishes

Anne

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 25 February 2026 16:01
To: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DTP1092 PXD Initiative

 

Hi Anne,

 

Sorry, I thought we’d agreed on the manual approach but clearly I’ve misunderstood. I don’t think there’s any issues with using the data loading process, 50 – 70 records is quite a large number so I understand why you’d want to take this route (although we haven’t seen how much effort the data loading will take of course!). Happy to support whatever’s needed, I just didn’t expect there to be a 25% turnover of staff records each year so assumed it would be more work than necessary (not for our team) to go down the data loading route.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Anne Mortimer <anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:anne.mortimer@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: 25 February 2026 13:07
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk> >; Crispin Muncaster <crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk <mailto:crispin.muncaster@it.ox.ac.uk> >; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Re: DTP1092 PXD Initiative

 

Hi Simon

We would have batches of new records where it would be far easier and less error prone to load them - and once a process had been established I don’t think this should present much work for HRS. I would envisage this would be 50- 70  records a year, most starting in October. Manually keying all new records would be a considerable burden on my team. What I thought we had agreed was any amendments would be updated manually (eg FTE changes, end dates) and thus no process would be required to load record amendments. I am particularly concerned about this as I have a recruitment hold on the admin post in my team.

Best wishes

Anne

________________________________

Sent: 25 February 2026 11:22
To: Crispin Mun

emails.subject: Re: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Before I respond to the Information Compliance team, can you confirm whether the automated cleardown process in PeopleXD is currently live?
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Data Protection <data.protection@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 25 February 2026 4:40 PM
To: HR Systems Support <hr.systems@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: ER/20260223/2 - Query related to PeopleXD data retention 
 

Dear All,

 

The Information Compliance Team has received a query and erasure request from an individual named Vanessa Beaumont, who has made a series of historic job applications (19) since 02-Aug-2016. She is concerned that data related to her previous unsuccessful applications is being stored for an excessive period of time. She has therefore requested these be removed from. 

 

Firstly, please can you review her profile and erase any application data where the outcome was recorded as unsuccessful in accordance with the stated retention period <https://hr.admin.ox.ac.uk/retention-periods-for-university-personnel-records#:~:text=6%20months%20following%20the%20end%20of%20the%20recruitment%20process%20(subject%20to%20rules%20in%20respect%20of%20sponsorship%20application%20as%20set%20out%20above)>  of 6 months for unsuccessful job applicants? Please email me to confirm once this has been actioned.

 

I have two further queries related to this matter from our perspective:

 

*	I understand that there has been some misalignment over the 6 month period linked above and the 26 month period listed in the job applicant privacy notice <https://compliance.admin.ox.ac.uk/job-applicant-privacy-policy#collapse966156:~:text=If%20you%20are%20unsuccessful%2C%20your%20data%20will%20be%20deleted%20no%20more%20than%2026%20months%20after%20the%20final%20decision%20is%20recorded%20on%20the%20vacancy%2C%20unless%20it%20is%20required%20to%20be%20retained%20for%20longer%20to%20comply%20with%20our%20legal%20or%20reporting%20requirements.%C2%A0> . May I ask if we are any closer to resolving this conflict?
*	I understand there is an ongoing issue with applicant cleardown in HR Systems. Has any progress been made with the supplier to resolve this issue? Other than ensuring we are complying with the storage limitation principle described in the UK GDPR, these requests are likely to recur without a scheduled deletion process in place, so it is within our interest to resolve this issue without undue delay to prevent unnecessary work.

 

Secondly, they believe that HR staff in all departments can see (via PeopleXD) an applicant's full application history across the University, including where/when they applied and outcomes, and that this could introduce unfairness into the recruitment process. They have asked us to confirm:

 

*	Which staff groups/University of Oxford departments can see my cross-department application history, and for what purpose? and;
*	What safeguards exist to prevent bias or misuse of this information?

 

Any insight you can provide on these points would be helpful.

 

Please let me know if you have any further queries.

 

With kind regards

 

Joshua Hodgson

Information Compliance Officer

 

Information Compliance Team

University of Oxford, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

https://compliance.admin.ox.ac.uk <https://compliance.admin.ox.ac.uk/>

Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>

Hi Kevin,

 

Can we please add this item to the scoping meeting? It’s another issue that is critical to our system, but we’ve been signposted towards using CAP again.

Best wishes,

 

James

 

From: Cority Customer Support <helpdesk@cority.com>
Sent: 20 February 2026 15:10
To: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Primary Job Position (PJP) not updating for inactive clients [ ref:_00DE0JBMl._500OFUCgDV:ref ] 00488061.

 

Hi James,

I tested the behaviour on a test site.

If we uncheck the primary job position, the most recently assigned primary job position will remain displayed on the registration page. This also applies if the job position is deactivated in the lookup table. Please see the attached recording.

BR-100551 is a Cority out?of?the?box business rule that is used by almost all customers without any issues. Since you have custom configuration, we recommend raising a CAP request to further review the case.

Thanks,

Alessandra





---------------------------------------------------------------
02/17/2026 09:54:14 AM EST [james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> ]
---------------------------------------------------------------

Noticed a related issue when running a different Business Rule in the Post Title entity. UOX_ClearanceStatus runs as expected. However, the Employment Details section (GDDLO) is not updated. I would expect this to be picked up by the BR-100551 Business Rule. However, if I go to the individual record and simply click 'Save' the Employment Details is then populated.

---------------------------------------------------------------
02/17/2026 06:22:19 AM EST [james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> ]
---------------------------------------------------------------


To: helpdesk@cority.com <mailto:helpdesk@cority.com> 

[Attachments: image001.png, image002.jpg, image003.png]
Hi Alessandra,
Thank you for looking into this for us.
I’ve checked the reports that were flagged, and they do appear to interfere with the unticking of the Primary Job Position. I disabled the UOX-PJP-03 business rule in the Test environment, and the unticking no longer occurs, which is good news. However, I’ve now noticed that the Cority?created rule BR?100551 conflicts with our intended logic, so we are currently reviewing all Primary Job Position business rules internally.
I just wanted to confirm one point with you:
When a client leaves the University and all Post Titles become inactive (and therefore no primary job position is ticked), should the system display the most recently ticked Primary Job Position in the Employment Details (GDDLO) section? Or would this require additional logic for that behaviour to occur?
Best wishes,
James Salas
Senior Functional Analyst
HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

emails.subject: Re: DSE file uploads - Clarification on the process and interim and long term solution
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
I agree with James’ response.
There was no formal agreement following our follow-up meeting with Ralph and Claire that HR Systems would take ownership of the manual uploads.
The proposed interim method would be to add new starters to the most recent spreadsheet rather than generate a completely new full extract each time, to minimise the risk of unintended changes.
I have advised Ralph that we have a monthly report covering new starters within a defined date range. This will help quantify the changes required each month.
As outlined in James’ email, the question of where this responsibility will sit remains open, with Ralph confirming he would check Safety Office admin capacity.
Best,
Kevin
________________________________

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 24 February 2026 1:28 PM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: DSE file uploads - Clarification on the process and interim and long term solution 
 

Hi Marie, 

 

As outlined in Rachel’s email, it was anticipated that there would be some remaining work after project closure. This is why I asked for documentation on the data‑import process into the system. That documentation was only received a week after the project officially closed, following a meeting that Kevin and I attended with Ralph.

It’s important to note that the documentation still included the question: “Who would carry out this process?”

 

The same question was raised again during our most recent meeting with Ralph on 12th February, which Kevin and I also attended.

My understanding from that discussion was that Ralph would check whether the Safety Office admin team had capacity to take this on. Weekly uploads were suggested (by Chris Williams), although monthly uploads were considered more manageable, given the task takes approximately two hours to complete before the file can be uploaded into the Cardinus system.

 

In response to your questions:

 

*	Did you agree to take over ownership of uploads from Ralph during the project?

No, I did not. I specifically requested the documentation to understand the level of effort involved, not to take ownership. I did agree to take on the work with the AIP Team regarding testing the automation with different possible data import scenarios. 

 

*	Who will oversee these uploads, and am I confident I can take this on?

From the way discussions have progressed, it appears this may fall to HR Systems if the Safety Office does not have capacity. I can take this on temporarily if absolutely necessary, but it will require protected time (two hours per week or 8 hours per month depending on the agreed frequency). If the import is to be done weekly it will also require either Kevin or someone from the team to run the import if I’m on annual leave for longer than a week. 

 

*	Has the Integration Team been in contact regarding DSE?

Yes. I attended the Sprint Review this morning, and DSE work is scheduled to begin in the sprint starting 25th February. Sprint Reviews take place every Tuesday at 10am and cover all University systems, not just DSE. I’ve forwarded the meeting to Kevin for awareness.

 

Final point, I am willing to support this process if it cannot sit elsewhere, but I do need my time protected to ensure I can complete the uploads accurately and sustainably. I’m also conscious that this is BAU work that wasn’t part of the project handover plan, so clarity on long‑term ownership would be helpful as there is still no deadline for when the automation will be completed.

 

Happy to discuss further if needed.

 

Best wishes,

 

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department| University of Oxford

 

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting> 

 

Whatfix: Discover our self-help tool for People

emails.subject: Re: P5 Task assigned to your team - Task# 50930353 for Incident# 11660588
emails.body:
Hi Michael,
As the error appears to be occurring at parameter level rather than within the SQL itself, could you please enable “Allow null value” for the Personnel Number parameter in the test environment and re-run the report with Personnel Number left blank.
The SQL is written to handle a NULL value and this aligns with the published guidance, so this will confirm whether the current behaviour is being driven by the parameter configuration.
Let me know if you need my help.
Best,
Kevin
________________________________

Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: P5 Task assigned to your team - Task# 50930353 for Incident# 11660588 
 

Hi Kevin, 

 

We’ve received a query about the PERDEP09 Whitebook Full Details report, specifically that the Personnel Number parameter is now being treated as mandatory.  Given that the report doesn’t appear to have been updated since Jun ’23, it seems odd that this behaviour would suddenly change, although this report isn’t run very often, so it may only just have been noticed.

 

I’d really appreciate some help with this one, please….

 

 

The SQL currently includes the following condition:

 

(PRBI_HR_PERSON_MASTER.PERSON_REFERENCE = :PersonnelNumber OR :PersonnelNumber IS NULL)

 

 

Parameter set as below.

Best wishes,

Michael

Task Details:

Via Information Compliance

 

I work in the University’s Information Compliance Team, which is responsible for processing requests made by individuals in exercise of their rights under the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR).

 

BACKGROUND

 

Under the UK GDPR, an individual has the right to request an organisation to erase the data that it holds about them (the right to be forgotten). There is no requirement to give reasons for such a request. The right to erasure is not absolute; an organisation is obliged to comply with a request only if one or more of the following conditions are met:

 

a.	the data is no longer needed;
b.	the data has been processed on the basis of the individual’s consent and the individual then withdraws their consent;
c.	the individual objects to the data being processed, and there are no grounds for overriding their objections; or
d.	the data has been unlawfully processed.

 

REQUEST

 

Petar RADANLIEV (DOB: 07/08/1979) has requested that the University erase all previous job applications from the portal older than 26 months, in line with the University’s retention policy.

 

WHAT YOU NEED TO DO

 

Please advise whether you have any concerns about the consequences of deleting the da

emails.subject: PeopleXD DPIA v0.2 – Review
emails.body:
Hi Simon,

I’m sharing the updated  ​PeopleXD Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) v0.2 <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/HumanResources-HRSystems/Shared%20Documents/Functional%20Analysis/Data%20Protection%20Impact%20Assessment%20(DPIA)/PeopleXD%20-%20Data%20Protection%20Impact%20Assessment%20v0.2.docx?d=w3fd9253391e84a708d065333d969075b&csf=1&web=1> ​ for your review ahead of a meeting to discuss.

The document has been updated following comments from Ruzha Toneva in the Information Compliance Team.
Key updates include:

*	
	Scope clarified — Workforce Management explicitly excluded and cross-referenced to the separate B2 WFM DPIA; AI/analytics removed
*	
	Processing activities expanded — all personal data categories documented, including Bio fields, recruitment data, and special category data
*	
	Lawful basis corrected — Article 6(1)(b) and 6(1)(c) applied in place of Public Task; Article 9 conditions updated accordingly
*	
	Retention periods aligned with the University’s published HR retention schedule
*	
	Data sharing table updated to include HMRC and college arrangements
*	
	Risk assessment updated — residual risk ratings corrected and previously missing medium risks added to Stage 6


Could you please review the document ahead of our meeting for discussion

I'll find us a slot in the next week or so.

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

emails.body:
Dear Subhankar,

 

Thank you for your email and for your interest in working for the University of Oxford.

I can see from the system logs that you've requested a password reset a few times. It's possible that the reset emails may have been filtered into your spam or junk folder, so please do check there if you haven’t already.

To log in, please follow the steps below:

1.	Go to the login page and enter your email address
2.	
	Enter the temporary password: 546585328
3.	Click the Login button

Please note: it’s best to type the details manually rather than copying and pasting, as this can sometimes cause recognition issues.

Once logged in, you'll be prompted to enter your existing temporary password (546585328) and then set up a new password.. Your new password must meet the following criteria:

*	Between 16 and 30 characters
*	Must include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character
*	Please avoid using the following characters: @ * & $ £ %

If you experience any further difficulties when updating your password, I recommend trying an alternative web browser to see if that helps.

Please don’t hesitate to get in touch if you need any further support or clarification.


Best wishes

 

Recruitment Technical Support Team
HR Systems |People Department

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: Action raised in CDR/Professional Development meeting this morning
emails.body:
Hi Lindsey,

Apologies for not providing this update sooner. Following the meeting with Access Group Development regarding the Talent template permissions, I can confirm the outcome. 

Access Group reviewed the configuration and confirmed there is currently no functionality available to restrict edit or clone permissions separately within Talent. Permissions to the Talent Admin dashboard is effectively all-or-nothing, and templates cannot be made read-only for some users while retaining edit rights for others.

As such, there is no system fix currently available. In the absence of a technical solution, the mitigation is user education and clear guidance on how templates should be cloned and assigned.

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Sent: Tuesday, February 17, 2026 11:58 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Action raised in CDR/Professional Development meeting this morning 


Hi Kevin

 

Thanks for joining us today. I’ve had a look at the transcript for the last CDR/Professional Development meeting and I now think I understand what this action was about:

 

HR Systems (Kevin) to explore permission controls preventing departments from editing shared templates 

 

It seems that CDR templates were inadvertently cloned by another department and there was a glitch around departments being able to basically mess with the templates. Rachel had asked how we can prevent that in the future. You had mentioned that you were currently looking into it and that a discussion had taken place with Access Group to see whether we can prevent that from happening again. You also mentioned the need to look into regarding permissions and see if we can lock them down being an area of concern, but couldn’t confirm at the time if that could be done. You had given all the information to Access Group, and they had taken it away and were going to come back if there was any solution.

 

If there has been any update, please let me know and I will communicate this to the other attendees.

 

Many thanks

 

Lindsey

 

 

Lindsey Spriggs (she/her)

Research Culture Facilitator

emails.subject: Re: Support model for HR Case Management - ownership by HR
emails.body:
Hi Bea,
Thank you for checking.
I have no objections to approval notifications being sent to you and Issy directly. That said, for best practice, a shared mailbox would help prevent duplicate responses, as you would both be working from a single inbox. It also provides better visibility and continuity, with a clearer audit trail and simpler access management when staff join or leave, without needing to involve the OSM team.
Let me know if you need support with raising the request for a shared mailbox.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

-Bea (she/her)

 

Bea Krzysztan

HR Business Partner, Medical Sciences Division

5 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

Sent: 13 February 2026 12:03

Cc: Isobel Stokoe <isobel.stokoe@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Support model for HR Case Management - ownership by HR

 

Hi Bea

 

As they say no question is a silly question, and yes this is how you would want to raise a support call if you were having technical issues etc with Halo. It is like raising a normal IT support call, you just need to ensure you select the Halo CRM (ER) Support option. Key thing to remind users of is no personal case information should be included as this is going to an IT individual who should not be aware of anything sensitive.

 

Regards the shared mailbox I don’t think it should be an issue if it is your own inboxes, but I will invite @Kevin Lelitte <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>  to comment as he will be more informed than I am.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

emails.subject: College Data – Recommended Support Level and Next Steps
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

Following our discussion with Simon and the team, and review of Access Group’s clarifications, we recommend proceeding with Level 2 support for the College Data non-payroll company setup.

While Level 1 is technically workable, it would leave menu profile configuration, ESS account setup, and associated access administration to be delivered internally. Given the complexity of menu profile design, this would require dedicated internal resource to manage. Level 2 provides support in these areas while retaining internal control of the company structure and hierarchy configuration.

Although Level 3 includes additional configuration and impact review support, configuration ownership and downstream validation would still require our oversight. The additional cost does not provide sufficient added value beyond Level 2.
Access Group have confirmed that review of alerts and reporting behaviour has not been included within the scope in Level 2, but can be included if required.

We recommend that the following are explicitly included within project scope:

*	
	Formal downstream impact assessment (alerts, reporting, interfaces, data warehouse feeds)
*	
	A project UAT testing resource, with provision of dedicated mobile hardware for app testing
*	
	A project nominated administrative resource (separate from the tester) to manage account setup, access maintenance, and day-to-day administration following go-live


Please let me know if you require any further details.

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/>

Hi Crispin,

 

I can confirm that there will be no charge to DTP1092 for this work. Based on your note about the project focus shifting next month, delivery by the end of February is achievable, provided the work is scheduled promptly.

 

Please see the clarification below:

 

Objective
Enable the addition of new onboarding checklist fields in PeopleXD (e.g. ORCID) without triggering retrospective onboarding notifications for existing staff. This allows ORCID capture to be introduced safely as part of onboarding.

 

Steps

*	Confirm and document the scripted “task clear-down” approach validated by Access Group (HR Systems / Access Group).
*	Finalise the ORCID checklist requirements (DTP1092 / business owners).
*	Deploy the new checklist item in PeopleXD onboarding (Access Group).
*	Execute scripted clear-down to prevent legacy notifications (Access Group, supported by HR Systems).
*	Validate outcome and confirm readiness for use (HR Systems).

 

Timings

*	Scoping and confirmation of requirements: 1 week (Already done but will require final confirmation)
*	Scheduling and deployment: 1–2 weeks
*	Validation and sign-off: 1 week

 

 

Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.to: AnnMarie Topliss; Amy Coogan; Mike West
emails.body:
Hi Ann-Marie,

Following your recent email to Marie, we have reviewed the L1 and L2 scope options for the non-pay employee implementation. I’d like to clarify a few points before we confirm the level of support.

Could you please confirm the following in terms of Access Group responsibility under Level 1 and Level 2 support:
Data loading

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to data loading — does this include execution of data loads and investigation/resolution of load failures, or is support only limited to training and review?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, what additional responsibility does Access Group take in relation to data loading and any issue resolution?

Configuration

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to configuration — does this include applying configuration changes (e.g. hierarchy items and reference data) in UAT and Live, or is support limited to training and guidance?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, does Access Group take responsibility for configuration activities such as the Non-Pay Pay Group setup, and are there any additional configuration responsibilities included?

Test accounts

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to test account creation and management — is this activity included within L1 support?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, does Access Group take responsibility for test account setup and ongoing management during UAT?

Roles, access, and profiles

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to roles, access, and profiles — does the activity “Set up of new menu profile for new non-pay employees” include broader access configuration such as org roles or security model updates?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, does Access Group take responsibility for any additional access or security configuration required?

Alerts and reporting

*	
	Under Level 1 support, what is Access Group responsible for in relation to system alerts, onboarding communications, and reporting behaviour for non-pay employees — is any review or validation of these included?
*	
	Under Level 2 support, does Access Group provide additional review or validation of alerts, communications, or reporting impacts?


Thanks in advance, and happy to discuss if a short call would be easier.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: Leavers process
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

Removal from Teams channel and meeting chats is a manual process, there is no it OSM self-service for this.

emails.subject: Re: Quick Q on My Development functionality: Annual CDR and professional development data for the Concordat Action Plan
emails.body:
Hi Rachel, Lindsey
Apologies for the delayed reply.
Yes - it is technically possible for departments to use My Development to log professional development activity only, without using the P/CDR elements of the module.
However, My Development was implemented to support Career Development Reviews for Researchers, and any use outside that core purpose would be unsupported beyond the existing published guidance.
In relation to the Concordat data, departments using My Development can access standard reports within PeopleXD/Insight to view the information they have recorded. However, we are not able to pull reports on behalf of departments or map system data to align with the questions used in the survey.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Rachel Bray <rachel.bray@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, February 05, 2026 1:48 PM
To: Lindsey Spriggs <lindsey.spriggs@socsci.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Quick Q on My Development functionality: Annual CDR and professional development data for the Concordat Action Plan 


Hi Kevin,

Sorry to fire another question your way – I think and hope it will be easy and quick.

 

Please could you remind me: is it possible for departments to use My Development for logging professional development days only? i.e. without using the P/CDR elements of the module?

 

This question came up in Research Staff Working Group because several departments are understandably unwilling to run different PDR/CDR systems for different staff groups, so want to use My Development for logging professional development time only, until it is possible to use it (or another replacement system) for annual reviews for all staff.

 

Thanks and all best

Rachel

Hi Kevin

 I am getting in touch regarding the work that has been underway to collect data annual CDR and professional development data for the Concordat Action Plan. Rachel has recently been using the following questionnaire to manually survey departments that do not currently use My Development to capture this information: Annual reporting data for the Concordat Action Plan - baseline data to December 2025 <https://forms.office.com/Pages/DesignPageV2.aspx?origin=NeoPortalPage&subpage=design&id=G96VzPWXk0-0uv5ouFLPkdtbhC7F_yZGox4v3Q0GvuFUQzk3NlBKTVY5TzZONEJWUFNOQ1AyVTdVNSQlQCN0PWcu&analysis=false> . However, to get a full picture, we would also like to obtain comparable data from depts that *are* using My Development. We wondered whether there were reports you might be able to pull from People XD that approximate (at least some of) the questions we have asked in the survey I have just shared? Happy to have a quick call to discuss in more detail, if this would be helpful.

On behalf of Rachel Bray (CCed) and myself, 

 

Best wishes

 

Lindsey

emails.subject: Re: WFM Rollout Timing – ESS Alignment
emails.body:
Hi Tracy,
Thanks for the update.
Yes, this relates to the ESS API. Do you have a view on when you would prefer this to go live, given the decision pending on the two larger areas?
The integration team is ready to proceed. From our side, our preference would be to align go-live with completion of the final WFM department rollouts, to avoid manual workload and reduce additional support queries for departments not yet live.
Happy to have a quick call to discuss once you have an update on the decision.
Best,
Kevin

Is this the ESS API?  It is part of the WFM project so I need to keep updated on rollout as I need to report back to board on this.

In terms of departments going live, we envisage the last one's to go live in March at the latest and we're aiming for early March with those.  However, I have board today as we're waiting on a decision regarding 2 big areas who are requesting a go live later this year after the project team are no longer here so I can update you once that decision is made.

Kind regards
Tracy

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 06 February 2026 16:30
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Athena Artuso <athena.artuso@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: H&S Management System

 

Hello

The project team would like to do a briefing session with selected members of team (ie that will find it of interested and can contribute), regarding the purchase of the H&S management system. This will allow us to input into the functional and non-functional system requirements.

 

Can you please share any names on this thread that you would like to attend?

H&S High level Requirements

This section converts findings from Focus and our stakeholder engagement sessions into clear goals to inform solution design and to support when meeting with potential, future suppliers:

a) The University requires an enterprise-wide capability to create, maintain, review, approve and monitor health and safety risk assessments across all departments, divisions, buildings, and risk types using consistent standards and workflows to reduce likelihood of accidents, incidents and near misses.

b) The solution must support the creation, retention and retrieval of auditable records that demonstrate compliance with Health and Safety legislation and regulatory requirements, including clear evidence of approvals, reviews and controls to reduce the likelihood of fines, and improve evidence of compliance.

c) The University requires a capability to clearly define, monitor and enforce mitigation measures for specific risk areas, this also includes fire, waste, biosafety, radiation, hazardous chemicals, and biological agents.

d) The solution must support a structured assurance framework, enabling inspections, audits, self-assurance activities and formal reporting at departmental, divisional, and institutional levels to improve assurance and governance and establish clear responsibilities.

e) The University requires a centrally managed mechanism to assign, track and escalate actions from risk assessments, inspections, audits, and incidents, including responsibilities and timescales.

f) The solution must provide accurate, aggregated management information which includes dashboards, reports and analytics to support trends, performance and risk profiles.

g) The solution must support Health and Safety processes for research activities, including high risk research, and provide evidence of compliance with regulation requirements.

h) The solution must have capabilities to integrate with relevant, wider used, institutional systems such as identity, building information, estates, incident reporting, asset management, training records, data warehouse to reduce data inconsistencies.

i) The solution must be scalable to support a phased implementation and adoption across departments and divisions that have varying risk profiles but also maintaining consistent governance, standards and controls.

j) The solution must offer modern, user-friendly f

emails.body:
Hi Tony,
Apologies for the delayed response.
@David Hickman <mailto:david.hickman@it.ox.ac.uk>  – thanks for the detail in your email below.
Following our internal discussions, we would appreciate your team’s support in helping us gain assurance around the API’s initial live deprovisioning behaviour. Specifically, could you provide an output showing the list of records that would be deprovisioned on the first live run, without performing the deprovisioning itself? This would allow us to validate first-run outcomes and confirm there is no unexpected loss of access when the API is enabled in Live.
Once we’ve reviewed that output, we’ll be in a position to confirm next steps and timing from our side.
Happy to discuss if helpful, but hopefully this clarifies what we’re asking for.
Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Fw: Organisational Structure update - February 2026 - Supplemental change, and change to approval process
emails.body:
________________________________

From: Organisational Structure <orgstructure@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 05 February 2026 2:18 PM
To: 'orgstructure@maillist.ox.ac.uk' <orgstructure@maillist.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Organisational Structure update - February 2026 - Supplemental change, and change to approval process 
 

Dear all,

 

In this email:

Supplemental change to Org Structure

Changes to the approval Process for making changes to the Org Structure

 


Supplemental change to Org Structure


The University Organisational Structure has been slightly updated in this supplemental update to revise one of the earlier changes at the request of NDM for administrative purposes, and a time sensitive change and addition to UAS. This update was approved to be made outside of the usual twice a year publication schedule. As such we have isolated these changes in the schedule, but included the final change schedule shared earlier in the week is included in the final published structure also so all adjustments for the period (since September 2025) are included in one document.

 

The latest version of the file can be found on our website: https://governance.admin.ox.ac.uk/university-organisation-structure

(Download link is at the end of the ‘About the Organisational Structure’ section)

 

Updating local systems:

If you are the owner/administrator of a local system (eg. People XD, Card database, Salto, Oracle etc.) please ensure you reflect these changes in those systems, and get in touch if we can support you with this. 

 

Key Changes:

 

Division

Entity

Change

MSD

NDM (2B02-B9)

The update of the name L3 name from:

L3 full name: Tropical Medicine - Centre for Global Health Research 

L3 name: Tropical Medicine - CGHR (Oxford)

 

To:

L3 full name: Centre for Tropical Medicine

L3 name: Tropical Medicine

UAS (Changes)

L2: Academic Resources and Information Systems (8HP0)

 

L3: Academic Resources and Information Systems (8HP0-ML)

Changing the name of L2 entity 8HP0

From: 8HP0 - Academic Resources and Information Systems

To: 8HP0 - Digital Information Services

 

Changing the name of L3 entity ML

From: ML - Academic Resources and Information Systems

To: ML - Data Office

 

UAS (New entities)

L2: Data Office (8HP0-ML) formerly Academic Resources and Information Systems

Adding new L3 entity:

C5 - Cyber Security

 

Adding new L3 entity:

E7 - Office of the CDIO

HESA cost centres 202 for both new entities

 

Previous changes:

The changes approved in the previously published draft can be found below in this email chain for reference.

 

Notes:

*	We have used highlighting in the change schedule to try to make it clearer what changes are happening for the ease of our colleagues whose role it is to update the organisational structure in their own systems – if any other changes to the format for this update could be made that would make this process easier for you please do let us know.

 


Update to Organisational Structure approval process:


 

PACS are delighted to share that the process for approving organisational structure changes has been updated to make the process simpler and more flexible for our departmental and divisional colleagues. Rather than all changes at Level 2 and 3 being approved by divisional Board (or equivalent) it is now up to divisions to decide the level of approval that is required depending on the impact of the change; with the notifying officer (which for divisions is the Divisional Registrar) being accountable for ensuring that an appropriate governance procedure has been followed.

 

The hope is this will make things a bit simpler, and give divisions more discretion for determining appropriate governance procedures for changes.

 

You can find updated guidance detailing this new process on the Organisational Structure Webpages <https://governance.
