From: changenotifications@it.ox.ac.uk <changenotifications@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 06 February 2026 17:03
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Approval# 10112817 for Change# 20019742 - PeopleXD – Switch Email Delivery to Access Group SMTP

 

 

Dear Marie Cooksey

You have been added as an approver to the following Change. Please respond prior to the CAB Expiration Date.

By clicking the button below and selecting the Self Service Role, you will be taken directly to your "Approval" to take the appropriate action.

Change: 20019742
Summary: PeopleXD – Switch Email Delivery to Access Group SMTP CAB Expiry Date: 06/02/2026 18:02:00 (UTC) - Etc/UTC
Priority: 5
Category: Configuration

Open:Approval <https://oxford.saasiteu.com/Login.aspx?Scope=SelfService&role=SelfService&CommandId=OpenMyItem&ItemType=FRS_ApprovalVoteTracking&ItemId=4752D37B9E684428939E87499B61B2FB,string> 

Change Details:
1. Details – What is changing?
PeopleXD email delivery will be switched from the legacy BT mail relay to The Access Group–hosted SMTP server (smtp.accessacloud.com).
The sending email address will remain peoplexd@accessacloud.com <mailto:peoplexd@accessacloud.com> . No University DNS changes are required.
________________________________________
2. Justification – Why is the change necessary?
The change is required to modernise email delivery, improve reliability and deliverability, and align PeopleXD with the supplier’s supported SMTP infrastructure. The existing BT relay is legacy and no longer appropriate for ongoing support.
________________________________________
3. Related Changes – Are there dependent changes?
No dependent University changes.
Supplier-side configuration only. UAT has been completed prior to this change.
________________________________________
4. Impact on dependent services
No impact on downstream or connected University services.
Emails continue to be sent from the same address and domain, with no change to routing, recipients, or integrations.
________________________________________
5. Impact – Specify downtime or at-risk period
No planned downtime.
Email delivery may be briefly at risk during the SMTP switch window, but rollback is immediate if required.
________________________________________
6. Impact – Effect of not applying the change
If not applied, PeopleXD will continue to rely on a legacy mail relay, increasing the risk of email delivery issues and limiting supportability.
________________________________________
7. Risk Assessment – What are the risks to the service?
Low risk.
Risks are limited to potential email delivery issues, mitigated by:
• Prior UAT
• Supplier-managed configuration
• Immediate rollback capability
________________________________________
8. Testing – Who will test it and how?
UAT conducted by Kevin Lelitte (Functional Analysis - Team Lead ) with support from:
• Access Group (SMTP configuration and confirmation)
• QAE (test approach oversight)
Testing validated:
• Internal and external email delivery
• SPF/DKIM authentication
• No unintended notifications
________________________________________
9. Implementation Plan – Who will implement it and how?
The Access Group will implement the change by updating PeopleXD configuration to use smtp.accessacloud.com in the LIVE environment,

emails.subject: Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
Thanks — I agree with the points raised.
Before confirming the support level, I want to ensure there is clear agreement with AG on where responsibility sits.
I’ll go back to AG with a small number of clarification questions to confirm this and will copy you and Simon. Once we have that clarity, we should be able to confirm whether L1 is sufficient or whether L2 is the safer option.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, February 09, 2026 9:38 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup 


Hi Simon

 

Thank you for your observations.

 

@Kevin Lelitte <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>  – as you know I don’t want to bring large amounts of work into the team for the duration (unless we seek backfill) of the project or afterwards in BAU. Can I suggest you review, and if you have any further questions, you reach out to AG, before you give me your decision. But, I do not this as soon as possible, as we do need to move forward.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 06 February 2026 15:51
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup

 

Hi Marie,

 

I’ve reviewed this, here are my observations:

 

Difference between L1 and L2

*	Session to walk through loading of templates – the project will get training on how to do this and we have experience of loading data via templates. It takes a bit of getting used to, but I think we can cope without this. The loading of the templates is something we said we could take on as BAU, providing the upload templates are prepared for us and any failures are investigated by the project. This needs to be confirmed with the project to make sure it’s properly resourced beyond AG’s quote.
*	Additional 1 day for UAT support – I think this might be a call for the project, but I would imagine 4 days of UAT support under level 1 would be more than enough. We would need to know if any support would be expected from us to support UAT as this isn’t something we’ve discussed.

 

Difference between L1/2 and L3

*	Configuration of new hierarchy in UAT and LIVE – honestly this is something I was expecting Access Group to be doing. They are doing 0.5 days training on how to add reference data so presumably they think we could cover this ourselves under L1, but again, I don’t think we’ve discussed whether we could support this in BAU. It’s probably ok, I can’t imagine it would take too long as long as requirements were established but we just need roles and responsibilities to be worked out. As team lead for the team who could end up doing this work I think Kevin needs to comment on this one.
*	Set up of test accounts – this kind of thing I hadn’t thought of, and is the type of thing which may be a hidden cost if we don’t go for L3 support. Again, we have the skills to do this, but do we have capacity? Could someone on the project pick this up or would it be as much effort (or more) to train someone as it is to do the work?
*	Update new access / org role requirements – This is another thing I thought AG would cover under L1, depending on what this actually means.  The L1 item “Set up of new menu profile for new non-pay employees” I thought might cover any set up of org roles or anything else that might be needed, but if not then I do have concerns that this would be down to us without the support of AG to guide us on what we needed to set up. Maybe I just don’t understand what this involves?
*	Review update and system alerts or Insight reports – this is also potentially something we’ll need to have someone cover if AG don’t do it. For example, will onboarding tasks / email be sent t

emails.subject: Re: Halo Case Management (ER) and IT Self Service
emails.body:
Hi Carla,
Thanks for the update and outlining the current position.
On the point about the remaining Case Management service requests, I’m not yet clear which specific request types these refer to beyond the two Request for Help forms already in place. If you can provide a bit more detail on what is being considered, that would help.
I’m very happy to work with you to sense-check approvals and process touchpoints, and to help identify where input is needed from other teams, so we can be clear on responsibilities and avoid overlap.
Let me know the best way to pick this up.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 








________________________________

From: Carla Thornley <carla.thornley@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, February 04, 2026 1:07 PM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Louise Piper <louise.piper@it.ox.ac.uk>; Samantha Bateman <sam.bateman@it.ox.ac.uk>; Viktoriia Vasyliuta <viktoriia.vasyliuta@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Halo Case Management (ER) and IT Self Service 


Hi both,

As I promised Kevin, I wanted to provide an update on the steps we’ve taken this morning to put a initial Halo CM support route in place.

Although Lindsey was the only program representative available (in an interim PM capacity), we have the start of a working group and Ben Hill  attended this morning. Louise has now established a recurring fortnightly meeting to progress all IT Self Service Halo offerings.

We have agreed that Halo CRM will be presented to customers as a distinct area within IT Self Service, and to begin with that customers will be advised to raise support calls via IT Self Service.

As the most immediate challenge is the lack of any visibility of Halo within the Service Desk, coupled with unclear guidance for customers on how to raise issues, we have started by defining two “Request for Help” forms: one for Case Management and one for Oxford Events. In both cases, the resulting incidents will generate a task to the CI Squad team (one of Ben Hill’s Software Solutions OSM teams). The Case Management RFH stipulates very clearly that no personal or sensitive information should be included.

As agreed by Sam Bateman, for the next 6 months, throughout their lifecycle, these incidents will be owned by the Central IT Service Desk; however, it is anticipated that the majority of the work will be carried out by the CI Squad. As such, it will be important to closely monitor anything coming in.

With regard to the remaining service requests required for Case Management, I think your involvement will be essential—particularly to help guide approvals and associated processes. While Louise’s fortnightly meeting will ensure progress continues, we are aware that each product will require engagement from different teams. Identifying and securing the right stakeholders has been challenging so far.

Could you let me know how you think we should best manage this, and whether there is anything else you would like us to factor in over the coming months?

Thanks so much

 

Carla

 

Carla Thornley

(interim) Deputy Director, Customer Services

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

emails.subject: Re: Department update - 6 February 2026
emails.body:
Hi Lois,

I wanted to ask for clarification on the parking notice for the spaces inside Gloucester Green bus station, adjacent to 4 & 5 Worcester Street. The notice states that the spaces are reserved for University operational staff, contractors, and deliveries, but also asks staff not to park there. 

Could you please confirm whether Blue Badge holders are permitted to use these spaces, or if alternative accessible parking is provided nearby.

Hi Joanne,

From: Joanne Grant <joanne.grant@ecoonline.com>
Sent: 20 January 2026 4:19 PM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Michael Hanson <michael.hanson@ecoonline.com>
Subject: EcoOnline /University of Oxford - Meeting notes and actions 20/1/26 
 

Information classification: Public



Hi all 

 

Thanks for your time today and here is an overview of notes and actions from our meeting.

 

I can see Mike and Amanda have availability for a call currently :- 

 

*	Tuesday 3/2/26 ( 12pm onwards) 
*	Wednesday 4/2 ( 2pm onwards)
*	Friday 6/2/26 ( Between 11-2 )

 

Let me know which time works for you and I’ll send out the invites.

 

@Michael Hanson <mailto:michael.hanson@ecoonline.com>  can you please provide the API costs to Marie again this week – in case they have changed since last issued. 

 

Many thanks

Jo 

 

 

Recap

Joanne from EcoOnline, along with Christopher, Marie and Kevin from The University of Oxford, discussed a change request from the university to improve the user experience and data collection within the Info Exchange system, focusing on incident reporting and alignment with health and safety best practices. The next step is a technical discovery call with Amanda from EcoOnline to assess the scope and cost of the changes, with the university aiming to implement the changes before the end of July. Marie also requested updated cost information for an API integration and raised concerns about the location field functionality. 


Key points

1.	Meeting Purpose: Joanne explains the call is a discovery session for a change request .
2.	Customer Process Request: Marie requests a documented process for handling change requests and has additional business to discuss.
3.	Change Request Rationale: Chris states the changes originate from a user engagement group, aiming to align with health and safety best practices, improve data quality, and enhance user experience.
4.	Supervisor Field Modification: The first change involves relocating the "supervisor name" and "supervisor email" fields to a new section and making them non-mandatory to facilitate near-miss reporting.
5.	Incident Classification Update: The second change proposes renaming and moving the incident classification field to the DSO review section, adding new options, and migrating existing data.
6.	DSO Review Section Redesign: The third change is a comprehensive redesign of the Departmental Safety Officer (DSO) review section to improve logical flow and usability for trained safety officers.
7.	EcoOnline's Process Clarification: Joanne details EcoOnline's change request process, including discovery calls, technical consultation, quotation, purchase order, project management, and review.
8.	Pre-Commitment Technical Call: Marie requests a technical call to assess feasibility *before* committing to a purchase order, which Joanne agrees to arrange with Amanda for the week of February 2nd.
9.	API Cost Update Request: Marie asks Joanne to follow up with Mike for an updated cost quotation for introducing an API, which was previously delayed.
10.	Account Review & Future Solutions: Joanne suggests a separate call to review the account and discuss mixed feedback on Info Exchange, mentioning potential alternative

emails.subject: Re: Halo Case Management (ER) and IT Self Service
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
I’ve been reviewing an update from Carla on the initial Halo CM support route that’s being put in place.
One point I’m unclear on is a reference to the “remaining Case Management service requests,” where my involvement is suggested to help guide approvals and associated processes. At the moment, I’m not sure what specific request types this is intended to cover beyond the two initial Request for Help forms already defined.
Before I respond, I wanted to check your understanding of what is meant by these remaining service requests, and whether there is any assumed ownership.
I’d appreciate your thoughts on this.
Best,
Kevin



________________________________

From: Carla Thornley <carla.thornley@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, February 04, 2026 1:07 PM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Louise Piper <louise.piper@it.ox.ac.uk>; Samantha Bateman <sam.bateman@it.ox.ac.uk>; Viktoriia Vasyliuta <viktoriia.vasyliuta@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Halo Case Management (ER) and IT Self Service 


Hi both,

As I promised Kevin, I wanted to provide an update on the steps we’ve taken this morning to put a initial Halo CM support route in place.

Although Lindsey was the only program representative available (in an interim PM capacity), we have the start of a working group and Ben Hill  attended this morning. Louise has now established a recurring fortnightly meeting to progress all IT Self Service Halo offerings.

We have agreed that Halo CRM will be presented to customers as a distinct area within IT Self Service, and to begin with that customers will be advised to raise support calls via IT Self Service.

As the most immediate challenge is the lack of any visibility of Halo within the Service Desk, coupled with unclear guidance for customers on how to raise issues, we have started by defining two “Request for Help” forms: one for Case Management and one for Oxford Events. In both cases, the resulting incidents will generate a task to the CI Squad team (one of Ben Hill’s Software Solutions OSM teams). The Case Management RFH stipulates very clearly that no personal or sensitive information should be included.

As agreed by Sam Bateman, for the next 6 months, throughout their lifecycle, these incidents will be owned by the Central IT Service Desk; however, it is anticipated that the majority of the work will be carried out by the CI Squad. As such, it will be important to closely monitor anything coming in.

With regard to the remaining service requests required for Case Management, I think your involvement will be essential—particularly to help guide approvals and associated processes. While Louise’s fortnightly meeting will ensure progress continues, we are aware that each product will require engagement from different teams. Identifying and securing the right stakeholders has been challenging so far.

Could you let me know how you think we should best manage this, and whether there is anything else you would like us to factor in over the coming months?

Thanks so much

 

Carla

 

Carla Thornley

(interim) Deputy Director, Customer Services

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

emails.subject: Re: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
I think it’s helpful to separate two distinct but related points: API readiness and an internal HR Systems operational dependency.
As discussed with Asta, from an API perspective she’s comfortable with the solution and the work completed to date gives confidence in production readiness. The outstanding point, as left in the meeting with Tony, was whether any additional assurance is required around end-to-end behaviour in Live, including clear visibility of provisioning and deprovisioning outcomes. That distinction does not yet appear to be fully reflected in the thread, and I think it’s important we align internally before we respond.
The issue that remains is operational. ESS notification emails are still sent manually, and today that process is triggered using an internal daily list of newly provisioned users. Once provisioning becomes automated via the API, that visibility disappears unless a replacement daily list is in place.
If that list isn’t available, there is no way to identify who the notification email should be sent to. In that scenario, users would not receive the expected email and access queries would surface via First Line, without a clear way to confirm which accounts have been provisioned.
Given this directly affects our ability to operate on day one, I think it’s sensible for us to align internally on a plan before responding further. I’ve sent a short meeting invite for 1pm tomorrow to talk this through.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, February 05, 2026 10:04 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: ESS Integration – UAT Position update. 


Hi Simon,

 

I think from my perspective, I just wanted to make sure that the end-to-end process testing points are clearly captured and that we’re aligned on the level of risk (however small) we’re comfortable accepting.

 

In all honesty, I think the risk is minimal around deprovisioning (what could go wrong?) and I am not uncomfortable with the API from a production-readiness perspective. The work completed so far gives me confidence in the solution, and my comments were more about shared understanding rather than any specific concern that the API isn’t ready.

 

In the scenario of going live, I need:

1.	A list of users provisioned by the API on a daily basis – for the email notification
2.	An update to SECSUP40 so that it identifies which accounts require additional WFM employee menu profiles, including Timesheets.

 

Many thanks,

Asta

 

Asta Palmer

Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

 



 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 05 February 2026 09:43
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.

 

Hi Kevin & Asta,

 

Could you provide a response to these emails please? I’d like to keep momentum up on this one, although I know there’s a lot going on!

 

My take is that the suggestion of David to provide some manual reports following go live seems sensible, if that first month or so can provide assurance about how the API is working then we should hopefully have enough trust that it’s working. However, there’s a point about the fact that there’s no notifications going to the end user, so how will we be able to know who to send emails to if there’s no report or anything telling us?

 

There may be something we can do to build our own report, I get the feeling that updates to sec

Further to the issue discussed in the briefing relating to the TIMDEP reports not returning all staff data, we can confirm that our team are actively working to address this and provide a solution shortly.

 

If Support are contacted by departments, they could just send a standard email with the following wording:

 

Support template email:

The TIMDEP reports not returning all staff data, we can confirm that our team are actively working to address this and provide a solution, in the meantime we are unable to run reports on behalf of departments.  For now, departments should run PERDEP01 Staff in Post report (which provides a full list of staff) and where there are missing records on a TIMDEP report, the required details will need to be obtained from PeopleXD.

 

Thankfully we haven’t had any departments contacting Support requesting the reports to be run since the briefing.

 

Thank you for your help.

Julie

emails.subject: Re: Auto job alert notification email - text changes
emails.body:
Hi Laura,
Following our discussion, I wanted to share a clarification we’ve received from the supplier while reviewing the reports previously requested, which conflicts with the earlier information provided.
They’ve confirmed that for the Internal Job Board, when an employee logs in via PeopleXD, the board includes all open vacancies, including those advertised internally only. As a result, if an internal employee sets up an email notification from the employee dashboard, they will also receive notifications for internal-only roles, provided these match the selected vacancy type and departments.
In light of this, the current job alert wording does not fully reflect how internal job alerts work for internal users.
I wanted to flag this as the proposed wording change may not be necessary.
Happy to discuss further if helpful.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

Subject: Auto job alert notification email - text changes 
 

Hi Kevin

 

Thanks for your time yesterday. It’s good to here that we can edit the text on the job alert for vacancies – especially as you confirmed that the email excludes internal only vacancies.

 

We have decided to update the text – as shown in the document below. I have marked the changes in yellow – happy to chat through if helpful.

 

Looping in Phil who has approved these changes too.

Auto job alert - changes.docx <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/HumanResources-StrategicWorkforcePlanning/Shared%20Documents/General/Talent%20mobility/Mobility%20framework/Mobility%20Principles/Auto%20job%20alert%20-%20draft%20changes.docx?d=w5b327328d2f1407d89753d6a02a0078b&csf=1&web=1&e=y0cjhd> 

 

Let me know if you need me to approve any tests etc before the revised format goes live.

 

Thank you, Laura

 

Laura Porter

Talent Mobility Specialist

People Department | University of Oxford

laura.porter@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:laura.porter@admin.ox.ac.uk> 

Please note that I work Monday to Thursday

emails.subject: Re: RE. Process for updating People XD
emails.body:
Hi Katherine,

I’d find it helpful to have a bit more context around what is being asked here. I'm happy to have a short call to talk this through, just so I’m clear on the changes and what support is needed around PXD and ongoing HR management.

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 28 January 2026 5:07 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: TIMDEP reports update? 
 

Hi Kevin,

 

Thanks for responding.

 

From my point of view I think we can utilise the example Julie has given to test a fix to this issue. And I totally agree with Emma’s point that Asta needs to be involved in the discussions and testing a solution. 

 

I think the broad steps will be as follows:

*	Deploy the affected reports to the TEST HR Reporting server, which is pointed at UOXZ.
*	Reset the password for Rachel Wilcock’s back office / reporting account in the UOXZ environment so that we can use the account to test running reports.
*	Reset the password for Beth Gray’s back office / reporting account in the UOXZ environment for the same reason.
*	Run each of the reports from the TEST HR Reporting server and compare the results with the attached file to see if we get the same result in UOXZ, confirming the issue in that environment and setting a baseline.
*	Update the join within the affected reports to join the appointment history view to the core time view(s) using an inner or left join
*	Save the updated reports with a new version and deploy to the TEST server
*	Alter the settings for CoreTime back office security to give all users University Wide access, removing the need to set up access to individual work groups per account
*	Re-run the reports each of the reports from the TEST HR Reporting server and compare the results with the pre-fix baseline to see if the report now returns all of the expected employee records.

 

There may be some differences at this point as the full set of employee records may not match between UOXP and UOXZ but hopefully it’ll be close enough that any remaining differences can be quickly investigated. Either that or the proposed fix will fail and we’ll be back to square 1!

 

I’m happy to be involved in discussions and planning out the work if that helps. Perhaps we could set up a specific session to discuss this next week when we formally get Michael back from the project?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

emails.subject: ESS Integration – UAT Position update.
emails.to: Tony Boydell; Erika Braverman; Andrew Goff (IT Services); Simon Burford; Asta Palmer
emails.body:
Hi all,

I wanted to set out the current UAT position, the residual risks that remain, and the assurance we believe should be required before providing, in writing, final sign-off for go-live.
From a formal testing perspective, Asta is content with the scenarios that have been executed to date. API testing against manually selected records has completed successfully, and the outcomes align with expectations. There are no concerns with the logic as demonstrated under these controlled conditions.
The remaining risks relate to production behaviour that has not been fully exercised in UAT. In Live, the API will run autonomously and identify eligible records based on rule-based criteria. That end-to-end, rule-driven behaviour has not been replicated in the test environment, as doing so would require additional build work that is currently out of scope and unfunded. As ESS deprovisioning has not been exercised in a bulk, rule-driven context, this introduces a risk of unexpected outcomes when eligibility changes are processed automatically in Live. There is also no confirmed operational reporting in place for HR that clearly identifies which records have been created or updated by the API.
Before providing final sign-off, we believe further assurance would be required either through end-to-end, rule-based testing that mirrors production behaviour (including deprovisioning), or through clear operational reporting that provides visibility of API-driven activity. Alternatively, if this additional assurance cannot be completed, the above risks would need to be explicitly understood and accepted in order to proceed.
I hope this provides clarify on the current position ahead of any decision on next steps.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

________________________________

Hi all,
I wanted to set out the current UAT position, the residual risks that remain, and the assurance we believe should be required before providing, in writing, final sign-off for go-live.
From a formal testing perspective, Asta is content with the scenarios that have been executed to date. API testing against manually selected records has completed successfully, and the outcomes align with expectations. There are no concerns with the logic as demonstrated under these controlled conditions.
The remaining risks relate to production behaviour that has not been fully exercised in UAT. In Live, the API will run autonomously and identify eligible records based on rule-based criteria. That end-to-end, rule-driven behaviour has not been replicated in the test environment, as doing so would require additional build work that is currently out of scope and unfunded. As ESS deprovisioning is not currently in use, this may introduce a risk of unexpected outcomes when eligibility changes are processed automatically in Live. Also, there is no confirmed reporting or audit output in place that clearly identifies which records have been created or updated by the API.
Before providing final sign-off, we believe further assurance would be required either through end-to-end, rule-based testing that mirrors production behaviour (including deprovisioning), or through clear operational reporting that provides visibility of API-driven activity. Alternatively, if this additional assurance cannot be completed, the above risks would need to be explicitly understood and accepted in order to proceed.
I hope this provides clarify on the current position ahead of any decision on next steps.
Best,
Kevin

We apologize for the inconveniences this could cause and assure you our Dev Team are working with high priority on finding a fix for this.

 

We’ve not been advised a change that needs making. We’ve had inte

emails.subject: Re: Support model for HR Case Management - ownership by HR
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
I’m supportive of the proposed approach and the way this is being taken forward. It feels reasonable at this stage and gives us an opportunity to review how things are working.
I don’t have any concerns to raise at this point.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, January 29, 2026 12:22 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Support model for HR Case Management - ownership by HR 


Hi Both

 

I would welcome your thoughts on this approach. It seems supportive and sensible to me, especially the review of calls after the 6-months so that we can get a feel for what is coming through. I am conscious that if it appears a lot are being passed through to HR Systems, during this 6-month period, that we Clockify all these, as I will need evidence to support any further request for additional resource. But if this an issue, I suspect we will be aware, well before the 6-month mark.

 

Unless I hear back from you by 10am tomorrow (recognising Simon is on leave today), with concerns I have not considered, then I will accept this kind offer.

 

Best wishes

 

Marie

 

From: Samantha Bateman <sam.bateman@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 29 January 2026 11:52
To: Stuart Lee <stuart.lee@digital.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Luke Geraghty <luke.geraghty@digital.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Viktoriia Vasyliuta <viktoriia.vasyliuta@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Ian Teasdale <ian.teasdale@digital.ox.ac.uk>; John Ireland <john.ireland@it.ox.ac.uk>; Ricardo Goncalves <ricardo.goncalves@digital.ox.ac.uk>; Natasha Heaton <natasha.heaton@digital.ox.ac.uk>; Kate Forbes <kate.forbes@it.ox.ac.uk>; Carla Thornley <carla.thornley@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: Support model for HR Case Management - ownership by HR

 

Hi Marie,

 

Our IT Services/DGU contingent met this morning in a wider CRM meeting and after discussion I have a proposal for you around support?

 

I propose that for now our Central IT Service Desk are FPOC for Halo HR Case Management. We will simply triage and send any queries over to Halo Application Support/CI Squad, who will contact your team when there are questions around data or information that we need from People XD, or potentially if HR systems business process type of advice is needed?

 

Then after 6 months we meet and jointly review what has come in, what was needed to resolve the incidents and then either confirm that the process is working well or identify gaps and how those should be handled in the future.

 

I have looked in OSM under Halo Case Management and so far no incidents have been logged for this service, however I just wanted to check if you have had any logged under the HRIS service? If so please can you provide a me with a list of those with symptoms?

 

Does that sound a sensible solution to you?

 

Kind regards,

 

Sam

emails.subject: Re: TIMDEP reports update?
emails.to: Julie Hickman; Simon Burford; Marie Cooksey
emails.body:
Hi Julie,
Thanks - that’s really helpful.
I’m happy to proceed with the interim approach discussed. We can confirm that HR Systems are looking at a potential solution, and let users know that we’ll provide a further update by the end of February.
Alongside this, I’ll work with Michael to outline what would need to be covered to take this forward, so we have a clearer view of what’s involved and can consider how best to support the work.
Happy to agree the wording before anything goes out.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, January 28, 2026 10:46 AM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Emma Fitz-Gibbon <emma.fitz-gibbon@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: TIMDEP reports update? 


Hi Simon,

 

I really appreciate you helping with this – thank you!!

 

Emma and I have met this morning and have the following answers and updates below.

 

*	Thankfully up until now we have not had many requests coming through, however we’re concerned that Anna’s message yesterday will prompt an increase.
*	We’re unable to locate any requests prior to 19 January 2026.
*	A more recent example of an issue is incident 11652683, Beth handled this one. See the analysis she made attached and summary below:

 

Work group

Count

Missing Beth

Missing Dept

JS

315

58

241

JU

36

3

30

JW

148

14

15

KE

60

5

60

LW

12

2

12

ZD

17

1

17

*	Answers to your questions:

	*	The report name – TIMDEP02
	*	The user who requested the report - Rachel  Wilcock
	*	The department(s) they needed it run for - Estates
	*	The reason why they needed it run (ie which employee could they not see?) – After running the report, Rachel spotted she had missing staff, Beth asked her to identify who was missing (by running PERDEP01 – staff in post), Beth then ran the TIMDEP report and she also had staff missing (but different staff).  See attached for more info.

*	In terms of communications, I think we just guidance on what to say in the following scenarios.  Should we be advising staff to run staff in post and identify missing records then look their details up (eg leave balance) in the system) and the Support team are unable to run reports at this time?

	*	A: Department ask us to run report on their behalf
	*	B: Department contact us and notice staff are missing on TIMDEP reports

*	Based on your investigations the messaging hopefully can change shortly.
*	If needed, Support and I can run TIMDEP reports for departments a few weeks, but we need to be clear when contacting users that if the work group issue is not resolved, there will still be missing records and inaccurate reports. 

 

Do you want to take a look and maybe catch up later this afternoon and then agree the message?

 

Many thanks

Julie

emails.subject: Re: Organisational Structure update - January 2026 - FINAL
emails.body:
Hi Katherine,

Thank you for today’s meeting.
To confirm my understanding, the recent update relates only to the Entity Full Name, where the “Nuffield Department of Medicine –” prefix has been removed to shorten the name. No other attributes or structures are changing, and existing names can be overwritten with the updated values.
However, after checking our local system, I can confirm that we currently use the short name (highlighted in yellow), as the full name exceeds the character limit permitted for the system change. Therefore, please could you confirm that this remains acceptable in light of the updated naming?
Best,
Kevin

Subject: RE: Organisational Structure update - January 2026 - FINAL

 

Dear all,

 

The University Organisational Structure has now been updated with changes with effective dates up to and including 26th January 2026.

 

The latest version of the file can be found on our website: https://governance.admin.ox.ac.uk/university-organisation-structure

Please ensure that if you updated your local systems based on the draft that you reflect this further change.

If you are the owner/administrator of a local system (eg. Card database, Salto, Oracle etc.) please ensure you reflect these changes in those systems, and get in touch if we can support you with this.

emails.subject: Re: ESS Formal Integration Test Scenarios
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Just to add to Asta’s email and clarify where we landed - Asta was asked to set out in writing her acceptance position for UAT — confirming she is content with the scenarios already tested, and clearly documenting what further assurance she would need before final sign-off for go-live, including the specific risks she is seeking to mitigate.
The intention was to make those risks and conditions explicit, so that a clear decision can be taken on whether further funding is sought or whether the remaining risk is accepted and the integration proceeds to Live as it stands.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, January 28, 2026 2:41 PM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: ESS Formal Integration Test Scenarios 


Hi Simon,

 

As far as I understand there’s no more money left in the pot, so if we (HR Systems) are not totally happy to go live with it as it is and without this last step, they will put another proposal through to agree on next steps and extra budget. Andy could see where I was coming from in raising this as a concern, but simply speaking Tony wanted to push the solution to Live.

 

Currently I do send joining emails manually when new ESS accounts are created, so I would need a report or something that shows what records have been created by the API – unless joining emails could be sent automatically. I do not know how to get about this tough, do I need to talk to WFM project?

 

 

Many thanks,

Asta

 

Asta Palmer

Functional Analyst

 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford

Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

Hi Simon,

Kevin and myself had a meeting with Andy, Sherry and Tony to discuss the next steps around formal testing and moving ESS automation to the go-live stage.

So far, the API testing against specific, manually selected records (as in Sherry’s screenshot below) has been completed and validated successfully, I have no concerns. As things stand, from the formal testing perspective, IT Services are ready to push the integration to Live.

I have flagged however, that in the live environment, the API will run independently and identify eligible accounts based on the defined rules. To be confident in production behaviour, I mentioned that I would like to replicate this same approach in our test environment with the API selecting eligible accounts on its own rather than relying on hard-coded identifiers. emails.subject: Re: HRIS and SSO support
emails.body:
Hi John,
I hope you're well.
I’m just following up on the HRIS and SSO support discussion we had in December, as it’s been a little while and I wanted to check whether there have been any developments since that meeting.
Please let me know if there have been any updates, or if there is anything further you need from our side at this stage.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 





________________________________

From: John Jones <john.jones@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, November 24, 2025 4:32 PM
To: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Richard Jessett <richard.jessett@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: HRIS and SSO support 


Hi Simon,

 

At a high level - We are looking at a wider program where users that are currently part of the managed desktop service would move to using their SSO accounts to log on to their desktops over their CONNECT accounts, this offers many benefits through being able to log on to many services automatically and heading to the more integrated desktop experience in line with overall strategic direction.  For any users that do not have a managed desktop they will be able to use the same credentials they use to get in to many of the Universities services without the need for a separate CONNECT account. Overall purpose is reducing complexity.

 

This initial call is to share this vision, then for us to start to understand impact and feasibility of moving HRIS – reporting, options that we have including running SSO accounts in parallel to the users current connect accounts, possible scripting to automate migration and possibly sandboxed views (trial possibly in a dev or test space the teams need to be comfortable before we move forward).

 

We are not pushing a hard deadline and appreciate that you have many projects and activities underway, this is just to highlight the option, the wider intent and then spawn calls (at a suitable juncture) to look to investigate with your teams the use of SSO accounts.

 

Feel free to invite team members & if we need to delay or move the call happy to, Richard Jessett on CC is our Technical Architect and has been involved with HRIS backend from our team perspective and will be happy to provide input.

 

Hope that helps around context,

 

Many thanks

 

John

 

 

 <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

John Jones

Deputy Team Lead

Microsoft Platform Services Team

IT Services | University of Oxford

T: +44 (0) 1865 616346  int: (6)16346

www.it.ox.ac.uk <https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/> 

 

 

 

 

 

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 24 November 2025 14:56
To: John Jones <john.jones@it.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Asta Palmer <asta.palmer@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: HRIS and SSO support

 

Hi John,

 

Are you able to provide a bit more context as to what this means so I can ensure the right people are in the meeting? This sounds like something that Kevin and Asta might be best positioned to take forward but also could be potentially strategic, particularly if this means more work for our team.

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems

 

People Department | University of Oxford

Suite 4, Littlegate House,

16-17 St Ebbes Street, Oxford, OX1 1PT



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Re: TIMDEP reports update?
emails.to: Simon Burford; Julie Hickman; Marie Cooksey
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
I’ve spoken with Michael and, based on his initial view, the testing required to address the underlying PeopleXD security behaviour means this is likely to be a longer-running piece of work rather than something that can be resolved in the short term.
In the longer term, there may be options to explore via Insight, the data warehouse, or a discussion with the Access Group, but these would require scoping and prioritisation. In the meantime, it seems sensible to proceed on the basis of the current interim arrangement.
I’m happy to pick up any follow-up discussion on next steps.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

Hi Simon,
Yes - I’ll check with Michael and provide an update.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, January 22, 2026 2:38 PM
To: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: TIMDEP reports update?


Hi Kevin,

 

Before we make any decisions around this can you check with Michael how long he thinks it will be before we have a solution in place?

 

I don’t think the development will take too long but it’s extremely important to get the testing right, so that’ll be the time consuming thing, but I’m hoping we could maybe carve out some dedicated time for him to focus on this?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 22 January 2026 12:27
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: TIMDEP reports update?

 

Good morning all,

 

Thank you for the update.

 

Given that we have had this issue for approximately four years and the current project team have been unable to resolve it, I’m assuming it’s not going to be a quick fix (no offence to Michael’s very capable skills), please can we therefore agree what the process should be in the meantime as the Support team don’t have time to run these reports on behalf of departments.

 

I have spoken with Emma and we feel that all we can do is create a standard email which can be sent to users when required, which states something like:

 

“We are aware that the Workforce Management related reports are not returning all staff data and our team are actively working to address this and provide a solution, we are unable to run reports on behalf of departments”.

 

For background, if we did take this on these are the steps we would need to take.  We’re just not resourced to do this every time and I would argue that given the time it would take, this isn’t a typical firstline responsibility.

1.	Request HR team run the required TIMDEP report along with the PERDEP01 report
2.	HR team notify Support who is missing from the TIMDEP report
3.	From the missing records, Support team identify where the issue has occurred due to the non-substantive appointment issue
4.	From the remaining missing records, Support identify the affected work groups
5.	Support request Asta to update access for all Support team members and all HR team for those work groups
6.	Where there are issues due to the substantive appointment being outside of the user’s security:

	1.	Support run report
	2.	Support export and save repor

Hi Nathan,
Thanks - I appreciate the advance notice.
It would be beneficial if this could be scheduled for 18 September. Michael is on annual leave w/c 21 September, and that period is likely to be particularly stretched from a resourcing perspective.
If the input can be done on or before the 18th, that would help us manage capacity more effectively.
Best,
Kevin

________________________________

From: Nathan Kirwan <nathan.kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 23 January 2026 3:09 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Sarah Rowles <sarah.rowles@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Notice of FA works required later in the year 
 

Hi Kevin,

 

I hope you had a great week. I’ve currently been planning the upcoming year’s work with my REF colleagues and there a some requests for support on a couple of items that I will need to log with the FA team in due course. 

 

One of which is very time critical due to the nature of the HESA annual return timelines. We need to receive data from our Research Services colleagues that will need to go into a PXD HESA UDF very rapidly. This is because we need to allow sufficient time for staff to appeal their REFQUALCON status before we formally submit the data as part of the annual HESA process. Having worked with Nick Sharkey recently where he uploaded the files for me last year it did not seem particularly onerous to do, but still requires resources and time to deliver. Could I flag with you the time period that I would be looking to have this work completed in the hopes of securing some FA time in the future? 

 

The formal HESA data return schedule has not been released yet but I would be looking to have the data input around the 18th – 22nd of September and the task would be a single upload file. 

 

Many thanks for your help.

 

Nathan Kirwan

Senior HR Analyst (Research) 

People Department | University of Oxford

Office base:  4 Worcester Street OX1 2BX

Email: Nathan.Kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:Nathan.Kirwan@admin.ox.ac.uk>  

Phone: Please use Teams



 

Whatfix – have you tried using our self-help tool for PeopleXD <https://hrsystems.admin.ox.ac.uk/whatfix>  yet?

emails.subject: Re: PeopleXD DPIA – Draft submitted for informal consultation
emails.body:
Hi Simon,
Thanks for taking the time to review this and for putting together the draft data flow diagram.
Is there a way we could have this provided as a PDF so it can be appended to the DPIA, and do you also think we would need to append the Condensed Mapping spreadsheet as well?
Best,
Kevin

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, December 22, 2025 4:39 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: PeopleXD DPIA – Draft submitted for informal consultation 


Hi Kevin,

 

I’ve finally found the time to review this!

 

As we don’t seem to be getting much traction on the data flow point from infosec I’ve created a draft data flow diagram at the level that I think we could realistically produce one:

 



 

This diagram can be supported by a condensed mapping document (a starting point is attached), which describes at a high level the types of data which are input/processed at each stage/process (the red bits) of the diagram, as well as a full interface diagram which can pad out what the full list of downstream systems and interface technology is. Although I do think there is a separate DPIA for each interface technology, so perhaps our DPIA and dataflow diagram should end at the point where the data is passed into interfaces.

 

I haven’t seen the actual DPIA document so we will need to make sure that the diagram and the condensed mapping document reflects everything in the DPIA but this is hopefully a good starting point. The original data flow diagram is in Visio – I’ve given you edit access: ​PeopleXD Data Flow <https://unioxfordnexus-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/g/personal/admn2716_ox_ac_uk/IQDxGYUsrp8RRZcVSqJ94z3nAVBpaycC83s4eG_EaGrFXgs?email=kevin.lelitte%40admin.ox.ac.uk&e=maBWvk> ​

 

Do you want to set up a meeting where we can discuss next steps? Or cover it off at a 121?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

​PXD DPIA Review.docx <https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/HumanResources-HRSystems/Shared%20Documents/Functional%20Analysis/Data%20Protection%20Impact%20Assessment%20(DPIA)/PXD%20DPIA%20Review.docx?d=w09a06f7c87a741bb823de7299cb50db7&csf=1&web=1&e=XaaV2q> ​

Please add any observations or updates directly to the document so we can maintain a single, consistent record.

Best,
Kevin

From: Information Compliance <information.compliance@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:information.compliance@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: 03 November 2025 11:54 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Information Compliance <information.compliance@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:information.compliance@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: RE: PeopleXD DPIA – Draft submitted for informal consultation

 

Hi Kevin,

 

Many thanks for submitting the PeopleXD draft DPIA for a preliminary review and for your patience.

 

UK GDPR requires a DPIA to contain a ‘systematic description of the envisaged processing operations and the purposes of the processing’. This means that a DPIA will need to include a complete and detailed description of all processing operations, taking into account the full lifecycle of the personal data from when it is first processed to its destructi

emails.subject: Re: #External# PeopleXD SMTP change – UAT readiness and confirmation
emails.body:
Hi Mike,
Thanks — I’ve now received the detailed UAT email control steps via the support portal, which are clear.
You mentioned that, with the test-run override in place, the nominated inbox could receive a “large quantity of emails”. To help plan the UAT window properly, could you give an indication of what that typically looks like in practice (for example, tens versus hundreds of emails)?
That will help to confirm the timing and keep the approach manageable.
Best,
Kevin
________________________________

From: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com>
Sent: 21 January 2026 8:31 AM
To: Jonathan Tighe <jonathan.tighe@theaccessgroup.com>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Andrew Goff (IT Services) <andrew.goff@it.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: #External# PeopleXD SMTP change – UAT readiness and confirmation 
 
Hi Kevin 

The Support team will confirm this on the support case but the process from our side on the UAT email situation is as below:


1.	Ensure the test run indicator to "on"
2.	Note the username and email in EMS username and administrator email
3.	Change the email field to a users email address for the test. So likely one of the users involved in this project.
4.	Ensure the client sets the original email back.


This will ensure that all emails sent by the system only go to the one email address I assume likely to be yours? 

The only negative here is that your email address would get all system generated emails which could be a large quantity of emails being sent. To narrow that down further would require the switching off of various emails and alerts in system which is a significantly more laborious process and would require me raising a consultancy case and needing to use flexpoints for this. 

Let me know your thoughts 

Thanks 


Mike West

Senior Customer Success Manager




M: 07525766674  l www.theaccessgroup.com <http://www.theaccessgroup.com/> 

 

 <https://www.theaccessgroup.com/en-gb/campaigns/hcm/pxd/peoplexd-training-courses/?utm_source=signature>

Thanks 


Mike West

Senior Customer Success Manager

 <https://www.theaccessgroup.com/en-gb/campaigns/hcm/pxd/peoplexd-training-courses/?utm_source=signature>

Subject: RE: TIMDEP reports update? 


Hi Kevin,

 

Before we make any decisions around this can you check with Michael how long he thinks it will be before we have a solution in place?

 

I don’t think the development will take too long but it’s extremely important to get the testing right, so that’ll be the time consuming thing, but I’m hoping we could maybe carve out some dedicated time for him to focus on this?

 

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX



Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

From: Julie Hickman <julie.hickman@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 22 January 2026 12:27
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: TIMDEP reports update?

 

Good morning all,

 

Thank you for the update.

 

Given that we have had this issue for approximately four years and the current project team have been unable to resolve it, I’m assuming it’s not going to be a quick fix (no offence to Michael’s very capable skills), please can we therefore agree what the process should be in the meantime as the Support team don’t have time to run these reports on behalf of departments.

 

I have spoken with Emma and we feel that all we can do is create a standard email which can be sent to users when required, which states something like:

 

“We are aware that the Workforce Management related reports are not returning all staff data and our team are actively working to address this and provide a solution, we are unable to run reports on behalf of departments”.

 

For background, if we did take this on these are the steps we would need to take.  We’re just not resourced to do this every time and I would argue that given the time it would take, this isn’t a typical firstline responsibility.

1.	Request HR team run the required TIMDEP report along with the PERDEP01 report
2.	HR team notify Support who is missing from the TIMDEP report
3.	From the missing records, Support team identify where the issue has occurred due to the non-substantive appointment issue
4.	From the remaining missing records, Support identify the affected work groups
5.	Support request Asta to update access for all Support team members and all HR team for those work groups
6.	Where there are issues due to the substantive appointment being outside of the user’s security:

	1.	Support run report
	2.	Support export and save report
	3.	Support check user’s access
	4.	Support share report with HR team 

 

Should Michael need any input from myself, I’d be happy to assist.

 

Just to confirm I will continue to run the monthly timesheet report for the Physics HR team.

 

Thank you again.

Julie

Subject: Re: TIMDEP reports update?

 

Hi Julie,

Apologies, this dropped off my radar.

I’ll confirm the position with Michael separately during our handover session and will let you know once next steps are confirmed.

Best,
Kevin

_______

emails.subject: Re: Halo Case Management - Reconfirmation of PeopleXD responsibilities discussed on 8 December
emails.body:
Hi Viktoriia,
Thank you for setting this out and for sharing the updated support mapping.
The listed PeopleXD-related symptoms may involve HRIS in a limited, supporting capacity, limited to confirming PeopleXD source data accuracy if queried via the established route. These items were not intended to position HRIS as the first point of contact or as the owner of Halo-related incidents.
In terms of supporting materials, there is currently no dedicated HRIS documentation or training in place for handling Halo-related scenarios. During the discussion, it was noted that supporting documentation would be produced as part of the wider programme activity. There was no expectation of HRIS involvement beyond data confirmation, with queries raised via the already established route.
For clarity, our proposed updates for HRIS in the support mapping are as follows:

*	
	Line 17: HRIS involvement would be limited to confirming the accuracy of PeopleXD data if queried. HRIS would not own the incident or act as the first point of contact.
*	
	Line 22: Where a person is missing from the lookup field, this may be related to PeopleXD data or integration behaviour. HRIS may be asked to confirm source data if required but would not act as the first point of contact.
*	
	Line 29: Where the same person appears more than once, this may relate to PeopleXD data or integration behaviour. HRIS involvement would be limited to data confirmation if queried and would not be the first point of contact.

I hope this helps to confirm the agreed position. Happy to discuss further if useful.
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

emails.subject: Fw: Halo Case Management - Reconfirmation of PeopleXD responsibilities discussed on 8 December
emails.body:
Hi Marie,

I’m forwarding the email below from Victoriia regarding the Halo support model. It appears that further amendments have been made, and HRIS are noted as the FPOC for three symptoms.

I’m not clear how this affects our meeting today, particularly as neither Ricardo nor Luke has accepted the invite, however I've drafted a proposed response for confirmation, as follows:

*	
	Line 17: While PeopleXD is the source system, HR Systems’ role is limited to confirming data accuracy if requested. HRIS should not be the First Point of Contact.
*	
	Line 22: This behaviour relates to PeopleXD integration scope and HALO lookup configuration. HR Systems may confirm source data if required, but should not be the First Point of Contact.
*	
	Line 29: This issue relates to PeopleXD integration behaviour. HR Systems’ role is limited to confirming source data accuracy if requested. HRIS should not be the First Point of Contact.


Could you please let me know your thoughts and whether you’d like to proceed with the meeting as planned?

Best,
Kevin

emails.subject: Re: #External# College Data in PeopleXD – Technical Discussion
emails.body:
Hi Mike,
Thanks for clarifying.
Yes, this builds on the ‘People XD Multi Company setup discussion’ held in November last year. Since then, the work has continued internally, and we’re now at the point where we need to drill into the technical detail to help inform planning and next steps.
We understand this will require a consultant to be assigned, which is why I referenced using our flex points.
There’s no immediate delivery urgency, but there is a desire to have an early technical conversation to support internal decision-making following an upcoming project update.
Please go ahead and raise the case. Once that’s in place, we can pick up availability for a technical session, even if this falls after this week.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

If Oxford which to continue this conversation into formal scoping and quoting I can raise a case and ask for a consultant to be assigned but I will struggle to get a meeting in this week as this has not yet been raised as a request with our technical teams.

Can I ask if there is a reason for the urgency does this work need to be completed soon?

emails.subject: Re: ORCID - Progress with Access Work
emails.body:
Hi Crispin,

I can confirm that there will be no charge to DTP1092 for this work. Based on your note about the project focus shifting next month, delivery by the end of February is achievable, provided the work is scheduled promptly.

Please see the clarification below:

Objective
Enable the addition of new onboarding checklist fields in PeopleXD (e.g. ORCID) without triggering retrospective onboarding notifications for existing staff. This allows ORCID capture to be introduced safely as part of onboarding.

Steps

*	
	Confirm and document the scripted “task clear-down” approach validated by Access Group (HR Systems / Access Group).
*	
	Finalise the ORCID checklist requirements (DTP1092 / business owners).
*	
	Deploy the new checklist item in PeopleXD onboarding (Access Group).
*	
	Execute scripted clear-down to prevent legacy notifications (Access Group, supported by HR Systems).
*	
	Validate outcome and confirm readiness for use (HR Systems).


Timings

*	
	Scoping and confirmation of requirements: 1 week (Already done but will require final confirmation)
*	
	Scheduling and deployment: 1–2 weeks
*	
	Validation and sign-off: 1 week



Best,
Kevin


________________________________

emails.subject: PeopleXD SMTP change – UAT readiness and confirmation
emails.body:
Hi Mike, Jonathan,
Following our discussion, I just wanted to clarify the following point. As the sending address remains peoplexd@accessacloud.com, our Networks and Infrastructure team have advised there is no need to make any DNS changes. SPF for accessacloud.com is owned and managed by Access Group, so confirmation from your side is required rather than an update on ours.
Before we schedule the UAT window, could you please confirm:

*	
	That PeopleXD UAT will be switched to use smtp.accessacloud.com.
*	
	That SPF for accessacloud.com already authorises smtp.accessacloud.com.
*	
	What email safety controls are in place in UAT to prevent unintended notifications during testing.
*	
	When you would be available to support the UAT session.

@Mike West <mailto:Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com> — separately, and outside the scope of this change, could you confirm what Access Group involvement would be required if we decided to move to an @ox.ac.uk sending address in future, including whether this would require flexpoints?
Best,
Kevin


Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 






Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

I wanted to flag this message below from John Ireland in case these domains relate to HRIS have any current or legacy dependency.
hrislogin.ox.ac.uk and hris.ox.ac.uk are included in the list proposed for removal. 
Best,
Kevin
________________________________

From: John Ireland <john.ireland@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Thursday, January 15, 2026 1:16 PM
To: itss-announce@maillist.ox.ac.uk <itss-announce@maillist.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: [ict-a] Unowned domains 

We have a number of subdomains of ox.ac.uk that are not currently assigned to an IPAM group. This is most likely to due to incomplete records from before we moved to Hydra. We would like to improve the situation, and will be getting in touch about a series of 3 or 4 specific clean-up activities that we'd like to take forwards across University and college ITSS.

Activity 1: removal of unnecessary domains

There are 13 domains that are not assigned to any unit / team, have no records showing in Hydra, and are not new (these were created many years ago).

Please would everyone review the list below and reply directly to me urgently if any of these relate to your area, or if you can see any reason why removal of these could adversely impact University or college operations.


*	
	cair.ox.ac.uk.
*	
	hrislogin.ox.ac.uk.

	undevoscc.ox.ac.uk.

Thank you, John


JOHN IRELAND

Director, Software Solutions (interim)

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square, Oxford OX1 2JD

Teams: Start Chat - Web: https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/

At this stage, it’s a DNS change only, to publish DKIM and SPF records to support email sent via the supplier’s SMTP server. I’m not aware of any firewall changes being required, however any firewall requirements should be identified as part of the request.
Best,
Kevin

Hi Kevin,

I would imagine that would be a change request to the Microsoft Platform Services Team but it might be the Nexus team, given that it's email related. I would suggest raising it with the helpdesk so they can route it to the correct resolver team for you.

Is it a DNS change and/or a firewall change?

Thanks,

Andy

Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: Testing resource - Task# 50895319 for Incident# 11629527 
 
Hi Andy,
Thanks again for confirming, and joining the call tomorrow.
I had one quick question ahead of that. Access Group have confirmed there will also be a DNS update required on the Oxford University side to allow their SMTP service to send on our behalf. This would involve adding the following SPF record:

*	"v=spf1 include:spf2.accessacloud.com ~all"
	and the sending server/IP is:
*	smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>    (212.54.130.143)

Could you advise who in IT Services would normally handle this type of DNS change, and whether this should be raised via a specific OSM request? It would also be useful to know whether someone from that team would typically need to be included in the call, or if this is something we can follow up separately.
Best,
Kevin

Andy

Subject: Re: Testing resource - Task# 50895319 for Incident# 11629527
 
Hi Andy,
Just to confirm, would 11:00–11:30 work for you?
DKIM stands for DomainKeys Identified Mail. It’s an email authentication mechanism that allows receiving mail systems to verify that an email was genuinely sent by the specific stated domain and hasn’t been altered.
Best,
Kevin

Subject: RE: Organisational Structure update - January 2026 - Draft

 

Dear all

 

Please find attached a draft copy of the Organisational Structure including changes with effective dates up to and including 13th January 2026.

 

We will be circulating a final version in the week of 19 January – if there are any errors or omissions, please let us know as soon as possible and no later than Tuesday 20th January 2026.

 

Key Changes (from Change Schedule tab of attached file):

 

Division

Entity

Change

UAS

International Engagement Office (KS)

Entity moved from parent DAE (8H60) to VC, Registrar & PAD (8H20)

 

 

 

MSD

National Perinatal Epidemiology Unit (B2)

Entity moved from parent Nuffield Dept of Population Health (2B29) to NDWRH (2B04)

MSD

NDM (2B02-C3)

Creation of the Nuffield Department of Medicine - Cyrus Poonawalla Vaccine Research Building (C3) - HESA cost centre 101

MSD

NDM (2B02-B9)

The update of the name of Centre for Tropical Medicine (B9) to: Nuffield Department of Medicine - Tropical Medicine - Centre for Global Health Research / Tropical Medicine - CGHR (Oxford)

MSD

NDM (2B02-A9,C9,D9,L9)

The creation of 3 more entities alongside B9 for the main national centres of the Centre for Tropical Medicine in NDM as follows:

A9: Nuffield Department of Medicine - Tropical Medicine - KEMRI-Wellcome Trust Research Programme / Tropical Medicine - KWTRP (Kenya)

C9: Nuffield Department of Medicine - Tropical Medicine - Oxford University Clinical Research Unit / Tropical Medicine - OUCRU (Vietnam)

D9: Nuffield Department of Medicine - Tropical Medicine - Mahidol Oxford Tropical Medicine Research Unit / Tropical Medicine - MORU (Thailand)

L9: Nuffield Department of Medicine - Tropical Medicine - Core Ops / Tropical Medicine - Core Ops

All HESA cost centres 101 (100%)

 

Notes:

*	We have used highlighting in the change schedule to try to make it clearer what changes are happening for the ease of our colleagues whose role it is to update the organisational structure in their own systems – if any other changes to the format for this update could be made that would make this process easier for you please do let us know.

 

Best wishes

Katherine

From: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 13 January 2026 3:57 PM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: RE: #External# Re: Follow-up on case 68208871 (SMTP/DKIM Implementation) 
 

Hi Kevin,

 

Do you know who in IT Services (presumably) will need to carry out this step:

 

1.       On the client's side, they need to add the below spf record:

o    "v=spf1 include:spf2.accessacloud.com ~all"
and the sending server/IP is:

o    smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>    (212.54.130.143)

Many thanks,

Simon

 

Simon Burford

HR Systems Analysis and Insights Manager

HR Systems 

 

People Department | University of Oxford

4 Worcester Street, Oxford, OX1 2BX

From: Mike West <Mike.West@theaccessgroup.com> 
Sent: 13 January 2026 10:04
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Sarah Murphy <sarah.murphy@theaccessgroup.com>
Cc: Simon Burford <simon.burford@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: #External# Re: Follow-up on case 68208871 (SMTP/DKIM Implementation)

 

Hi Kev 

 

I have asked the  DBA team to come on a call as has the support agent. 

 

I also wanted to send you the below from our DBA team which should help explain more of the approach and includes a question I could do with you answering please highlighted in red:

 

Now, the end goal is to switch the client from using BT's mail relay 'mail.esat-x.com <http://mail.esat-x.com/>  ' to using the CE hosted smtp server 'smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>  '. The only difference for the customer is that the new mail relay will be hosted in Access group data centres in Woking & London, as opposed to the BT mail relay which is hosted in Citywest, Dublin. The logs on the Access group SMTP server will contain only the from and to email addresses and the logs are retained for 30 days only

1.	Currently, the UAT environment and LIVE environment are both on the BT's mail relay respectively

2.       As the client wants to use their own email sending address, we will need that sending address from them. for e.g - is it donotreply@ox.ac.uk <mailto:donotreply@ox.ac.uk> ? If not, can they provide their sending email address, please?

3.       We can switch from using BT's mail relay 'mail.esat-x.com <http://mail.esat-x.com/>  ' to using the CE hosted smtp server 'smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>  ' on the UAT environment first and then to LIVE environment. However, the main thing to note down here is that the DKIM key setup for the client domain will be used on both UAT and LIVE environment respectively. Thus, we can't have separate DKIM keys setup

4.       On our side, we will update the client environment to using the CE hosted smtp server and let them know once complete

5.       On the client's side, they need to add the below spf record:

o    "v=spf1 include:spf2.accessacloud.com ~all"
and the sending server/IP is:

o    smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>    (212.54.130.143)

If needed, we can switch the client from using BT's mail relay 'mail.esat-x.com <http://mail.esat-x.com/>  ' to using the CE hosted smtp server 'smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>  ' on both UAT and LIVE environment together for them to test it throughout as reverting back to the current setup is also straightforward and won't take much time

 

Thanks 

 

Mike West

Senior Customer Success Manager

 

M: 07525766674  l www.theaccessgroup.com <http://www.theaccessgroup.com/> 

 

 <https

emails.subject: Re: Testing resource -  Task# 50895319 for Incident# 11629527
emails.body:
Hi Andy,
Thanks again for confirming, and joining the call tomorrow.
I had one quick question ahead of that. Access Group have confirmed there will also be a DNS update required on the Oxford University side to allow their SMTP service to send on our behalf. This would involve adding the following SPF record:

*	"v=spf1 include:spf2.accessacloud.com ~all"
	and the sending server/IP is:
*	smtp.accessacloud.com <http://smtp.accessacloud.com/>    (212.54.130.143)

Could you advise who in IT Services would normally handle this type of DNS change, and whether this should be raised via a specific OSM request? It would also be useful to know whether someone from that team would typically need to be included in the call, or if this is something we can follow up separately.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

Andy

Hi Andy,
Just to confirm, would 11:00–11:30 work for you?
DKIM stands for DomainKeys Identified Mail. It’s an email authentication mechanism that allows receiving mail systems to verify that an email was genuinely sent by the specific stated domain and hasn’t been altered.

Kevin
