emails.subject: Advanced Insight Reporting: Practical Build
emails.body:
Hi Mansi,

Ahead of Thursday’s session, I wanted to check whether it would be ok to use a manager Insight report as a practical module to build during the training.
The idea would be to create something that gives managers direct access to their PeopleXD data, such as annual leave entitlement, sickness absence, PDR status, headcount, start dates, and fixed-term contract start/end dates.

This would help ground the session in a realistic use case. Please let me know if you’re happy with this approach or if you’d recommend any adjustments.

Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

emails.subject: Fw: Halo Case Management - list of users
emails.body:
Hi Marie,
Just to follow up on my previous email, as Viktoriia has since come back with further clarification in the email below.
She’s indicated that HRIS was defined as a key contact for application-related questions and that, in a meeting on 12 December, it was agreed HRIS would have broader access to Halo for visibility and to help users (excluding access approval, which sits with HRBP).
Based on my understanding and the discussions I was involved in, this was not clearly agreed, and there was no defined scope around what “broader access” or “help users” would mean in practice.
I wanted to flag this before our meeting.
Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Viktoriia Vasyliuta <viktoriia.vasyliuta@it.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Monday, January 12, 2026 11:52 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: Halo Case Management - list of users 

Hi Kevin,
Just to double check that I am asking question in the right.

IHRIS was defined as a key contact for application relating questions for users. In a meeting on 12th December we agreed that HRIS have to get broader access to have more visibility and be able to help users. It does not include granting/revoking/approving access - this is HRBP area, which also agrees. 
Now, I want to be very clear, if, of course, you have this information:  does HRIS users have access to the Halo Case Management? What kind of access they've got? How many people?  Did these users have a training session on application usage? 

Kind regards,
 
Viktoriia Vasyliuta



IT Support Architect

IT Services, University of Oxford

Dartington House, University Offices, Wellington Square

Oxford OX1 2JD

Home | IT Help <https://help.it.ox.ac.uk/>

I'm reaching out to ask you please to provide me with the list of HRIS users who have to be granted access to the Halo Case Management application?
To keep you on the same page of the current situation:

Thank you in advance.

Kind regards,
 
Viktoriia Vasyliuta

emails.subject: Fw: Pending Cority support requests
emails.body:
Add SMS 


________________________________

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: 09 January 2026 10:05 AM
To: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: FW: Pending Cority support requests 
 

Hi Marie, 

 

I’m just forwarding the email below regarding the open support requests with Cority, which includes the support ticket for the Compliance Report (Case Number: 00484688).

 

Update on the Compliance Report (as of 08/01/26)

 

Cority have pointed us in the direction of an existing hard-coded report called ‘Exposure Group Compliance’, which meets some of our requirements. However, as this report is hard-coded, we’re unable to amend it. We had already started building our own compliance report, but due to the lack of direct linkage between Surveillance Exposure Groups and Clearances, we weren’t able to report compliance accurately. This is why the issue was subsequently raised with Cority Support.

 

Below is Cority’s latest response:

 

“I understand this can be quite challenging to manage. If you need a similar report built in Query Builder, you would need to liaise with our consultants, as creating this type of report requires significant time and falls outside the scope of customer support.

Our functional consultants manage training and configuration support within our Professional Services team. Would you like me to put you in touch with our Sales Account Management Team to provide a quote for working with our consultants on this topic?”

 

Best wishes, 

 

James

 

From: James Salas Guillen 
Sent: 07 January 2026 11:18
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Christopher Sanders <christopher.sanders@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Pending Cority support requests

 

Hi Kevin,

 

As discussed on Monday, below are the items that should be raised at this Friday’s supplier meeting.

 

1. Smart Notes – ability to save as draft or edit after saving

Status: In progress - with Cority Support (Case: 00476764)

Raised: 11/11/2025

This is still with Cority Support. It would be useful to understand whether this is an issue specific to our system or a known limitation more generally. This is lower priority, but an update would be helpful.

 

2. Health Surveillance (SEGs) Compliance Report

Status: In progress (support requested from Cority) (Case: 00484688 and 00479415)

Raised: 06/01/2025

Related ticket: 25/11/2025 (same issue, different report)

 

This relates to accurately reporting compliance for clients enrolled in Health Surveillance. Specifically, we need to be able to determine whether a client is compliant (clearance certificate in date in relation to their Health Surveillance enrolment) or non-compliant (expired or no clearance).

Currently, there is no direct link between Health Surveillance enrolments and their corresponding Clearance Certificates. This issue has come up previously during the DWH work that Chris and I were involved in, but it has become increasingly important as we rely more on these reports operationally.

 

The key questions for Cority are:

Is this link something that exists elsewhere in the system but is not currently exposed in reporting?

If not, Is this on their product roadmap?

are we expected to manage this via workarounds?

 

As this is currently high priority, we will have to manage with a workaround but it isn’t ideal.

 

Response from Cority Support (26/11/2025):

“Thanks for joining the call with me today. Upon further testing the reports, the issue raised falls under the expertise of our Professional Services (PS) team, typically managed through our Cority Advantage Program (CAP). Please reach out to your CSM or, if you need help connecting with one of them, let me know and I can assist.”

 

Best wishes, 

 

James

emails.subject: Fw: Payroll Module Process Review (Case No: 67285209) Follow-up and Next Steps
emails.body:
Hi Mike,

I sent the below to Amanda and wanted to share it with you for awareness.

Best,
Kevin


________________________________

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Sent: Friday, January 09, 2026 12:50 PM
To: Amanda Mcdonnell <amanda.mcdonnell@theaccessgroup.com>
Cc: Tracy Roberts <tracy.roberts@admin.ox.ac.uk>; Joanna Taylor <joanna.taylor@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: Payroll Module Process Review (Case No: 67285209) Follow-up and Next Steps 

Hi Amanda,
Happy New Year!
It seems this has fallen off our radar, so I wanted to follow up and agree how we progress this.
Following our Payroll Module Process Review session on 20 October 2025, we spent time walking through retrospective appointment and salary changes, pension recalculation behaviour (particularly for tiered schemes), FPS considerations, and payslip handling. The session was helpful in exploring current system behaviour and highlighting areas that would benefit from further review.
As this activity sits under the agreed Payroll Module Health Check, it would be good to now move this forward into a more formal outcome. We would welcome a follow-up review meeting this month to complete the review and formalise the findings, confirm any conclusions on current behaviour versus best practice, and agree any recommendations or next steps.
Please let me know your availability over the coming weeks so we can get a session booked.
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 

HR Systems |People Department

emails.subject: Payroll Module Process Review (Case No: 67285209) Follow-up and Next Steps
emails.body:
Hi Amanda,
Happy New Year!
It seems this has fallen off our radar, so I wanted to follow up and agree how we progress this.
Following our Payroll Module Process Review session on 20 October 2025, we spent time walking through retrospective appointment and salary changes, pension recalculation behaviour (particularly for tiered schemes), FPS considerations, and payslip handling. The session was helpful in exploring current system behaviour and highlighting areas that would benefit from further review.
As this activity sits under the agreed Payroll Module Health Check, it would be good to now move this forward into a more formal outcome. We would welcome a follow-up review meeting this month to complete the review and formalise the findings, confirm any conclusions on current behaviour versus best practice, and agree any recommendations or next steps.
Please let me know your availability over the coming weeks so we can get a session booked.
Best,
Kevin



Kevin Lelitte

Functional Analysis - Team Lead 


HR Systems |People Department

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX

University of Oxford <http://www.ox.ac.uk/> 







Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details>

From: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Sent: 12 December 2025 15:12
To: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk> >; James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: Re: OSM Service Requests message

 

Hi Rob,

Just to add to this - You may wish to discuss this with the relevant stakeholders, as we would require formal sign-off before proceeding.

Below is an example of the message we would propose sharing with customers. The intention would be to add this to the H&S automated email triggered when OSM support tickets are raised.

 



 

Best,

Kevin

 

 

________________________________

From: James Salas Guillen <james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:james.salas@admin.ox.ac.uk> >

Sent: Friday, December 12, 2025 3:01 PM
To: Rob Green <rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk <mailto:rob.green@safety.ox.ac.uk> >
Cc: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk <mailto:kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk> >
Subject: OSM Service Requests message

Hi Rob,

I wanted to check whether you would be happy for us to add the message below to our H&S automated email when raising OSM support tickets.

Since mid-October, we’ve seen a noticeable increase in OSM service requests, with volumes remaining high throughout November and now into December. Given the current very limited support capacity, I think it would be helpful to manage expectations more clearly while reassuring users that queries are still being handled in line with our SLA and backlog priorities.

The two-working-day timeframe aligns with our agreed response targets, but adding this message should help provide context, reduce follow-up emails, and avoid duplicate tickets.

Proposed wording:

“We aim to respond within two working days, though times may vary due to limited support capacity. All queries are managed through our service management system, with time-sensitive matters prioritised when required. To avoid delays and duplicate tickets, please refrain from sending follow-up messages. Thank you for your understanding.”

Please let me know if you’re happy for this to be added, or if you’d like the wording adjusted.

Best wishes,

James Salas

Senior Functional Analyst

HR Systems | People Department | University of Oxford

IRIS: https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/incident-reporting

 

Find us online <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/>  | Contact the team <https://hrsystems.web.ox.ac.uk/contact-details> 

 

 

 <https://safety.admin.ox.ac.uk/everydaysafe>

I wanted to follow up on the ongoing Talent appraisal template issue we discussed.
I have now cancelled the related change request, as the proposed scripting work would only address the immediate symptom and does not prevent the issue from recurring. At present, there appears to be no mitigation or control available within the product, which leaves us exposed to the same risk.

Let me know if you wish to proceed as this will require need to be approved by Marie

Kevin

Sent: Tuesday, December 16, 2025 10:31 AM
To: Kevin Lelitte <kevin.lelitte@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Cc: Marie Cooksey <marie.cooksey@admin.ox.ac.uk>
Subject: People Forum - 20 November 2025 


Dear Kevin,

 

I hope this finds you well.

 

I wanted to get in touch as I understand you were unable to join us for the in-person People Forum on Thursday 20 November.

 

If you sent your apologies and the message didn’t reach us, please let me know — occasionally things slip through the cracks, and I want to make sure we’re not missing a communication from you. Otherwise, I would be grateful if you could share what prevented you from attending the session.

 

These events are valuable opportunities for us to come together and strengthen relationships with colleagues across the department. If you are unable to attend, it’s useful to understand why, to ensure we can maximise the value – and attendance - of future events. If there are barriers that make these events difficult for you to attend (timing, workload, format, etc.), I’m happy to discuss how we can make them more workable.

 

I look forward to hearing from you.

 

Best wishes,

 

Markos

 

 

 

Dr Markos Koumaditis FCIPD (He/Him)

4 Worcester Street, Oxford OX1 2BX
